people.name: Aakash Kesavarapu
people.bio: Aakash is a writer and performer based out of New York. He performs stand up all over and improv at UCB, the Second City New York and the Brooklyn Comedy Collective. His credits include Asian & New York Comedy Festivals (two different festivals).

people.name: Aaron LaRoche
people.bio: Aaron LaRoche is an absurdity enthusiast/ comedian based in NY. He can be be seen performing with his indie teams My Momma’s Biscuits and Branded Silk as well as at Second City. Alongside comedy he plays drums in the bands Skorts and Ghost Kitchen.He used to perform with Lloyd teams Dog People and Dimepiece and is excited to enter a new era with the new Harold team Tip Toe. If you don’t know the Harold is a longford improv style brought to NY by way of Chicago by the UCB 4, originall…sorry. I have to stop. The police are at my door.

people.name: Aaron Wilson
people.bio: For more than a decade Aaron has been a musical director for improv, sketch and musical comedy at Upright Citizens Brigade, performing in Magic To Do, Diamond Lion, Quick and Funny Musicals, and Baby Wants Candy. As a film and TV composer, his work has been featured in ads for Fabletics, VH1, The History Channel, and Discovery.

people.name: Abby Blanchard
people.bio: Abby Blanchard is an actor and improvisor originally from Da Bronx, NY. You can see her performing with Jinx on Lloyd Night. She’s also just really happy to be here!

people.name: Abra Tabak
people.bio: Abra Tabak is an actress, improviser, and writer based in New York City. She has appeared in The Other Two, Dickinson, Late Night with Seth Meyers, FBI, and more. She’s performed in and hosted numerous improv and sketch shows at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre such as Grandma’s Ashes and ASSSSCAT 3000.

people.name: Achilles Stamatelaky
people.bio: Achilles is a veteran UCB writer, director, and performer. He is currently the Artistic Director of UCB Theatre NY. Shows include ASSSSCAT 3000, Airwolf, Harold Night, and Maude Night. He is also a screenwriter (Broad City, MTV, Broadway Video) and has appeared in many national commercials. Achilles is a long-time improv and sketch teacher and has taught TV pilot writing at NYU. He is a member of WGA East and SAG-AFTRA.

people.name: Acquah Dansoh
people.bio: Born in Virginia and raised in Miami, FL Acquah fell in love with comedy watching Eddie Murphy along with other legends like Richard Pryor, Gene Wilder, Martin Lawrence and Bill Murray.The only thing he loves more than comedy, is baked goods.

people.name: Adam Cawley
people.bio: The Second City Mainstage, Just for Laughs, and three-time Canadian Comedy Award winner, including Best Male Improviser. TV credits include St. Denis Medical (NBC), Ghosts (CBS), MayDay (Discovery+), Baroness Von Sketch Show (IFC), Workin’ Moms (Netflix), and NFL on FOX sketches. Former staff writer for the International Emmy-nominated Workin’ Moms and current writer for CBC’s Because News . His podcast, The Backline , takes him around the world performing comedy.

people.name: Adam Cayton-Holland
people.bio: Adam Cayton-Holland is a nationally touring comic who has appeared on Conan, Corden, Comedy Central, and a bunch of other great stuff that starts with the letter “C.” Along with his cohorts in comedy troupe, The Grawlix, he created, wrote, and starred on the TV show, Those Who Can’t, which aired for three seasons. The Grawlix also host a popular podcast called, “The Grawlix Saves the World.” Adam has six albums you can listen to wherever you do that sort of thing, as well as a special, “Wallpaper,” currently streaming on Hulu. His first book, Tragedy Plus Time, is available everywhere.

people.name: Adam Frucci
people.bio: Adam has been performing at UCB and other theaters since 2005. He wrote and directed the feature film “The Disruptors,” was the Head of Development at streaming service Dropout, and founded the comedy website Splitsider. He recently moved back to NYC from LA and couldn’t be happier about it.

people.name: Adam Keller
people.bio: Adam Keller is a NC-born, NYC-bred, LA-based actor and improviser (he has lived in three places). Selected credits include – Supportive White Parents, Degrassi: The Unauthorized Musical, Love Chicken, Assistant City. Adam performs musical improv with Small Town Mayor in Sing Out, Louise! at UCB, and with indie teams All Show No Boat, Aunt Hill, and more.

people.name: Adam Mueller
people.bio: Adam Mueller is an improviser based in Austin, TX. He performs in various troupes and is pumped to be performing with The Rose at DCM26. His main personality trait is that he’s tall.

people.name: Adam Winney
people.bio: Originally from Kansas City, Adam is a comedian who very much lives in a musical theatre universe. He plays original comedy songs on the ukulele, and even has a drag alter ego named Sugar Free, named after her gorgeous case of Type 1 Diabetes.

people.name: Adrian Snow
people.bio: Adrian Snow is a Los Angeles based actress and writer. Originally from Houston, Texas, she received her BFA in Acting from The Theatre School at DePaul University in Chicago. She can be seen in the recent seasons of The Unicorn, Kidding, L Word: Generation Q and is a writer on Maude.

people.name: Adrianna Diaz
people.bio: Adrianna Diaz, otherwise known as Adri, is an LA based comedian. She does stand up, improv, sketch and produces her own successful comedy show, “Fresh Fish Variety Hour”. She’s performed at numerous famous spots like UCB, The Comedy Store, Hollywood Improv, Hollywood Fringe and various places in LA. She is commercially repped by Reign Agency, managed by Avalon Management but is seeking theatrical representation.

people.name: Adrien Pellerin
people.bio: Adrien Pellerin is a writer-actor based in NYC. His digital series, Night Crew, was released by Comedy Central after winning the Comedy Central Development Award at New York Television Festival. His series Early To Rise won the Audience Award at SeriesFest and was released by DUST. And Adult Swim released his short, STAYDIUM. He’s written and performed in multiple musical sketch comedy shows, and co-created Stupid Music Videos, an unscripted digital series. He produces Shitzprobe (The Improvised Broadway Musical) and has music-directed numerous improvised musicals all over the world, including Baby Wants Candy at the Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe Festivals, and Blank! The Musical and Your Love Our Musical in NYC. His work has also been featured by Funny Or Die, Vulture, Mashable, UCB, The PIT, and NYC Public Access TV. Insta: @adrienpellerin More at www.AdrienPellerin.com

people.name: Adrienne Ianniciello
people.bio: Adrienne is a New York City-based actor and writer. At UCB NY, she wrote and performed original characters on Characters Welcome and was an actor on Maude Night. Her sketch team Young Douglas has been featured in NY Comedy Festival, Toronto Sketch Festival, Chicago’s IoFest, Vulture, & Time Out. She has 2 mustaches, 50 wigs, and a BFA in Drama from NYU.

people.name: Ahsohn Williams
people.bio: Ahsohn is a DJ/Comedian/Writer based in Los Angeles, Ca. As a DJ, Ahsohn has traveled the country curating the perfect party for each and every crowd he encounters. As a comedian, he strives to bring that same energy and ethos to the stage.

people.name: Aidan O'Connor
people.bio: Aidan is a Chicago deep dish king, turned Los Angeles pressed juice aficionado. She devotes her time to performing sketch comedy and improv at numerous theaters around Los Angeles. She is so excited to be a part of UCB’s Maude night! Aidan is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Aidan Toole
people.bio: Aidan Toole is from Virginia and likes hats. He wrote and edited Batman Begins: The Visual Album – a fan edit replacing Hans Zimmer’s score with Soundgarden and the like. Ask him about graphic design, or catch him on his improv team Siblings (Siblings 50th Show Anniversary, Del Close Marathon, Cagematch, Hartford Improv Festival, The Rat) and so much more!

people.name: Aimée Lutkin
people.bio: Aimée Lutkin is a writer and performer from New York City. She previously performed on Lloyd Night in NYC, Harold Night in LA, and sketch teams at The Magnet Theater, which also hosted her one-person show, Peggy’s Orchard. She is the author of one book, The Lonely Hunter, with Penguin Random House. Aimée appeared on a single episode of The Other Two and would enjoy being on TV again, if anyone ’s interested.

people.name: Air Durnell
people.bio: Air Durnell is a Los Angeles–based comedian, writer, and performer who has performed on UCB LA’s Betty Night and UCB NY’s Maude Night. Their writing has appeared in McSweeney’s, Reductress, Clickhole, AWF Mag, and The Women in Comedy Festival. Air was a winner of the 2021 Yes And Laughter Lab and was later named a finalist for the 2025 ATX TV Festival Pitch Competition and ScreenCraft Pilot Competition with their original comedy pilot Camp Exodus .

people.name: AJ Lubecker
people.bio: AJ is said to, “Kill it with sharp one-liners,” by the Chicago Tribune and described as “off-beat and intricate,” by the Daily Herald.

people.name: Akmal Tajihan
people.bio: Akmal Tajihan has been performing comedy in New York since 2016. In that time he has performed at UCB and still performs there. Catch him being a problem both on stage and the internet.

people.name: Al Samuels
people.bio: Actor, writer, improvisor. Creator/co-star on NBC’s “Sports Action Team”, “The Venue” (winner Comedy Central Pilot Competition). Second City MainStage Chicago, Second City National Touring Company.

people.name: Alan Johnson
people.bio: Alan Johnson is a writer/performer from Dallas, TX. He has an MFA in Dramatic Writing from NYU, the funniest school of all time. Alan hosted a podcast called On Comedy Writing, which was featured on The AV Club, Lifehacker, and Vulture. He’s currently on WE team TUCCI, a member of the Artistic Committee at The Pack Theater, and hosts/produces Mr. Horsepower, a monthly variety show at UCB. He is currently a writer on the UCB sketch team Smokeshow. Follow him on Twitter and Instagram @alanjohnson____

people.name: Alan Starzinski
people.bio: Alan Starzinski is an actor, improvisor, and writer that has been a performer at the Upright Citizens Brigade since June 2007. He has written and starred in the over a dozen one man shows. He is most known for playing young Brett Kavanaugh on American Crime Story: Impeachment . He was a performer in the 2016 JFL New Faces Characters. He is represented theatrically by Griffin Talent.

people.name: Alana Johnston
people.bio: Alana Johnston is for real hilarious.

people.name: Alejandro Rios
people.bio: Alejandro Rios is an LA based improv and stand-up comedian, writer, and video editor originally from Texas. He has done shows all over Los Angeles at places like The Comedy Store, The Hollywood Improv, The Upright Citizens Brigade, The Ice House…blah blah blah the same credits that everyone has BUT what he really wants to brag about is how one time when he was a PA on a TV show, Sam Elliot clasped his hand on Alejandro’s shoulder and said, “You did good work today Alejandro.” Now THAT’S exciting.

people.name: Alessandra Migliaccio
people.bio: Alessandra is an actor, voiceover artist, and performer living in Astoria, NY. She has performed in exactly a million shows over her long time with UCB, including Sketch cram and most recently her big win in Gays Vs Italians in The Assignment. She’s just happy to be here, thanks!

people.name: Alex Benjamin Weber
people.bio: Alex Benjamin Weber is originally from Chicago, Illinois, and is currently a student at the University of Southern California, where he is pursuing his BFA in Musical Theatre. After arriving at USC, he discovered a passion for improv comedy, as well as writing and performing sketch comedy through joining ‘Commedus Interruptus’. Since then, he co-founded the social media group, ‘Barrel Spider’ – which he and his friends use as a platform to share their comedic ideas and put on live comedy revues. Alex also knows how to do four push-ups. He can’t figure out how to get to five.

people.name: Alex Berg
people.bio: Alex Berg has been a regular fixture at UCB LA since it opened, first with Sentimental Lady (est. 2006), and then with Convoy (est. 1845). In that time he’s managed to teach, coach, artistic direct, and do pretty much everything else that can be done at a comedy theater (exterminate rats, get injured during a show, paint the stage, normal things like that). If you try to talk to him after a show and he seems cagey, he’s overstimulated and needs to go home. If he’s not cagey, you better be ready to talk about whales or squid or mushrooms or jellyfish or whatever crap he’s reading about this month because that’s all you’ll get out of him.

people.name: Alex Bigelow
people.bio: Alex “Biggs” Bigelow is a writer and comedian who hails from Chicago but now lives in Los Angeles. Biggs is currently a writer on “Only Murders in the Building” season 6 and has worked on the show since Season 3. She studied improv at UCB and performs regularly with SHAG- a popular all women’s improv group.

people.name: Alex Bueermann
people.bio: Southern California native Alex Bueermann is an Actor/Writer/Improviser/Cool Guy living and performing in LA! He can be seen often on the Eastern Side of LA performing at The Shared Experience Studio, WGIS, the Pack, Outside In Theatre, or the Clubhouse! Most notably, he was seen getting kicked in the face as the Referee for the UCB Midnight Rumble. And finally, his webseries “I’m Not a Virgin Why Are You Asking?” debuted in August of 2025 and can be found on his Instagram!

people.name: Alex Cheng
people.bio: Alex is a Brooklyn-based writer, performer, and filmmaker. He has performed at the Kennedy Center and reported for NPR, and he once directed a short film that screened at a small sculpture park outside Minneapolis. (It was a beautiful evening.) At UCB, he appears on Harold Night and with Asian AF.

people.name: Alex Dickson
people.bio: Alexandra Dickson (Alex) is an actor and improviser living in Brooklyn, NY. She started her acting career in Chicago as an understudy for the Second City Touring Company, went to Circle in the Square for acting school, and began performing and teaching at Upright Citizens Brigade NY in 2013. She can now be seen performing at Caveat Theater in And Scene and at UCB in The Stepfathers and Asssscat! You also may have heard her on Everything is Alive; various Audible podcasts like Trust Fall, White Hot Heist, and Summer Oath; or seen her in that one episode of Last Week Tonight or maybe a commercial. www.alexandra-dickson.com

people.name: Alex Fernie
people.bio: Alex Fernie is a writer/director/performer originally from New Hampshire, the state where famously terrible president Franklin Pierce was born! He got his start performing sketch and improv at UCB. He has written and/or directed things like Adult Swim’s Childrens Hospital and NTSF:SD:SUV::, Seeso’s Bajillion Dollar Properties, HBO’s Last Week Tonight, Hulu’s Hotwives of Orlando as well as created original pilots for a bunch of different places. He’s appeared in front of the camera on Reno 911, Conan, Terriers and Key & Peele and more. He used to be allergic to cats, but not anymore.

people.name: Alex French
people.bio: Alex is a New York City-based writer and performer who’s appeared on Late Night with Seth Meyers, The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon, and a Wendy’s commercial that only aired on TBS. He’s also written kid-friendly content for the Calm app and Wondery’s Little Stories Everywhere.

people.name: Alex Guaglianone
people.bio: Alex is a Brooklyn based writer who’s had several screenplays advance in the Nicholl Fellowship, as well as Austin Film Festival, and the Sundance Labs. He has a sci-fi comedy feature optioned with Lucky Chap and Votiv media. He wrote and directed a short film, “The Debut”, that was selected as a finalist in the Just for Laughs Short Film Showcase. He’s been a writer for several seasons of UCB’s Maude Night, where he’s gotten sketches featured in Vulture’s “Best Comedy Shorts of the Month”. His indie team, “Kiss on the Lips”, enjoyed a three-year run doing a monthly show at the UCB. He’s also one of the directors of Sketch Cram.

people.name: Alex Kagy
people.bio: Alex Kagy is a New York-based comedian, writer, and performer. He got his start with the Improv Asylum in Boston and returns to direct shows. He can be seen at the Upright Citizens Brigade on Lloyd Night, performing at The PIT in NYC and clubs across the country with his comedy band, Tall Boy Special. Check them out on Spotify!

people.name: Alex Paisley Stedman
people.bio: Alex is a comedian, writer, and actor based in LA. This means she spends most of her time doing improv, musical improv, making “content,” avoiding parking tickets, eating El Pollo Loco, and overcommitting to bits. Ah, the starving artist life. Check her out on The Craft and Unsupervised Musical Improv.

people.name: Alex Song-Xia
people.bio: Alex Song-Xia is a writer, actor, and comedian from Toronto by way of New York. They have written for TV shows including Rick and Morty, Praise Petey, Little Demon, and The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. On screen, they can be seen in The Week Of (Netflix), High Maintenance (HBO), and Dimension 20 (Dropout). Alex began taking classes at UCB New York in 2012 and has since written on Maude Night, performed on Harold Night, toured with the UCB Touring Company, and played with ASSSSCAT. Their proudest role yet is that of father (to their dog).

people.name: Alex Waddell
people.bio: Alex is an actor, improvisor, and writer based in NYC. He performs on Harold Night weekly with Mugshot!. He’ll make you laugh one of these days.

people.name: Alex Yang
people.bio: Alex is a Brooklyn based comic originally from Philadelphia. You can find him performing improv at Lloyd Night with Grunch and with his indie team Cabin Fever. You can also find him doing stand up wherever and whenever anyone lets him.

people.name: Alexa Russo
people.bio: Alexa Russo is an actor, comedian, and recovered musical theatre major from the wild wild West Coast! After playing the recurring character, “Hazel”, in Disney Channel’s Austin & Ally, and the intensely hyped, “Blaze”, on Nickelodeon’s Henry Danger, Alexa has become known for her kooky personality, goofy laugh, and infectious energy. Her main mission is to spread light, laughter, and joy through her talents. Alexa can also be found rolling hand-crafted Js and advocating for body positivity and sexual liberation! Love to all.

people.name: Alexander Maystrik
people.bio: Alex is an Improviser/Creator from Katy, Texas. He currently performs on UCB Lloyd night and bi-weekly shows with WGIS House Team Toretto. He also is apart of a comedy duo called Durafest2 on Social Media (TikTok: 676K Followers, Instagram: 85K Followers).

people.name: Alexander Noland
people.bio: I’m just a little guy in a little show.

people.name: Alexandra Nader
people.bio: Alex Nader is a New York City actor, writer, and comedian. You may have seen her on shows like America’s Got Talent! (NBC), Story Pirates SPTV (PBS), or in her recent nationwide commercial for Bob’s Discount Furniture. She was a Forbes 30 Under 30 semi-finalist, and her sketches have been featured by Funny or Die, Occupy Democrats, and The Huffington Post. She’s currently a house team member on Maude Night, The Upright Citizens Brigade’s premier sketch show. She is a proud Venezuelan-Lebanese American and Massachusetts stan.

people.name: Alexandria Conti
people.bio: Alex is a teacher and performer who got her start doing improv at Penn State in 2014. After being UCB trained in New York, she moved to Pittsburgh where she currently teaches and coaches at UCB, Arcade, and other theaters around the city. She performs with teams Fondue for Two, The Mon, and Barbara, a Mother.

people.name: Alexandria Iona
people.bio: Alex Iona is a comedian, actor, and improviser living in Brooklyn, NY. You can catch her on UCB’s Harold Night. Alex can play the Vanderpump Rules theme song on a steel drum. SAG eligible.

people.name: Alexis Lambright
people.bio: Alexis is a NYC-based comedy performer, writer, and voice actor. She can be seen doing standup, storytelling, and improv all over (just book her on a show! She loves that!). You can catch her every 3rd Thursday of the month in “Entirely From Memory” at Littlefield in Brooklyn.

people.name: Alexis Murphy
people.bio: Alexis is a ColdTowne Theater trained improviser who’s been performing in Austin for 8 years – the last 3 of those years every Sunday with All Night All Night All Night.

people.name: Alexis Rhiannon
people.bio: Alexis started her improv career at UCBTNY on the teams Horse Girl (Lloyd) and Highwater (Harold) before moving to Los Angeles in 2019. Since then, she’s been on the Harold teams Cheeks, Hearsay, XOXO and This Is She, garnering rave reviews like, “Never stays out, always complaining about her bedtime.” During daylight hours, Alexis writes for a tech startup and cleans up after her cat Bonebag.

people.name: Alfred Aquino II
people.bio: Alfred Aquino II is a writer, performer and filmmaker from Long Beach, California. He has been a writer on Maude with the teams The New Deal and The Summer of, has performed for Characters Welcome/Betty, has been featured on the Asian AF and Filipino AF shows, and has had a run of his show, “Gilfred” at UCB. He currently content creates (makes memes) for Xbox and can be seen dancing in a pair of giant pants on TikTok.

people.name: Ali Ghandour
people.bio: Ali Ghandour is a performer, teacher, and current UCB Artistic Director. His past and present shows include ASSSSCAT, Bangarang!, Benetton, and The Dragons. Some TV credits include The Unicorn, Superstore, The Guest Book, Goldbergs, and Drunk History. Ali was also selected as a performer for the ABC Discovers Talent Showcase, but his mother is still most proud of a commercial where he ate a big sandwich.

people.name: Ali Gordon
people.bio: Ali Gordon performed for nearly a decade at UCB New York on Lloyd Night, Harold Night, Maude Night, and with Rumpleteaser Musical Improv. A recent resident of Los Angeles, you can now find Ali performing musical improv with Candy Apple Red and Baby Wants Candy. Ali also writes musicals ( Power Trip , Gross Prophets ) and novels ( We Have Reached the End of Our Show ). You can find out more at www.whatdoesaligordondo.com !

people.name: Ali Grusell
people.bio: Ali is originally from Maryland and moved to LA over a decade ago to pursue a career in entertainment. Since then, she has had every job imaginable, but her favorites are improvisor and actor. Special shout out to the time she worked briefly as a welder.

people.name: Alice Brenner
people.bio: Alice Brenner is a writer, comedian, and actor originally from Scottsdale, Arizona. She studied Screen-acting at Chapman University, where she was one of the first members of the school’s sketch team. As part of her stand up, they have created the show Dry Mouth which runs monthly in Los Angeles. She regularly performs across town and you should come see a show!

people.name: Alice Polocoser
people.bio: Alice is an LA-based comedian, performing stand-up and characters all around the city and improvising with her Lloyd team at UCB. In many ways, she is a real housewife.

people.name: Alice Wetterlund
people.bio: Alice Wetterlund is a stand-up comedian, improviser and actor originally from Minneapolis, Minnesota. In addition to her tv appearances doing stand up on Conan, Comedy Central, and her hour special ‘My Mama is a Human and So Am I’ streaming on amazon, you can see her in SYFY’s critically-acclaimed series Resident Alien.

people.name: Alisa Hernandez
people.bio: Alisa Hernández is a born and raised Austin, TX improviser. They are a graduate of ColdTowne’s improv school and a two-time cast member of The Rose. In addition to improv, they are an actor, writer, musician, songwriter, and podcaster.

people.name: Alise Morales
people.bio: Alise Morales is an actor, writer, and performer on UCB’s Betty Night. She is a 2023 CBS Showcase alum and the co-host of the Go Touch Grass podcast on Headgum.

people.name: Alison Rich
people.bio: Alison is an actor, writer, and director. Her narrative short Pathological (written, directed & starring) premiered at the 2024 Sundance Film Festival. Her narrative short Training Wheels premiered at the 2022 Sundance Film Festival. Her debut narrative short The Other Morgan premiered at SXSW. Alison’s acting credits include The Goldbergs , The Other Two , Party Over Here , Drunk History , and more. She’s written for The Goldbergs , Saturday Night Live , and Billy on the Street . She’s also directed commercial and social media spots for various brands including Arrae and Metro PCS, in addition to directing for Roku, the PGA Awards, and CollegeHumor.

people.name: Aliya Kamalova
people.bio: Aliya Kamalova is a writer and performer currently residing in Los Angeles. She has written for Hallmark, Lionsgate, McSweeney’s Internet Tendency, Team Coco Digital, Reductress, and the UCBLA Maude. For 5 years, Aliya co-hosted and produced The Best Friends Show, a storytelling show performed monthly at Meltdown and The Virgil, as well as in festivals like New York Comedy Fest and Riot Fest L.A. She currently performs in a two-women interactive tech-comedy show called Password 1234 that she co-wrote and co-directed with her comedy partner, Kim Seltzer. Aliya is the host of the comedy podcast, Science Communicatorz, on the Consider This Media network. You can see her independent sketch pilot All This For What? here: https://vimeo.com/kimandaliya/atfw

people.name: Alli Wulfert
people.bio: Alli is an LA-based actress, singer, and writer. Select credits include A Scream in the Dark (Amazon Prime), My Dating Disaster (rel. 2026), Shut Up and Do (rel. 2026). You may have seen her on the interwebs (@alliiiwulfert), where she shares comedy sketches. Catch her on Harold Night with her team, S.O.F.T. Eeeep!!!

people.name: Allie Jennings
people.bio: Allie Jennings has been doing long form improv for over a decade. Currently she is an actor and a writer who resides in LA. In the future, she hopes to one day belong to The Cat Fanciers’ Association.

people.name: Allie Kokesh
people.bio: Allie loves ghost stories, hand-building ceramics, and tennis. She does not love that she lost to John Cena at the WGA Awards.

people.name: Allie Kroeper
people.bio: Allie improvises alongside 7 geniuses in “Cereal Mascots” as part of Armory Comedy. They also perform sketch at the Magnet Theater on veteran team, “Money & Things”. Sometimes they produce “The Anti-Gay Agenda: A Satirical Sketch Comedy Show” throughout NYC.

people.name: Allisha Edwards
people.bio: Allisha Edwards is a performer and writer based in Brooklyn. Raised in a military family, she hails from the diverse strip-mall that is Northern Virginia. She recently premiered her sketch play “Night TV” off-broadway at ARS NOVA ANT Fest. Currently you can find her on the 2024 UCB Maude Sketch Team “VILLAIN!”, the all black improv team “PWI” (magnet, PIT, CAVEAT), and in her love advice improv show Shoulda/Coulda/Woulda (BCC). Other credits include assistant director at the Vineyard Theatre, 2024 Literary Intern Vineyard Theater, Bleecker Street BIPOC Mentee, Finalist SNL/UCB Mentorship, SPACE on RYDER farm greenhouse residency finalist, among others.

people.name: Allison Reese
people.bio: New to Maude Night, Allison Reese is a Queer, Black Comedian/Writer known for her Kamala Harris impression on TikTok (Viewership in the millions worldwide.) Allison recently made her film debut in the Judd Appatow film BROS and was named a 2022 Just For Laughs New Faces of Comedy. She also voiced the characters Lydia Parker and Nina Powell on Stephen Colbert Presents: Tooning Out the News on Comedy Central. Allison is an alumnus of the 2022 CBS Sketch Comedy Showcase and The Second City Chicago.

people.name: Allison Shea Reed
people.bio: Allie is a Canadian-American improviser, playwright, screenwriter, and actor—and an unrepentant Monty Python and the Holy Grail superfan. She can be found performing musical improv with Sing Out, Louise! at UCB and with indie teams Emotionally Available and Occasional Goblins around LA. A proud founder of RedWit Theatre, she creates work that spotlights neurodivergent and underrepresented voices. Her plays and pilots have been recognized by the Austin Film Festival, the Los Angeles International Screenplay Awards, and beyond.

people.name: Ally Beardsley
people.bio: Ally Beardsley is a writer and improviser who spent 10 years begging God to be straight while surrounded by dirt-biking Christian hotties. They spent 3 years in the cast at the legendary Boom Chicago (curiously not located in Chicago, but rather The Netherlands) and had the pleasure of publicly working out their gender in front of millions of people on YouTube while in the cast of CollegeHumor. You can find them at any show listed here or in their Dungeons & Dragons actual play show, Dimension 20!

people.name: Ally Bonino
people.bio: Ally Bonino (she/her) is pleased as punch to be back with her favorite band of humans. Last year, she made her Broadway debut in SUFFS, and since its closing in January, has been featured prominently on the Farmington River in her new kayak. For more non-kayak-related info, check out allybonino.com, or say hey on Instagram @allybo123.

people.name: Ally Davies
people.bio: Ally Davies (She/Her) has completed her final year at the University of British Columbia. With years dedicated to the university’s Improv club as both a performer and club executive, she is excited to end her wonderful time with UBCI at the 2026 DCM!

people.name: Allyn Pintal
people.bio: Allyn Pintal is a writer and performer living in Los Angeles. She was born in the Philippines and raised in the San Francisco Bay Area. You can watch her sketch collaborations with Wong Fu Productions on YouTube, as well as her own original sketches on her Instagram. She loves romances books and taking long walks through the cemetery.

people.name: Allyson Phillips
people.bio: Allyson is beyond thrilled to be back for her fourth season of Harold Night at UCB! She has performed comedy all over the world from Scotland to Australia and all over the USA. She is also currently waiting to start her run on Sunday Company at The Groundlings Theater.

people.name: Alyssa Davis
people.bio: Alyssa wears a trash bag and runs around as part of the Award-winning alt avant garde ‘improv’ group Bad Magicians and performs regularly with the musical improv teams Baby Wants Candy, Shamilton, and It Was a Dark and Stormy Night. She has been to the Adelaide and Edinburgh fringes and can’t wait to go there again! She loves British TV, musicals and Survivor.

people.name: Alyssa Eells
people.bio: Alyssa Eells (she/hers) is a content creator, comedian, and gay icon based in Los Angeles. Her character comedy team, Back Pocket, has a residency at Upright Citizen’s Brigade Franklin. She wrote and stars in the series Pain in the Neck , an official selection of Dances with Films. Alyssa was awarded the 2017-2018 Trustees Scholarship from the National Academy of Television Arts & Sciences, and has presented awards at the Pacific Southwest Emmys since 2018. She graduated from Emerson College in 2021 with a BFA in Comedic Arts. You can find her @itsalyssaeells on all platforms.

people.name: Alyssa Sabo
people.bio: Alyssa Sabo is a comedian, writer, and actress who is a current performer on Maude night. She has been on so many game shows that she actually lost track. Check her out on STARZ mini series “Gaslit” or on Tik Tok where she reads her old Christian teen journals (@alyssasabo)

people.name: Amadeo Fusca
people.bio: Amadeo has toured the world with his signature character bit and now hourlong solo show “The Conductor” recently playing Edinburgh Fringe, NY Comedy Fest, Netflix is a Joke, Moontower Austin, SXSW and Soho Theatre in London. His TV/Film credits include: NCIS, That Damn Michael Che, Marvel’s Daredevil and the upcoming feature Alto Knights. His social security number is 170-64-53…

people.name: Aman Adumer
people.bio: Aman Adumer is a TV writer and performer. He wrote on Grownish, YWC, and My Adventures with Superman. He’s acted in stuff too.

people.name: Amanda Barlow
people.bio: If a group of comedy writers stood around a fire saying incantations, Amanda would likely appear as a result. You can find her on Netflix, Apple TV, in just about any theater around LA performing improv, and now, you can see her every other Monday night at UCB! She’s an actor, a writer, a director *and* she can cry on cue. Incredible.

people.name: Amanda Bonar
people.bio: Amanda is an LA-based improviser, sketch comedian and producer. Past and present shows include The Sober Show, Dick Cannon Wins the Lottery, Do the Dumb, Wild Indie Nights and House Party.

people.name: Amanda Breen (AB)
people.bio: Amanda Breen (AB) is an improviser and writer who wears many hats, literally and figuratively. AB is the Director of Improv and the Training Center Director for UCB. They teach improv at UCB NY, Second City Brooklyn, and the Brooklyn Comedy Collective. AB performs weekly with ASSSSCAT, biweekly with veteran team Duchovny, and around the city with indie team soft. They also produce and host a monthly show “What the Hell? An Incredible Improv Show” which brings together veteran and up-and-coming improvisers to put on a damn good improv show, some might even say… an incredible improv show.

people.name: Amanda Giobbi
people.bio: Amanda performs improvisational comedy, sketch comedy and character showcases. She is both a writer and an actor. She has been in film, television and commercials. Amanda is represented by Micky Shera at Innovative Artists New York and Ken Treusch at Bleeker Street.

people.name: Amanda Lehan-Canto
people.bio: Amanda Lehan-Canto is currently writing and performing with her Maude team and also performing her solo show, “Keep it Together, Woman!” right here in Los Angeles. She’s also Sunday Company Alumni at The Groundlings Theater and a regular cast member on the Youtube show, SMOSH.

people.name: Amechi Ngwe
people.bio: Amechi Ngwe is a improviser and writer based in Houston, Texas. He has performed with Can’t Tell Us Nothing for over 10 years. They were winners of the national Contest4Improv4Humans in 2018 and the 2024 & 2025 Funniest Team In Houston competitions and have performed at numerous festivals across the country.

people.name: Amey Goerlich
people.bio: Amey performed at UCB NY from 2001-2016 and was on the Harold Team Filth, Krompf & Film Noir. She moved to LA and taught at Westside Comedy. She now owns and runs Denver’s Best Improv Theater Chaos Bloom Theater.

people.name: Amir Khan
people.bio: Amir is an actor and comedian that started out at UCB New York, where he was a regular performer. He was on Harold Night with Promises and Days of Thunder, as well as a member of Asian AF New York. Outside of UCB he performs character and sketch and has even appeared in a couple of movies. Heck, he’s even taught at a few theaters! He can currently be seen performing around Los Angeles as part of the team Goat Sauce.

people.name: Amy Charowsky
people.bio: Amy is an actress, singer, and improviser based in Brooklyn. She started her improv journey at UCB in 2008 and can currently be seen with the musical improv group Mona Lisa at the Magnet Theater.

people.name: Amy Hessler
people.bio: Amy Hessler is an actor, comedian, dancer and choreographer who grew up in Midlothian, Virginia. She has been a UCB main stage performer in LA since 2016 appearing in Harold Night, Mess Hall, Legitimate Goddamn Theatre, The Score and more! Amy has made several TV appearances including Atypical on Netflix and recurring roles on NFL comedy shorts on Fox. Amy also wrote, produced and starred in 30 and Booked, a comedy mini series that was accepted into the LA film festival Dances With Films. Amy’s unique intersection of dance and comedy was highlighted in long running LA variety show Tarantina, where she played Alabama Worley with audience “participant” Quentin Tarantino himself. Amy is currently producing a comedic play in Los Angeles she co-wrote titled Forrest Gump a One Woman Show.

people.name: Amy Knop
people.bio: Amy performs, directs and teaches improv at UCB and all about Austin, Texas. Raised by cheesesteaks and the Phillie Phanatic, her shows at DCM26 include: Teacher Feature (guest), Candy Necklace (Harold), and The Rose (reality TV parody). As past managing director of a festival (Austin Sketch Fest), Amy wants to give a shout out to everyone sweating it out behind the scenes to make this happen.

people.name: Amy Poehler
people.bio: Amy Poehler is one of the founders of the Upright Citizens Brigade and is one of Hollywood’s most versatile and sought after talents. She is perhaps best known for her starring role on the Emmy-nominated NBC comedy series Parks and Recreation as well as her performances on SNL from 2001-2008. Poehler’s film credits include Moxie, Wine Country, Sisters, A.C.O.D., Inside Out, Baby Mama, Blades of Glory, Mean Girls, and Wet Hot American Summer, the former of which she directed. She will return in her role of Joy in Inside Out in 2024.

people.name: Amy Vorpahl
people.bio: Amy Vorpahl is a comedy internet nerd known for her big laughter. You might have seen her on various Dropout TV shows including “Breaking News,” “Um, Actually,” and “Dimension 20.” She’s also a professional Dungeon Master, and has published adventures and jokes in D&D official hardback books. You should say hi to her–she’s really nice!

people.name: Ananya Roy
people.bio: Ananya Roy is a performer based out of Austin, TX and a cast member of Y’all We Asian and Father Figures. Off stage you can find her making matcha, exploring thrift shops in Austin, and tending to her too many houseplants.

people.name: Anderson Bandy
people.bio: Anderson is an actor and comedian from Fayetteville, Arkansas. She can be seen all around LA performing with her team, Pyramid Scheme, or hosting trivia. If you’ve got a check, she’s got a talent 😉

people.name: Andrea Coleman
people.bio: Andrea is a Brooklyn based stand-up comic, storyteller, playwright and lawyer originally from Virginia. Her long running law comedy show “Wack or Woke? Andrea Coleman Judges the Law” was featured in the NY Times, TimeOut NY, NPR and aired on PBS. Andrea has performed stand-up and storytelling at Joe’s Pub, Comic Strip, Women in Comedy Festival, San Francisco Sketch Fest and Austin Sketch Fest. You can currently see and hear Andrea on AppleTV and Amazon Prime in either of her 2 reality shows, “Iconic Justice” and “Hot Haus.” Catch her inspiring and hilarious solo show “3 White Guys Named John” about how John Hughes, John Mayer and Jon Bon Jovi changed her life.

people.name: Andrei Buna
people.bio: Andrei is a comedian and producer based in Brooklyn, NY. He hosts and producers a weekly comedy show at the Alligator Lounge every Sunday, as well as two other monthly shows at The Gutter and Lume Studios. He’s also 6′ 5″. Pretty nice.

people.name: Andres Parada
people.bio: Andres Parada is a comedian from a tiny town in Southern Arizona. He performs at UCB on Harold: Headbutt, Maude: Smokeshow, and is alum of Characters Night. When not at UCB you can catch him on Groundlings Sunday Company.

people.name: Andrew Barlow
people.bio: An actor, comedian, and CODA based in New York City. Credits include Netflix’s upcoming Black Rabbit, Sunscreen (premiered at SOHO International Film Festival), and Dancing Girl (SheARTS NYC). At UCBNY, Andrew hosts the weekly competitive improv show Cage Match. He performs stand up across the city. Andrew is a Child of Deaf Adults, fluent in American Sign Language. He is also Colombian and Italian, speaking neither languages, disappointing both grandmothers.

people.name: Andrew Coppola
people.bio: Andrew is an LA based actor, comedian, and fantasy basketball runner-up.

people.name: Andrew Farmer
people.bio: Andrew Farmer is a disabled writer/comedian who has been commissioned by The Museum of Modern Art, but also wore a bunch of wigs as a “New Face” at the Just For Laughs Festival. TV writing credits include Miracle Workers , Best Foot Forward and Praise Petey in which he voices various spinsters, cowboys and himbos. He’s developed projects with Universal, CBS and National Lampoon and is a recipient of The Jonathan Larson Grant for Musical Theater. With Anna Drezen, he hosts the podcast “Scary Stories to Tell on the Pod.”

people.name: Andrew Grace
people.bio: Andrew Grace is an actor and comedian originally from New York City. He is currently on Harold Night. Andrew got a surfboard when he moved to Los Angeles and has used it 2 or 3 times.

people.name: Andrew Hobson
people.bio: Andrew is a filmmaker as well as a stand-up and improv comic based in Los Angeles. He was in the Groundlings Sunday Company.

people.name: Andrew Knox
people.bio: Andrew Knox is an actor, writer, and improviser who grew up in Vermont. He went to college to play football but, like so many linebackers, quit to do improv and acapella. Before moving to LA he wrote and performed in 4 original revues on The Second City’s Mainstage and ETC Stage in Chicago. More recently Andrew was named a New Face of comedy at the 2021 Just For Laughs festival.

people.name: Andrew Lindh
people.bio: Andrew Lindh is an Actor and Comedian from Charlotte, NC. You can see him starring in ads for Dave & Busters, Amazon, Carnival Cruise, NASCAR, FOXSports, Realtor.com and Birddogs. He performs monthly at UCB with his sketch team ‘Smokeshow,’ and his two man sketch team ‘Aquamen’ won UCB’s Sketch Showdown every month for a whopping TWO YEARS! While he doesn’t quite have a southern accent, he does have a southern drawl that can be exaggerated upon request.

people.name: Andrew Miculka
people.bio: Andrew Miculka is an improvisor, writer, and stage actor living in Austin, Texas. He is one half of the improv duo ‘The Blade’ as well as one of many clowns on the variety show ‘GUTS’. Having trained at ColdTowne Theater in Austin, Andrew has been stinking it up for the last 10 years.

people.name: Andrew Santiago
people.bio: An OG from pre-hipster Brooklyn, Andrew Santiago is living his comedy and acting dreams out in LA. He is a member of the Maude-team-turned-weekend-team Moon Goon. TV appearances include NBC’s Night Court NBC, CBS’s The Neighborhood, and Apple TV+’s Mr. Corman. He’ll be making his feature film and animated TV debuts in 2024. He’s an abuela’s boy, through and through.

people.name: Andrew Warner
people.bio: Andrew is an Actor, Improvisor, Comedian, and Podcaster who’s been plying his creative trade in the city since aught eight! He is an alum of sketch comedy collective BoogieManja, and currently performs around the city with indie sketch comedy group 15 Minutes Away. He has studied acting at HB Studio under the tutelage of Jonathan Lynn and Mercedes Ruehl. He has an improvised satirical “podcast” called Cranium Infinitum that he invites you all to enjoy on TikTok. He loves movies more than life itself and will engage you in conversation on that topic until the heat death of the universe.

people.name: Andrew Young
people.bio: Andrew is an improviser, writer and composer. He’s trained at UCB since 2015 and traveled the world doing comedy festivals. He performs Musical Improv, Regular “Talkie” Improv, and writes and performs Hopefully-Better-Than-Regular Musicals. He also has a film degree and does Visual Effects…but don’t we all?

people.name: Andy Daly
people.bio: Andy Daly starred in and executive produced the Comedy Central series Review and has played regular or recurring roles on Veep, Lessons In Chemistry, Modern Family, Black-ish, Silicon Valley and Eastbound and Down. He’s been heard in lots of animated shows including Bob’s Burgers, The Simpsons, Rick and Morty, Adventure Time, Big Mouth and Harley Quinn and is the creator and host of the podcasts The Andy Daly Podcast Pilot Project and Bonanas For Bonanza. Film credits include Semi-Pro, Kimi and Unfrosted. Andy lives in Los Angeles with his wife, two amazing kids and two charming dogs.

people.name: Andy Vega
people.bio: Andy does improv all the time with clint, makes basketball videos @nbaresdev, and acts as Chief Justice on Abolish Everything (stream now on Nebula).

people.name: Andy Wiggins
people.bio: Andy is a musician and improviser based in the NYC area. He performs regularly at the Magnet Theater as a musical improviser and accompanist, and has appeared in the Off-Broadway musical improv show The Others of Oz. He has worked as a touring musician and is the creator of Painting a Song, a songwriting collective with over 100 volumes of original music. Andy holds a PhD from Drexel University, where his work focused on music technology.

people.name: Angel Yau
people.bio: Angel Yau is a comedian, actor, writer, and filmmaker from Queens, NY. She created work for UCB digital teams Scraps and Lash, performed on Maude team What Would Do, and the character team Good Bones. She currently does improv with Joy F*ck Club and performs as “Quirky Rice” in the musical comedy group AzN PoP! Angel produces and hosts Asian AF, Asian American Film Thing, and Shoes Off, Mouth Off—shows featuring AAPI artists, filmmakers, and storytellers. She is best known for her lobster material. She’s been featured on HBO Max, Netflix Is a Joke, and BBC Reel. She gave a TEDx talk about peeing.

people.name: Angela Batuure
people.bio: Angela Batuure is a writer and performer based in Los Angeles. She is a recent graduate of Columbia University and has studied improv at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade in NYC and LA. She is the recipient of the SNL/NBC Scholarship. In her spare time, she enjoys talking about her Rubik’s cube and pretending to be Idris Elba’s daughter to sneak into movie premieres.

people.name: Angela Batuure
people.bio: Angela Batuure is a writer and performer based in Los Angeles. She is a recent graduate of Columbia University and has studied improv at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade in NYC and LA. She is the recipient of the SNL/NBC Scholarship. In her spare time, she enjoys talking about her Rubik’s cube and pretending to be Idris Elba’s daughter to sneak into movie premieres.

people.name: Angela Giarratana
people.bio: Angela Giarratana is an actor, singer, writer, and musician from Los Angeles, California. Angela’s been performing at UCB on house teams such as Musical Mess Hall (Almost Midnight), Maude Night (Moon Goon) and Characters Welcome (Sweet Ass). Most recent credits include: Maggie (HULU), MINX (HBO), Danger Force (Nickelodeon), and the ABC Discovers LA Talent Showcase. Outside of UCB, you can catch Angela performing with Team Starkid and Smosh.

people.name: Anika Ramlo
people.bio: Anika Ramlo is an actress and writer with a BFA in acting from University of North Carolina School of the Arts. In 2022, she performed her solo show “Risk of Elopement.” Now she spends time writing and filming with her sketch group. Anika is a proud LA native excited to be a part of Lloyd Night for the first time!

people.name: Anisha Jagannathan
people.bio: Anisha is an actor, comedian, and writer in Los Angeles and an alumni from USC, from where she started her journey with improv and UCB. You can catch her on Shameless, Good Trouble, and Quantum Leap. She aims to please but is working on that in therapy. Laugh at her!

people.name: Anja Boltz
people.bio: Anja Boltz is an improviser who studied comedy in Chicago at Second City and Columbia College. Now, in LA, she’s deeply involved in the scene through WEimprov, WGIS, The Harold Experience, and of course, UCB. She owes her wit to her Irish grandma, and her silliness to her Malagasy ma.

people.name: Ann Marie Yoo
people.bio: Ann Marie was born and raised in New York and is the definition of a stereotype: Carnegie Hall performing, Ivy League engineering, figure skating Korean American. She is extremely excited to get to be stupid -oops- funny!(?) again on Maude Night with returning team Ponyboy! TV credits include Law & Order: OC (NBC), The Equalizer (CBS), The Late Show with Stephen Colbert (CBS), East New York (CBS), That Damn Michael Che (HBO), Gotham (NBC). Film credits include Dumb Money (Craig Gillespie), Side Effects (Steven Soderbergh). Theater credits include CLUE, Alma Baya.

people.name: Ann Nelson
people.bio: Ann Nelson is a writer and comedian based in Brooklyn. She is a writer for Dropout, and writes for Game Changer and Make Some Noise. She is 1/3 of the sketch group Tiny Idea, which has amassed over 500,000 followers and 90 million views across social media (@tinyideastuff). Ann’s work with Tiny Idea has been featured in Vulture and NYMag. She loves to smile 🙂

people.name: Anna Aubry
people.bio: Anna Aubry (she/they) is a comedian, actor, and writer based in Brooklyn. She can be seen on Harold Night performing with Club Gator (previously: Kiss Kiss, The Library, CONGRESS), Brooklyn Comedy Collective’s house improv shows What Makes [You] Laugh? and Home Teams, and as host of the duo-improv show Are Jon and Anna Better as Friends? around NYC. As a theatre actor, Anna specializes in new work and has performed off-Broadway and downtown. Her solo show Doom Tank sold out its five night run at the Under St. Marks Theater.

people.name: Anna Bezahler
people.bio: Anna Bezahler (she/they) is an actor, writer, and improviser from Philadelphia, PA. They perform at UCB with the Harold team Cowboy Mama and were a series regular on the Tubi original show, The Z-Suite . As a writer, Anna creates pilots and comedy projects that explore anxiety, sexuality, and the weirdness of life. You might see them in commercials, sketches, on the internet, and on the Comedy Bang! Bang! podcast. You can also check out their own podcast, Anna and Isabella Do Improv!

people.name: Anna Garcia
people.bio: Anna Garcia is an actor/comedian originally from Beverly Hills, MI, who most recently wrapped a role on Showtime’s Party Down reboot, having recently appeared on HBO Max’s Emmy-winning series Hacks and FX’s long-running comedy, It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia. Additionally, Anna was a featured performer in the 2022 CBS Comedy Showcase, and sort of embarrassingly had a brief Tik Tok making stint, amassing her 200K+ followers. To see more of Anna, catch her hopping in her friends shows at UCB, hosting her own variety show, Scrambled Eggs, appearing as a cast member for College Humor’s subscription media service, DROPOUT, or her feature film debut in FLY ME TO THE MOON.

people.name: Anna Mejorada
people.bio: Anna Mejorada is a Manhattan-based writer, improviser, and proud pom mom from Texas who moved to NYC ASAP. She performs regularly with her improv team Siblings and was selected for the 2026 UCB Training Center Showcase, where she also received a diversity scholarship. She loves animals, sewing, napping, dancing, and exploring local parks.

people.name: Anna Rajo
people.bio: Anna Rajo is an improviser, actor and writer from Los Angeles, CA. You can see Anna perform with her Harold team Lohan certain Monday nights at UCB Franklin. If you’re looking closely, you can spot Anna in a few commercials and on one episode of NCIS: Los Angeles. Anna is incredibly talented and should be hired.

people.name: Anna Rock
people.bio: Anna Rock is an actor, director, and teacher based in NYC! She was forged in the fires of improv and sketch on teams such as Feral Christine, New Greta, and Deb, and is currently riding a 5+ year career wave in Shakespeare. Happy to be back at DCM for the first time since 2017!

people.name: Anna Salinas
people.bio: Anna is a Cuban-Swedish TV writer, filmmaker, and actor originally from Florida. She’s written on shows including Loot (Apple+), Human Resources (Netflix), and Mo (Netflix). She’s also the creator of the webcomic Badcomixbyanna, and one half of the duo ‘John Baxter,’ known for their viral parody “A Star is Korn.”

people.name: Anne Lane
people.bio: Anne has performed at UCB on Harold Night and at the Groundlings as a Main Company Member. She has written for Rick & Morty and Tuca and Bertie.

people.name: Annie Bobbitt
people.bio: Annie Bobbitt is an improvisor, artist, and educator from Baltimore, Maryland. She is a big believer in finding fun in everyday moments.

people.name: Annie Bond
people.bio: Originally from a chicken farm in Texas, Annie Bond is a writer, musician, and actress. She also works as an advocate for other young breast cancer survivors like herself. And she loves all the dogs.

people.name: Annie Griggs
people.bio: Annie Griggs is a Los Angeles based writer from Chicago. She has written for UCB, Mcsweeney’s, Reductress, and comics for Oni Press. I was watching Marcel The Shell With Shoes on when I wrote this. Pretty good!

people.name: Anthony Atamanuik
people.bio: Anthony Atamanuik is known as the creator, executive producer, and star of Comedy Central’s THE PRESIDENT SHOW and its subsequent specials, A PRESIDENT SHOW DOCUMENTARY: THE FALL OF DONALD TRUMP and MAKE AMERICA GREAT-A-THON. Anthony has been performing and teaching improv at UCB since 2002 and is a member of the legendary improv group Death by Roo Roo. He can be seen recurring on the FX series WHAT WE DO IN THE SHADOWS as “Sean,” recurring on Adult Swim’s THREE BUSY DEBRAS, and his other TV credits include SEARCH PARTY, UNBREAKABLE KIMMY SCHMIDT, 30 ROCK, DIFFICULT PEOPLE, and BROAD CITY. Selected as one of Variety’s 2016 Comics to Watch, he has received critical acclaim and a fervent new fan base with his pitch-perfect Donald Trump impression.

people.name: Anthony Clark Griseto
people.bio: Anthony is an improviser and actor from Pennsylvania, not far from Scranton. He performs twice a month with the Lloyd team Hi Baby Gorgeous! Owning a dog is a very large portion of his personality.

people.name: Anthony DeVito
people.bio: Originally from New Jersey, Anthony DeVito is a NYC based comedian. He’s appeared on CBS The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, Comedy Central Stand Up Presents: Anthony DeVito, Comedy Central’s Adam Devine’s House Party, was one of the “New Faces” at the 2012 Montreal Just for Laughs Festival and has been on “This American Life.” In addition to stand-up, he was a writer on Netflix’s The Break with Michelle Wolf, wrote for the 2018 White House Correspondents Dinner, and he also has appeared on TV Land’s The Jim Gaffigan Show and HBO’s Pause with Sam Jay.

people.name: Anthony Gioe
people.bio: Anthony is a writer and performer based in Los Angeles.

people.name: Anthony King
people.bio: Anthony King is a Tony and Emmy-nominated writer, producer and performer. He co-wrote BEETLEJUICE THE MUSICAL and GUTENBERG! THE MUSICAL! for Broadway, and has written/produced for TV shows like Robbie (Paramount+), The AfterParty (Apple), Silicon Valley (HBO), Dead To Me (Netflix), Search Party (TBS/HBO Max), Broad City (Comedy Central), Wet Hot American Summer: First Day of Camp & Ten Years Later (Netflix), and more. For many years, he was the Artistic Director of the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in NYC. Also – he hates cotton balls.

people.name: Antoine Williams-Baisy
people.bio: Antoine is a Houston-born improviser and improv coach. He is most commonly found performing at Station Theater, but has been known to perform at Coronation Theater, Good Friend Theater, and local high schools. Antoine’s teams have performed in several large improv festivals, including the Chicago Improv Festival, Trill Festival, the LA Diversity in Comedy Festival, and the Del Close Marathons.

people.name: Anushka Thorat
people.bio: Anushka is a writer and moderately good person from Mumbai, India who moved to the US at thirteen and has been trying to understand ranch ever since. She currently works as the writers’ assistant on Abbott Elementary , the last comedy. Prior to that, she won the Humanitas David and Lynn Angell Award for her 1/2 hour comedy script. Anushka also worked for Judd Apatow and graduated magna cum Laude from Columbia University, neither of which her parents found particularly impressive.

people.name: Anya Johnston
people.bio: Anya is an actor/improviser originally from the SF Bay Area. There, she played pivotal roles such as The Magic Carpet in Aladdin Jr. before going on to study theater at UC Irvine. Currently, she works for an educational theater company where she performs stories authored by kids across Southern California.

people.name: April Rock
people.bio: April Rock is an Actor and Writer originally from New Haven, CT. You can catch her on Maude Night with her team Town Crier and doing sketch shows with her team Anointed Greens. She most recently served as a guest writer for Saturday Night Live , and as a staff writer for Tubi’s The Z Suite . Recent acting credits include Bel Air , People We Hate at the Wedding , and Studio C . When she’s not working, you can find April visiting million-dollar open houses because she’s nosy, or eating a triple dipper at the Chili’s in Encino.

people.name: Aran Abilock Clemmons
people.bio: Aran is a comedian, writer, and amateur video game designer from Brooklyn. He performs with the sketch group Queer Window, and has appeared in the NY Comedy Festival, SF SketchFest, and DC SketchFest. His solo show of original video games, Aran Plays a Game with You, has also appeared in ProjectorFest.

people.name: Ari Voukydis
people.bio: Ari has taught at UCBT, both in New York and LA, for more than 20 years. He has written, produced, directed and/or performed in roughly (and keep in mind this is not an exact number) one billion shows including ASSSCAT, Harold Night and WFQ (iykyk). He’s written for Ring Nation with Wanda Sykes, SNL Weekend Update, BuzzFeed, GQ, and Entertainment Weekly and has appeared on Conan, The Tonight Show, and almost 100 VH-1 shows like “The Best Week Ever” and “I Love the 80s.”

people.name: Aricia Skidmore-Williams
people.bio: Aricia (ah-ree-sha) is a Los Angeles-based writer, improviser, podcaster, and comedian. She hosts the award-winning podcast Even the Rich (Wondery), as well as the daily podcast Rich and Daily (Wondery), for which she received the Gracie Award for Best Co-Host/Ensemble in 2022. She performs improv weekly at UCB with her Harold team, XOXO. If she’s not recording her podcasts, doing improv, or tap dancing, chances are extremely high that she’s napping.

people.name: Arie Rich
people.bio: Arie Rich is a DC-based actor and improviser, an Ensemble Player and Harold program alum at Washington Improv Theater. She performs with four WIT ensembles: iMusical, Lena Dunham, Nox!, and You Better Call Tyrone — the first musical team to win FIST in its 18-year history. She is also a performer and writer for Bonnet Talk Comedy, whose show Help Wanted: Looking for a Black Woman will premiere at Atlas Performing Arts Center in D.C. June 21, 2026.

people.name: Ariel Gitlin
people.bio: Ariel Gitlin is a performer and comedy writer based in Brooklyn. Ariel is a member of the UCB Weekend Team, ‘The Foundation,’ with whom she’s performed at TOSketchfest, SF Sketchfest, NYC Sketchfest, NY Comedy Festival, and more. She was a writer for the Boogiemanja team ‘Both Hands,’ and she is currently directing the Maude Team ‘Dumptruck.’ Ariel has been featured in ‘Crashing’ on HBO, ‘The Iliza Shlesinger Sketch Show’ on Netflix, and The Truth Podcast. She is repped by Finer Entertainment Company. www.arielgitlin.com

people.name: Arik Cohen
people.bio: Born on a US Naval base in Japan and raised Jewish in a Virginia town that’s very much NOT Jewish, Arik’s outsider complex has helped him write jokes for MTV, BBC America, and more. He was a founding member of the long-running sketch team Bombardier, and currently directs sketch shows and teaches sketch comedy. He also writes and directs short films which have hit the festival circuit.

people.name: Artin Sarkisyan
people.bio: Artin Sarkisyan is a board-certified structural engineer, who diligently moonlights as an actor and improviser. Originally from Plovdiv, Bulgaria, he moves to Los Angeles, to study film but eventually transitions into engineering. He currently performs at UCB Harold night, and at many other comedy theaters in Hollywood. He sometimes performs even back home in Bulgaria.

people.name: Artoun Nazareth
people.bio: Artoun Nazareth is an improviser, actor, writer….and hypnotist! (Yes for real!) He is a series regular on the Biblical mockumentary The Promised Land , and he just finished touring with Paul F. Tompkins for Varietopia . His previous credits include Dumb Money , Ghostbusters Afterlife , The Muppets Now , The Conners , United States of Al , SEAL Team , Stumptown , The Starfield Videogame and a slew of Progressive Commercials. He performs weekly with UCB’s Harold team Dance Club Remix as well as hosting the weekly improv tournament Cagematch. He can also be seen on weekends doing improv with Leroy or sketch with his graduated Maude team Smokeshow. Follow @artounnazareth on instagram or listen to his podcast he started with his wife, The Cage Old Question (is Nicolas Cage the greatest actor alive…..spoiler: yes).

people.name: Ash Chen
people.bio: Ash Chen is a comedy writer who has written on UCB’s Maude Team “LA City Council” and is now writing for “The Family Business.” In addition to writing, she has also won the Academy Award for Best Actor for her portrayal of Jake LaMotta in the biopic drama Raging Bull and was further Oscar-nominated for her performances in Taxi Driver , The Deer Hunter , and Cape Fear .

people.name: Ash Herrild
people.bio: Ash Herrild is a Seattle-born actor, director, and comedian based in LA. When not acting or directing, Ash spends his time playing frisbee golf and hanging out with his dog, Debra. If you see this man around town, say “hi.”

people.name: Ash Wong
people.bio: Jester by birth, improvisor by choice. One who wears a name tag that just sings “I write absurd comedies, please ask :)”. Who would love to write for television, specifically a situational comedy starring Julia Louis-Dreyfus about a frazzled working woman… or something like that. Who would love to work with every creative under the sun (and the moon, they don’t discriminate).

people.name: Asher Perlman
people.bio: Asher is a comedian, a writer, and a cartoonist, based in Brooklyn. He has won a WGA Award and a Peabody Award, and has been nominated seven times for an Emmy Award. Currently he is a writer for “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert,” a cartoonist for The New Yorker, and a best-selling author of two books: “Well, This Is Me,” and “Hi, It’s Me Again.” He is an alumnus of The Second City, iO, and The Improvised Shakespeare Company. Follow along: @asherperlman

people.name: Ashleigh Hairston
people.bio: Ashleigh Hairston is an actor, writer, producer and NAACP, GLAAD, and EMMY nominated voice actor from Seattle, WA. She is currently a Co-EP and Co-Story Editor on an upcoming legacy series at Nickelodeon Animation Studios, and was recently announced as the new voice of the iconic Babs Bunny in Tiny Toons Looniversity, streaming this Fall on MAX. Her acting credits include, The Neighborhood, Single Parents, and Modern Family.

people.name: Ashley Everhart
people.bio: Ashley Everhart (she/her) performs original solo characters on UCB’s premier weekend character team Character Cabal. She can also be seen regularly at venues across NYC, including Union Hall, Joe’s Pub, and 54 Below, and has garnered awards from comedy festivals across North America. Her go-for-broke sketch and character comedy has amassed her a social media following of over 70,000. As a performer, she gravitates toward projects that champion women and the LGBT community, and she is known to incorporate her talents as a saxophonist, vocalist, and guitarist into her character and sketch work. She is an alumna of the Betty house teams Heyday, Big Noon, and Good Bones, and holds a degree in Theatre from Fordham University at Lincoln Center.

people.name: Ashley Grombol
people.bio: Ashley is a sketch writer with an acting background based in uptown Manhattan. She’s performed and written comedy at The PIT, Caveat, and obvi UCB. She wrote for Maude house team, Petty Crime, and is currently writing for house sketch team, Pamela. She has also written a years worth of haikus and a sassy baking blog, Domestic B(i)atch.

people.name: Ashley Parchment
people.bio: Ashley Parchment is a dynamic actress from Miami, FL who is passionate about her craft. Ashley is absolutely obsessed with improv & sketch comedy and is on a house team at the prestigious UCB Theatre. Her quick wit and knack for improv have not only entertained audiences but has led to numerous commercial and theatrical bookings. Ashley is also dedicated to empowering POC artists in the entertainment industry. As the founder of “It Takes A Universe,” she’s on a mission to create opportunities and amplify underrepresented voices in the world of film and television. As well as giving them a space to play with her sketch channel “Paper Pals Comedy.”

people.name: Ashley Rube
people.bio: Ashley Rube is a St. Louis based teacher, coach, actor, and 8-year veteran of the St. Louis improv scene. She teaches and tours nationally with Touch Baseball (featured on the Yes, Also Podcast), an mprov duo specializing in the monoscene. Ashley performs weekly with several independent and Improv Shop House Teams in addition to co-producing No Edit, Touch Baseball’s improvised one-act play in three acts. She is the Director of Operations and Business Services at The Improv Shop (STL) where she also serves on the faculty of the theater’s long form training center.

people.name: Ashley Ward
people.bio: Ashley is an improvisor, actress and singer who has performed everywhere from the Kennedy Center to The Sydney Opera House. She can be seen on Dropout TV’s Play It By Ear and in Hello Jack! The Kindness Show on Apple+ as Delivery Delores. She is also a Jeopardy champion!

people.name: Audrey Garrett
people.bio: “Smart” – BBC. Audrey Garrett is an improvisor, sketch writer and comedian trained by Philippe Gaulier, UCB, and Brooklyn Comedy Collective. She performs in NYC with sketch duo ‘Honey, Drop It’ at Club Cumming, Caveat and Edinburgh Fringe in 2024. “Stunning”- BBC Global News Hour affiliate.

people.name: Audrey Mattaino
people.bio: Audrey Mattaino is a Brooklyn-based comedy writer and improviser who has previously written for Boogiemanja Sketch Collective and Reductress. Sometimes she does claymation for fun.

people.name: Austin Elias-de Jesus
people.bio: Austin Elias-de Jesus is a Brooklyn-based comedian currently writing for Baby Todd on Maude Night. He is also a performer and writer on the indie sketch team Fun in Moderation. He has performed in the New York Comedy Festival, New York Sketchfest, Austin Sketchfest, and Philly Sketchfest, and his writing has appeared in The New Yorker, The New York Times, The Ringer, Slate, and a Google review of a diner that promised him a free dessert if he wrote something nice about them on the internet.

people.name: Austin Sanders
people.bio: Austin Sanders is an actor/writer/director based in New York. Austin has been seen doing improv shows with Entirely From Memory at Littlefield, Syndicated the improvised sitcom at the Armory, and the improvised 1940 sci-fi radio show X Plus One. He has also directed the sketch shows The Outer Zone at the PIT and New Wave Mixtape at the Magnet. Austin also has a new film appreciation YouTube channel called Popcorn Optimist and he’s very excited about it!

people.name: Austin Williams
people.bio: Recently named Just For Laughs: New Face in Comedy (Characters), Austin Williams is a performer on UCB Harold night known for her appearances on Comedy Bang Bang (Earwolf) and The Hawk (Netflix). You can also catch her on the viral sketch show Studio C (BYUtv) where she’s written and performed sketches with comedians like Will Forte and Pete Holmes.

people.name: Ava Bunn
people.bio: Ava Bunn is an actor, writer, and unicyclist from San Diego. Her solo comedy show “Cold Call” is currently playing here after a successful run at New York Comedy Festival. Previously, Ava wrote and performed in her comedy show “Hey Mom, Can You Pause the TV?” (Broadwater Theater) and recently starred in the staged reading of Alice Stanley Jr’s, “The Second Best School Shooting,” which was produced by Adam McKay and Shira Piven (The Elysian Theater). You can catch her showcasing new characters every month at Betty Night!

people.name: Ava Conway
people.bio: Ava Conway is heading into her third year at Virginia Commonwealth University, seeking a BFA degree in theatre performance. She has had the honor of playing on two VCU improv teams over the past year: Black Brown and Honey and The Stepkids. Ava is also a playwright with her plays “Shockingly Violent” and “The Bloody Diner” hitting VCU stages. Ava loves to focus on comedy in her writing and her performance, as to her, that’s where the truth lies. See her in VCU’s upcoming production of Romeo and Juliet!

people.name: Ava Wolesky
people.bio: I’m Ava Wolesky (she/her) and I am actor, eataly aficionado, improviser, and friend. In that order. Catch me performing with Entirely From Memory & LALAHAHA. NYU, Stewart Talent.

people.name: Avery Girion
people.bio: Avery Girion is a WGA writer, Harold Night improvisor and super cool third multi hyphenate. She’s written for Disney, 20th Studios and Tinder. 5’6″ and willing to be nude if her clothes can stay on.

people.name: Avery Lee
people.bio: Avery Lee is an LA-based writer, performer, improviser, and Asian person. In Chicago he performed at iO and Annoyance, and with The Second City Touring Company, and the Asian-American comedy group Stir Friday Night. Credits include “South Side”, and the film “Captive State”. He also co-created the NBC pilot and short digital series “Bobby & Iza”.

people.name: Ayana Taylor
people.bio: Ayana Taylor is an actor and Texas native, known and recognized internationally for her 70+ films at Dhar Mann Studios, a company that has amassed a following of 22 million subscribers in America alone. Ayana can be spotted in commercials, short films, and film festivals, where she won two Best Actress awards for her performance in Stuck in the Sand . Growing up watching shows such as Who’s Line Is It Anyway? and Saturday Night Live , Ayana has profound love and appreciation for comedy and has enjoyed training at Upright Citizens Brigade to hone her skills.

people.name: Aziza Stewart
people.bio: Aziza Stewart is a comedian, actor and writer from Pasadena, California. Since studying theatre at UC Irvine, she has been diving deep into the comedic waters of Los Angeles as a stand up comedian, a sketch writer/performer & an improviser. She’s your classic bisexual Sagittarius middle child. Lastly, she is excited to join The Faculty and write some funny stuff!

people.name: Bailey Gavulic
people.bio: Bailey Gavulic is an actress and improviser from Orlando, Florida. She can be seen in projects such as Fear the Walking Dead (AMC), The Sex Lives of College Girls (Max), The Pitt (Max), 9-1-1 (ABC) and more. She has been called “the girl next door, but a little feral” (in a good way) by casting directors and “the funniest person I know” by her mom.

people.name: Baker Saurage
people.bio: Baker Saurage is an NYC-based actor and writer. He is an NYU Tisch graduate majoring in Drama with a minor in Digital Art & Design. He has trained at the Stella Adler Studio of Acting, the Lee Strasberg Theatre & Film Institute, and Stonestreet Studios. Baker first fell in love with improv at just 11 years old through a small studio in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. At NYU, he was part of NYU’s premiere improv team, Dangerbox. He is also left-handed.

people.name: Balint Hancz
people.bio: Hailing from Hungary, via England and Canada, Balint Hancz is an improviser, writer, performer and data entry clerk. If you know an improv theatre in LA or Toronto, it is very likely that he performed there. He was an extra in a Ken Loach movie and once talked to Isabelle Huppert at a party. He made her smile.

people.name: Bam Alston
people.bio: Bam writes and performs comedy in New York City. He doesn’t know how to “snack.”

people.name: Becca Nix Tham
people.bio: Becca is a member of Stir Friday Night, Chicago’s longest running AAPI comedy team. She was featured at SF Sketchfest, Chicago Sketch Comedy Fest, and World Comedy Expo. You can follow her shows on IG @beccasayswhatnow

people.name: Becca Savoy
people.bio: Becca Savoy is an actor & improvisor from Massachusetts by way of Chicago. She has worked at Steppenwolf Theater, Goodman Theater, The Gift Theater, Northlight Theater, Griffin Theater, Greenhouse Theater, Chicago Dramatists, and others. Catch her on Lloyd night or around town with improv teams Platonica, Precious, and Zaby.

people.name: Ben Cumings
people.bio: Ben Cumings is an actor, improviser and writer from Chicago, Illinois. In Chicago, he has performed with the Second City where he was a Bob Curry Fellow and understudy for the 2021 Mainstage Revue, performed with the Harold Team Crocodile Nasties at the iO Theater, and has been part of numerous productions at the Annoyance Theatre including Splatter Theatre, Steamworks: The Musical, Scenes from an Italian Spaceship, and the house improv ensemble Fire & Beer. In Los Angeles, he performed with the sketch team Night Coffee at the Pack Theater.

people.name: Ben Dohring
people.bio: Ben Dohring is an actor, improvisor, and writer based out of Chicago. He’s performed on stages at Io, The Annoyance, and The Second City. He currently performs with Hark!, Wet Pizza, and Now That’s What I Call Sketch.

people.name: Ben Marshall
people.bio: BEN MARSHALL is one in the internet and cultural phenom comedy group, PLEASE DON’T DESTROY . They joined SNL three years ago as writer/performers and are the brain trust behind the show’s most viral sketches and digital shorts. Their first feature, THE TREASURE OF FOGGY MOUNTAIN, premiered in November ’23 with Judd Apatow producing for Universal. Ben co-wrote alongside John Higgins and Martin Herlihy, and they all starred and executive produced. In 2023, Please Don’t Destroy embarked on a 25-city live tour that had them selling out theaters and adding shows all across the country. New York Magazine/Vulture named them to Comedians You Should and Will Know in 2021 List , THR featured on the cover of their New York issue , and Variety named them to the New Power of New York List .

people.name: Ben Shiff
people.bio: Ben Shiff is a Jewish writer, musician, and performer originally from New York. Ben studied film at USC and currently is a production coordinator at Sony Pictures Animation. He performs Improv with his indie team Crowbar, and is making his UCB debut as part of Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Ben Siemon
people.bio: Ben Siemon is a LGBTQ writer and performer based in Los Angeles. As an experienced animation writer, he’s been on staff for shows including “Chibiverse” “Tiny Toons Looniversity,” “Ducktales,” and “TrollsTopia.” As a performer Ben is best known for his recurring role of “Grocery Clerk With Half an Eyelid” on “Crazy-Ex Girlfriend.” Ben is also a professionally trained puppeteer having performed with the Jim Henson Company’s show “Puppet Up!” Most people find him pleasant.

people.name: Ben Warheit
people.bio: Ben Warheit is an actor, writer, and artist. He has been a staff writer for Late Night with Seth Meyers on NBC since 2014, where he also regularly performs characters such as Seth’s nephew Derrick. Ben has written for the TV shows Animaniacs (Hulu), The Awesomes (Hulu), and developed his own pilot, Waco Valley, for Comedy Central. As an actor Ben is best known for his roles in the feature films Joker and A Rainy Day in New York. He can be seen next as Jackson in Netflix’s upcoming romantic comedy film, The Life List. Ben also has a bachelor’s degree in neuroscience, which might come in handy someday, who knows!

people.name: Ben Wietmarschen
people.bio: Ben Wietmarschen (pronounced “wheat-martian”) is an LA based comedy writer, improviser, and actor who currently teaches Sketch Writing classes at UCB. Ben has worked as a staff writer at Funny Or Die and has contributed writing to comedy institutions like The Onion , The Tonight Show , Just For Laughs, and Major League Baseball. He has also acted in movies and commercials, including ones for Hilton, Jim Beam, Google, Yelp, Wayfair, and one where he screams his head off for Take 5 Oil Change. He is co-creator of the long-running series Long Haired Businessmen (movie coming soon!). Ben is originally from Cincinnati and likes sports.

people.name: Benjamin Apple
people.bio: Benjamin’s work as a comedy writer and content creator has appeared across broadcast, cable, and digital platforms, including iconic entertainment outlets like The Onion, Funny or Die, and Late Night, and also in social campaigns for fast-growing DTC brands that rely on organic engagement (if you like that kind of thing).

people.name: Bennett Cohen
people.bio: Bennett Cohen is a comedian and musician from Los Angeles. Bennett performs at UCB with “Sing Out, Louise!”, at Clubhouse Fridays with Gravy Day and Tiny Fire, and on the indie circuit with Friday Friday.

people.name: Benny Wayne Sully
people.bio: Benny is a Sicangu Lakota actor. He’s appeared Off-Broadway and in Regional Theatre productions. He likes chess and cooking.

people.name: Bente Engelstoft
people.bio: Bente is a Mexican-Danish, first-gen American actor, director and 4-time Emmy award winning writer and producer based in Los Angeles. (It’s like “Brenda” without the “R”.)

people.name: Beth Appel
people.bio: Beth Appel is an LA-based writer/performer/director who currently writes for The Sex Lives of College Girls. As an actor, she has appeared in lots of commercials, television shows and movies, including Never Have I Ever, You, Comedy Bang! Bang!, Adam Ruins Everything, and Greener Grass. Previous writing work includes: Never Have I Ever, Comedy Bang! Bang!, Ty Burrell’s ABC Digital series, Boondoggle, and several projects for Comedy Central.

people.name: Beth Leckbee
people.bio: Beth graduated with a BFA from Texas State University and has graced the stages of Austin, New York, and Los Angeles. She was a founding member of the LA based musical improv group FRAUDWAY and has been a part of many amazing groups over the years. She is excited to be currently making sweet regency love with JANE AUSTIN IMPROV!

people.name: Beth Newell
people.bio: Beth Newell is the creator of the satirical website Reductress and author of There’s No Manual: Honest and Gory Wisdom About Having a Baby. Beth was named by Rolling Stone as one of the “50 Funniest People Right Now” and as one of Time Magazine’s “23 People Who Are Changing What’s Funny Right Now.” She gave birth to her daughter in the backseat of a Honda Fit.

people.name: Beth Stelling
people.bio: Beth Stelling is a stand-up comedian, writer and actress. She’s best known for appearing on CONAN, Jimmy Kimmel Live, and for her specials “Girl Daddy” on HBO Max and Netflix The Standups (season 1, episode 5). She’s written for the following shows: HBO’s Crashing, I Love You America with Sarah Silverman, The Last O.G. on TBS, Another Period on Comedy Central, Apple’s upcoming animated series Strange Planet and season 8 of Rick and Morty for Adult Swim. Beth is also a go-to hire for punch-up on feature films after getting her start as an on-set writer and Associate Producer for the summer hit Good Boys.
