Beth had a recurring role on Peacock’s comedy Rutherford Falls opposite Ed Helms and Jana Schmieding. She can also be seen in Red Oaks and Comedy Central’s Corporate.

people.name: Bethany Hall
people.bio: Bethany Hall is a writer, producer and comedian who spent 1,000 years on ‘The Chris Gethard Show’ on TruTV. She is the Creative Director of Comedy Initiatives at the Center for Media & Social Impact (CMSI) and Co-Creator and Executive Producer of GoodLaugh at CMSI. Hall is also proud to serve as the Creative Director of the Yes, And…Laughter Lab. She’s an Executive Producer on We Disrupt this Broadcast, a podcast made in partnership with CMSI and the Peabody Awards. She Executive Produced and co-created the series, ‘Thanksgiving’ on Amazon Prime as well as ‘‘By Us For Us,’ an original comedy by Color of Change. Hall has performed at Bonnaroo, SXSW, ‘Late Night with Conan O’Brien’ and as the TGS writer, Bethany, on NBC’s ‘30 Rock’.

people.name: Betsy Helmer
people.bio: Betsy Helmer is an actor and improviser, originally from Atlanta, GA. Her on-camera credits include: The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, FBI: Most Wanted, The Enemy Within, Goodbye Party, and The Launch. She has performed on stages across the country including: the Kennedy Center, New Stage Theatre, Pioneer Theatre Company and the NY Comedy Festival. She’s also toured with UCB’s Touring Company. She currently performs on Harold Night with ZUKO!

people.name: Betsy Sodaro
people.bio: Betsy is a comedian and actor from a little ski town in Colorado. You can see and hear her on TV, in movies and getting weird on stage with her friends at UCBLA!

people.name: Betsy Stover
people.bio: Betsy has been performing, writing, directing, and teaching at UCB since 1997. She is the host of the comedy podcast, “Why Mommy Drinks.”

people.name: Bill DiPiero
people.bio: Bill is a writer, actor, and comedian living in Brooklyn. You can find Bill on NYC stages performing improv and characters, or you can find him at a desk writing plays and TV pilots. Bill’s written work was chosen as an Official Selection at the NYTVF. Rare Crop, Bill’s production team with Paris Adkins, debuted short films at Tootsies VideoVault and hosted improv’s hottest show: The Assignment. All pretty good stuff, but, honestly, his parents would be proud of him no matter what.

people.name: Billy Domineau
people.bio: Billy Domineau is an unmistakably original writer/performer who has written for TV shows including Saturday Night Live , Family Guy , and In The Know . He has also developed shows with Fox Animation. Billy broke into the industry in 2016 thanks to the viral success of his post-9/11 Seinfeld spec script “The Twin Towers.” He is originally from Massachusetts and performed college improv and sketch at NYU with Dangerbox and Hammerkatz.

people.name: Billy Merritt
people.bio: Billy Merritt’s been performing and teaching at the UCB since its inception. Co-founding legendary Improv groups The Swarm and The Stepfathers in New York, and The Smokes in Los Angeles. Billy got his TV start on UCB’s Comedy Central show, that lead to multiple appearances on Late Night With Conan O’Brien, MTV’s Boiling Points, VH1’s Best Week Ever. Since moving to LA he’s appeared on great shows such as Reno 911, Happy Endings, Weeds, Parks and Rec and Brooklyn Nine Nine to name a few. Billy’s voice is heard on many commercials and cartoons, he’s the voice of Fryman from Cartoon Network’s Steven Universe, and host of Jameson’s Radio Network. Billy is a native Floridian and proudly maintains a Florida Man lifestyle.

people.name: Bini Yamin
people.bio: Bini Yamin is a stand-up comedian based in New York. In 2026, she made a webseries called Bushwick News Network with her friends Jacqueline and Dean Blizzard. She was a finalist in the ATX Pitch Fest in 2025 with her pilot co-written with her writing partner Sabrina Bhattacharya. She also produces an immersive comedy show featured in TimeOut called Giggle World at Dead Letters No. 9. But her greatest achievement must be her work as a pedestrian, as she has always waited for the light to change.

people.name: Blake Benson
people.bio: Blake Benson is an improvisor, game designer, and archetypal lesbian from North Carolina. She’s performed with Transpose Theatricals and The Joy Who Lived festival, and can currently be found her doing musical improv with Sing Out, Louise! at UCB.

people.name: Blake Edwards
people.bio: Blake is a writer and actor from Altadena, California (better than Pasadena). You’d never guess he’s a big nerd who went to Princeton and tutored the SAT since he plays the dumbest person you’ve ever seen in every improv scene. He was a segment producer on the last season of the NBC game show The Wall , and he performs at UCB with the Harold team CAKE and in Badpacks: An Improvised Trip, the show he co-created and co-hosts.

people.name: Blake Ung
people.bio: Blake Ung is a comedian with a background in stand-up, sketch, and musical comedy. A lot of people are saying he’s the hottest in the game right now. Currently seeking representation.

people.name: Bo Lucas
people.bio: Hello reader, I’m Bo! I’m a sophomore at Virginia Commonwealth University majoring in theater with an emphasis on Performance. I’m currently looking for representation and/or opportunities to get experience in the industry. Support your local theatres!!!

people.name: Bo Segrest
people.bio: Bo is a Boston-based writer and comedian. He has been performing at Improv Asylum for four years, was editor-in-chief of Yesterday’s Magazine, and is a member of the sketch comedy group Spare Tire. He loves listing off things he remembers about his life. One of those things was he saw a bird riding the escalator in an airport. Currently seeking writing/literary agent.

people.name: Bob Hsiao
people.bio: Bob is an improviser, former financial services Excel monkey, and dog lover who will 100% greet dogs before humans. He is an omnipresent fixture in the LA indie improv scene who can easily be spotted on stage most evenings at the Clubhouse, WE Space, or Shared Experience sporting an LA Kings jersey during hockey season. He has an unhealthy and unironic infatuation with the Harold and currently performs with his team High Score on Lloyd Night.

people.name: Bobby Austin
people.bio: Bobby Austin is a comedian aptly based in Austin, TX. Having started his improv journey in June of 2024, he’s officially entered his “terrible twos”. Buckle up. In the meantime, he’s excited to set sail with the cast of the Rose again for DCM ‘26.

people.name: Bobby Gross
people.bio: Baltimore based improvisor and person. Solid improvisor. Generally solid person.

people.name: Bogart Avila
people.bio: Bogart is a producer, performer, and massively influential business executive. He is a producer at Stapleview and the Head of Programming across music and comedy at Nico’s in Atwater Village. Bogart is also the founder of Swing Bigger Productions where they produce a wide range of live shows and festivals.

people.name: Bonnie Efird
people.bio: Bonnie is a Los Angeles–based actress, director, writer, producer, and improviser. She recently made her directorial debut with her rom-com short That’s My Girl and is developing her comedy pilot S.L.U.T.S., based on her mom’s wild Southern friend group. When not improvising with her besties, Bonnie makes branded TV/film/social content for healthcare companies (starring celebs, duh), obsesses over Dressage horses and cuddles her mini poodle, Charlie.

people.name: Brad Evans
people.bio: Brad Evans is a comedian who’s currently writing for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. Prior to that, he wrote for Billy on the Street, the podcast Comedy Bang Bang, and developed projects for Comedy Central and TBS. He was named a Comedian to Watch by Vulture and a New Face at the Just For Laughs Festival. With Nick Ciarelli, Brad wrote, directed, and starred in a bunch of comedy videos that you can watch on the awful website Twitter.

people.name: Brad Roberts
people.bio: Brad has been performing comedy since he was 11 years old and he’s just been getting worse and worse. He currently writes for the Fridge Magnet team on Maude Night. He has written and performed on Sketch Cram, The Trash King, and more at UCB and around NYC.

people.name: Brady O’Callahan
people.bio: Brady is a comedian, actor, improviser, writer, and music glutton based in Brooklyn, New York. He grew up in Cleveland, Ohio, where he learned to hate winter and love a losing baseball team. Brady writes a music newsletter called “Made You A Mixtape,” which features a collection of fresh tunes spanning all genres and eras delivered straight to your inbox every month. Brady works in the custom clothing business, where he dresses men for their weddings, careers, and daily lives. He also dresses himself.

people.name: Brandon Bassham
people.bio: Brandon has been at UCB since 2006. He’s been a member is sketch groups Bobby Chicago, Gramps, and Mixtape ’98. He’s a writer/director of filsm such as Fear Town USA, The Slashening films, and Bring On The Damned!

people.name: Brandon Dzirko
people.bio: Brandon is a Brooklyn-based writer, actor, improviser, and an across the board great hang. You can find him on Harold Night with The Prophecy. Outside of UCB, you can find him performing with his indie team Third Party or on TikTok @brandonandshane.

people.name: Brandon Gardner
people.bio: Brandon Gardner is a writer and improviser. He has performed and taught at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theaters in NYC and LA since 2007. Gardner co-wrote the short film DAVID which was nominated for the Palme d’Or for Best Short Film at the 2020 Cannes Film Festival. He co-created the half-hour comedy IN THE KNOW with Zach Woods and Mike Judge which was released on Peacock in 2024. This year, THE ACCOMPANIST , a feature film he co-wrote, will premiere at the Tribeca Film Festival.

people.name: Brandon Sornberger
people.bio: Brandon J. Sornberger is an actor, writer and improviser living in Los Angeles. He has recurred on Grandfathered, Switched at Birth and Adam Ruins Everything, and stars across Oscar-nominee Sally Kirkland in the feature film Buddy Solitaire. In 2000, he was accepted into NYU’s Neuroscience Doctoral Program. Instead he took a job in touring children’s theater as a rapping broccoli stalk.

people.name: Brandon Waters
people.bio: Brandon is a performer originally from New Jersey, like Bruce Springsteen or Snooki. He enjoys performing with Tucci – a team known for their distinguished style, Italian roots, and ability to identify cerulean.

people.name: Brandon Zelman
people.bio: Brandon is an improviser, writer, actor, podcaster and (most of all) etcetera. UCB Credits: Promises (Lloyd, Harold), Cage Match / Indie Cage Match (Host), Cardinal Goose (Digital Team), UCBW (Tag Team Champion). Podcast Credits: The Cat in the Hat Cast from Dr. Seuss/Wondery/Story Pirates (Host, Winner: Ambies Award for Best Children’s Podcast) and Who’s That Pokémon. Acting Credits: MTV, NJ Lotto, Nerdist. Writing Credits: “The Big Twist: A Musical Ozventure” and “BALLS: The Monster-Catchin’ Musical Comédy”.

people.name: Breck Cuddy
people.bio: Breck is a writer, performer and jack-of-all-crew-jobs. Typically known for playing monsters and murderers, he’s patiently awaiting his romcom role.

people.name: Brendan Dewley
people.bio: Brendan started writing comedy for local county board meetings in his Michigan hometown and is now a writer and performer in New York City. He co-created the sketch comedy team Craig, which has a sketch comedy residency at the UCB NY and The Players Theatre, where he writes and performs, as well as Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Brendan has been a finalist at the Austin Film Festival Comedy Spec competition. He currently works for NBC at 30 Rockefeller Plaza.

people.name: Brendan Jennings
people.bio: An alumnus of The Second City in Chicago. This New York born boy is an actor/writer who has many Hollywood highlights like A.P. Bio, Better Call Saul and ten to a thousand others. He has no lowlights. A first in this town.

people.name: Brennan Enright
people.bio: Brennan (he/him) is an occasionally funny character writer, performer and improviser. Before doing comedy full-time, “Bren” worked in finance for five years in Austin and NYC. Bren attended the University of Virginia where he discovered improv and performed as the mascot! Bren grew up in Charlottesville, VA, is the oldest sibling of four, and the proud son of Deirdre Enright and Rob Lee.

people.name: Brennan Lee Mulligan
people.bio: Brennan Lee Mulligan is the creator and GM of Dimension 20, co-founder and GM of Worlds Beyond Number and an executive producer at Dropout TV, CollegeHumor’s streaming platform. He has taught and performed at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, co-created the webcomic Strong Female Protagonist, and written dozens of LARPs for the Wayfinder Experience summer camp in upstate New York. He recently started putting a little bit of cinnamon in his morning coffee, which he has described as “a real game changer.”

people.name: Brett Maline
people.bio: BRETT MALINE is an award-winning, disabled multi-hyphenate. From small town Nebraska, he’s niche because he lives with a rare type of scoliosis and for a really long time drove a crappy PT Cruiser. Currently developing his half hour multicam with Party Over Here, he just came off a feature engagement with Running Point Productions and received an Adobe grant to develop another feature based on his short film WRONG GUY that was an official selection to Slamdance 2025. A 2024 CBS Paramount Showcase alum, he wrote on the 2nd season of Marvel’s Loki while developing his half hour comedy with Sony Pictures Television. Last year, Brett made his foreign film directorial debut with MAI , a film shot in Tokyo. With appearances on CBS, NBC, FOX, Amazon Prime, and Disney, Brett is a former cast member of the Groundlings Sunday Company and a UCB house sketch team veteran. His YouTube channel, Genuine Jerks, has been featured on the Today Show, Good Morning America, and Vulture.

people.name: Bri Giger
people.bio: Actress. Comedian. Writer. Voice Over artist. Improvisor, Rapper, DJ, Superwoman.

people.name: Bri Rodebaugh
people.bio: Bri Rodebaugh is a comedian and actor based in Los Angeles. She’s performed across Los Angeles and New York, and currently hosts the standup show That Rocks! With Bri Rodebaugh and co-hosts HEADLINERS: A Stapleview Comedy Show at UCB. Bri is also a monthly performer at the Upright Citizens Brigade with her improv team Tucci and has performed at Edinburgh Fringe Festival. Before performing full time, Bri was a dynamite mechanical engineer.

people.name: Brian Barrett
people.bio: Brian Barrett performed and taught at UCBNY for several years, primarily as a member of the Harold team Bastian. He currently resides in Birmingham, Alabama, where he performs at Faraway Theater with Pigeons and Ugly Baby. He is also the executive editor of WIRED magazine.

people.name: Brian Faas
people.bio: Brian Faas is an actor, writer and comedian — currently hosting videos for Page Six. He got his start as a cast member on Vh1’s Best Week Ever and recently appeared in You Hurt My Feelings starring Julia Louis-Dreyfus. He lives in Brooklyn with his husband and his dog, Dolly Barkton.

people.name: Brian Furey
people.bio: Best known for narrating paintings for the internet, Brian is also the host of both Honest History for Cracked, & Drunk Planet Earth at Easy Lover. He is a principal performer on And Scene at Caveat and former member of Lloyd, DCM New York All Stars, & Maude. CMU 2013

people.name: Brian Huskey
people.bio: With over 200 acting credits to date, Brian Huskey has earned the status as a “That Guy” actor appearing in the most cutting edge and popular comedies in TV and film. His TV work include Veep , Another Period , Childrens Hospital , New Girl , People of Earth and has played the fan favorite, Regular Sized Rudy, on Fox’s Bob’s Burgers for 15 seasons. In Huskey’s movie appearances, he has been paired with the likes of Seth Rogen in This Is the End , Neighbors , Neighbors 2 and The Disaster Artist and with Will Ferrell in Step Brothers , Land of the Lost and Semi-Pro . And his work in College Humor’s viral series “If Google Was A Guy” earned him a 2015 Webby Award for Best Performance. Huskey has also written two feature films for Paramount Pictures, Comedy Central’s Kroll Show , and earned an Emmy Nominations for his work on Comedy Central’s 2010 Night of Too Many Stars and Adult Swim’s Childrens Hospital . He starred in and co-wrote with Matt Walsh the original, fully improvised film A Better You . And he created, wrote and starred in two darkly comic television specials for Adult Swim, Mr. Neighbor’s House and Mr. Neighbor’s House 2 , the latter of which was chosen as a “Best of” for 2018 by The New York Times .

people.name: Brian McElhaney
people.bio: Brian is part of the sketch duo BriTANicK and has trouble finding his shoes.

people.name: Brian Olsen
people.bio: Brian Olsen is an actor, director and writer in NYC. He used to act all fancy with companies like The Pearl Theatre, Gorilla Rep, and the Washington Shakespeare Festival, but more recently he’s had way more fun as an improviser and as a member of the sketch team 15 Minutes Away and of UCB-NY’s Maude team After School Snack. Somewhat unconnectedly, but perhaps more interestingly, he’s also an author of queer sci fi and fantasy novels – more about that at brianolsenbooks.com.

people.name: Brian Perrault
people.bio: Brian is an actor/writer/comedian from Hingham, Massachusetts best known for his work on American Vandal (Netflix) and Players (Paramount+). He’s a graduate of the University of Miami’s conservatory program with a BFA in Musical Theater. As a writer, his work has placed in the Austin Film Festival, the Screenwriters Network’s TV Pilot Competition, the ScreenCraft Comedy Writing Competition and more. His pilot “Creative Differences” was named the runner-up in Brent Forrester’s TV Pilot Writing Competition. He can be seen at UCB every first and third Monday performing on Lloyd Night.

people.name: Bridget Holmes
people.bio: Bridget Holmes is a New York-based actor and comedian originally from Los Angeles, CA. She will be performing on Harold Night with the Tiptoe, and previously performed with the teams Falco, Highwater, Moose, & Slingshot.

people.name: Bridgette Rizkalla
people.bio: Bridgette is a writer and comedian born and based in Brooklyn, NY. You can catch her performing improv with her teams Raaaatscraps and Higgins, or doing standup around the city. Some things she’s done have been featured on Vulture, Elite Daily, Huffington Post and Funny or Die. Most importantly she will never be caught up on a single TV Show, so everything is a spoiler alert to her.

people.name: Brielle DeMirjian
people.bio: Brielle DeMirjian (“Brielle” to friends) is a writer/comedian/improviser from NYC. She is precisely 5’4” but aspires to be 5’7″, and her hands are 8mm longer than the universal average. “Brielle” (I’m her friend, I can call her that) has been a performer at UCB since 2017, where her performance is regularly considered “loud” and “while wearing boots in the summer”. She also performs monthly with her independent team Current Ex-Boyfriend, who champion emotional commitment, fluid character matching, and calling your mom every Sunday.

people.name: Brittany Charlotte Smith
people.bio: Brittany is a Canadian actor, singer, dancer, and an alumni of Toronto’s The Second City’s House Company. Selected credits include – The Naughty Nine (Disney), Heartland (Netflix/CBC), The Wedding Rule (Hallmark), I Won’t Let You Go (Lifetime), Suits (NBC). You can catch Brittany on Harold Night with her team CAKE and on Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Cailey Follet
people.bio: Cailey Follet grew up on a haunted boarding school campus in Wheeling, West Virginia. Now she’s a comedian, writer, and director. When she’s not thinking about comedy, you can find her playing old time fiddle or getting a tattoo.

people.name: Cait Raft
people.bio: Raft only needs one name, unless logistically you need them to have two names, in which case feel free to refer to them as Cait Raft. Raft is a comedian, writer, and performer from Manhattan Beach, CA. They are one of the funniest they/thems in Hollywood and have written for TV shows on HBO Max, Netflix, Cartoon Network, Quibi (RIP), and Comedy Central (RIP). They are one half of the famous all-trans comedy duo, Joan and Raft alongside their collaborator and BFF, Joan Haley Ford.

people.name: Caitie Mac
people.bio: Caitie Mac is an LA-based improviser and stand-up comedian known for her sharp wit and energetic stage presence. In addition to appearing on UCB’s Lloyd Night, she performs regularly at iconic venues like The Ice House and the Hollywood Improv. Caitie is also a member of the indie improv team Caboose. Offstage, she channels her comedic voice into screenwriting, bringing bold, fresh perspectives to the page. She frequently tries out her new jokes on her cat who remains unimpressed but supportive.

people.name: Caitlin Bitzegaio
people.bio: Caitlin is a veteran improv and sketch performer at The Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre where she also serves as a sketch teacher and director. Cool! Her recent television writing and producing credits include Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen, Magic For Humans, Girls Behaving Badly, The Rundown with Robin Thede, and Impractical Jokers.

people.name: Caitlin Frain
people.bio: Caitlin Frain ( St. Denis Medical , The Groundlings Sunday Company) is a comedic actor originally from Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. She was a performer in the 2020 CBS Showcase and has appeared in the Roku short film Born to Walk . Other credits include producing for game shows such as Kevin Hart’s Celebrity Game Face as well as hosting digital series on topics ranging from dating apps to children’s social-emotional learning to women’s athletics.

people.name: Caitlin Gorman
people.bio: Caitlin is a native Austinite and social worker who has unfortunately sold her soul and works in medical sales. She started improvising at Coldtowne Theater in 2017 and started Hot Goss in 2019 after the dissolution of her class troupe. Am I The Asshole? was her idea and she has the screenshots to prove it.

people.name: Caitlin Hollis
people.bio: Hollis began performing comedy in her hometown Atlanta at Dad’s Garage. Since then she has performed in both Chicago and New York at The Second City, iO, Annoyance and UCB theater. She will continue her comedy journey until her dog laughs at her.

people.name: Caitlin Puckett
people.bio: Caitlin is proud to be part of legendary teams like Ice Cold Bev, Higgins, UCB Tourco and indie darlings Tops and Curtains. She fled NYC during COVID and is tickled to make her return to improv while temporarily abandoning her husband and two children in Cleveland(they are fine!)

people.name: Calvin Beechner
people.bio: Calvin is excited to attend and perform at DCM for the first time. He has performed with On the Spot for the past two years. Calvin graduated from Kansas State University in May 2026 with a bachelor’s degree in computer science and cybersecurity.

people.name: Calvin Seabrooks
people.bio: Actor, Writer, Artist from San Diego. Head Writer (CBS Showcase), Grey’s Anatomy (ABC), 4400 (CW), Westworld (HBO), Games People Play (BET), Queer As Folk (Paramount+).

people.name: Cam Gavinski
people.bio: Cam is a Thai-American comedian/writer/director/artist originally from Dallas, Texas. In addition to performing at UCB, Cam performs standup around Los Angeles, and can be seen touring nationally with comedian Todd Glass. Cam has animated shorts for Comedy Central, Jim Jefferies, and NBC. He has also written, directed, and starred in multiple viral sketches that have amassed over 20 Million Views and he has over 130k+ Followers across social media platforms.

people.name: Camden Garcia
people.bio: Camden is a performer and writer, originally from Los Angeles, now based in NYC. TV credits include HACKS, Sprung, Raising Hope, Station 19, and My Name is Earl.

people.name: Cameron Esposito
people.bio: Standup comic, actor and bestselling author Cameron Esposito has been seen everywhere from cult favorite Sundance indies and big budget films to network dramas and late night TV. She also boxes.

people.name: Cameron Kelly
people.bio: Cameron is an actress/writer/comedian from Washington DC, though she grew up overseas in France and Chile. She’s recently appeared in Old Dads (Netflix, Dir: Bill Burr), Law & Order: SVU (NBC), FBI: International (CBS), Rosé All Day (Hulu), Rosewood (FOX), Masters of Sex (SHO). Cameron is the creator/writer/star of comedy series What the Elf?! — a workplace-comedy based on her time working as a Christmas elf at the mall.

people.name: Candice Yang
people.bio: Candice Yang is an 9-5er who occasionally does improv for fun, you cannot find her anywhere.

people.name: Capri Campeau
people.bio: Capri (she/they) is a queer advice and comedy creator with over 600,000 followers across Instagram and TikTok. Through the support of her online community, she crowdfunded and executive produced her comedy writing debut, How To Hook Up With Girls . She also starred in Fire Fucking Fire , which premiered at the 2024 Tribeca Film Festival.

people.name: Cara Meyers
people.bio: Cara started doing improv in Chicago in 2012, and never looked back. A little over 10 years later you can see her on Harold Night or with her team Wild!

people.name: Carl Foreman, Jr.
people.bio: Carl is a comedian, writer and actor raised in Baltimore. Carl co-created/co-wrote/co-starred in the critically acclaimed digital series “Frank & Lamar.” TV credits include Search Party (Max), Jon Glaser Loves Gear (TruTV) and The Following (Fox). He’ performed in the 2020 CBS Showcase, and was 2023 Just For Laughs New Face of Comedy: Characters. Carl also voiced two series regular characters on the Comedy Central animated series “Fairview.”

people.name: Carl Tart
people.bio: Carl Tart has been performing at UCB for a long time and has never been respected.

people.name: Carlos Orlando
people.bio: Born and raised in Los Angeles and the son of Mexican and Colombian immigrants, Carlos is an actor, improviser, sketch performer and writer who has been studying and performing at UCBTLA for over 10 years. Credits include Characters Welcome, Maude Night, Mess Hall and a bunch weird and funny shows. He is a founding member of the Latine comedy troupe Menudo but in general prefers pozole.

people.name: Carlos Santos
people.bio: Carlos Santos is an Actor/Comedian based out of Los Angeles. He is best known for playing Chris Morales on the Netflix original series, Gentefied. He’s also been featured on “Vacation Friends” (20th Century), “The Valet” (HULU), and the new comedy series “Primo” on Freeve. He was born and raised in Puerto Rico.

people.name: Carly Ann Filbin
people.bio: Carly Ann Filbin is the word’s most talented and beautiful storyteller. Check out her solo show, Coke Ass. She is the founder of Written in Brooklyn and her memoir is called Tears of a Blonde: Almost Completely True Stories of Love and Heartbreak from the World’s Hottest Comedian.

people.name: carly ciarrocchi
people.bio: Carly Ciarrocchi is a host/writer/producer/morning enthusiast who mostly makes TV + podcasts for kids and families. She also teaches clown and tells stories. Ask her about the Kingston Caves!!

people.name: Carly Hayes
people.bio: Carly is an NYC based actor, singer, and goofball. Recently, she originated the role of Caty off-broadway in the play “Bum Bum: or, This Farce has Autism.” Carly has performed at the Beacon Theater, Lincoln Center, Joe’s Pub, 54 Below and HBO Theater. On an average week you can catch her improvising with her indie team, Siblings, coaching neurodiverse performers with EPIC Players and petting her cat.

people.name: Carly Lant
people.bio: Lant is 5 11′, a natural brunette, and grew up in the PNW in order to develop her personality. She now lives in Brooklyn and regularly performs improv comedy with her indie team Clint. Lant is also a former D1 athlete and currently coaches rowing in the greater NY area.

people.name: Carly Monardo
people.bio: Carly Monardo is an artist and performer from Brooklyn, NY, who goes to bed at 9 pm every night. Credits include: Adult Swim, Nickelodeon, and Rude Tales of Magic.

people.name: Carly Silverman
people.bio: Carly Silverman is writer who’s written on house sketch teams at The Magnet Theater and UCB – additionally her writing has appeared on stage at NYU, The PIT, Caveat, and festivals across the country. Her writing has also been featured in Vulture, Paste Magazine, Cosmopolitan, Haloscope, Points In Case, and The Hard Times. You can catch her doing shows with her sketch team: Young Douglas.

people.name: Carolina Montenegro
people.bio: Carolina (She/Her) is a Latine actor/writer, LA/SoCal native, and is a proud ambassador of the forward slash (////). Growing up, as the first born in the States of her Argentine family, Caro played the mediator/translator/clown for her parents and younger siblings. To this day, she still practices all three but does the third one now professionally. This is her first year performing on a Maude Team and is super in love with her Loveseat teammates. Other things she loves: flicking noses, the iSeeker App, and the kind of bad joke where people shake their head as a response.

people.name: Caroline Cotter
people.bio: Caroline is an actor, writer, improviser and idiot. You might’ve seen her playing a deranged craft expert on CONAN or read her short humor pieces in The New Yorker ‘s Shouts & Murmurs.

people.name: Caroline Cummings
people.bio: Caroline is a clown, improviser and (God help us) a classically trained actor. She writes and performs comedy all around LA and sometimes New York and overseas. You can catch her performing tonight if you check her Instagram to find out where. 😉

people.name: Caroline Johnson
people.bio: Caroline Johnson is a filmmaker, director, and writer based in Los Angeles. Her work straddles genres and mediums, from narrative fiction to documentary, essay, and movement-based films. Her films have played at the Beijing International Short Film Festival, AmDocs, Chroma Art Film Festival, the Greenwich International Film Festival, the Ravenwood Film Festival, and others. She holds an MFA in Film/Video from CalArts.

people.name: Caroline Mann
people.bio: Caroline Mann is a stand up, improviser and writer based in Birmingham, AL.

people.name: Caroline Martin
people.bio: Caroline Martin is an improviser, writer, and actor. She co-created and starred in Astronomy Club: The Sketch Show , a sketch comedy series that premiered on Netflix in December 2019, executive produced by Kenya Barris and Dan Powell. She was a staff writer for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon . She most recently worked on the Hulu show How To Die Alone . She is the winner of the 2014 NBC & UCB Diversity Scholarship Showcase, and 2016 Comedy Central NY Comedy Festival Comic to Watch. She performs improv comedy around Los Angeles and received a B.A. in Ethnic Studies from Brown University.

people.name: Caroline Ulwick
people.bio: Caroline is a writer, comedian and improviser originally from Maryland (important). She performs with her Harold Team at UCBNY. She writes for the team “Cliff Hanger” with indie sketch collective BoogieManja, and her digital sketch comedy team, “No Worries If Not,” releases new videos every month or so. Caroline’s TikTok is fun and very not cringe.

people.name: Carolyn Egan
people.bio: Carolyn currently teaches improv at UCB NY and performs on UCB’s Harold Night with her team ZUKO. She can be seen performing and producing improv and character shows at various comedy theaters and can also be found writing and performing in online sketches in the deep pages of the internet.

people.name: Carrie McCrossen
people.bio: Carrie is a veteran of Harold and Maude. Her solo show “MILP: Moms I’d Like to Portray” ran at UCB NY in [year redacted because old.] Acting: Adam Ruins Everything, Divorce, The Rundown. Writing: The New Yorker Daily Shouts, Funny or Die. Carrie has written three Lifetime movies, two YA novels (with co-author Ian McWethy,) one cable sketch comedy show- and she still has zero health benefits, if you can believe! Carrie has two objectively perfect children under age five so please excuse the condition of her car/clothes/life.

people.name: Carsen Smith
people.bio: Carsen Smith (she/they) is a comedy writer from Jacksonville, FL. She’s written for UCB Maude teams VILLAIN! and BedRock. Formerly, she worked for Impractical Jokers on truTV. You can see her headlines on Reductress and around town with her indie sketch team One Bad Egg. Her first book series, Area 51 Interns, released with Penguin Random House in 2022.

people.name: Casey Feigh
people.bio: Casey Feigh is a comedic actor, writer, producer & director who can be seen opposite Al Pacino in Dead Man’s Wire , and in non-Al Pacino projects like: St. Denis Medical , Unprisoned , What We Do In The Shadows , For All Mankind , The Goldbergs , Night Court , Grown-ish , Out of Office , Together Together , Lessons in Chemistry …Casey has written for NBC, Comedy Central, Disney, FunnyOrDie, Pop TV and his work has over 70 million views on the internet. Casey’s a frequent guest on the podcast Comedy Bang! Bang! and has appeared in a ton of commercials. He’s also the creator of Holy Shit Improv, a touring improv show featuring the best comedy talent in LA that you can watch from anywhere in the world. ​At UCB, you can catch Casey improvising with Holy Shit Improv, ASSSSCAT and Friday Fix.

people.name: Casey Jost
people.bio: Casey Jost is a writer, actor, director and musician born and raised in Staten Island, New York. Casey can be seen as the host of TruTV’s INSIDE JOKES, an offshoot of the network’s popular IMPRACTICAL JOKERS series, on which Casey also serves as writer and executive producer. Prior to Impractical Jokers, Casey was a staff writer on LATE NIGHT WITH JIMMY FALLON and contributed to BILLY ON THE STREET. As a performer, Casey can be seen in the Paramount comedy, STATEN ISLAND SUMMER, Comedy Central’s INSIDE AMY SCHUMER and the Hulu series, DEADBEAT. Casey’s past UCB teams include GoodGirl, Smalls, Higgins, and currently he is a member of What I Did For Love. At UCB NY, you can catch him directing Yoni Lotan’s New York The Ride The Show!

people.name: Casey McCormick
people.bio: Casey McCormick (she/her) is an NYC-based improvisor whose writing has appeared in New York Magazine and Popsugar. Catch her on Harold Night with FKA Deb, doing the soundtrack with Woodstock, and more. Casey has performed around the country with UCB TourCo and independent teams, including one stunning performance at The Kennedy Center where she repeatedly used the word “DILF” on a stage where presidents have spoken.

people.name: Casey Rosario
people.bio: Casey Rosario is a queer writer, performer, and therapist originally from Clearwater, Florida. Her screenwriting has placed in national competitions, and she performs monthly with improv team Tucci at UCB and sketch team Ciao at The Pack. Please, please talk to her about pop music any time. Synth will save us!

people.name: Cassi Jerkins
people.bio: Cassi Jerkins is an actor and award winning podcast producer. She currently produces the hit podcast, “Office Ladies” at Earwolf/SiriusXM. Past credits include Adult Swim, College Humor, Socratica Kids, etc. Cassi likes to play basketball but is bad at it.

people.name: Cassidy Beckham
people.bio: I’m Cassidy and I’ve been doing improv for 8 years in both Austin and Chicago. Currently living in Austin and performing monthly with my troupe Hot Goss at ColdTowne theater!

people.name: Cat-Tam Huynh
people.bio: Cat-Tam Huynh is an actress from Southern California currently studying Acting, Cinematic Arts, and Business at the University of Southern California. She first discovered her passion for comedic acting through competitive speech and debate, where she used humor to advocate for social issues and was ranked #1 in the nation in her event. Since then, she has appeared in numerous short films and was honored with the Best Acting Award at the 2026 Westflix Film Festival hosted by Paramount Studios. Today, she performs with USC’s premier comedy troupe, Commedus Interruptus, and seeks to continue combining an interdisciplinary perspective with storytelling to uplift underrepresented voices in film and television.

people.name: Catt Crawford
people.bio: Los Angeles improvisor, actor, writer, and director from Texas. Plays well with others. Looking for a forever home.

people.name: Ceara O'Sullivan
people.bio: Ceara is an Emmy-nominated television writer, currently staffed on Saturday Night Live (seasons 48, 49, and 50). She is an improviser, character comedian, and co-host of the “Petty Crimes” podcast. Ceara was a 2022 Just For Laughs: New Face and did improv at a basement in Boston for literally a decade before moving to LA. Ceara wants to do all your improv shows!!!

people.name: Cecily Breaux
people.bio: Cecily Breaux has studied and performed at UCB and Groudlings. She was in UCB shows such as Keep Calm & Tampon and most recently Vanderprov. You can catch her on the HULU series “The Dropout,” Karyn Kusama’s “Destroyer,” Amazon’s “Looking For Her” and improving/doing stand-up around LA. Her true love is rewatching seasons of Summer House on Bravo.

people.name: Celia Gurney
people.bio: Celia is a writer and improviser from Seattle. She likes making French language learning comedy videos if you can believe that. Her writing has appeared in Mangoprism , Improv Nonsense , and Storytelling to Accelerate Climate Solutions (Springer Press). Catch her at UCB with her Lloyd team Jinx or around town with either In Retrograde or Sandwich (totally up to you).

people.name: Chad Pelsang
people.bio: from Long Island > Ridgewood > Phoenix > San Diego > North Hampton > San Francisco > Boulder > Los Angeles > to currently in Frogtown, LA

people.name: Chad Westbrook
people.bio: Chad Westbrook is an actor, writer, and director based in Los Angeles. At the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, he is a performer, teacher, and sketch director, having been a part of Maude Night for three years and the first LA Betty Team (formerly Characters Welcome). Chad performs monthly with his improv team, Rita Repulsa, and has appeared in national commercials, short films, and web series. He has also been a staff writer on various TV variety shows. Chad is queer and tall, with ambitions to simultaneously play Herman Munster while writing for The Golden Girls. This IS possible.

people.name: Chandler Dean
people.bio: Chandler Dean is a comedian and speechwriter originally from Houston, Texas, now based in Brooklyn. His satire has been featured in The New Yorker, McSweeney’s, Reductress, and Hard Drive. As the head of the humor practice at the speechwriting firm West Wing Writers, he has written jokes, roasts, and toasts for celebrities and political leaders (the good ones you like). He also hosts the comedy debate show Abolish Everything at Caveat, and co-hosts the humor reading mic Some Fun Lines at Fiction Bar. Previously, he’s been a Monologue Writer for Lovett or Leave It, and interned for The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, Late Night with Seth Meyers, and Full Frontal with Samantha Bee.

people.name: Channing Tookes
people.bio: Channing Tookes is an actor, improviser, writer, and comedian. He was trained in improv at UCB. You can catch him around NYC performing improv with Strange Darling and his all-black troupe The Blacksmiths and performing sketch comedy on various stages in NYC, and on the road, with Time To Kill.

people.name: Chanse McCrary
people.bio: F*nny C*nty H*re me Northwestern University | Second City | Baby Wants Candy | SMOSH | Moon Goon

people.name: Charles Maa
people.bio: Chuck is an actor/comedian/writer who began his career in entertainment as a professional dancer before focusing on comedy and writing. He studied and performs at the Upright Citizens Brigade theater on shows such as Harold Night and Asian AF. Currently, he’s working as a writer on the Amazon FreeVee show, Leverage: Redemption .

people.name: Charlie Berger
people.bio: Charlie Berger is a comedian, writer, and director based in New York City. Charlie received a BFA from Boston University in Theatre Arts with concentrations in directing and playwriting. Charlie is also an improvisational comedian and avid sketch writer, currently performing around the city on indie teams. Charlie runs tech at various theaters in NYC for scripted and unscripted comedy. Charlie was awarded the Milan Stitt New Works Award for excellence in direction of new plays at Boston University.

people.name: Charlie Fay
people.bio: Charlie Fay is a comedian and panic attack connoisseur from Connecticut. He’s written for the TBS sitcom Wrecked , as well as over 50 sketches for his sketch team Flambé! Comedy. As an actor he’s performed in dozens of commercials for the likes of Visa, Coke, Carvana, and more. He’s so god damned pumped to be writing on the Maude team The Faculty and performing on Lloyd night with his fellow freaks of Freak Trap!!

people.name: Charlie James
people.bio: Charlie James is a comedian, technically from San Diego. He’s made videos for Funny Or Die and written for Slackjaw and Good News. He hosts an all-transgender variety show, Gentlemen’s Club, at Dynasty Typewriter.

people.name: Charlie Kanter
people.bio: Charlie Kanter is a clown, improviser, SAG actor, and producer born and raised in Los Angeles. After studying at the Groundlings and completing UCB’s improv curriculum, Charlie moved to Pittsburgh to “quit comedy” and “take this city bridge by bridge.” She has since performed improv with Latchkey Kids, Penny Arcade, Improv Anonymous, and Allegheny Story Society, while debuting her clowning in Chad Damiani’s Standup and Clown, as well as her solo act, Your Driver Has Arrived. She is signed with The Talent Group and is a comedian who, almost daily, crosses a bridge without noticing.

people.name: Charlie Mihelich
people.bio: Charlie Mihelich is a writer and improviser from Southern California. He is the creator and host of CATHARSIS: DRAMATIC IMPROV at UCB, wrote for the Maude teams Donatella and Thirst Trap, and was a member of the Mess Hall (now Lloyd) team Yacht Club. He is the writer of several feature films, including “Infamously in Love” (2022), “A Royal Christmas Surprise” (due in 2023), “Love on the Vineyard” (due in 2023), and the upcoming thriller “The New Killer in Town” (2024), as well as episodes of the scripted podcast “Little Stories Everywhere” from Wondery. He also co-hosts the podcast “Baywatch Watch”, a Baywatch re-watch podcast, with fellow writer and improviser Zach Olsen. He lives in Eagle Rock with his fiancé, Alex, and their very good dog, Koffey.

people.name: Charlie O’Connor
people.bio: Charlie is a British performer and composer based in NYC. He writes music for film and tv as well as makes silly shows with his friends. Catch him and other LISA performer in NYC’s iconic band: Charlie & The Darlings

people.name: Charlie Sanders
people.bio: Charlie Sanders is an actor, director, producer, and Emmy Award nominated, Peabody Award winning, and WGA Award winning writer. He created the anthology sci-fi comedy series Weird City for YouTube premium. He was a writer snd producer for all five seasons of Key & Peele on Comedy Central and won a WGA Award for Outstanding Writing for a Variety Series for Blake Shelton’s Not-So-Family-Christmas on NBC. He was also a writer for The Chris Gethard Show on Fuse TV, Human Giant on MTV, and The Characters on Netflix. Charlie starred as Officer Joe Stubeck on MTV’s Death Valley and has also appeared on Curb Your Enthusiasm, The Mindy Project, The League, W/ Bob and David, Community, The Office, Brooklyn Nine-Nine, and much more. He started his career at the UCB Theater in New York City where he was a member of the award winning sketch and improv group Police Chief Rumble and was a regular sketch performer on Late Night with Conan O’Brien. At UCB LA he was a performer in the improv groups Shitty Jobs, Buffoons, and Facebook. Charlie is an avid player of Dungeons & Dragons and other tabletop and role playing games. He lives in Los Angeles and is a proud cat dad.

people.name: Charlie Todd
people.bio: Charlie Todd has performed at the UCB theatre for over 20 years. He was a member of the legendary UCB groups Reuben Williams, The Curfew, and C, C, & C Improv Factory. He also hosted CageMatch for over a decade with The UCBW, the comedy wrestling league he created. Outside of UCB, Charlie is the founder of Improv Everywhere, and specializes in creating and directing elaborate surprise performances in public spaces. He also works in TV, recently executive producing and directing Pixar In Real Life for Disney+.

people.name: Charlotte Marchioli
people.bio: Charlotte Marchioli (Commedus Interruptus, USC) is a New York-born comedian, actor, writer, singer-songwriter, and grilled cheese enthusiast currently studying theatre at the University of Southern California. This past year, she performed improv at UCB Los Angeles with her troupe and presented original sketch work at PIT’s 2025 Sketch Fest in New York City. Specializing in bizarre character work, Marchioli’s comedy embodies the specific awkwardness of wanting desperately to be cool while actively making everything worse. Seeking representation in comedy, acting, and writing.

people.name: Charlotte Polk
people.bio: Charlotte Polk (they/she) is an actor, writer, and comedian. They write for UCBNY’s Maude team First Wife and perform with the improv group Stress Dream. They are also a co-creator and co-star of the web series i’m so happy for you!!!!!!. She loves citrus and lying down and you can find more of her content on TikTok (@regularcharlotte) or Instagram (@ischarlottepolkstillalive).

people.name: Charlotte Swett
people.bio: Charlotte Swett is an actor, improv comedian, writer, and artist living in Brooklyn, NY. You can see her on S1E2 of “When Sharks Attack.. and Why” on Discovery Channel and on Lloyd nights at UCB. Some of her other skills include singing, playing tiny guitars, kung fu, and she is a classically trained ballerina. You can stalk her on instagram or tiktok @chardablob

people.name: Chaz May
people.bio: Like Chad, but gayer, Chaz is an actor, comedian, and writer who always wears his seatbelt! You may have seen him on TV or TikTok, or most likely sitting in a booth at your local Margaritaville.

people.name: Chelsea Morgan
people.bio: Chelsea Morgan has studied at UCB, Groundlings and The Idiot Workshop, under the guidance of John Gilkey. She’s currently part of UCB’s Betty Team, does stand-up all around LA, and performs regularly with her clown trio Best Actress. She’s been featured on Funny or Die, BuzzFeed, and has written and starred in numerous short film series for Channel 101. She voices a character in the Netflix/AGBO feature film THE ELECTRIC STATE .

people.name: Chelsea Wang
people.bio: Chelsea is a NY-based comic originally from Livonia, MI. Chelsea performs improv, standup, and sketch, as well as daily strained social interactions, all around the city. She/they/he is currently on indie groups Sad Sacks, The Slaysians, and The Unhinged.

people.name: Chessie Kauffman
people.bio: C.K. is a musical improviser who has studied and performed across NYC at The PIT, Magnet Theater, and UCB. She is also a writer of many incomplete works, which she insists are not abandoned, just dramatically paused. She brings big feelings, tiny details, and a suspicious number of inner monologues to the stage.

people.name: Chet Siegel
people.bio: CHET SIEGEL is an actress, comedian, and cook. She developed her solo show/ immersive nervous breakdown, MY DEAD WEDDING, as a 2022-2023 CAMP Artist in Residence at Ars Nova and performed it at Ars Nova, Union Hall, & Littlefield in NYC. Performing credits include – Uncle Peckerhead, Alternatino (Comedy Central), Search Party (HBO), Chicago Med (NBC), and many independent projects that have appeared in Sundance, SXSW, Palm Springs Film Fest, NY Fringe, ANT Fest and other really cool festivals. She directed, wrote, performed, and taught improv and sketch comedy at UCB theater and Magnet Theater, and was the artistic director of Magnet’s Sketch Comedy program.

people.name: Chine Ikoro
people.bio: Chine Ikoro is a comedian, actor and writer based in Brooklyn, New York. She is currently a mainstage performer on UCB’s weekend character team CABAL, where she writes and performs original characters each month. She’s also on the Core Company on UCB’s premiere sketch show ASSSSKETCH. You can find her regularly performing characters, sketch and standup around NYC. Check out her Instagram @chineikoro for upcoming shows!

people.name: Chism Cornelison
people.bio: Chism Cornelison is a comedian and producer from Austin, TX with over a decade of experience in all types of comedy. He currently hosts and produces GUTS at Fallout Theater: a space for experimental comedy blending sketch, improv, stand-up, and clown.

people.name: Chloe Bivona
people.bio: Chloe Bivona is a comedian living in New York City, which means she spends most of her time trying to be funny and avoiding eye contact on the subway. Follow her at chloe.bivona on instagram for show details.

people.name: Chloe Hale
people.bio: Chloe Hale is a Brooklyn-based comedian, actor, and writer. They perform as a sketch actor on Both Hands, part of the Boogiemanja Sketch Comedy Collective, and on the Woodstock Improv team. You can also read their writing on Reductress. Most importantly Chloe is a lesbian, and is seeking representation.

people.name: Chloe Radcliffe
people.bio: Chloe Radcliffe is a NYC-based standup comic, actor, and writer. She starred in COMMAND Z from Steven Soderbergh. She was also a staff writer on the series and has teleplay credit on 3 episodes. She has been seen on After Midnight, and her Comedy Central: Featuring set debuted in 2022. She staffed on the sketch writing team for The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon from 2019-2020.

people.name: Chris Bazemore
people.bio: Since his start in 2017, Chris Bazemore has been a FINALIST in several comedy festivals all over the country. On the Improv front he has been on reputable house teams, and even won or was a semi-finalist in competitions with his indie teams. Chris looks to continue spreading laughter throughout the country and eventually the world.

people.name: Chris Blanco
people.bio: Chris Blanco is just a little idiot who loves to laugh. He is also a actor, writer, director with an MFA in film from Columbia University. Winner of the Philip Johnston Comedy Grant for short films, Chris continues to create comedic content and performs at local shows around Brooklyn.

people.name: Chris De La Cruz
people.bio: Chris De La Cruz is a Dominican-American comedian, storyteller, and former high school teacher turned TV writer. He is a writer on I Suck at Girls (Netflix) and was a series regular in Sherlock Homies , an unaired HBO Max pilot. His students, however, were most impressed that he once handed a bunny to Kevin Hart on The Tonight Show . An alum of UCB, Magnet, iO, PIT, and Second City, Chris is a regular host for The Moth, co-host of Pilot Season, and performs on Harold Night. Still, no audience compares to half-asleep teens pretending they did the reading.

people.name: Chris Evan Simpson
people.bio: Chris Evan Simpson (he/him) is an actor and improvisor hailing from the great state of Connecticut. Currently, you can catch him every Thursday night at the Magnet Theater with his house team Secret, Secret! He has performed around NYC with the shows Baby Wants Candy, Shamilton, Shitzprobe, Undressed: The Musical, and Jukebox: Improvised Karaoke. Favorite theater credits include ‘Puffs, or Seven Increasingly Eventful Years at a Certain School of Magic and Magic’ (New World Stages), ‘Wicked Frozen’ (St. Luke’s Theater), and ‘Peter, Who?’ (NYMF). Chris once won a Zip Drive in a no-talent-talent-contest at yearbook camp by playing music on his face.

people.name: Chris Grace
people.bio: Chris Grace is best known for playing Jerry on NBC’s Superstore and can also be seen in PEN15, Broad City, Atypical, and more. He performs improv and stand-up all over the world, and recently won 1st Place at the Riot Festival National Comedy Contest in Houston. He recently was awarded Best of Fest at Big Pine Comedy Festival, Burbank Comedy Festival, Comedy Chateau Comedy Festival, and Laugh After Dark. He recently debuted his solo comedy show “Chris Grace: As Scarlett Johansson” at the Hollywood and Edinburgh Fringe Festivals. He loves tacos and dumplings in equal measure. IG/TikTok: @chrisgracecomedy

people.name: Chris Kula
people.bio: Chris Kula is a TV writer and producer who’s worked in sitcoms ( Community , Wrecked ), sketch ( MADtv , Funny Or Die), late-night ( @midnight ) and animation ( Close Enough , Star Trek: Lower Decks ). He’s also developed and sold original pilots to Netflix, CBS, TBS, Turner Sports and Disney XD (you know, the *EXTREME* Disney!). On the UCBLA stage he has improvised in ASSSCAT and Harold Night, and currently performs with long-running group The Smokes. Kula is a runner, a drummer and a graduate of the University of Michigan.

people.name: Chris Packard
people.bio: Chris is a Philadelphia-based improvisor with around 10 years of experience. You can catch him on Philly-based teams Tchotchke or Drums Drums Drums, or at Sidequest Theater in Odd at Sea or The Improvised Tony Awards. If you wouldn’t mind bringing him some eggplant parmesan to eat after the show he would really appreciate it.

people.name: Chris Renfro
people.bio: Chris Renfro is an Asian-American comedy writer, performer, and the current Artistic Director of UCB Los Angeles. Credits include Broadway’s “Oh, Mary!”, and “The Drowsy Chaperone” at Carnegie Hall. On screen, they can be binged on “Queer as Folk,” “The L Word: Generation Q,” “Good Trouble,” “Tom Swift,” “Reno 911!” and the MTV original movie, “Pretty Stoned.” They are the co-host of the award-winning podcast, “One of Us with Fin and Chris” where they’ve interviewed guests such as Dylan Mulvaney, Joel Kim Booster, and Margaret Cho.

people.name: Chris Tcholakian
people.bio: Chris Tcholakian is an improvisor and actor from New York City. He currently makes the Everything Now Show, a very cool interactive comedy livestream on Twitch, as well as performing weekly with the UCB Harold Team yikes!

people.name: Chrissie Gruebel
people.bio: Chrissie Gruebel (she/her) is a comedian, writer, and sketch director based in Brooklyn. You can catch her improvising every week at Magnet Theater (The Friday Night Sh*w and Metal Boy presents: Your Worst Idea). She’s also a proud alum of UCB’s Characters Welcome and Maude Night. Chrissie has performed and taught workshops all over the world with her longtime comedy partners, The Mannequin Room (a Time Out Chicago’s Critic’s Pick). There’s more to talk about but she enjoys maintaining an air of mystery.

people.name: Chrissy Bruzek
people.bio: Chrissy is originally from Chicago and will slip into a 1980s Chicago Dad accent whenever she’s nervous. She studied comedy and acting at Second City, iO, UCB, the Annoyance, and Stan Kirsch Studios. Along with writing/performing in multiple improv, sketch comedy, and musical sketch comedy groups, she is also a classically trained singer and played viola for 12 years. Also, her very first concert ever was Weird Al Yankovic, if that explains anything.

people.name: Christian Corpora
people.bio: Christian Corpora is an actor, writer, and former oboe player. He performed improv, musical improv, and sketch with Florida Studio Theatre in Sarasota, where he accidentally lived in a retirement community for two years. Christian is a proud graduate of Space Camp.

people.name: Christine Bullen
people.bio: Christine is UCB’s Senior Artistic Director leading both the LA & NY Artistic Director teams. Comedy credits include JFL New Faces, NY Comedy Festival – Comics to Watch, SF Sketchfest, Comedy Bang Bang, and numerous sketches for Fox NFL.

people.name: Christine Piñeiro
people.bio: Christine Piñeiro (MTV, VH1, Disney+) is a New York City-based Latina actor, improvisor, writer, and sketch performer from The Bronx. She is a winner of the 2016 & 2015 UCB Diversity Scholarship and has performed for NBC’s Diversity Showcase. Christine has also trained with Terry Knickerbocker at Terry Knickerbocker Acting Studios and can be heard on the Sounds Funny Radio Comedy Podcast, produced by Marc Evan Jackson, Nate DuFort, and James Quesada.

people.name: Christine Stemmer
people.bio: Christine Stemmer is an actor, comedy writer, and improviser based in Los Angeles. She performs with UCB character team Back Pocket and writes original TV pilots and comedy projects inspired by her time working at a small-town amusement park, her deeply specific knowledge of Gilmore Girls , and the Daniel Radcliffe fan fiction she spent years writing as a teenager. Her work has been featured on WhoHaha, and her videos have gained over 4 million views across TikTok and Instagram.

people.name: Christopher Darden
people.bio: Chris Darden is a comedy writer/performer based in Brooklyn, NY. He was previously a writer on Bedrock at UCB and Mom’s Night Out on BoogieManja. Please DM or text him for humorous content.

people.name: Christopher Eddins
people.bio: Christopher Eddins is a member of Lemon Pepper Wet, a screenwriter, improviser, and actor from Detroit, Michigan.

people.name: Cicley Alexander
people.bio: Cicley is an Actor, Writer, Producer and US Navy Veteran based in Houston, TX.

people.name: Claire Riffle
people.bio: Claire Riffle is a 65-year old actress and comedian trapped in a 12 year old’s body, originally from the tiny town of Houston, TX. She’s studied and performed at The Second City, UCB, and Groundlings since 2017. Claire also enjoys auditioning to play pre-teens, labeling everything as “yummy,” and being in bed by 9. It is the most delicious DREAM for her to perform on the UCB stage amongst the most sweetie, talented people in the world!!

people.name: Clarice Bruch
people.bio: Clarice earned her MA in Musical Theatre at the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland before moving to New York to make it big in Corporate America. When she’s not at Book Club, she can be seen knitting on the 7 train, plugging her ears when a siren goes by, or Being Really Brave on a citi bike.

people.name: Claude Hurd
people.bio: A comedy writer from San Bernardino, CA. I’ve written for the sketch show Studio C and currently write on the Maude team, Town Crier.

people.name: Claudio Saavedra
people.bio: Claudio Saavedra is a recipient of the iOWest Hollywood Diversity Scholarship and the Second City Bob-Curry Fellowship. In his free time, he enjoys playing tennis and indoor rock climbing. Claudio aspires to play a doctor on a soap opera. He used ChatGPT to write this.

people.name: Clay Davis
people.bio: I’m an actor and writer from Los Angeles. Love me some improv. Dance a lot too and fiend hot sauce.

people.name: Clifton Highfield
people.bio: Clifton Highfield is a performer on the Harold team Candy Necklace and has been teaching, performing, directing, and producing improv for over 20 years. He previously taught long-form improv to hundreds of students at The Institution Theater in Austin, TX and currently teaches with UCB at Coldtowne Theater. Festival credits include Out of Bounds, Chicago Improv Festival, San Francisco Improv Fest, Toronto Improv Festival, and more. Clifton is a two-time B. Iden Payne Award winner for ensemble work (The Suitcase, Sextet) and trained at iO Chicago, iO West, Second City, and UCB.

people.name: Cody Lindquist
people.bio: Cody Lindquist is a New York-based actress, comedian, podcast host, and voice-over artist who has been regularly performing at UCBNY since 2006. Currently, she is a co-host of the political comedy round table Not in My Backyard. You may recognize her from TV shows like Our Cartoon President, Broad City, Law and Order: SVU, Master of None, and Vice News Tonight as well as 50 billion TV commercials. Cody is originally from Ohio but is happily raising two native New Yorkers with her Husband Charlie Todd.

people.name: Cole Koehler
people.bio: Cole hails from Homer, Nebraska. And so far his only writing credit is adding the motto: “Little But Lively” to his hometown’s wikipedia page.

people.name: Colin McGurk
people.bio: After studying at The Actors Studio in NYC Colin moved to LA and joined the UCB Theater performing on the improv group: BITCHIN’. Credits include: TED ; The Neighborhood ; A to Z ; Criminal Minds ; In Time ; and several short films and live productions.

people.name: Colin Murphy
people.bio: Colin is a comedic actor and writer who’s been on numerous house teams at UCB over the years. In addition to being on both Harold Night and Characters Welcome, he’s co-written for multiple shows at the theatre including: The Show That Never Was, Big Party Presents, and Merry Axed-Mas. He can currently been seen in the cast of ASSSSKETCH. He is also an alum of The Groundlings Sunday Company, the CBS Diversity Showcase, and the Disability Belongs Fellowship. When he’s not performing or writing comedy, he’s searching for the best sandwiches in LA because there is nothing carbs and processed meats can’t do.

people.name: Colton Dunn
people.bio: Colton Dunn is a writer/ performer whose began working with the UCB theater in the late 1990’s. He has written for MadTV, Key and Peele and Superstore. His on screen work includes Parks and Rec, Superstore, Grand Crew and Netflix’s The Recruit. He loves you.

people.name: Conner McCabe
people.bio: Former precocious child annoyance Conner McCabe is an actor, writer, and podcast host from Modesto, CA. He’s been at the UCB theater for *gasp!* 10 years and currently plays with XOXO on UCB’s Harold night. You might know him from commercials, a TV show that never released, and hopefully soon you’ll see him on more than just his podcast “Call Me By Your Game.”

people.name: Conner Shin
people.bio: Conner is a non-binary writer/performer. Written for Harley Quinn Animated Series. Appeared in “Will & Grace” and “And Just Like That”

people.name: Connor Johnston
people.bio: Connor Johnston (he/him) is an actor/writer/improviser from Portland, Oregon, but now calls himself a New Yorker after living here for 15+ years. He holds a BFA in Drama from NYU’s Tisch School of the Arts, and you may have seen him on Netflix, NBC, CBS, Newfest, Tribeca Film Festival, or a random commercial. He was also briefly TikTok famous but that’s not really his thing anymore.

people.name: Connor Ratliff
people.bio: Connor Ratliff is a New York-based actor/comedian and creator of the award-winning, critically acclaimed podcast, Dead Eyes. A veteran UCB performer (ASSSSCAT 3000, The Stepfathers, Stone Cold Fox), he also created the long-running variety spoof show, The George Lucas Talk Show. TV & Film credits include: The Marvelous Mrs Maisel, The Great North, Search Party, Orange Is The New Black, Last Week Tonight With John Oliver, The Daily Show, Don’t Think Twice, Pinball: The Man Who Saved The Game, Mean Girls (2024).

people.name: Corey Podell
people.bio: Corey Podell is an actor, director, and teaching artist. She’s best known for her work in alternative comedy and clown, having directed the critically acclaimed stage shows “Vanessa 5000,” “Mr. Chonkers,” “The Underground Monk Show,” “Gutterplum,” and most recently, “Goblin.” Corey has studied at UCB LA and is an alumni of the Groundlings Sunday Company. She is a founding member of the LA based clown ensemble, “Clown Zoo” and her stage work has been described as “stupidly beautiful and beautifully stupid.” As an actor, Corey can be seen in The Comeback , Transparent , Two Broke Girls , The Joe Shmo Show Reboot , and a bunch of indies that no one sees, but everyone appreciates. As a teaching artist, Corey has brought her workshops “Clown Curious,” “Entrances + Exits,” “Build+Break,” and “Holding the Room” throughout the United States.

people.name: Cory Webster
people.bio: Cory is an improv and sketch comedian who primarily performs with his long time team, Dick Cannon! Their sketch show Dick Cannon Wins the Lottery had a year long run at UCB and they currently host the bit show Do the Dumb. If he isn’t out at a comedy show every night you will probably catch him at an LAFC or Angel City game. He’s got a good butt and if you’re lucky you might even see it on stage from time to time!

people.name: Cory Willis
people.bio: Cory Willis is an actor and writer currently residing in Los Angeles. He’s been a big ol goof his whole life, but started doing it professionally in 2013. Cory has been on TV, voiced a character on Pokémon, and written for Adult Swim. An avid Formula One fan, he cohosts “The F1 Files” podcast with his childhood best friend every week.

people.name: Courtney Pauroso
people.bio: Courtney Pauroso is an actress, writer, director, and comedian known for her one woman clown shows GUTTERPLUM and VANESSA 5000, both of which had sell-out runs at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival and the latter of which will soon be available to watch as a special on Dropout. She was recently named one of Vulture’s 2024 Comedians You Should and Will Know.

people.name: Courtney Theophin
people.bio: Courtney is an LA-based actor, comedian, writer, video editor and a bunch of other stuff to uphold the Caribbean stereotype of working a lot of jobs. From Gainesville, Florida (352 in the building!!) he’s happiest when he’s being silly on stage, don’t matter if it’s sketch or improv.

people.name: Craig Methven
people.bio: Craig is a Scottish improviser based in Edinburgh. He trained with The Improverts, Edinburgh Fringe’s longest running improv troupe, and London’s Free Association. At the FA he performed in house Harold team Supreme Leader, The Last Supper and VHS. He also co-created hit Fringe B-Musicals Vampire Hospital Waiting Room and Apocalypse Cruise Ship Love Affair.

people.name: Curran Connor
people.bio: Curran is a an Actor and Improvisor who’s worked in film, television, and theater all over the country. He can be seen on ‘The Other Two’, ‘Jessica Jones’, ‘The Good Fight’, ‘Deadbeat’, ‘High Maintenance’, and ‘The Deuce’ to name a few. He received his BFA from the Mason Gross School of the Arts.

people.name: Curry Whitmire
people.bio: Actor/Musical Improviser/Dog dad! Currently playing with Baby Wants Candy NY, Your Love Our Musical, Undressed: The Musical, and Musical Megawatt at the Magnet Theatre. Currently on UCB NY Maude night!

people.name: Curtis Retherford
people.bio: Curtis Retherford is an improvisor and writer from New York City, but before that he made doughnuts in Seattle. Before that? Who knows. He has been on various Harold teams, each one more famous than the last, and is the host of Improv, Beat by Beat, the audio textbook of improv, served one delicious concept at a time.

people.name: Cymere Lasean
people.bio: South Jersey native Cymere Lasean is a multi-hyphenate artist whose talents span music, dance, writing, and performance. Known for his charisma, creativity, and crowd-engaging energy, Cymere thrives on the connection between artist and audience. Whether he’s singing, storytelling, or commanding the stage, one thing is certain: when Cymere performs, you’re encouraged to let loose and have a good time!

people.name: D'Angelo Reyes
people.bio: D’Angelo is a queer Filipino-American improviser and writer from The Bay Area, who works as a production designer and artist for theme parks, theater, film, and haunts. You’ll usually find him talking about walkable city planning, extinct Disney Parks attractions, or past productions of The Phantom of the Opera . D’Angelo performs musical improv with his indie team Super Super Cool, and makes his UCB debut with Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: D.J. Mausner
people.bio: D.J. is an award winning standup, sketch, and improv comedian. They have performed in both the Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal and JFL42 in Toronto, winning the Homegrown Comics Competition (now known as Canadian New Faces) and appearing in the New Faces Characters Showcase. D.J. penned and executive produced the WGA Award nominated film Prom Dates , a Hulu Original released in May 2024. You may have seen D.J. in HBO’s Insecure , Amazon Prime’s Puppy Love , or DropoutTV’s Make Some Noise . D.J. is a dual US/Canadian citizen and grew up in Toronto, so they still say ‘house’ weird.

people.name: Da'rrel Stacy
people.bio: Da’rrel Stacy is a Queer LA based singer, actor, dancer & scenic painter/set dresser. When not working behind the scenes in the art department, you can find him performing on the UCB stage with Sing Out, Louise! and Friday Block Party. Or his indie teams Postcards To No One and all POC musical improv team The Seasoning.

people.name: Dahlya Glick
people.bio: Dahlya (they/them) is a vocalist, musician, dancer, writer, actor, and musical improviser. UCB event staff and seasoned performer, they have performed in over 300 UCB shows over the years (or something like that) as a member of UCBTLA’s first musical house team back in ol’ 2015, and currently with Quick and Funny Musicals. Proud member of Story Pirates and comedic band, Femmedy Trio (@femmedytrio). Other credits include- Theater: A Transparent Musical (Center Theater Group), A Wicked Soul in Cherry Hill (The Geffen Playhouse), Emojiland (First Nat’l Tour), and various TV roles!

people.name: Dakota Lane
people.bio: Dakota Lane is a writer, improviser and artist who has performed on both international stages and in sticky dive bars. She recently moved to San Francisco, but maintains undying loyalty to her NYC-based improv team Siblings. She’s also an emergency physician, which is basically long form with higher stakes and worse lighting.

people.name: Dan Black
people.bio: Dan Black is the actual funniest guy ever, ask anyone.

people.name: Dan Gregor
people.bio: Dan Gregor is a TV and film writer-director-producer. Most recently having written and produced Disney’s award winning “Chip N’ Dale Rescue Rangers” movie, and Paramount’s upcoming “The Naked Gun” reboot. Past TV credits include CW’s “Crazy Ex-Girlfriend,” CBS’s “How I Met Your Mother,” FX’s “The Comedians” and Adult Swim’s “NTSF:SD:SUV.” His feature film directorial debut “Most Likely To Murder” premiered at SXSW, and was purchased by Lionsgate and Hulu. Gregor got his start at as a founding member of the award winning sketch theater troupe, Hammerkatz, started at New York University and housed at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, where he still regularly performs live comedy.

people.name: Dan Hanf
people.bio: Dan has been improvising for many years, even though some of his teachers have suggested he stop. HE IS NOT SEEKING REPRESENTATION, BUT IS ALWAYS OPEN TO TALK!

people.name: Dan LaRose
people.bio: Dan is a NYC-based writer and comedian. He currently writes for the Maude team Ponyboy, and has previously written for the Maude team Yes, Chef! He has been featured on Netflix and Comedy Central as an uncredited audience member.

people.name: Dan Leahy
people.bio: Dan is most known for winning “Reddest Hair in Chicago” in 2004 or for playing Nurse Brandon on St. Denis Medical (NBC). You may also know him from his recurring + guest roles on Reboot (Hulu), Margo’s Got Money Troubles (Apple), Barry (HBO), Dave (FX), and others. He randomly studied Economics + Musical Theatre at Northwestern University. He performs monthly with Rodeo Baby and his sketch show TWO LOUD GAYS when he isn’t fighting with the other gay. Ask him for LA deep dish recommendations.

people.name: Dan Lippert
people.bio: Dan performs with Big Grande and Winslow. You can find all Big Grande related content at biggrandewebsite.com and listen to his improv and conversation podcast ManDogPod wherever you get podcasts. Onscreen he’s been in Paranormal Activity: Next of Kin, Brooklyn 99, The Rookie, Superstore, Workaholics, American Princess, Son of Zorn and more! (not much more)

people.name: Dan LoPreto
people.bio: Dan LoPreto is an editor based in Minneapolis.

people.name: Dan Milliken
people.bio: Dan Milliken is a singer/actor/comedian/writer who grew up in Hawaii and the Washington, D.C. area. While in D.C., Dan performed with Washington Improv Theater house team iMusical: The Improvised Musical, and also founded the all-gay indie team Ugh. These days you can catch him at UCB in the musical improv power block Sing Out, Louise! Offstage, Dan enjoys gardening and watching televised drag competitions.

people.name: Dan Moore
people.bio: Dan Moore is a veteran Harold Night performer (since 2019!), writer/director, and streamer. He is a member of the Harold team FKA Deb and has streamed over at twitch.tv/comedy_commons with Sean O’Reilly and sometimes Kelley Quinn since the pandemic. He’s played a lumpen boyfriend in Patrick Keane’s Carroll Gardens Cobble Hill and a deranged manosphere guy in Pu$$y Getters with Dorothy Nguyen. He also wrote for Game of Zones on Bleacher Report.

people.name: Dan Pavatich
people.bio: Dan is an Australian comedian, writer, and performer based in Los Angeles. He began his career in Melbourne, where he was a Raw Comedy 2016 finalist and performed with leading improv companies including The Big HOO-HAA!, The Improv Conspiracy, and Improv Theatre Sydney. He created the monthly sketch show Champagne Factory, was head writer for Sydney’s Sketchy at Best, and has performed solo sketch shows including Please Stay, Big Strong Boy, and Stick A Fork In Me, I’m Dan. Dan trained at Second City, iO Chicago, The Annoyance Theatre, The Groundlings, WGIS, and UCB, and has toured with improv productions including Bear Attack, Dough Dough, Game For A Laugh, and Now Showing: The Improvised Movie. Now based in Los Angeles, he performs improv and sketch, producing Content Cram, and Game For A Laugh and performing with Dough Dough, Dog Dog, and Platonica.

people.name: Dana Costello
people.bio: Dana Costello is thrilled to join Shiz after having (dis)graced the Broadway stage in SUFFS, PRETTY WOMAN, FINDING NEVERLAND, and JEKYLL & HYDE. To stalk; check out @dana_coz on instagram or visit danacostelloactress.com. Enjoy the show!

people.name: Dani Colucci
people.bio: Dani is a comedian, actor and musician from Chicago who is currently on UCB’s Maude team Town Crier. She recently shot a lead role in a feature and some of her TV credits include Chicago PD , Empire , Proven Innocent and Mind Games . She got to voice Kristin Chenoweth’s pet dog in Netflix’s Our Little Secret —her Mom is confused by that credit too. Dani also plays guitar and sings in the band Supershrew.

people.name: Dani Schindler
people.bio: After growing up in Sandy, Utah and studying media arts at Brigham Young University, Dani moved to Los Angeles to pursue comedy writing. Dani is currently a writer on the Maude team Lasers. When not writing, Dani can be found following the rules, being nice, and supporting your dreams.

people.name: Daniel Tepper
people.bio: Daniel Tepper is an actor and musical comedian based in New York City. He has performed in improvised musicals all over the city, the country, and dare her say… the world (he does dare!). Fun fact: Daniel’s strangest credits include acting in a commercial for a straw that sucks snot out of a baby’s nose, and playing a freestyling hot dog named “Lil Doggie” for Sonic Drive Thru’s snap chat account.

people.name: Danielle Grace
people.bio: Danielle Grace is a writer/ actor in NYC. You’ve seen her on Harold Night where she was very funny. You’ve also seen her as the host of Indie Cage Match and Backyard Brawl and in the shows I’m Your Mommy and A Back Line where she was also extremely funny. You will see her in more shows and she will be exceptionally funny. In conclusion, Dani is the funniest person alive says everyone who has ever lived and also the dead ones.

people.name: Danielle Shvartsman
people.bio: Danielle Shvartsman comes all the way from West Hills, CA. She graduated from Emerson College with a BA in Writing for TV and Film and is a Groundlings Sunday Co alum. Catch her performing standup, improv, and sketch all over this beautiful city, or anywhere else she gets the invite! Dramatic acting, too, probably! Idk!! Follow her everywhere at @stanleytushi

people.name: Danika Lyle
people.bio: Dani Lyle (she/they) is a stand-up comedian, writer, and actor from the Bay Area. When they’re not writing and performing sketches with the production company American High, they work as a Content Associate at a creative world building studio, specializing in narrative development.

people.name: Danny Lempert
people.bio: Danny is a comedian and actor from Brooklyn, NY. He has performed at Caroline’s on Broadway, UCB, The Second City, The Friar’s Club and more. At UCB he’s performed on Harold team Promises and Maude team Presley and currently performs on Harold & Betty nights. He is a previous winner of the NBC/UCB Diversity Showcase & Scholarship. Danny is a proud Story Pirate (and often the stock photo face of bad men for Reductress).

people.name: Danya Granado
people.bio: Danya Granado is a Trinidadian-American comedian and improviser based in Los Angeles.

people.name: Darnell Eaton
people.bio: Darnell Eaton is a Washington, DC-based improv actor and teacher with 15 years of performance experience. He performs regularly with Washington Improv Theater ensembles and has appeared on stages in New York, Chicago, Philadelphia, and throughout the DC area. His theater credits include productions with the DC Capital Fringe Festival, Atlas INTERSECTIONS Festival, and numerous local theater companies. In addition to performing, Darnell teaches improv to adults and children and serves as a producer with Songs of the Goat, a theater company specializing in Greek plays.

people.name: DarylJim
people.bio: DarylJim (also known as Jamie Ary) is an LA based actor and comedian. He’s trained at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade, World’s Greatest Improv School, and The American Musical and Dramatic Academy. Improv comedy credits include: UCB Mess Hall, Quick & Funny Musicals, WGIS Harold Nights, Yes And Broadway (creator and host), SF Sketchfest, Hawaii Improv Comedy Festival, Out Of Bounds Comedy Fest and more. You may have seen him in Patreon, Sam’s Club Holiday, and Credit Karma commercials/ads. TV appearances include 2018 Oscars “This Is Me” (from The Greatest Showman ) performance.

people.name: Dave Ahdoot
people.bio: Dave has appeared as a frequent guest on The Tonight Show, and has performed at over 300 colleges and universities across the country. He executive produced, wrote and starred in his own original series on NBC digital, called Dave and Ethan: Lovemakers. He also was writer on HBO’s, Emmy-nominated, Night of Too Many Stars, hosted by Jon Stewart and recently performed his show “Ethnically Ambiguous” to sold out crowds at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival.

people.name: Dave Buckman
people.bio: Dave trained at iO Theater and The Annoyance in Chicago in the late 90’s and has worked behind the scenes as a Director, Producer and Teacher and on stage at The Second City (Chicago & Cleveland), Boom Chicago (Amsterdam) and currently is the General Manager and Executive Producer of ColdTowne Theater in Austin Texas, now an outpost for Upright Citizens Brigade.

people.name: Dave Murray
