people.bio: Dave Murray is one of our most treasured improvisers, having performed in such historic UCB shows as “the Harold where everyone was stuck under a gold tarp,” “UCB Presents MTV2’s Scarred featuring Jacoby Shaddix,” and “301 Class Show.” He lives in Queens and loves magic. Now you know everything about him.

people.name: Dave Ross
people.bio: Dave Ross is a comedian in New York City. Every single person on earth likes him. He’s been on the Late Late Show with James Corden, This is Not Happening, Drunk History, WTF with Marc Maron, Why Won’t You Date Me? with Nicole Byer, Behind the Bastards, The Daily Zeitgeist and many many more. He was a New Face at Just For Laughs Montreal in 2016, and since then has been touring the country, headlining every single type of room — from a tent at Warped Tour to a theater at SXSW to The Hollywood Improv, New York Comedy Club and your basement. His first album The Only Man Who Has Ever Had Sex is really really good and that’s why everybody likes him so much.

people.name: Dave Theune
people.bio: Dave been performing at UCB since 2010 has appeared in/on lots of movies, TV shows, comedy podcasts, commercials, as well as having been a main stage improvisor at ComedySportz Milwaukee & LA, and ImprovOlympic Chicago & LA. Let’s go.

people.name: David Andrew Morton
people.bio: David is a comedian who currently writes for ‘The Onion.’ He performs with Young Douglas, a sketch team formed by UCB Maude Night. He has appeared in the Broadway national tours of ‘To Kill a Mockingbird’ and ‘Jersey Boys.’ You can also catch him as Graham on ‘FBI: Most Wanted’ (CBS).

people.name: David Bluvband
people.bio: David Bluvband is excited to be apart of the sketch team Baby Todd, which you can catch on Maude Night at UCB. He’s a native New Yorker who’s acting credits include TV shows like The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, Kaleidoscope, Hollywood, Difficult People Broad City, and the Sci-Fi Indie RomCom Molli and Max in the Future starring Zosia Mamet. The script for his pilot, The Champ from Bleecker Street was an official selection of the Big Apple Film Festival Festival Screenplay Competition of 2022. David loves old black & white movies, pizza and his dog, Smokey.

people.name: David Carl
people.bio: David Carl was on Maude for three seasons (PRESLEY and YES, CHEF) before Covid and the Betty (Character) Teams GOOD BONES and WIG!. His TV/FILM work includes a recurring role on The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, The Tender Bar, Tires, And Just Like That, Zero Day, Blue Bloods, Mr Robot, Divorce, and Guiding Light. He and his wife Katie Hartman (Betty Team UCB NY) have performed their dark musical comedy DAVID AND KATIE GET RE-MARRIED all over NYC and on the road, including the Edinburgh Fringe 2025 where they received 4 and 5 star reviews. David has taken Gary Busey’s One-Man Hamlet and Trump Lear across the country (including Chicago Shakespeare, Texas, Alabama, New Jersey and Colorado) and to Edinburgh Fringe 2015, 2018, 2019 for many sold out shows. The New York Times called his Busey Hamlet a “deliciously deranged…head-spinning performance” and his Trump Lear “quite damning” and “a biting satire”. He also has a show called 200 Impressions in 60 Minutes.

people.name: David Danipour
people.bio: David Danipour is an actor in Los Angeles, CA. He’s made TV appearances on The Good Place (NBC), Westworld (HBO), and The Dropout (Hulu) among others. He is excited to be a part of The UCB Theater and loves the community of creative people it fosters.

people.name: David Donnella
people.bio: Hailing from the Philadelphia area, David Donnella is a writer/performer bringing that no-nonsense east coast sensibility to Los Angeles. For instance, sandwiches are too expensive out here! You can see him on Harold Night with Local Tycoon.

people.name: David Ferguson
people.bio: Dave is an Emmy-nominated writer and producer. His credits include Comedy Bang! Bang!, Between Two Ferns, Michael Bolton’s Big Sexy Valentine’s Day Special, John Mulaney and the Sack Lunch Bunch, I Love You America, That’s My Jam, Funny or Die, and award shows like the Emmy Awards, the Spirit Awards, and the MTV TV and Movie Awards. As an actor, he has appeared in shows like The Office, Superstore, The Good Place, and Conan. He is a member of The Birthday Boys, which first performed at the UCB Theatre and went on to create their Bob Odenkirk-produced sketch show at IFC.

people.name: David Fouhy
people.bio: David Fouhy spent a lot of money going to Emerson College for a Theater Degree and now he does amateur improv. A member of Improvised History since 2014, he also performs with several other Boston-area teams: Tales of Enstonishment (a Twilight Zone riff), Junebug (an improvised space western) and It’s A Living (a two man show about working stiffs).

people.name: David Greenslade
people.bio: GI Frum NC N BK

people.name: David Guzman
people.bio: Originally from Toledo, Ohio, and southeast Michigan, David Guzman has written for the UCB Maude teams (212), Charles, Bundt, and Pretty Boys. He also directed the show Netflix Presents: You’ve Got Mail at the UCB Theatre in New York. David has written on the video staff for The Onion and is a regular contributor to the website. His humor writing has been featured in The New Yorker, McSweeney’s, Funny Or Die, Points in Case, and other outlets. He’s the writer and creator of the satirical podcast miniseries Welcome To Wealthy. His comedic novel Pole Antarctica: The Doomed Gabriel Expedition To The Bottom Of The World will be available Winter 2024.

people.name: David Leppert
people.bio: David Leppert is an actor/singer/mover who graduated with his BFA in Musical Theater from the University of Florida. He is elated to be making his UCB debut with Sing Out, Louise! In his spare time he enjoys negotiating his love of roller-coasters with his fear of heights.

people.name: David Luong
people.bio: David is a writer-performer originally from Santa Ana, CA. When he’s not hunched over a laptop or yelling onstage, David likes to munch on popcorn, watch romcoms, and collect CDs. His dream is to sell a feature script and/or be featured in a Taco Bell campaign.

people.name: David Saliterman
people.bio: David is a comedy writer, performer, and cartoonist based in NYC. He currently writes on UCB Maude Night with his team Mom’s World. His parents are very proud.

people.name: Davy Gardner
people.bio: Davy is an award-winning writer, director, and curator who oversees all podcast and audio programming at The Tribeca Festival. He is a filmmaker, playwright, and improvisor. He writes and performs comedy with The Foundation at The Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in New York City.

people.name: Dayna LoBosco
people.bio: Dayna is a comedian and actor from Staten Island, New York. You can see her perform with Leroy or as a hot mom in commercials. She would love to make you a baked ziti and talk about the Sopranos.

people.name: Dean Blizzard
people.bio: Dean Blizzard is an improviser and actor based in Brooklyn, NY. You can see him performing with his improv teams Blumpkin and Fancy Pants all across the city. He also is the co-creator and producer of the web series Bushwick News Network which he started with his sister Jacqueline Blizzard and his friend Bini Yamin. He hopes to one day write this really awesome play idea he has.

people.name: Deborah Tarica
people.bio: Deborah Tarica was born and raised in Los Angeles, and took a brief hiatus from her small-town life to earn a degree in film studies from UC Santa Barbara. She started at UCB in 2007 where she performed for several years on Delicatessen and The Great War on Harold Night, with Rough Cut: The Improvised Movie, Assembly (A UCB Faculty Show) and co-hosted Required Viewing, among other shows. She was a member of the UCB Touring Company, has been teaching at UCB since 2012 and was voted best teacher 10 years in a row by no one, because that’s not a thing. Deborah is a writer and actor who has appeared on Reno 911!, Bajillion Dollar Propertie$, Do You Want to See a Dead Body?, Hulu’s The Hotwives (of Orlando and Las Vegas) and more! You can follow her on most things @instantdeborah, but not on an escalator please. People who stand too close are weird.

people.name: Dejen Tesfagiorgis
people.bio: Hi, my name is Dejen Tesfagiorgis and I’m a Brooklyn-based writer/performer. I’m a writing fellow with Sesame Street and PBS, have been a livestreamer with a few kids brands, and come to the comedy world from past lives as a saxophonist and tech seller at Microsoft. I’ve had the pleasure of being featured in comedy videos for Disney+, Cosmo, Bleacher Report, Dose, Harlem Film Festival and Cortizone-10. Through consistent posting I’ve grown my YouTube sketch channel Deja Deja Comedy to 5k subscribers, posting 2 new sketches monthly. Join me here for more updates –> http://bit.ly/funnyreel

people.name: Delmar Dualeh
people.bio: Delmar is a New York City based improviser performing across the city’s comedy scene. He has hosted and produced themed shows including The Mingle at the Magnet, a speed-dating show, as well as several all-Black improv showcases like It’s Almost Black History Month. He currently hosts Friends w/ Benefits, a biweekly stand-up and improv show in Brooklyn.

people.name: Dennis Budde
people.bio: Dennis Budde has been writing and performing comedy in Texas since 2011. He is a member of the Austin improv group Hot Goss, the JTS Brown group Folxxx, and was a writer-performer in the sketch show “Deep in the Heartless Texas” directed by Dave Buckman. During the pandemic, Dennis collaborated with Pronoia Theater to start the podcast Presidential Death-Match, as well as to produce a semi-live virtual show written by Dennis called “Back to the Normal”. Dennis has co-founded two sketch troupes and written, performed and produced for dozens of sketch shows.

people.name: Derek Demko
people.bio: Derek Demko is an LA-based actor, improviser, and musician. You can catch him in feature films (Camp Manna, Dinner in America), national commercials (5-Hour Energy), and performing improv comedy in Shamilton (UCB), Baby Wants Candy (UCB), and Mission IMPROVable (Westside Comedy Theater). Under his rap pseudonym, “Demko,” the DIY artist and producer has gained over 400k streams worldwide, released songs with Grammy-nominated artists, and connected with fans in 150+ countries. Derek’s family has 7 dogs.

people.name: Derek Hayes
people.bio: Philly/DC

people.name: Deric Brown
people.bio: Deric Brown is a stand-up comedian, improviser, podcaster, and writer hailing from Northern Virginia. Now based in Los Angeles, he got his start with the Pittsburgh improv group Crocodile’s Dilemma. Beyond performing, Deric has ghostwritten for hip-hop artists like Drake and Kendrick Lamar.

people.name: Derrick Hackett
people.bio: Philadelphia native, graphic tee enthusiast and performer on Harold Night.

people.name: Derrick Matthew Gary
people.bio: Derrick is an actor, stand-up comedian, and writer hailing from Atlanta, Georgia; not to be confused with Atlanta, Ireland. Notable credits include “pilot that never made it to air,” “scene in a big Hollywood movie that got cut,” and “going viral on TikTok that one time.” Derrick can’t believe he gets to make “good” and “ha-ha” on the UCB NY character house team “WIG!” Follow him yeah? ig: @derrickdicaprio tiktok: @derrickdicaprio twitter: @derrickdicaprio

people.name: Desi Domo
people.bio: Desi is a comedian, actor, and writer based in Brooklyn, NY. You may recognize her from things like Inventing Anna, Last Week Tonight with John Oliver, A Thousand and One, The Conjuring, and Clown Parade, a character comedy podcast by Will Ferrell, Bowen Yang, and Matt Rogers — in which she wrote and performed four episodes entitled “The Me in Mysticism” about a narcissistic mystic, Katinka. She received training from Davidson College (BA in Drama), The Accademia Dell’Arte (Commedia), and The Royal Shakespeare Company. She is from Cleveland Ohio and it does, in fact, rock.

people.name: Devin Bockrath
people.bio: Devin Bockrath is a New York based actor, improviser, and comedy writer. A native of Chicago, she began her career at the iO and Annoyance Theaters. Upon moving to New York, she trained as a dramatic actor with the Atlantic Theater School and currently teaches at The Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Her credits include Joe Pera Talks With You (Adult Swim/Max), The Daily Show (Comedy Central), The Other Two (Comedy Central), Girls 5 Eva (Peacock) and That Damn Michael Che (Max). In addition to performing with The Roster at UCB, you can catch her monthly improv team Olestra at BCC.

people.name: Devin Field
people.bio: Devin Field is an improviser, writer and stand up comedian originally from Seattle. He has written for Jimmy Kimmel Live, Workaholics, Billy on the Street, The Emmys and The Oscars, as well as his own half hour comedy special released on Comedy Central. He can be seen performing regularly with Yeti.

people.name: Devin Gant
people.bio: Devin is an actor, writer and musician living in Brooklyn. He was named one of JFL’s New Faces Creators in 2023 and has had his work featured multiple times in short film festivals and Vulture comedy showcases. He’s been an actor and writer with the sketch team ‘Young Douglas’ since 2019. Where is he? Probably referencing SpongeBob or WWE somewhere idk.

people.name: Devin O'Brien
people.bio: Devin O’Brien is a writer, performer, and filmmaker from New Jersey. He served as Head Writer for two seasons of the Philadelphia sketch comedy show Temple SMASH and was a writer and performer for the Pack Theater house team Night Coffee. He currently writes for the UCB Maude Night team Smokeshow. You can also find Devin producing shows with comedy band Up the Down and indie comedy team Duck Duck Duck.

people.name: Devin O'Neill
people.bio: Devin is a NY-based performer/writer/director and only child (yikes!). Her short film, We Broke Up, won the 2021 Raindance Script Competition. In 2022, Devin wrote and directed, “Kourtney Kardashian Alone Without a Phone” for Bustle. She has performed all over NYC, her improv team, The Cast, was featured in the New York Times, and her two-woman play “Opening Night” received critical acclaim at its NYC Fringe Festival debut. She has a long-haired dachshund named George and she really wants to tell you about him.

people.name: Devin Ritchie
people.bio: Devin is a writer, comedian, and artist from Northern California. He has been featured on UCB’s Harold Night, Characters Welcome, as well as being an improv instructor at UCBNY for many years. Devin has hosted and produced many variety shows around NYC including, Cool Shit/ Weird Shit (UCB), State by State (Caveat), and Smoothie Talk (The PIT). Devin makes short films with Jefferson White’s (Yellowstone, Paramount) production company Means of Production, which just produced a sketch comedy pilot called ‘High Life’

people.name: Devon Drew
people.bio: Sketch, improv, and character comedian. Currently performing on Maude night with Family Business. Her solo show Pop Star about her real life experience trying to be a teen pop star in 2010 went to Edinburgh Fringe 2024 and New York Comedy Fest 2025.

people.name: Diana Aydin
people.bio: Diana Aydin is a writer, performer, and comedian, currently on the UCB Lloyd team, Grunch. She’s a staff writer on the animated TV show, Alma’s Way, and her original animated series, Unibrow, was optioned by Very Big World Entertainment. Her comedy is inspired by her Syriac culture, her quiet suburban New York upbringing, and that one time she had brain surgery.

people.name: Diana Chang
people.bio: Diana Chang is an improviser and musician from North Carolina. Diana is part of a long and storied history of Dianas in Revenge Dresses; she can currently be seen in her UCB Sing Out, Louise! team, Revenge Dress.

people.name: Diana Witt
people.bio: Diana Witt is an LA based comedian and industry professional. She is a graduate of The Second City Chicago’s Comedy Studies college program and has studied at UCB and The Groundlings. Diana has also appeared on several daytime talk shows (it was a phase). She performs and writes monthly at The Pack Theater as a member of the sketch team Moxie. Diana is also an earnest pop artist and is 1/3 of ADJ, a famous pop trio (streaming on all platforms).

people.name: Dickie Copeland
people.bio: Dickie currently works for ABC as a comedy development executive. She co-hosts the famously spooky and unhinged live show and podcast THE TAROT SHOW with Hannah Garcés and currently coaches the UCB house team GAG. She performed for almost a decade with The Dragons and indie teams such as Area 51, Friday, Jr., and D’artagnan. And famously, she wrote and starred in her original show: MARIE ANTOINETTE 2.

people.name: Dione Kuraoka
people.bio: Dione Kuraoka is just happy to be here. She is a graduate of University of Cincinnati, CCM for dramatic performance and was in the 2016 CBS diversity showcase. Her favorite things are performing, laughing and being with her dog, Mochi.

people.name: Divya Gunasekaran
people.bio: Divya Gunasekaran is a New York-based writer and standup comedian. She was in the inaugural cohort of the Warner Bros. Discovery Access Comedic Voices Program in 2022, participated in The Onion’s writing workshop in partnership with the Diverse As Fuck Comedy Festival in 2023, and is currently a writer on a UCB house sketch team. She is committed to showcasing diverse, underrepresented voices. She produces a weekly open mic and monthly show featuring women and LGBTQIA+ comedians. Outside of comedy, Divya’s life goals include cage diving with great white sharks and winning a pie eating contest.

people.name: DJ Rouse
people.bio: Born and raised in Nebraska, he’s a classically trained actor who can be seen performing around Los Angeles… on purpose.

people.name: Dominic Finseth
people.bio: Dominic (or actually, you can call him Dom) can be a performer or even a writer, but mostly he is spreading the good word of the improv-deity, CLiNT (which is actually just his improv/sketch team). Additionally, Dom is based right here in NYC/Brooklyn and looks good in most all shapes of sunglasses.

people.name: Dominique Nisperos
people.bio: Dominique Nisperos performs with Betty Character Night’s WIG! and Maude Night sketch team VILLAIN! at the Upright Citizens’ Brigade Theatre in New York where they also teach sketch, standup, and character. They have been honored as a Yes And Laughter Lab Fellow and a Ford Foundation Fellow with credits on ABC, Funny or Die, Blue Man Group, TVLand, ABS-CBN Global, Edinburgh Fringe, SF Sketchfest, and WorldStar among others. Their comedic sensibilities are like a disgusting pubescent boy who got really into Audre Lorde: low brow humor merged with doctoral training in sociology. They’re a proud to Chicanx and Filipino American kid from Stockton, Ca. Seeking representation.

people.name: Don Fanelli
people.bio: Don has been performing both improv and sketch with the UCB Theatre for over a decade, was a member of the longest running improv team in NY, The Stepfathers, and founded indie rabble-rousers Fuck That Shit. He also was a long time teacher, coach, Touring Company leader, and facilitator of corporate workshops. He performed at the Just for Laughs Festival in the New Faces: Characters division, wrote some screenplays, and acted on the ol’ TV.

people.name: Donald Chang
people.bio: You’ve heard his stupid voice on Netflix’s Squid Game and WB Games’ Gotham Knights. UCB teacher since 2019. Was on Lloyd Night with teams Horse Girl and Din Din and Harold Night with Dunce, Havoc and then Calico, and Characters Welcome Team, Mallrat.

people.name: Donna Steele
people.bio: Donna Steele (she/her) is an actor, improviser, and writer based in NYC. She performs weekly on Harold Night with babygirl and on Magnet Theater’s house team, Janet.

people.name: Dorcas Tejeda
people.bio: Dorcas Tejeda is a Chicago-born, Atlanta-raised actress, improviser, writer, and producer. She wrote, produced, and starred in a short film, “Sisters” that hit ABFF and snagged a Fan Favorite nod. You may have seen her on; BMF (STARZ), Criminal Minds (CBS), General Hospital (ABC), Our Kind of People(FOX)—and yes several commercials! IRL, she bakes a mean cheesecake and eats her way through the world—no crumbs left behind. Catch her @justdorcas

people.name: Dorshae Middleton
people.bio: Dorshae Middleton is a first generation American and an LA native. He loves making people laugh and he’ll probably pretend like he didn’t write this himself. He can be seen at UCB with his team Jinx and if you’re in the community and wondered “Where have I seen that guy before?” It might’ve been on his previous house teams: Hubble and Bug Juice. He will probably be in more things or maybe not but either way say feel free to say hey. Also, if you see him walking by the theater with his headphones on, he’s probably pretending to be in a movie.

people.name: Doug Moe
people.bio: Doug is a long-time improv performer and teacher at UCB (Teacher Feature, Mother, Doug Moe is a Bad Dad, etc.) and currently performs around town with Old Yorkers. He’s a comedic actor (“Inside Amy Schumer,” “Difficult People,” “30 Rock”), appearing most recently in Nicole Holofcener’s film “You Hurt My Feelings.” His book “Man Vs Child: One Dad’s Guide to the Weirdness of Parenting” was named one of Amazon’s Best Humor Books of 2017 and he’s the co-author/ghostwriter for a number of books, including three books for D.L. Hughley.

people.name: Douglas Prodor
people.bio: Douglas Clayton is a San Diego native and longtime improviser who’s been performing since 2015. He co-founded Rat City with his best friends. Douglas also directed Junk Mail: The Variety Show, which was featured in the 2024 San Diego Fringe Festival, and has trained with UCB, Chad Damiani, The National Comedy Theatre, and Mockingbird Improv. When he’s not making things up onstage, Douglas creates videos, music, and sketches that blur the line between heartfelt and absurd.

people.name: Doy Roberts
people.bio: Doy looks like an old timer, but she hasn’t been in the game all that long. She’s finally found her home in the Austin, Texas improv scene. She’s thrilled to try to find love representing ColdTowne Theater in The Rose.

people.name: Drew Coolidge
people.bio: Drew has been improvising for over 20 years and is a graduate of UCB-LA and iOWest. He has performed with former UCB team Daddy Babies, toured with Second City’s BoatCo, and performed with numerous iOWest mainstage Harold teams including festival favorite, MAX. He’s performed and taught in festivals in Texas, Utah, New Mexico, Illinois, Pennsylvania, Alaska, and all over California as part of the duo Ranger Danger and the Danger Ranger. He has lived in Austin for the past 8 years where he’s taught at Fallout Theater and performed at Coldtowne Theatre in Stool Pigeon and as part of the Sunday Night Harold powerhouse, All Night All Night All Night.

people.name: Drew DiFonzo Marks
people.bio: Drew DiFonzo Marks has taught and performed at The UCB Theatre since 2005. He is a former Artistic Director of the LA theatre, and a contributing author of the UCB book; The UCB Comedy Improv Manual . He is also an alumni of the Amsterdam based comedy theatre ‘Boom Chicago’ where he lived performing sketch and improv for 2 years. Drew has performed/taught at improv festivals and schools in Beijing, London, Sydney, Dublin, Glasgow, Omaha, Austin, NYC, and many more all over the US with the UCB Touring Company. He was also an adjunct professor for the Comedic Arts Major at Emerson College for 5 years. You can see him in TV shows like Reno911 , Comedy Bang!Bang! and Brooklyn99 . He was a writer for the Comedy Central digital sketch platform, and is co-creator of the web-series “2Bed/1Bath” with over 1 million views. His improv team, Last Day of School, performed weekly at UCB for over 13 years and was on the sketch Maude team Bonafide for 5. He loves this stuff!

people.name: Drew Sieber
people.bio: There are two wolves inside me, one is lies and the other tells the truth.

people.name: Dylan Eshbaugh
people.bio: Dylan Eshbaugh is a New York-based writer and actor currently writing for Golden Hour on Maude Night. He was a first-wave YouTuber in 2006 with his web series Dylan’s Couch, which has gone on to gain over 5 million views and was awarded three Webby Honors – Best Host, Best Comedy Series, and Best Viral Video. You can see him star in the upcoming horror-comedy feature film Gothic Slayers, which he also co-wrote. Dylan is an alum of NYU Tisch and the NBC Late Night Writers Workshop.

people.name: Dylan Fugel
people.bio: Dylan Fugel is a writer from Frankfurt, Germany, by way of London, England. You can see his work with Young Douglas if you’re cool, and with Story Pirates if you’re cool and have kids. He cares about Borussia Dortmund, Virginia Woolf, and postpunk music, in that order. He is aware that his accent is confusing.

people.name: Dylan Smith
people.bio: Dylan auditioned for his college improv group 10 years ago and hasn’t looked back since (his neck mobility is atrocious). He performed on house teams at Improv Asylum Boston, The Armory, and Upright Citizens Brigade (NY). Like everyone from Long Island, Dylan is Jewish, plays basketball, and has divorced parents.

people.name: E.A. Moss
people.bio: EA is an LA-based improviser performing primarily and enthusiastically with her indie team, Pyramid Scheme. Though now residing beach-side, she maintains the comedic stylings of being formerly based and trained in New York. EA is also a beginner figure skater and amateur painter, two things she brings up quite a lot, just a heads up.

people.name: Eddie Feeley
people.bio: Eddie Feeley is a Boston based improviser who has been performing improv since 2015, minus a brief pandemic-related pause. He performed with Comedykazi from 2018–2020, creating long-form narrative shows inspired by nerdy genres out of the back of a comic book shop. Since then, he’s explored everything from character-driven sci-fi stories with the space western show Junebug to historical epics with Improvised History and fully improvised musicals with Uncomposed!. He especially enjoys long-form storytelling, collaborative worldbuilding, and finding comedy in wildly specific ideas.

people.name: Edgar Momplaisir
people.bio: Edgar Momplaisir is a TV writer and filmmaker. He’s worked on Blackish, Dear White People, Everybody Still Hates Chris, and the Blackest show of all — Star Trek: Lower Decks.

people.name: Edson Montenegro
people.bio: Edson Montenegro (they/them) is a queer, Latinx comedian/actor/writer based in Brooklyn and originally from Los Angeles. Aside from performing on UCB NY Maude Night, they’ve appeared on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, originated the role of Toby in Silver Foxes directed by Michael Urie, have performed at SF Sketchfest and New York Comedy Festival, and written comedic essays with Insider. Also, they’ve lowkey gone viral for parodying RuPaul’s Drag Race ( peep here ). IG/TikTok: @ edsonmountaineggroll

people.name: Eduardo Arrufat
people.bio: Spanish actor, director and producer. Eduardo started improvising in Berlin in 2014 and is now a NY based, part-time rocket scientist, full-time late, comedian. Cocreator fo Sci-Prov, a science inspired improv show at Caveat Theater, writing sketches with D.R.ED Comedy, performing with EVPG, directing short-films and eternally writing his next TV pilot

people.name: Ele Woods
people.bio: Ele Woods is an actor, writer and UCBLA Maude night alumni. She starred opposite Keke Palmer in her Emmy award winning comedy TURNT UP WITH THE TAYLORS and has been a guest star on shows like THE GOOD PLACE for NBC and AMERICAN PRINCESS for Lifetime. She was a story editor on Kakao’s upcoming animated comedy THE RYAN SHOW, wrote on BIRDGIRL for Adult Swim, and was a producer for Youtube’s BLACK RENAISSANCE. At UCB she used to host the long running Cool Shit Weird Shit and her and Jessica Haymond’s stage show, Bad Business (2018 NYTVF Scripts official selection) ran for over a year. In her spare time she trains with the Independent Shakespeare Company of Los Angeles and made her debut as Macbeth with OpenDoor Shakespeare.

people.name: Elena Dole
people.bio: Elena Dole is an improviser and musician from Los Angeles. Elena performs at UCB with “Sing Out, Louise!”, at the Clubhouse for Club Louise, and on the indie circuit with Curl Pattern. Born and raised in LA, she was in her first commercial at the age of 6 months old, where she played a baby swimming underwater. She loves roquefort and a strong black tea with a splash of milk.

people.name: Elena Martinez
people.bio: Elena Martinez is a quadruple threat – improviser, clown, sketch writer/performer, and accountant. She performs improv and sketch all over LA on several teams. Last year she won Best in Immersive & Games at Hollywood Fringe Fest with her solo debut Funeral Show.

people.name: Eli Gonzalez
people.bio: Eli is a native Angelino and full-on homosexual. He speaks Spanish fluently and is looking to sign with the best reps in town. Or anyone who’ll have him.

people.name: Eli Newell
people.bio: Eli Newell is a veteran UCB performer, world traveler, and creator of Blind Date Live! He hosts an annual wiener dog race and once drove across Uzbekistan for fun.

people.name: Elijah Crocker
people.bio: Currently teaching and performing at Faraway Theater in Birmingham, Alabama, Elijah is a Rochester, NY raised improviser and UCB Harold Night Alumni. Super psyched to be returning to the UCB stage with friends old and new!

people.name: Elisa Ballou
people.bio: Elisa Ballou (e-LEE-za) is a Brooklyn-based comedian, improvisor and puppeteer originally from beautiful Staten Island. You can catch her performing at UCB with her team The Board and in various shows around the city. Ask her to make you a puppet!!

people.name: Eliza Kingsbury
people.bio: Eliza is a comedian, musician, artist, and drag king (Dom Parinyon – Winner of Drag Duel Season 2 on Youtube – @domparinyon IG/TikTok). She is based in NYC and CT. Her UCB credits include Maude Teams ‘Pamela’, ‘Yes Chef!’, and ‘Presley’ as well as her solo shows ‘GAGA: Portrait of a Lady’ and ‘Pop Stars Are Dumb (But We Love Them)’. Other comedy festivals and credits include San Francisco Sketchfest, Toronto Sketchfest, Montréal Sketchfest, Reductress. She writes original comedy songs, including the oft-performed smash hit drag king number ‘Your Pussy Made me Cry (Godfried’s Lament)’ available on Spotify and other streaming platforms.

people.name: Elizabeth Slack
people.bio: Elizabeth Slack is a writer, improviser and singer originally from Columbus, Ohio. She has a bachelor’s degree in vocal performance from the Eastman School of Music and an MBA from the University of Florida Warrington College of Business. She also performs weekly with Premiere: the Improvised Musical at the Magnet Theater.

people.name: Ella Jenkin
people.bio: I love to act, write and direct as well as make stuff up. In my past life I was a figure skater. I’m from London and love the rain.

people.name: Ella Richards
people.bio: Ella (El) Richards (they/she) is in their final year studying English literature at the University of British Columbia. As last year’s co-president of UBC Improv, El fell head over heels for improv with the help of such a lovely community of student performers. She can’t wait to perform alongside so many talented comedians and improvisors at the 2026 Del Close Marathon!

people.name: Ellen Lang
people.bio: Ellen Lang is a comedy writer currently working on an upcoming NBC series from Jimmy Fallon. She has worked on variety specials for NBC and Netflix featuring Nikki Glaser, Aubrey Plaza, Kevin Hart, and more. Prior to that, Ellen spent two years in variety and live television development, and prior to that, she was a ghostwriter for an eccentric food entrepreneur (ask her about her doomed treatment for ‘ Master Chef in space’). Her comedy videos have received millions of views on social media along with the heartwarming comment “5/10.”

people.name: Ellie Martino
people.bio: Ellie Martino is an LA-born, NY-raised comedy writer, artist, and playwright. She grew up watching cartoons and musicals, so her writing style typically involves people either singing or falling down. Ellie is also a cartoonist and character designer and sold her animated pilot, THE SHELBYS, to Sony Pictures Television. She is a member of LA indie sketch team, Critical Booby. Ellie is THRILLED to join Maude team, Baguette, as a writer! Ooh la la!

people.name: Elyse Ruppert
people.bio: Elyse is an LA-based comedian who you can catch performing improv, standup, and more all around the city. Originally from the Midwest, Elyse trained at the Second City and Annoyance Theatre in Chicago before landing in Los Angeles. Elyse is an Enneagram 7, a Myers-Briggs ESFJ, and thinks all personality tests are bogus.

people.name: Emerson Boatwright
people.bio: Emerson is an actor based in LA and doing the thing! He spends lots of time on stage in musicals and being an idiot.

people.name: Emily Davidove
people.bio: Emily Davidove is a comedy writer and director from New Jersey (brag). Her work has been performed at UCB NY, including a run of Hint! A Murder Mystery with sketch team Deathbird, as well as the People’s Improv Theater as part of the BoogieManja sketch collective. Emily has written for various publications including Reductress, NY Magazine, Funny Or Die, and Above Average. Previously a writer for the Maude team Wishbone, you can catch her sketches at UCB LA with her current team, Detention!!! every month.

people.name: Emily Dayton Evans
people.bio: Emily Dayton Evans (she/her) is a SAG/AFTRA actor, comedian, writer, and award-winning filmmaker. As a proudly gay woman, Emily centers feminism, sexuality, and mental health in her writing. As an actor, Emily most often plays straight women. Onscreen credits include: The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, The Other Two, The Loudest Voice, and Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt.

people.name: Emily Gonzalez
people.bio: Emily Gonzalez is 1/3 of the podcast Too Scary; Didn’t Watch.

people.name: Emily Kraft
people.bio: Emily Kraft is a NYC based Singer and Improviser.You can catch her performing regularly with the indie improv team Siblings or bursting into song at any moment across the city. When she is not busy getting applause, you can catch her at Homegoods searching for the perfect lamp or at Central Park with her beloved pup.

people.name: Emily Maya Mills
people.bio: Emily Maya Mills is an LA comedy person and San Francisco native. She’s written and performed hundreds of shows at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre– as a member oof several Maude teams, a standup and a character actor. The LA Weekly once called her solo show, “…a sagacious autopsy of the frailties and foibles of life,” and asked, “Could Emily Maya Mills be the next Carol Burnette?” (Um. No.) She’s been a series regular on Disney’s Right Now Kapow and appeared on Orange is the New Black, Marvel’s Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D., Key & Peele, The UCB Show, Comedy Bang! Bang!, The Birthday Boys, Parks and Recreation, Harry’s Law, Crash and Bernstein and a bunch of commercials. Recently, Emily earned an MFA at USC and has directed several award winning shorts. She now lives in Burbank and plays a fictional version of her husband’s wife on the podcast, Valley Heat.

people.name: Emily Murnane
people.bio: Emily is a writer, director, producer, and editor in Los Angeles. She directs and produces short films that have been featured at Sundance Film Festival and Short of the Week and was also a producer for NBC’s “A Little Late with Lilly Singh”. She and has also written/directed/edited content for outlets such as Condé Nast, Hearst, VICE, Warner Music, Facebook/Meta, amongst others. She is currently a writer for a UCBTLA Maude team.

people.name: Emily Olcott
people.bio: Emily Olcott is an actor, singer, writer, comedian, and director. Off-Broadway: Kafkaesque! (Theater 154); Live Laugh Love… (solo show developed at Ars Nova’s CAMP residency). Comedy: Joe’s Pub, Union Hall, Ars Nova, The Bell House, Lincoln Center, JFL Toronto, and The Kennedy Center, opening for Amber Ruffin. Directing: The Story Pirates National Tour; My Dead Wedding (UCB); Beth (Joe’s Pub); Bada Swing! (Joe’s Pub). Emily performs musical improv with UCB house team Rumpleteaser and indie team Cookie, and also co-hosts a comedy podcast about spirituality called Savior Complex. You may have seen Emily in a long-running commercial campaign for the New Jersey Lottery, in which she was wearing a lab coat. www.emilyolcott.com

people.name: Emily Wilson
people.bio: Emily Wilson is a New York comedian known for her comedy special, “Fixed: How I Got Rejected by Reality TV,” which was featured in the New York Times, The Guardian and Nylon. She was nominated for Best Newcomer at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival and regularly opens for SNL’s Please Don’t Destroy.

people.name: Emily Wrolson
people.bio: Emily is an actor, writer and sketch comic from Minneapolis, MN. She has also trained and performed at The Second City – Chicago and you can catch her around town performing in her sketch duo: Nette & Nellie!

people.name: Emily-Mae Kamp
people.bio: Emily-Mae is an actor, writer, and choreographer born and raised in Los Angeles! She studied Theatre at USC with a minor in Dance and Choreography. She now runs her own youth theatre company, Studio for Live Arts, performs with sketch and improv teams around the city, and tours performing improv for kids with The Imagination Machine. Emily-Mae has been attending UCB shows since the day she turned 16 and got her license, and started taking classes as soon as she was 18, so performing here is truly her dream come true.

people.name: Emma Campbell
people.bio: Emma Campbell can do it. An improviser, writer, and actor based out of New York, Emma has performed on House teams in Washington State and California including The Upfront Comedy Theater and End Games. Find her performing around the city with her indie improv/sketch teams Clint and Candle & Lantern. She is also a member of Second City’s Grad Review Conservatory. Most common review about Emma? “She’ll do it.”

people.name: Emma D'Ambrosio
people.bio: Emma left her hometown of Las Vegas when her skin got too dry and left Boston after college because getting hypothermia at a 5k fun run was not the vibe. Thankfully her skin and body temperature are thriving in LA so you can see her perform on the UCB stage.

people.name: Emma Holder
people.bio: Emma Holder has been improvising, writing, and performing at ColdTowne Theater since 2008. Despite this, she is not old.

people.name: Emmett Spaw
people.bio: Emmett is a performer by nature and a writer by trade- His work has been produced nationwide, and he was recently contracted by the Eisenhower Presidential Library to produce an original comedy for America250. When operating in the private sector, his interests include MarioKart and chuckling.

people.name: Enzo Ventura
people.bio: Enzo Ventura is an NYC based actor originally from Chicago. He is a recent graduate from NYU Tisch School of the Arts where he studied Drama at the Lee Strasberg Institute and Experimental Theatre Wing. Enzo has been on Dangerbox for three years and has made the best memories in college with them.

people.name: Eric Austin
people.bio: Eric Austin is an actor, improvisor, and voice actor. He regularly performs comedy shows around NYC with Ten Bones Theater, Random Access Theater and Rising Sun Theater.

people.name: Eric Berey
people.bio: Eric is a multi-talented LGBTQ+ and Jewish identifying actor, dancer, and sketch comedy writer based in Los Angeles, CA. He was most recently featured in Bagel Boss the Musical , on Jimmy Kimmel LIVE! (ABC) and performed in the 1st National Broadway Tour of Fiddler on the Roof . Eric was a company member of Third Rail Projects and performed in their Bessie Award winning immersive theatre show, Then She Fell . His work has been featured in the New York Comedy Festival, Bushwick Film Festival, Los Angeles Comedy Film Festival and the 92Y Mobile Dance Film Festival. Eric graduated with a BFA in Acting from Emerson College.

people.name: Eric Crumwell
people.bio: You can catch Eric performing on Harold Night

people.name: Eric Feurer
people.bio: Eric is a Brooklyn based writer and performer. He is the co-host of Characters Welcome and has been directing, teaching, and performing character comedy in the city for the past 11 years. His writing has been developed and optioned by Tiny Reparations & Disney TV Animation Studios, and has sold sketches to TruTv and College Humor (it’s NOT his fault neither of them exist anymore). He runs the Characters Welcome Youtube page, which sits at 210k subscribers and nearly one billion worldwide impressions. Ask him about when the Blue Man Group hired him and then hated all of his ideas.

people.name: Eric Gersen
people.bio: Eric is a pianist, composer and comedian from NYC who has composed music for Comedy Central, TruTV, TBS, Improv Everywhere as well as web series, podcasts, short films, and videos across the internet. He has toured around the world with acts like Matt Rogers, Colin Mochrie, and Off-Book. He’s the Associate Music Director for the Story Pirates and their award-winning Podcast, as well as a contributing composer to the Calm App. He was also on America’s Got Talent and Simon liked him, so that’s cool. He’s the writer, lyricist, and composer of the musical Seventeen Seventy-Something, and founder and director for the musical improv team Rumpleteaser.

people.name: Eric Greenbaum
people.bio: Eric Greenbaum is an actor/comedian based out of Brooklyn, NY. He was a member of The Second City’s Touring Company out of Chicago, and is an active member of his sketch comedy duo, Disco Horses, which performs nationally and internationally (notably Edinburgh Fringe Festival, New York Comedy Festival, and SF Sketchfest). Eric is also a graduate from NYU Tisch’s drama program, and writes and performs in comedy, drama, film, tv, and more for money *dollar sign eyes emoji*. Eric is currently seeking theatrical representation and management. If you’re reading this, Eric has a crush on you, but is too nervous to say anything.

people.name: Eric Michaud
people.bio: Kinda sings. Sorta dances. Definitely gay.

people.name: Eric Scott
people.bio: Eric Scott is a teacher & performer at the UCB Theatre. In 2020, he co-created/developed Bugs Bunny Builders for WB Animation, currently airing on Cartoon Network & Max. He’s written for MTV, CN, Fox/4Kids, E!, Disney, Facebook Watch, Comedy Central, and more. In 2012, Eric created and developed a live-action sitcom for Nickelodeon.

people.name: Erica Diederich
people.bio: Erica is a queer actor, writer and comedian living in Brooklyn, NY. She can be seen performing characters and sketch all around New York City as well as in numerous commercials playing on a digital screen near you! Erica is a lover of all things horror and paranormal, as is evident in her one-woman show ‘Stay Funny. Stay Scared.’

people.name: Erica Strickland
people.bio: Erica is a thespian-turned-improviser who has been active in the Boston comedy community since 2018. When memorizing lines and blocking for a play felt like a chore, she took an improv 101 class at Improv Boston and never looked back. When she’s not doom scrolling, manifesting, or making girl dinner, she is thinking about how grateful she is to have the opportunity to perform at such a rad festival.

people.name: Erik Olson
people.bio: Erik Olson grew up singing, dancing, and improv-ing. After studying neuroscience at the University of Minnesota for a year, Erik transferred to Syracuse University where he graduated with a BFA in Musical Theater. Erik is so excited to be making his UCB debut in Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Erik Tanouye
people.bio: Erik is a long-time performer and writer at UCB. He currently performs with Old Yorkers and at a variety of shows across the city. From 2009 to 2020, he ran the UCB Training Center in New York, where he also taught improv and sketch writing. Outside of the comedy world, he has published several crime novels under a pseudonym and belongs to the Teamsters.

people.name: Erin Bartley
people.bio: Erin started her time at UCB doing improv and then started her sketch career. When she isn’t travelling the country with her indie team Wicked City she is performing sketch with The Foundation one of UCB’s longest running sketch teams.

people.name: Erin Cholakian
people.bio: Erin Cholakian is a Los Angeles based stand up comedian, improviser, and actress. She’s best known for creating the nationally touring Heated Rivalry Comedy Show and for saying “can you call my mom and tell her that?” after receiving any form of compliment.

people.name: Erin Michele Davis
people.bio: Erin Michele Davis is a Brooklyn-based improviser and writer. She can be seen performing at UCB’s Lloyd night, Young Ethel’s Bar-Prov Experiment, and around the city with her indie team Anne Rice (no affiliation with the author – just deep admiration).

people.name: Erin Smith
people.bio: Erin Kelly Smith is that classic multi-hyphenate that describes every dang creative living in Los Angeles (say it along with us!): “Writer, director, editor, improv/character comedian, short story author, film critic, cartoonist, pop-up book artist, brown hair, glasses, from Seattle originally!”

people.name: Erin Whitehead
people.bio: Erin Whitehead is a writer, actor and visual artist. She’s known for her performances with improv team Wild Horses, appearances in HBO’s Animals, Craig of the Creek, UCB Harold Night and more. She co-hosts the Comedy Bang Bang World podcast College Town with Seth Morris.

people.name: Esther Oh
people.bio: Created in Philadelphia and now living in Chicago, Esther is a improvisor, writer, actor, and probably a real human. She was a 2024 Victor Wong Fellowship fellow at Second City and currently performs with Stir Friday Night, Chicago’s longest running AAPI comedy group, Mischief, an iO Theater house team, and with a unspecified number of other groups. She comes with glasses, contact lenses, or with blurry eyesight, and is fun for ages 0-100+!

people.name: Ethan & Gigi
people.bio: Ethan & Gigi are an artistic duo from Brooklyn, New York whose work includes the dreams, idiocy, and vulnerability of clown, the cleverness and biting satire of sketch, and the urgency and fervor of agitprop (aka “agitFLOP”). They met in an improv class at Upright Citizens Brigade and have taken clown and physical theater workshops from master teachers such as Aitor Basauri, the clowns at Brooklyn Comedy Collective, Alex Tatarsky, Dr. Brown, and John Norris and Corey Podel.

people.name: Ethan Beach
people.bio: Ethan Beach is a Canadian-born film/television editor and comedy performer living in New York City. He has most recently worked as an editor for the Film Unit (Digital Shorts) on ‘Saturday Night Live’ for the past two seasons (S48-49). Prior to that, he was an onstaff editor at Comedy Central for several years (while they were still making stuff). His long-running Brooklyn comedy shows “Oh, Such A Good Show, Oh!” & ”Oh, Such A Huge Show, Oh!” have played at Union Hall, The Bell House & The New York Comedy Festival and have been written up in The New York Times, Vulture and Impose Magazine.

people.name: Eva Nemiroff
people.bio: Eva Nemiroff is a writer and comedian based in Brooklyn. She is a writer for Dropout shows Game Changer, Make Some Noise, and more. Eva’s 1/3 of the sketch group Tiny Idea, which has amassed over 500,000 followers and 90 million views across social media (@tinyideastuff). Her work with Tiny Idea has been featured in Vulture and NYMag. Eva also just started her own digital show @outsideloveshow which is kind of like if viral vertical dating shows and Between Two Ferns had a DEMONIC baby.

people.name: Evan Altshuler
people.bio: Evan Altshuler has been comedy person with varying degrees of relevance in New York City for over a decade. At UCB, he’s been a Senior Producer on digital teams Cardinal Goose and Pocketwatch, Writer for “Night Late,” and Performer in many shows including, “Hint! A Murder Mystery,” portraying the big dog himself – Kelsey Grammer. You can currently catch him improvising every Lloyd Night with The Board! Evan is also a McSweeney’s contributor and the producer behind “Live Dubbed Sitcoms,” a NY Times Critics Pick, and “The URDB World Record Appreciation Society,” which was featured on “Late Night with Jimmy Fallon.” Perhaps most appropriately, his on-camera TV credits include IFC, TV Land and OWN. Evan would love to direct your sketch show or coach your improv team, so please reach out to him right away!

people.name: Evan Barden
people.bio: Evan Forde Barden has been doing improv and sketch comedy since he went to college in 2004, where he unfortunately studied Finance. You can see him performing with his sketch team Young Douglas (usually) at Caveat and doing improv with The Friday Night Sh*w at Magnet Theater. He’s been a part of Maude, Lloyd, and Harold Nights at UCB and has had the absolute pleasure of gracing the stages at SF SketchFest, Toronto Sketch Fest, Hell Yes Fest, Montreal SketchFest, Chicago Improv Festival, and ImprovBoston. Yes, that is his real hair!

people.name: Evan Cleaver
people.bio: Evan Cleaver has a B.A. in Acting from Dillard University, an HBCU in New Orleans. After narrowly escaping Katrina he moved to California where he got his MFA in Acting from CalArts. He’s booked several jobs in Film/TV and as a filmmaker has made two shorts that played in Oscar qualifying festivals. He’s thrilled to be part of the UCB community!

people.name: Evan Ho
people.bio: I’m a performer at the Harrisburg improv theater. I’ve been with rockstar for a while now. It’s the best.

people.name: Evan Mills
people.bio: Evan Mills (He/Him) is an actor, writer and musician from Chicago, IL where he most recently wrote and performed in three Main Stage revues at The Second City. He was named one of the Chicago Tribune’s “Ten Best Theater Performances” two years in a row (2022, 2023), has won a Jeff Award for Best Performer in a Revue (2023), can be seen in the 2023 CBS Showcase, and was named a Just For Laughs New Face of ‘23. @awkvanmills on all platforms.

people.name: Evan Roder
people.bio: Evan Roder is an improviser, stand-up and writer from Cleveland, Ohio. He has studied improv at Imposters Theater since 2023, and can be found on their house teams IKEA Near Me and The Riff, with the indie team Sorry For Your Loss and the improvised Dungeons and Dragons show, CHAOTIC. In 2025, he was the winner of The Gauntlet: a standup, sketch & improv competition. He once squatted Will Hines.

people.name: Ezra Parter
people.bio: Ezra Parter is a comedian and actor from Madison, Wisconsin but has more of a Seattle vibe after going to high school and college there. After college Ezra focused on performing, studying in Seattle, Chicago, and New York, and eventually made it to LA in 2018. You can catch Ezra at UCB on Harold Night or on holidays with church parody show The Message, and around the city and internet with long running sketch group Public Breakup and the news satire team at The Daily Misinformer.

people.name: Faith Grego-Nagel
people.bio: Faith Nagel is an actor and writer from Kansas City, MO. Her solo show The Other Woman won multiple awards at Hollywood Fringe Festival. She’s been featured in various national commercials and has led multiple vertical miniseries.

people.name: Faith Howes
people.bio: Faith is a drama and elementary educator with a passion for arts integration. She has worked as a performer and teaching artist all over world and across the US, working for companies such as Disney Theatricals, Warner Brothers Entertainment, Microsoft, Amazon, Seattle Children’s Theatre, and Seattle Theatre Group. She is currently a 3rd Grade math teacher in Harlem!

people.name: Fallon Boles
people.bio: Fallon Boles is an actress, comedian, and native New Yorker living in Brooklyn. You can see her perform on Monday nights with her UCB MAUDE team, Ponyboy, and also around town with her sketch team, Young Douglas, featured in the Time Out NY, NY Comedy Festival, and the Toronto Sketch Festival. She also performs original characters at her open mic, Loose Lips, every Saturday at Pete’s Candy Store. Her children’s book, Oh, the Places You’ll Glam!, was based on and endorsed by baby gorgeous herself, Lisa Barlow of the Real Housewives of Salt Lake City. You can check it out on Fallon’s website or Lisa’s Instagram!

people.name: Farshad Khansari
people.bio: Farshad Khansari is a comedian and actor based in New Jersey, but really it’s a hop and a skip away from New York. You can currently find him on the team VILLAIN! on Maude night, but you may have seen The PIT, Gotham Comedy Club, Caveat, and various sketch festivals in the US and Canada. He is also a recipient of the PIT/SNL Scholarship. He gravitates to a spectrum of comedy from playing larger than life characters to just being his witty self and roasting his friends. Farshad speaks 4 languages fluently and many not fluently. The fluent ones are Persian, French, and Japanese. What’s the 4th one? Well you’ll just have to ask him!

people.name: Fernanda Brigneti
people.bio: Fernanda Brigneti is a writer and performer based in Brooklyn, NY. Born and raised in Peru, where she was first introduced to comedy through improv, Fernanda has written and performed in various sketch shows, from Second City to BCC. She recently participated in Ars Nova’s ANT Fest and was an artist in residence. She continues her improv journey as a member of improv teams “¿Qué?” and “God’s Website”. She is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Fernanda Perez
people.bio: Fernanda’s sketches have been featured in UCB’s Maude Night, Pack Theater’s Sketch Night, Second City’s LA Diversity in Comedy Festival, Latinx Comedy Pachanga shows, and other indie theaters. She started her comedy career doing stand-up in Japan, where it is considered rude to laugh loudly. Currently, she works as a copywriter by day and writes comedy and fiction by night. You can watch her sketches at the UCB stage or her improv at various indie theaters.

people.name: Fin Argus
people.bio: Though it seems impossible to top their first credit as “Kidz Bop Kid in hit children’s music franchise Kidz Bop”, Fin Argus (they/she), continues to dazzle as both an actor and musician. Hailing from northwest Chicago, you may recognize them from her characters on “The Other Two”, “Queer as Folk”, or “Clouds”. But true fans would know her from iconic drag performances as Pig Milk, and busking on the third street promenade at 16. With a debut indie rock album on the horizon, Fin is true multi-hyphenate, and would like your attention please, or else what was the point of moving to LA?

people.name: Florence Friebe
people.bio: Florence is a comedian/actress/singer/Victorian ghost who can be seen performing regularly with Asian AF, the Armory’s current longest running house team, Cereal Mascots, heard on Sounds Funny Radio, an improv podcast alongside some of the cities best improvisers and creating new forms with Looper, an improvised scene on repeat. She has been entrenched in the NYC comedy scene since 2012 and has spotted regularly sitting in your spooky attic window.

people.name: Fran Gillespie
people.bio: Fran Gillespie is a writer and performer originally from Chicago. Some of her credits include: Saturday Night Live, Big Mouth, Killing it on Peacock and John Mulaney Presents: Everybody’s in LA on Netflix. Her favorite food is pickles of course!

people.name: Frank Caeti
people.bio: Frank Caeti acts, improvises, writes, directs, and teaches anywhere they will let him. He’s honored to return to UCB, previous UCB credits include: Harold Night and directing Ithamar Has Nothing To Say and Supportive White Parents. Frank was a series regular and contributing writer on the long-running FOX show MADtv. Other tv credits include: Modern Family, Key and Peele, Jimmy Kimmel Live, Adam Ruins Everything, Chicago Fire, NCIS, and over three dozen commercials. Regional theater credits as a performer, writer, and director include: The Second City National Touring Company, The Second City etc, Kennedy Center, Goodman Theatre, Kirk Douglas Theatre, La Jolla Playhouse, Laguna Playhouse, and Dallas Theater Center. He currently teaches BFA acting students at California Institute of the Arts.

people.name: Frank Garcia-Hejl
people.bio: Frank is a writer, actor and director who has been part of UCB for almost 20 years. You might be familiar with his work as a writer and actor for Broadway Video’s Latino internet channel Más Mejor or may have seen him on USA’s First Impressions with Dana Carvey. He performed in the 2018 CBS/VIACOM Showcase, wrote for the 2020 showcase and returned in 2021 to Associate Direct and Produce it. He was named one of New Faces: Characters for the Montreal Just For Laughs Comedy Festival in 2016 and also one of Comedy Central’s Comics to Watch 2015.

people.name: Freddy Boyd
people.bio: Freddy is an ATLien living and performing in LA. Check him out in various shows about time or breaking it down at your favorite karaoke spot. You can also see him for seconds at a time in shows on Hulu, Disney+, AMC, and more. Come say hey and let’s make a secret handshake.

people.name: Fu Goto
people.bio: Fu is a New York-based improviser/writer originally from Tokyo, Japan. She improvises regularly around the city with RaaaatScraps, Magnet, Asian AF, etc. She loves improv! Her current life goal is to post more stuff on Instagram :/

people.name: Gabe Greenspan
people.bio: Gabe is a born and bred Los Angelino, escaping only briefly for college to study theater at Yale. Nowadays, he writes and performs as one half of the sketch comedy team The Idiot Chimney and with Quick & Funny Musicals. He’s found a niche in parody musicals, including THE SOLVE-IT SQUAD, THRONES! THE MUSICAL PARODY, and POKEMON: THE MEW-SICAL (which he also wrote). He’s the cool stepdad to two scruffy dogs, and the dad-dad to one scruffy toddler.

people.name: Gabriella Bottoni
people.bio: Gabriella Bottoni is a comedian, actor, writer/director, & weirdo babe from NJ currently living, working, and missing good bagels here in Los Angeles. She performs weekly at the UCB Theater with her improv Harold team “Branzino” (the first full Lloyd team in UCB-LA history to be promoted to Harold – @branzino.improv), and around LA & online with her improv/sketch trio of diabolical girl besties, “Triple Homicide” (@triplehomimprov). Gab was a two-time finalist in the Sundance Institute Episodic Lab, a semi-finalist in the Second City Sitcom Contest, and more triumphantly, her mommy’s biggest baby (5’10”).

people.name: Gabriella David
people.bio: Gabriella David (pronounced the Filipino way: Duh-veed) is an actor and comedian from Dallas, Texas. You might’ve seen her in national commercials for Starbucks, Apple, and Chewy, but her real claim to fame? “Chicken Sandwich Girl” in a Jollibee ad. When she’s not doing sketch and standup around LA, you can catch her at AMC Burbank 16 or hanging with her diva pug, King.

people.name: Gabrielle Fleck
people.bio: Gabrielle Fleck is a Brazilian actor and producer. She’s an NYU-Tisch grad and loves playing pretend so much that she moved to the US on her own at 14 to pursue a career in the arts. She wishes life were a musical, and musical improv is the closest thing to making that a reality.

people.name: Gaku Kelliher
people.bio: Gaku Kelliher is attempting to be the first person to have one million followers on Instagram. Follow his journey @gakukelliher. He also performs on the improv teams The Lake at Dawn and caucAsians, who can be seen all around New York.

people.name: Gareth O’Connor
people.bio: Gareth is an improviser and teacher at The Free Association in London. He is a member of former house Harold team ‘NANA’, headline show ‘JACUZII’ and produces the shows ‘Fabled: The Improvised Fantasy Adventure’ and ‘PIGPEN’. As an actor he played Eamon in the original West End cast of ‘Once’, and has been featured in video games like Crimson Desert, Dragon Quest, Evil West, Hellblade II, Warhammer: Age Of Sigmar and Vampyr.

people.name: Gavin Speiller
people.bio: Gavin Speiller was born and raised in Queens, NY. He became a regular performer at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in 2005 and started teaching classes a couple years after that. As an improviser Gavin has been a regular cast member in Asssscat, Harold Night, Death By Roo Roo and Shitty Jobs as well as other shows and ensembles with ridiculous names. He was also a member of UCB’s National Touring Company for a number of years performing hundreds of shows at various colleges, theaters & festivals across the country. As a teacher he’s taught every level of the UCB curriculum at both the NY & LA theaters. He moved to the Austin area with his wonderful wife and two children in 2021 and is very excited to continue doing more of the stuff he’s described in this bio.

people.name: Genevieve Joers
people.bio: A Seattle native and New Yorker by way of Columbia University, Genevieve is an actor, writer, and a joy to have in class. Recent credits include Not Suitable for Work (Hulu), Sesame Street: The Musical (Off-Broadway), and a Google commercial that ultimately rendered her a woman in STEM. Genevieve has also held internships at Late Night with Seth Meyers (Writing) & Full Frontal with Samantha Bee (Production).

people.name: Geoff Kaufman
people.bio: Geoff is an alumni of Maude Night.

people.name: Geoff Ross
people.bio: A comedian, writer, and filmmaker originally from Minnesota by way of Illinois. He was Howard on the Netflix show Pretty Smart, the Funeral Director on Reno 911!, a comedian on the Netflix show You, himself on Make Some Noise, Chief Brody in Anime Crimes Division season 2, in far too many commercials to name, a former Features Contributor for The Onion, the former host and senior writer for Doin It Live on The CR!SP Network, and was chosen for the 2nd annual ABFF/NBCUniversal Hosting Workshop in 2016. He was trained at the Upright Citizens Brigade in New York and LA as well as ImprovBoston in Cambridge, Massachusetts and holds a bachelor’s degree in Film and TV production from NYU and a master’s degree in Film from Boston University.

people.name: Geoffrey James
people.bio: Geoffrey James can be seen performing on Maude Night with The Faculty. He also hosts The Headgum Podcast , which has recorded live shows at the Gramercy Theatre and Littlefield in NYC. He was a cast member in the 2023 CBS Showcase, and his acting credits include The Neighborhood (CBS), Bedrock (Fox/Warner Bros. Animation), Lonely & Horny (Dropout), and some commercials that his parents’ friends have texted him about a lot.

people.name: George C. Owens
people.bio: In August of 2024, George took his one man show Holy Sh*t I Lived to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival where, despite George’s best efforts, it received a 5 star rating. He has multiple films premiering soon including, Not Without Hope , Ghost Complex , American Schemers , and The Likes of You . You can follow George on social media @george_c_owens.

people.name: Georgie Aldaco
people.bio: Georgie Aldaco is a Latinx writer, actor and director who won a WGA award for her work on INSIDE AMY SCHUMER. She also worked on two seasons of Hulu’s KOALA MAN, Comedy Central’s ALTERNATINO, and was a staff writer on Simon Rich’s MIRACLE WORKERS (TBS). Georgie is also a member of the comedy collective that produces Sketch Cram (a sketch show written in a single day!) once a month at the UCB Theatre, and she has contributed material to the animated sketch show RIGHT NOW KAPOW, TEENAGE EUTHANASIA, and Funny or Die’s scripted podcast SCIENCEOLOGY. Georgie’s sketch comedy album, THESE WERE HUMANS, was an official selection in the Tribeca Film Festival and can be downloaded wherever you get your podcasts!

people.name: Gibbee Stone
people.bio: Gibbee Stone he/him is a sketch, improv, and standup comedian from Long Island. He was a musician and actor in his youth before moving towards sports due to his megafauna stature and competitive disposition. A back injury made him choose to go back and pursue comedy.

people.name: Gil Ozeri
people.bio: Gil Ozeri is an American comedian, actor, writer, and producer. He began his career with the UCB in New York and has written for Big Mouth , Brooklyn Nine‑Nine , Happy Endings , and the upcoming A Hundred Percent . As a performer, he’s appeared in Kevin , #blackAF, Curb Your Enthusiasm , Search Party , and Uncorked .

people.name: Gilli Nissim
people.bio: I’m from LA and will defend it against jealous haters with my dying breath. At UCB, I can be seen improvising with the house team Winslow and hosting my cathartic and frankly filthy talk show We’re Gross. On TV, I’ve written for Twisted Metal , all 3 seasons of The Other Two , AP Bio , Pretty Smart , and am supervising this really fun animated show in development at Paramount. So that’s me, how’ve you been?!

people.name: Ginny Sanborn
people.bio: Ginny is a Brooklyn based improviser who you might remember from some pre-pandemic Lloyd rosters!

people.name: Gio Schwab
people.bio: Gio Schwab is an LA-based actor, writer, and director. After graduating from the prestigious Professional Children’s School in New York City, Gio moved cross-country to hone his skills in Hollywood. Notable credits include ABC’s Fresh Off the Boat and Grey’s Anatomy , and Adult Swim’s Helpers . Currently, Gio can be found performing onstage at The Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in Los Angeles.

people.name: Glo Tavarez
people.bio: Glo is a comedian. Glo has been on Harold and Maude night. Glo has been in many shows at the theatre like, The Female Gaze and Fifty Cincuenta. Glo has also been on tv and in movies.Glo makes Youtube videos. Glo is funny. Watch Glo!

people.name: Grace Avery
people.bio: Grace Avery is an LA-based improviser having a splendid time rn. She has a deep love for the LA improv community, and a deep fear of corners. Yay!

people.name: Graham Techler
people.bio: Graham Techler currently performs on Betty Night with The Roster and appears sporadically on Characters Welcome. He contributes writing to McSweeney’s Internet Tendency and The New Yorker, and writes for the musical variety show STAGE TIME WITH PJ ADZIMA (The Slipper Room). As a playwright, he won the 2025 Richard Rodgers Award and his work has been produced Off-Broadway at 59E59 with Less Than Rent and with the Ensemble Studio Theatre’s Obie-winning collective Youngblood. TV: FAIRVIEW (Comedy Central), STEPHEN COLBERT PRESENTS TOONING OUT THE NEWS (Paramount+), OUR CARTOON PRESIDENT (Showtime).

people.name: Grant Cleaveland
people.bio: Grant Cleaveland (he/ him) is a Brooklyn based actor, improviser, and comedy writer. Grant is currently performing on Maude Night with his team Golden Hour, and was an actor on the team Eleven Eleven in 2025. Grant can be seen performing all over NYC with his comedy trio SLOP, who won 10 rounds of UCB’s Cagematch in 2024. Grant is also a cast member on DEMON TIME, a weekly sketch comedy show at Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Finally, Grant makes “content” on TikTok, where he has 140,000+ followers.

people.name: Grayson Niles
people.bio: Originally from Ottawa, Canada, Grayson grew up in Houston before moving to LA and doing the one thing that freaked him out the most when he was a child: being on stage. He’s made great strides from the quiet child who spent more time with legos than people and now does comedy on stage all the time! He’s thrilled to be on Maude night with his new team Rodeo Baby, and is also learning to juggle! Ask him about it!

people.name: Greg Hess
people.bio: Greg Hess is a founding member of Cook County Social Club and the Improvised Shakespeare Company. He has developed original comedies for Warner Bros, Comedy Central, Showtime, Pop, UCP, and Adult Swim. His hit podcast MEGA was named “Best Satire” by Vulture Magazine, and his humor writing has appeared in the New Yorker “Shouts & Murmurs.”

people.name: Greg L. Smith
people.bio: Greg Smith is a director, writer, actor, and songwriter from Detroit. He’s directed award-winning short films, webseries, and theatre; written for CBS, Shudder, and the Internet; acted for CBS, Pop TV, and countless commercials; and written songs for College Humor and G4. He loves improv, sketch, musical comedy, horror, and iced chai lattes.

people.name: Greg Roman
people.bio: Greg Roman is an irrationally confident douchebag who has appeared at UCB on MAUDE NIGHT (Nephew), HAROLD NIGHT (Cheeks, Hearsay), and CHARACTERS WELCOME (as both a performer and a director). In 2022, you might have seen him playing an irrationally confident douchebag as one of the leads in the movie DIVORCE BAIT (Samuel Goldwyn Films), or also in the films MENORAH IN THE MIDDLE (Hulu) and WHITNEY HOUSTON: I WANNA DANCE WITH SOMEBODY (TriStar Pictures). In 2023, you can look for him in the revival of the Starz series PARTY DOWN (he plays an irrationally confident douchebag), the festival film JACK & LOU: A GANGSTER LOVE STORY (he plays an irrationally confident douchebag), and the wildly popular streaming show THE CHOSEN (he plays a very humble nice guy – just to mix it up). Past favorite roles include guest stars on BONES, CHUCK, and THE MENTALIST (and yes, he displayed irrational confidence in all of them).

people.name: Greg Santos
people.bio: Gregory Santos is a Cuban-American Actor & Comedian based in Los Angeles, CA. He’s been in several TV Shows and National Commercials and would love to be on more!

people.name: Greg Tuculescu
people.bio: Greg is an Emmy-nominated writer and performer who has been with the UCB since 2004. His film and TV credits include Bridesmaids, I Love You, Man, The Office, and Curb Your Enthusiasm. Greg was also head writer and producer of Comedy Central’s Drunk History for four seasons. He currently lives in Los Angeles with his wife and two daughters.

people.name: Gregory Cornejo
people.bio: Greg Cornejo is an NYC based Comedian. He’s done Stand Up, Improv and is currently a writer on the Maude team Dumptruck. Helps produce the Diversity Sketch Lab @ the Magnet Theater. CREDIT: Someone told me I remind them of Rihanna.

people.name: Gregory Wallace
people.bio: Born in Humboldt. Raised in Los Angeles. Actor. Writer. Own a dog. Like Beach.

people.name: Gustavo Brito
people.bio: Born and raised in Rio de Janeiro, Gustavo is a multifaceted Brazilian-American actor, director, singer, and writer based in New York City. He has a BFA in Musical Theatre from Emerson College and is an avid performer/student of improv and sketch comedy, having trained frequently at UCB and other comedy theaters in NYC. Gustavo has worked with companies such as RWS Entertainment Group, Holmdel Theatre Company, and Missoula Children’s Theatre. He is also a member of indie improv teams Sweet Treat (@sweettreatimprov) and Ms. Coach (@mscoachinprov).

people.name: Gwen Hollander
people.bio: Gwen has appeared in recurring roles on Showtime’s Kidding (w/Jim Carrey) and Hulu’s Future Man (w/Seth Rogen and Josh Hutcherson). Film: Marriage Material (Searchlight Pictures – Best Actress, Florida Comedy Film Festival), T-R-O-U-B-L-E! starring Anthony Rapp. Favorite theatre: Bway/Natl. Tour of Little Women (Amy, Carbonell Award), Les Miserables (Eponine, Carbonell Award), & Beauty and the Beast (Belle). Her parody videos have been regularly featured on Whohaha.

people.name: Hailey Irwin
people.bio: Hailey Irwin (she/they) is a writer, director, and actor based in Brooklyn. They graduated from NYU Tisch with their BFA in Drama and a minor in Producing ‚Äî there, she trained at the Atlantic Acting School and the Classical Studio, and was selected for the Producing in London Summer Program. During their time at NYU, they fostered a passion for comedy; Hailey was the president of NYU‚Äôs premier improv team, Dangerbox, and executive producer of NYU sketch comedy team, Comic Sans. Website: https://hirwin08.wixsite.com/haileyirwin IG: @hailey_irwin_

people.name: Hal Rudnick
people.bio: Hal has been a part of UCB LA since 2006. He has enjoyed being in The Midnight Show, 6 Harold teams, Tournament of Nerds, and Characters Welcome among many other shows at the theater. Hal was also on the bowling team in high school, achieving a varsity letter for it.

people.name: Haley Hepworth
people.bio: Haley Hepworth is an actor and writer based in New York. She was one of the New Faces of Character Comedy at the Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal, has played crazy ladies and normal ladies in sketches on The Daily Show and Jimmy Kimmel Live, and can be seen in the upcoming MGM+ show American Classic. She currently performs on ASSSSKETCH and as a member of solo sketch superteam CABAL.

people.name: Haley Mills
people.bio: Haley Mills is an actress and improvisor originally from Great Falls, MT. She has been doing improv in LA since 2018. National Treasure is her favorite movie.

people.name: Haley Stiel
people.bio: Haley Stiel is a comedian based in Los Angeles, CA. Critics call her the “lesbian Matt Rife.” She’s part of the sketch group Babe Motel and runs her own shows called Improv But Good and Totally Haley. She performs all over LA and NY and has been featured in The New York Comedy Fest, SF Sketch Fest, and more. You can see some of her work online @haleyuka

people.name: Hallie Haas
people.bio: Hallie is a Northampton, MA-raised, LA-based actor and comedian who loves, above all else, dressing appropriately for the weather. She recently finished her first short, Sourdough, which she wrote, co-directed, and stars in. Hallie has appeared in SEARCH PARTY (HBO Max), ALTERNATINO (Comedy Central), the feature films FORT TILDEN (SXSW Grand Jury Prize) and ASKING FOR IT (opposite Stephanie Hsu), as well as videos for Comedy Central Digital and The New Yorker, among others. She is amped to be on the UCBLA stage after performing at the New York theater with Characters Welcome, Maude Night, and her one-person show Masterpiece Classic: Women in Art and is especially amped to dig into her ol’ bin o’ wigs.

people.name: Hank Funderburk
people.bio: Hank Funderburk is an actor and comedian based in Los Angeles, CA. He can currently be seen as a performer on Lloyd Night. Hank is the son of a lawyer and a minister, who couldn’t be more thrilled that their first-born is pursuing the equally-estimable career of doing bits.

people.name: Hanna Barlow
people.bio: Hanna Barlow is an Asian American improviser and writer born, raised, educated, and stuck forever in LA. She performs musical improv at UCB with “Sing Out, Louise!” and “regular” improv through WGIS and the beloved indie circuit.

people.name: Hannah Berkowitz
people.bio: Hannah Berkowitz is a writer, director, and performer, from Las Vegas, Nevada. She was selected as a performer for UCB’s first class of Maude Fresh Faces. She performs and writes for her independent sketch comedy group, Same Great Taste, that was recently a part of the Hollywood Fringe, and puts on shows bimonthly at the Elysian and the Yard Theater. Hannah penned and directed her debut short film, Shpilkes which premiered at the LA Shorts International Film Festival and played at the Michonne Film Festival. Her reach has expanded to TikTok, where she has accumulated over 19 million likes and 50 thousand followers. She is also visually gay, but in a way that is palatable.

people.name: Hannah Florence Chin
people.bio: Hannah Florence Chin has performed in 6 Broadway shows, 3 Broadway tours, and several regional and Off-Broadway productions. Hannah is currently in Romy And Michele The Musical Off-Broadway at Stage 42. @hannahflorencechin

people.name: Hannah Garcés
people.bio: Hannah is a waitress and comedian from Portland, Oregon. She co-hosts the hair-raising and rowdy live show (and podcast) The Tarot Show with Dickie Copeland. Please clap.

people.name: Hannah Hedrick
people.bio: Hannah Hedrick is actress, writer, and creative, is 5’6″, and based in LA. She produces the Shortlist Show with Caitie Mac in Los Angeles and is so excited to be performing at DCM! She recently acted in 2 short films, Conditional Love & Quick Fix, and an up-coming parody web series to be released in October. In her spare time, she does Pilates, makes her friends laugh, and yearns.

people.name: Hannah Moore
people.bio: Hannah Moore is an actor, writer, and natural redhead from Dallas, TX. As Director of USC’s premiere comedy troupe Commedus Interruptus, Hannah performed improv at UCB’s LA College Comedy Night, and wrote and produced sketches for the 2025 PIT NYC Sketchfest. Most recently, she wrote and produced her one woman comedy show, “Verve, Intensity, and Personality” at the Madnani Theater in Los Angeles. Ask her about her specials!

people.name: Hannah Solow
people.bio: Hannah Solow is an NYC based actor, singer, and writer. She is currently understudy to Mary Todd Lincoln and Mary’s Chaperone/Bill in “Oh, Mary!”, written by Cole Escola and directed by Sam Pinkleton, at The Lyceum Theatre on Broadway. She received her BFA in drama from New York University’s Tisch School of the Arts. She traveled to over 20 cities across the country performing as “Gertie Cummings” in Rodgers and Hammerstein’s “Oklahoma!” directed by Daniel Fish. She performed on Broadway at The Cort Theatre as the “understudy” to Mike Birbiglia in his show, The New One. She performed every Monday at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade with musical improv team, Rumpleteaser, and monthly on Maude Night. She has performed her original standup, characters and songs at Union Hall, Caroline’s, Club Cumming, Littlefield, and many more places/basements.

people.name: Harper-Rose Drummond
people.bio: Harper-Rose is a comedian and co-host of the audience interactive podcast Tea Time. In addition to standup, the last two years she dived into musical improv and is on four indie teams, in addition to Sing Out, Louise! at UCB. She loves to make everyone but her mother laugh.

people.name: Harris Solomon
people.bio: Harris Solomon is an actor, writer, and reluctant Jewish sex symbol based in NYC. While at Columbia University, he wrote and performed for the Varsity Show (a 130 year old tradition with an overly-specific Wikipedia page). After graduating, he trained at The Groundlings Theater in LA before joining UCB’s Maude Night. He also wrote and starred in a play (Bard Overboard, Off-Broadway) and is currently developing a new musical, several film projects, and a strongly worded email to his landlord.

people.name: Harrison Merkt
people.bio: Harrison has been preforming improv from Los Angeles, Harrisburg, and Philadelphia since 2013. He taught improv and sketch comedy as a professor at USC School of Cinematic Arts before committing himself to music and percussion full-time.

people.name: Harsha Vardhan Dasarathy
people.bio: Harsha is a person, originally from Bangalore and now based in New York. You can see him on stage at UCB or on 6th Ave dodging people in the bike lane.

people.name: Hayley Huntley
people.bio: Hayley Huntley has been performing with UCB since 2013 on Harold Night (Belmonte, The Dragons), in Cagematch (The Holdup), and with Shitty Friends (f.k.a. Shitty Jobs). Her TV/film credits include Review (Comedy Central), Lopez (TVLand), Scream Queens (Fox), and Babylon (dir. Damien Chazelle).

people.name: Heather Anne Campbell
