people.bio: Heather Anne Campbell is a writer and Executive Producer on Rick & Morty. Her credits include Saturday Night Live, The Twilight Zone, The Eric Andre Show, Creepshow, Corporate, and Miracle Workers. She is a regular performer on Whose Line is it Anyway? and releases two weekly podcasts: Get Played, and Get Anime’d.

people.name: Heather Daughtry
people.bio: Heather Daughtry is a multi-hyphenate creative born and raised in South Los Angeles whose work spans performance, writing, and event production. She is the co-producer of Who All Gon Be There, an all-Black improv show that has sold out every month since its launch. Passionate about music, theatre, film, television, and tech, Heather is dedicated to inspiring, uplifting, and making people laugh. Her mission is to create purposeful work that resonates deeply within her communities and beyond.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

people.name: Heather Higginbotham
people.bio: Heather Alarcón Higginbotham is a producer, actor, and independent filmmaker from Miami, FL. She’s produced podcasts for various networks including Earwolf and Starburns. She’s also 1/2 of the sketch comedy duo, John Baxter, whose viral sketches have been featured on Mashable, Splitsider, Whohaha and more. Currently she works in production on several live action Star Wars shows including The Mandalorian and Ahsoka.

people.name: Heather Mari
people.bio: HEATHER MARI is an actor, improviser, writer, content creator & dancer from Rockville, MD while currently residing in Chicago, IL. Heather was in the inaugural Victor Wong Fellowship at The Second City in 2023. You can also find her performing throughout Chicago in Stir Friday Night, Wet Pizza, Clued In, Hitchcocktails, Improv: The Play, iO’s House Team Fever Dream, and more. You can find her on IG/TikTok at @theheathermari

people.name: Heather Woodward
people.bio: Heather Woodward is an actor, singer and writer originally from everyone’s favorite state, New Jersey. At UCB, Heather was on Harold night for 4 years with the teams ROCOCO and YETI, has directed the Maude teams TRAINWRECK and MAMA, and has written, performed and worn wigs in one million other shows at the theater on both coasts. She’s best known for writing the award winning AAA game “APEX LEGENDS” at Respawn Entertainment/Electronic Arts. Currently, Heather is writing for everyone’s favorite comedic video game franchise: “CALL OF DUTY” at Infinity Ward. There are also a bunch of other TV acting and writing credits, and prior to comedy, Heather toured the country in multiple musical tours and has performed in 39 states.

people.name: Henrik Blix
people.bio: Henrik Blix is a writer for The Problem with Jon Stewart on AppleTV+. He previously starred in Second City’s Deck The Hallmark Holiday show, and performed in “Best of the Second City” at the Kennedy Center with Rachel Dratch and John Glaser. He’s a two-time winner of Chicago’s Laugh Battle as a solo performer, a former Headline Contributor for The Onion, and he makes very good bolognese. You can follow him online @henrikontheweb or in real life if you are stealthy and have a good disguise.

people.name: Henry Block
people.bio: Henry Block is a comedy writer and performer living in Brooklyn, NY, originally from Chicago. In addition to writing sketch with his UCB Maude team, Gorgeous Stank, he writes humor and satire for the Onion, Reductress, and McSweeney’s. He also regularly performs improv, characters, and stand-up around the city.

people.name: Henry Kaiser
people.bio: Henry Kaiser is an Actor/Singer/Comedian who has performed/written in over 100 shows at UCB Theatre over the years. He has also participated in many Improv and Comedy festivals throughout the world. He’s starred in the film Detective Detective Detective (dir. Justin Edwards) and was a recipient of a Creator First Grant on Clubhouse, the Social Audio app, where he created 13 episodes of NNPR–Not National Public Radio (now called Small Town Almost Radio). He loves performing/singing/creating in all types of places and can’t wait to see you at his next show!

people.name: Herbert Benjamin
people.bio: Being the offspring of a DJ and the Diva of Education, Herbert Benjamin is a passionate, quirky” cousin “ to the city of New Orleans.Some of his recent performances have been Martin ( Humidor) , Capulet ( Romeo and Juliet) ,Collins ( Rent) and his sold-out solo show , The Tea .Also, Herbert’s a member of NYC’s improv troupe Entirely From Memory.

people.name: Hillary Anne Matthews
people.bio: Hillary Anne Matthews is an actor, improviser, and writer. She’s guest starred on Abbott Elementary , Modern Family , Ghosts , Florida Girls , Future Man , and Drunk History , and was a series regular on ABC’s Alex Inc . She also does voice over (which sometimes means putting a blanket over her head like a halloween ghost).

people.name: Holly Celeste
people.bio: Holly is originally from Portland, Oregon and currently having the best time living in LA. Holly is an improviser and performer who loves to do lots of stuff with lots of people. She has studied at WE, WGIS, and UCB. Holly talks too much about her cats.

people.name: Holly Prazoff
people.bio: Holly Prazoff has been doing comedy as long as she can remember. Originally from Toronto Canada Holly has won a Canadian Comedy and performed with the Second City Toronto Touring Company. She has appeared on The Late Late Show, Comedy Bang Bang on IFC and The Birthday Boys. Holly has been a member of two Maude teams at UCB and performed as part of many character and bit shows.

people.name: Howard Hendrix Powell
people.bio: Howard Hendrix Powell is a New York-based actor, comedian, and creator performing weekly on Harold Night at UCB, monthly in THEATER: The Improvised Play, and Off-Broadway in Drunk Shakespeare. He creates sketches and absurd characters for social media and YouTube. Howard has performed original comedy at UCB Franklin (LA), Brooklyn Comedy Collective, The Second City (NYC), Asylum, Caveat, and iO theatre (Chicago). He’s appeared in a national CNN commercial, various digital campaigns and UGC spots. His upcoming indie short films include Something Happened (dark comedy) The Vacuum Seal (psychological thriller) Janet (dark comedy) and The Rainbow Chaser (mockumentary). Visit www.howardhendrixpowell.com for his portfolio and upcoming shows or just ask him about it irl… he’s often described as approachable.

people.name: Hrannar Björnsson
people.bio: Hrannar Björnsson is a comedian from Iceland. He’s a member of the sketch group Tiny Idea and he currently writes for Dropout.

people.name: Hunter Nelson
people.bio: Hunter’s been a staff writer for The Onion’s video department, a contributor to ClickHole, and the director of seemingly hundreds of sketch shows for the UCB stage. These days he writes audio fiction for Jonathan Mitchell’s groundbreaking “The Truth” podcast (including “Danslang” and the 5-part “The Body Genius”), and is one of the producers of the arguably impossible to produce NYC Sketch Cram – in which an entire sketch show is written, blocked, memorized, and performed in a single day.

people.name: Ian Herrin
people.bio: Ian Herrin is a teacher and performer at UCB with 10+ years of longform improvisational experience. Ian is the host of the long running 9+ year improv show The Ian Herrin Improv Hour (IHIH) which has featured Special Guests from Saturday Night Live, Late Night with Seth Meyers, Comedy Central, Logo TV, E! Network, TLC, VH1, IFC, truTV, et al. But let’s face it, you really know Ian from that one Selena Gomez commercial he did years ago!

people.name: Ian Lockwood
people.bio: Ian Lockwood is a Brooklyn-based comedian and pop musician. He is the host of Ian Lockwood’s Girlfriend Pageant and his work was featured as one of Vulture’s Best Comedy Moments of 2023. Ian’s comedy concert experience, Ian Lockwood: The Farewell Tour, is currently touring the country ahead of a month-long run at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival. His work has been featured in Paste Magazine, Vulture, Time Out New York, and more. He is an alumni of NYU Tisch’s Experimental Theatre Wing, Second City’s Comedy Studies, UCB, and The Annoyance Theatre NY.

people.name: Ian Mellencamp
people.bio: Ian Mellencamp is an actor, musician, and improviser (both in music and comedy). He has performed in numerous Broadway readings, starred in indie pilots, sang on stages with music legends (Willie Nelson anyone?), and appeared in national commercials. As a writer, Ian enjoys crafting songs and sketches. He is currently a UCB Maude Performer on THE FACULTY and can be found improvising on LA based teams HOT SET and FREAKUS. If Ian asks you to repeat yourself, it’s because he grew up around loud music and still listens to it way too loud. Huh?

people.name: Ian Roberts
people.bio: Ian Roberts is a founding member of the Upright Citizens Brigade and an Emmy and Peabody Award winning showrunner.

people.name: Ibhan Kulkarni
people.bio: Ibhan Kulkarni is a standup comic, writer, improviser, American, Christian, and liar based out of Brooklyn, NY. He performs all over New York and beyond, both individually and as a member of the groups, Chicken Big and James & Ibhan. Most recently, Ibhan has been featured on America’s Got Talent, the Flyover Comedy Festival, New York Comedy Festival, and he was named a New Face of Comedy at the Just For Laughs Festival.

people.name: Ihea Inyama
people.bio: Ihea Inyama (he/him) is a comedian, writer, and actor from Ewing, New Jersey. He is is a co-creator and star of the web series i’m so happy for you!!!!!! . He has been awarded stand-up comedy residencies at Broadway and Greenwich Village Comedy clubs, and was a semifinalist in the 2025 Comedy Mob Festival. He hosts and performs in the monthly variety show Mind the Gap! and the improv show Stress Dream – LIVE! , and regularly performs stand-up and improv around NYC. He currently writes for his Maude team, Reunion Tour.

people.name: Iliana Inocencio
people.bio: Iliana Inocencio is the current UCB NY Co-Artistic Director, as well as an actor, writer, acting coach, and producer. She was on Maude Night with Peach and Presley and had a long-running UCB show with her musical comedy group, AzN PoP! Since then, they’ve gone on to perform regularly at Joe’s Pub at The Public Theatre. Her favorite credits include Orange is the New Black, The Other Two, The Iliza Shlesinger Sketch Show, NBC Diversity Showcase, and Broadway Bares. Iliana is passionate about community service and currently spearheads UCB Corps. She loves acting coaching, collaborating with people who champion diverse perspectives in decision-making rooms, and saying hi to dogs.

people.name: Inessa Frantowski
people.bio: Inessa is a Canadian Screen Award and Canadian Comedy Award winner. She spent 4 delightful years on Maude and has since directed for Maude and Characters Welcome. She was selected as one of Just for Laughs 2022 New Faces Characters and you can see her doing stand-up or on TV!

people.name: Isaac Garza
people.bio: Isaac Garza is a filmmaker and performer currently based in Austin, Texas. He was part of the inaugural HBO Pa’Lante Promise Incubator Program for emerging Latino filmmakers. His collaborations have played at Tribeca, HBO Max, Bend Film, Austin Film Festival Short of the Week, and Vimeo Staff Picks. He likes to hang out, play tennis, and go climbing. Catch him weekly at All Night All Night All Night every Sunday evening at Coldtowne Theater.

people.name: Isaac Washington
people.bio: Isaac Washington is a Brooklyn-based writer, improviser, and comedian hailing from South Carolina. He regularly performs improv with his indie team boy basement and sketch comedy with his group Hey There Neighbor!

people.name: Isabella Escalante
people.bio: Isabella Escalante is a writer and improviser living in Los Angeles. She wrote an episode for Wu-Tang: An American Saga. She likes downing two iced coffees then wondering why she’s anxious.

people.name: Isabella Gerasole
people.bio: Isabella “Izzy G” Gerasole is a comedian and James Beard Award Winner from Chicago. Recent credits include The Pansexual Bachelor (Elysian Theater, Winner), Funny or Die Lab Cycle Series 1 (Dynasty Typewriter), She’s Loud, He’s Quiet! (Elysian Theater).You can see her performing standup all around the city, and in her monthly show Joy’s Bed & Breakfast (Elysian Theater)

people.name: Itanza Saraz
people.bio: Itanza Saraz is a Los Angeles-born-and-raised actress, improviser, and singer/songwriter who, no big deal, shares a birthday with Beyoncé. She performs regularly with the Upright Citizens Brigade Harold team Gas Money and co-produces and performs in Friday Block Party, a monthly BIPOC comedy mashup that’s everything your office happy hour wishes it could be. She’s trained at the Upright Citizens Brigade (LA), Stella Adler (NYC), and the Pearlman Acting Academy (LA), and has toured her original music across the U.S. and internationally, playing countless intimate Sofar Sounds shows along the way. Itanza brings grounded honesty, emotional depth, and just a touch of the unhinged to every performance. She’s currently seeking theatrical representation. And snacks… Always snacks. 🙂‍↕️

people.name: Ivan Ngo
people.bio: Ivan is an improviser from LA (SGV 626 LA PUENTE!). He is on team Goat Sauce and The Headliners. He is known for calling people “Big Dog”

people.name: Izzy Roland
people.bio: As seen on HBOMAX! Dropout! Amazon!

people.name: J.r. Youngblut
people.bio: J.R. Youngblut is a comedian, writer, and performer based in New York City known for viral TikTok sketches and his extensive knowledge of Mesozoic paleontology. He is the creator and co-host of the local sketch morning show, New Day! New York! and a performer on the anthology improv team, Will of the People. This is his first DCM and he’s very excited to bring a little of that old school, punk rock, rats-in-the-basement energy back to UCB.

people.name: Jace Armstrong
people.bio: Jace is an actor-writer-director-producer whose credits include The Daily Show, Nathan for You, Cake, Chad & JT Go Deep, Comedy Bang! Bang!, The Eric Andre Show, a Samsung commercial in which he eats a huge nacho, a GEICO commercial in which he eats a normal burrito, and hopefully someday a Nissan commercial in which he eats a tiny chile relleno. His podcast with Justin Michael, Nature Talks to Itself, has been called a “sweet crude wacky laugh riot 🙂 great idea luv you :)” by his Aunt Cathy. He’s been on several UCB house teams and can currently be seen in his variety show Punchin’ It! with Sylvester Stallone, or his solo character show The Audition which he’s performed all over LA and NYC.

people.name: Jack Brown
people.bio: Jack Brown is an improviser and character performer originally hailing from Sacramento, CA. Jack performs on the Harold team yikes!, the character team Betty, and also hosts and performs in the character show, Career-Defining, Life-Altering Industry Showcase, at UCB.

people.name: Jack de Sanz
people.bio: Jack de Sanz is a writer, improviser, and actor hailing from San Francisco, CA. After graduating from NYU Tisch with a BFA in Acting and a focus in Shakespearean verse, he left it all behind to do Zip Zap Zop and Seth Rogen impressions. As a voice actor, Jack can be heard in the 2023 film Strays and the SyFy series Psycho Psalms. He is a sketch writer for the Maude team Smokeshow, performs improv weekly with his team STEM Kidz, and is maybe the 3rd best UCB-trained D&D Dungeon Master. Jack was the recipient of the coveted “Pen and Letter” writing award at his middle school, and he’s been chasing that high ever since.

people.name: Jack Dominick
people.bio: Jack Dominick is from Birmingham, Alabama, but currently uses a New York City Library Card. He performs comedy on stages across the city, and on screens across wifi. You may recognize hit tv shows “Mythbusters” or “Inside the NBA” – as of this recording – Jack has not worked with these programs, but welcomes the email that proves otherwise.

people.name: Jack Maloney
people.bio: Jack Maloney is a Comedian/Actor/Content Creator/Writer with over 100,000 followers across platforms. Jack currently works as a Comedy Video Producer at Betches Media and has trained in stand-up, sketch, and improv at UCB, the PIT, Manhattan Comedy School, the Comedy Cellar, and Second City Chicago. He was also a 2025 Webby Award Nominee and 2023 YASSS Fest Semi-Finalist. BFA in Musical Theatre from Penn State University and @thejackmaloney on all platforms!

people.name: Jack Styles
people.bio: A graduate with a homespun demeanor, Jack Styles’ boy-next-door charm hides a depth that moves audiences to tears. From Bedford Falls to the battlefield, he’s America’s everyman hero.

people.name: Jackie Clarke
people.bio: Jackie Clarke is a writer, producer and director living in Los Angeles. Her credits include Happy Endings and Superstore.

people.name: Jackie Ferro
people.bio: Jackie Ferro is an award-losing screenwriter and musical improviser from New York. She performs at the Magnet Theater every Thursday night as part of Musical Megawatt and at festivals and indie shows around New York with her indie teams Hey, Handsome! and Ephemera. When she’s not writing or making up musicals, she can be found playing board games, teaching digital media classes for teens at the New York Public Library, or re-watching Guardians of the Galaxy 3 for the 47th time.

people.name: Jackie Jennings
people.bio: Jackie is a director-writer-host with a TBR pile full of fantasy novels. She was lead host for the SYFY channel for years. Before that, she was a staff writer at The Chris Gethard Show, story producer for My Brother, My Brother & Me on Seeso, director of Baited with Ziwe and senior writer at Above Average.

people.name: Jackie Presedo
people.bio: Jackie Presedo is an improviser in Boston, MA and happy to return to DCM for the 3rd time.

people.name: Jackie Young
people.bio: Originally from Boston, Jackie is a sketch performer, writer, retired athlete and proud member of the Beyhive. Recently, she filmed two TV shows that are in post-production and has performed her solo character variety hour Jackie Young: Live and A Person in LA, New York and Boston. She is a current performer on UCB’s Maude team Loveseat, a Groundlings Sunday Company alumni, and worked on Season 1 of YouTube’s Emmy Nominated Tab Time starring Tabitha Brown.

people.name: Jacklyn Uweh
people.bio: Jacklyn Uweh (she/her) is an actor, writer, and producer. She performs nightly as part of The Second City New York Mainstage ensemble. Born and raised in Stockton, CA, Jacklyn started performing in local community theater productions. While at UC Irvine, she found her comedic voice as a founding member of UCI’s sketch ensemble, The Soulstice League. A performer at heart, Jacklyn’s stage credits include Hairspray (Chance), Striking 12 (Chance), Second City Theatricals, and the 2020 CBS Showcase. She’s been spotted in national commercials for McDonald’s, Plenity, and Cricket Wireless and lent her voice to Paladins and Wondery Media. Find her on the internet with the SMOSH gang!

people.name: Jacob Ben-Shmuel
people.bio: Jacob is an NYC-based Actor/Composer/Improviser currently playing Omar in Disney’s Aladdin on Broadway. He is also member of Shitzprobe and he puts on concerts of his live music once a month at The Gin Mill. He’s very tired.

people.name: Jacob Wysocki
people.bio: Jacob Wysocki is often described as a ‘good breeze’ or a ‘fine mist.’ He’s been performing sketch and improv for over 15 years. Notable credits include: Reno 911!, Pitch Perfect, Unfriended, Loosely Exactly Nicole, Terri, and countless videos for failed digital media companies. D.I.Y or DIE.

people.name: Jacqueline Blizzard
people.bio: Jacqueline Blizzard is a stand-up comedian and improviser in NYC! You can see her performing stand-up every weekend and improvising with Uncle Tough on Lloyd night! She also plays Heather Cummings on the web series Bushwick News Network, which she created alongside her best friend, Bini Yamin, and her brother, Dean Blizzard. Her Instagram is @stinkyweirdgirl, but she’s actually a very nice-smelling, normal girl. Her dream is to make indie comedy movies with her friends.

people.name: Jacqueline Emerson
people.bio: Jacqueline Emerson is a multi-hyphenate actor, writer, and musician, most known for her role in THE HUNGER GAMES. Recently, Jackie just wrapped back-to-back leading roles in both the comedy feature WINE CLUB (2023), and the heist-drama ART THIEF (2023), as well as a role in Paramount’s WINTER SPRING SUMMER AND FALL (2023). Upcoming films include KENT STATE (opposite Alec Baldwin), and the motion capture LIMINAL VIBES for John MacInnes (CALL OF DUTY). She has voiced characters across TV, film, and video games, including projects for DISNEY and DREAMWORKS. As a writer, she has multiple projects for film and television that have been sold and / or are currently in development, including two original musicals.

people.name: Jacques Chevelle
people.bio: Born in Atlanta and raised in nearby Lithonia, Jacques Chevelle discovered his love of acting and improv in high school production of Shakespeare’s Comedy of Errors .You can find some of his previous work on Paramount+. He is connoisseur of mac & cheese and is excited to join teammates on the legendary UCB stage.

people.name: Jacquis Neal
people.bio: Jacquis Neal is a tv/film and voice actor, host, comedian and podcaster. He hosts Crowd Control on DropoutTV and has played Damion on NBC’s Grand Crew , appeared on Last Week Tonight with John Oliver , Physical , How I Met Your Father , and Liza onDemand . He’s also the host/producer of the hit live game show, Comedian Clash . In addition to Crowd Control , he’s a regular cast member and writer on Dropout. He’s also a voice actor who you’ve seen and heard in many national commercials, Nickelodeon, shows and films on Netflix, video games, and more.

people.name: Jahred King
people.bio: Actor/Producer Jahred King was born in Vista, CA. He spent his smaller-sized years growing up in Richmond, Virginia. Jahred’s work in commercials, television, and broadway all started with his love for improv. All credit goes to God and my mama.

people.name: Jaime Lutz
people.bio: Jaime Lutz is a performer on the Harold team The Mall. She previously performed on the house teams Blush, Hotcake, and Ritual. Her thirty-minute sketch show “The Bride of Murdery Heights” ran for six months at UCB and was adapted into a web series. She co-hosted the podcast Dead Meet, which Time Out New York called “hilarious and disturbed,” as the character “Tonya Plank,” and has starred in a few low budget horror movies. She’s also written for NBC’s The Wall and HQ Trivia.

people.name: Jake Bettermann
people.bio: Jake is the first person ever to be both a writer and an actor. He lives in New York City, which is also very unique.

people.name: Jake Compton
people.bio: Jake Compton is the owner/operator of the Harrisburg Improv Theatre. Jake started improvising in 2004, taking classes at both the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater and the Magnet Theatre. He has toured the country teaching and performing with his duo show, Missed Connections, as well as his improvised band practice show, Rockstar Goes Supernova.

people.name: Jake Cornell
people.bio: Jake Cornell is a standup comedian based in Brooklyn, New York, who has amassed hundreds of thousands of followers online. After successful sold-out shows at Joe’s Pub with his hour “Hey Big Guy,” he’s currently touring all over America, hitting cities like Chicago, Dallas, New Orleans, Seattle, and LA with a whole new show. Most recently, Jake wrapped up his first-ever U.K. tour with a sold-out show in London. At the UCB, Jake was a member of the Harold teams Promises and Falco.

people.name: Jake Delaney
people.bio: Jake Delaney is a writer, actor, director, and most importantly, a single gay man living in Los Angeles. Outside of Maude, you may have seen Jake star as Daddy in Mommy which premiered as a part of REDCAT’s 2025 NOWfest or as Tali in The Real Housewives Parody Musical or as Roscoe in A Finger Pointing at The Moon which enjoyed a 5 night sold out run at The Elysian. His directorial debut short film, Matches , which he co-wrote and stars in is set to begin its festival run this summer. Previously, Jake co-wrote, produced, and starred as Max in The Filth , a 5 episode digital series which won Best Writing in a Comedy at SeriesFest in 2019 and premiered on Revry TV in March 2021. He earned his Bachelor of Arts in Screenwriting from Boston University and has studied and performed improv and sketch comedy at The Groundlings and Upright Citizens Brigade in LA.

people.name: Jake Fallon
people.bio: Jake Fallon is an actor, writer, and comedian from Denver. He currently performs on UCB’s Lloyd Night, BCC’s Home Teams, and hosts a monthly show called The Show Show Show at Pete’s Candy Store with his duo team The Go Go Go’s. He’s also done a good amount of commercial work which is why he may look vaguely familiar. Learn more about him at jakeyjakey.com

people.name: Jake Jabbour
people.bio: Born and raised in Colorado, moved to Los Angeles in 2009 with his cat Lemon and 14 years later they’re both in the same apartment next to UCB. Co-creator and co-host of We’re Gross with Gilli Nissim, founder/owner/director of WE Improv, and co-host to The Meat Improv. Author of the non-fiction book, Training to be Myself: An Indulgent Odyssey…

people.name: Jake Regal
people.bio: Jake is an actor and comedian originally from The Big Apple (New York City). He’s spent 10+ years on UCB Harold, Maude, and house improv teams, perhaps most notably with longtime team Cardinal Redbird. With over 30 television credits and dozens of national commercials to his name, he is a budding “that guy from that thing.” Recent credits include High Potential (ABC), St. Denis Medical (NBC), and The Neighborhood (CBS).

people.name: Jake Smith
people.bio: Jake Smith is an improviser, actor, and musician based in New York. He is a member of the UCB musical improv team Rumpleteaser as well as the seven time Cage Match winning indie-team Scrambled Legs. He starred in and co-wrote the original musical comedy “Enlargement” (named “Best Bet” at the 2018 NY Fringe Festival). He releases original music under the artist name Jake the Myth.

people.name: Jake Wallack
people.bio: Jake is a Jewish and queer actor, comedian, and writer. Originally from Plantation, Florida (I know, but I didn’t name it), Jake has returned to the land of his people and now resides in Brooklyn. When not performing on Maude Night, you can find him in theater around NYC. Jake has recently gotten into bouldering and won’t shut up about it.

people.name: Jamal Newman
people.bio: Jamal Newman is a comic, writer, and podcaster based in Washington, DC. He performs regularly with Lena Dunham Improv at the Washington Improv Theater. He is also the creator and host of Pod Chef, an ambitious rewatch podcast dedicated to recapping the entire series of Top Chef.

people.name: James Butler
people.bio: James is an attorney and improviser living in New York. James performed on Lloyd Night with Din Din and Kingship, and for many years with his indie team 2 Chairz.

people.name: James Dwyer
people.bio: James is an actor and comedian who you may have seen on TV in shows like “What We Do in the Shadows,” “Godfather of Harlem,” or perhaps you saw him in a series of commercial with ALF the puppet. James has performed at the UCB since 2011, formerly on Harold Night, The Law Firm, ASSSSCAT, A Big Dumb Thing, Strife, and many others. You can see him performing around the city with his current team Duchovny.

people.name: James III
people.bio: JAMES III is a writer, actor, and director, best known for co-creating and co-executive producing ASTRONOMY CLUB: THE SKETCH SHOW . He was a punch up writer for GOOD BURGER 2 and wrote on FAIRLY ODDPARENTS: A NEW WISH and the ALL THAT reboot. His TV appearances include ABBOTT ELEMENTARY , I THINK YOU SHOULD LEAVE , A BLACK LADY SKETCH SHOW , HUMAN RESOURCES , BIG MOUTH , and BLACK MIRROR . He co-hosts the podcast BLACK MEN CAN’T JUMP (IN HOLLYWOOD) and has written comics for Archie, Dynamite, and Papercutz including Disney’s ALADDIN , Disney’s STITCH , and POWERPUFF GIRLS .

people.name: James Jelin
people.bio: James is an LA-based actor, writer, and comedian. Catch him at Harold team Dance Club Remix, and follow him on TikTok and Instagram! @James_Jelin

people.name: James Kriz
people.bio: James Kriz is a comedian, actor, screenwriter, and author, trained at NYU Tisch, UCB, Second City, and the People’s Improv Theater. He’s proud to be a writer on Maude Night at UCB. He’s performed improv and written for/performed in sketch shows at Second City, the PIT, and the BCC. His comedic prose has been published by McSweeney’s and The Weekly Humorist, and his screenplays have been optioned to a number of companies, including Cassian Elwes’ Elevated and former Disney executive Steve Sahlein’s Empire Media Partners.

people.name: James Mannion
people.bio: James is an improviser and actor who has been performing at UCB Theatre since 2016. He currently performs with the team Leroy and as part of the ASSSSCAT cast. He has appeared on various comedy podcasts including improv4humans and Comedy Bang! Bang!

people.name: James Mastraieni
people.bio: James Mastraieni has been performing live comedy for two decades at UCB, Second City, iOWest. A freelance writer’s freelance writer, he’s written for Florida Girls, Comedy Central, funnyordie and a bunch of internet stuff. He also has 20,000 hours of teaching and directing comedy and is the founder and director of The Shared Experience Studio.

people.name: James Pope
people.bio: James Pope is an actor (SAG | AEA), improviser, writer, director, & comedian whose content has been featured on Funny or Die, The Today Show, Good Morning America, Buzzfeed, and The Second City Network. His credits include: The Revolution Will Be Improvised (The Second City, u/s), You Can’t Take It With You (Spotlighters Theatre), The Greatest Sketch Show in America (BIG), BIG Time (BIG), Baltimore’s Got Talent (BIG), Improvised Clue (BIG), Dark City: Beneath the Beat and dozens of super cool commercials selling windows and such. He is the co-creator and director of the comedic TV pilot, Grinding, a hip-hop sitcom.

people.name: James Rosenblath
people.bio: James Rosenblath is a senior at NYU Tisch, where he has studied at The Lee Strasberg Institute and the Royal Academy of Dramatic Arts in London. He does improv when people let him.

people.name: James Werner
people.bio: James is a writer, director, actor and live musician from Brentwood who most recently co-wrote the graphic novel sequel to the 1980s Blues Brothers film for Dan Aykroyd and Z2 comics. James performs all over Los Angeles and co-hosts a third-best improvised podcast: Crazy Story with Laney Serface and Harold Night performers Anna Bezahler and Isabella Escalante. He is also a very good son (according to his mom).

people.name: Jamie Denbo
people.bio: JAMIE DENBO currently serves as CO-EP on ABC’s smash hit, Grey’s Anatomy. She was a CO-EP on ABC’s Rebel in 2021 and was the creator, EP and showrunner of 1 season wonder, AMERICAN PRINCESS (2019, still available on Amazon!), which was based on her true-life experiences working on the Renaissance Festival circuit. Jamie has written and developed pilots for Showtime, IFC, FX, Comedy Central, FreeForm and CBS. She has also written feature scripts for Universal, Disney and Vendome Pictures. As an actor, Jamie has over a 100 on-camera and voice credits. For twelve years, Jamie was one half of the comedy act and hit podcast, Ronna & Beverly. Jamie trained, performed and taught for many years at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater. As a live performer, Jamie still appears in regular improv shows, including PAY THE SITTER with Rachel Bloom and Dan Gregor and SUPERMARRIED with her husband, John Ross Bowie. She also occasionally performs as her 70-something alter ego, Beverly.

people.name: Jamie Gaul Cullen
people.bio: Jamie is a writer based in Los Angeles. She’s a Southern California native which means she’s always cold even when the weather is nice. Catch her at UCB performing with Cardinal Redbird.

people.name: Jamie Linn Watson
people.bio: Jamie has been performing all over the city with groups like LISA, A Crazy Amazing Friendship, The Story Pirates, and now with UCB’s Betty Team, Wig! You may have seen her on FX’s What We Do in The Shadows and in the new movie, Please Don’t Destroy: The Treasure of Foggy Mountain. Follow her on ig and tiktok: @jamie.linn.watson

people.name: Jamie Meeks
people.bio: Jamie Meeks is a comedian and writer who has spent time in Chicago, Los Angeles, and Austin. With credits ranging from small basement theaters you’ve never heard of to world-renowned theaters, Jamie has a diverse range of experience and background that she brings to every scene she’s in.

people.name: Janette Angelini
people.bio: Janette (Nette) is a Chicago native now living in Los Angeles. After receiving her BFA in Acting and a Minor in musical theater, she moved back to Chicago to perform, write sketch comedy, and do improv at Second City Chicago. Since relocating to Los Angeles, Nette has become 1/2 of the sketch comedy duo, Nette & Nellie.

people.name: Jani Chakravarti
people.bio: Jani is a comedy performer, writer, and improviser based in Brooklyn. After training and performing at the late Annoyance Theater in Brooklyn, you can now see her monthly at UCB on Maude Night with her current team, FREAK JONES. She previously performed on Maude Night with Dumptruck and is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Janie Stolar
people.bio: Janie Stolar is an LA-based comedy writer/director/performer whose credits include “Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen,” “Billy on the Street,” SMOSH and CollegeHumor. At UCB in New York, Janie performed on Harold Night and co-created The Female Gaze, a long-running daytime talk show parody which she’s now producing/hosting in LA!

people.name: Jared Bronen
people.bio: Jared is a Brooklyn-based, New Haven-raised comic and filmmaker currently writing for Ponyboy on Maude Night. Since 2019, he has co-hosted The Trash King at Brooklyn Comedy Collective, a tournament of bits that was recently featured in the New York Comedy Festival. He recently wrote and directed the short film “Pick a Random Number”, which has won a handful of awards at small indie film festivals around the country. Before that, he wrote for several indie sketch groups, the PIT team Like Butter, the Boogiemanja team Cherry, Cherry, and for CollegeHumor in 2012-2013, where he had a recurring role in the Jake & Amir web-series as Mike Fink.

people.name: Jared Eimicke
people.bio: Jared Eimicke is a writer/improvisor from New York. He wrote for Above Average and The Kicker, and was on Maude Night for four seasons. His sketch shows, Escape from Cartoon World, Shkreli! Portrait of a Pharma Bro, Hockey Cops, and My Dad Dumped Jesus, all ran for several months at UCBNY. His sketch team, Dollar Pizza, created the web series Poolers for Comedy Central’s Snapchat Discover channel.

people.name: Jared Gerard
people.bio: Jared Gerard is an improviser, writer, and comedian from Phoenix, Arizona. He studied Comedy Writing and Performance at Columbia College Chicago and Second City. He is on Lloyd Night, and he is nice.

people.name: Jared Lomeli
people.bio: Jared Lomeli is a writer and performer from Chino Hills California. He has performed improv and sketch all around Orange County and LA County. He has been trained at the Pack Theater, UCB LA, UCB NY, and The Squirrel Theater. You can see him on the veteran Pack sketch team Friendo, and Maude Night as a writer.

people.name: Jared Palmer Kirsch
people.bio: Jared Palmer Kirsch is an actor and comedian based in New York. You can find him performing all over in theater, film, TV, and live comedy with his group ¿QUE?. You can also catch him as Brett in HULU’s Not Suitable For Work. See what’s up and say hi @jkirsch98 on Instagram!

people.name: Jasmine Don
people.bio: Jasmine Don is a writer, producer, and performer born and raised in L.A. She is a writer for the sketch team Moon Goon and worked with Critical Role as a writer for The Legend of Vox Machina (Amazon Prime Video) and as Fiedra Marrow in the D&D live play series EXU: Divergence . She can be found performing comedy at UCB and System of a Down covers in her car.

people.name: Jasmine Kojouri
people.bio: Jasmine is a Sing Out, Louise performer you’ve seen around town performing various improvised and scripted (often musical) comedy. Formerly a producer and actor with Drunk Lady Productions, Jasmine also has performed with Downtown Menagerie doing Tennessee Williams improv, Kissmet’s Bachelor -themed improv show, and Pack Theater house sketch teams Darling and Room Service. Jasmine is currently seeking representation!

people.name: Jasmine Wattar
people.bio: Jasmine Wattar is a Syrian American writer/actor from a small farm town, where she grew up with a pet turkey, SweetPete. Spoiler: he wasn’t sweet and often attacked her. As a comedy writer, Jasmine was selected for the WARNER BROS. DISCOVERY ACCESS WRITERS PROGRAM ’25 (comedy fellow), studied TV writing under the mentorship of acclaimed writer/director BEN FALCONE (Thunder Force), and co-wrote/produced films like THE THINGS THAT HAUNT US (Amazon Prime). As an actor, Jasmine studied advanced scene work with JASON ALEXANDER (Seinfeld), and is known for FOIL (Apple TV), NELK BOYS, and David Spade’s FAMELESS (TruTV). A passionate advocate for diversity/disability representation, Jasmine received the SNL AWARD, NBCUniversal WRITING SCHOLARSHIP, participated in the WARNER BROS. DISCOVERY ACCELERATION LAB with MENA ARTS ADVOCACY, and her feature script Luchador was recognized as a semi-finalist for INEVITABLE FOUNDATION’S ACCELERATE FELLOWSHIP. As a queer, mixed-race gal with Legg-Calvé-Perthes Disease, her work explores resilience, reflecting the beauty, messiness, and hope in each of us as we navigate life and its obstacles.

people.name: Jason DeRosse
people.bio: Canadian (sorry) alumni of the Second City Resident Company and The Comedy Bar’s flagship show, Sunday Night live. He’s a 4 time Canadian Comedy Award Winner, was listed as #1 Artist to Watch and received Comedy Show of The Year from NOW Magazine. Catch him in UCB’s 7 Minutes In Heaven!

people.name: Jason Siegel
people.bio: Jason is a comedian, writer, and producer living in Brooklyn, NY. He has humor writing published in The New Yorker and Points in Case. He was also the winner of season 2 of Abolish Everything! on Nebula.

people.name: Jason Spiro
people.bio: Jason Spiro is an actor, musician, and photographer, with an abiding interest in long form improvisation.

people.name: Jason Sweeten
people.bio: Jason is a Brooklyn-based comedian who currently performs on the character team Wig! and teaches sketch at The UCB Training Center. He wrote on Maude Night for four years, and his work was featured on Queerty, LGBTQ Nation, and Vulture Comedy, which described his work as, “a perfect mix of the simple and the absurdly complex.” He also penned the mildly infamous “Do You Still Have A Job At BuzzFeed?” and was a UCP Pitchfest finalist.

people.name: Jazz Lewis
people.bio: Jazz Lewis is an LA-based improv comedian and stand-up. Her comedy is best described as being LOUD. Jazz has performed in the Burbank Comedy Festival, Wasatch Improv Festival, the Red Rock Improv Festival and won Audience Favorite at the 2024 Black Women in Comedy Festival. Jazz also owns Crowdsourced Comedy, Utah’s only black woman & queer owned comedy theatre, known for running the longest all women comedy show in the city’s history. You can see Jazz performing monthly at UCBLA.

people.name: Jean Villepique
people.bio: Jean Villepique has performed at The Second City, Chicago, at the iO Chicago and LA with Quartet…and at UCBLA in Soundtrack. Credits include AP Bio, Party Down, Better Call Saul, Sharp Objects, BoJack Horseman, The Office, 30 Rock and I Love You, Man.

people.name: Jeanell Allen
people.bio: Jeanell Allen is a New York City-based actress, writer, filmmaker and producer. She reigns from the chaotic Sunshine State a.k.a. Florida. In her work with her production company Dramatic Millennial, she explores the themes of Black femininity, mental health, and out-of-this-world scenarios while centering the Black experience. She creates highly-developed worlds yet to be seen on stage and screen, and loves to make the audience laugh and cry at the same time.

people.name: Jeff Hiller
people.bio: Jeff Hiller has been performing at the UCB Theatre since 2001. TV: “Somebody Somewhere” (HBO), “American Horror Story: NYC”, “American Horror Stories”, “The Watcher”, “Psych”, “Playing House”, “Broad City”, “30Rock”, “Community”, “Crazy Ex-Girlfriend” and others. Jeff is also a stand-up and has performed on Broadway, off-Broadway, and Shakespeare in the Park.

people.name: Jeff Topolski
people.bio: Jeff Topolski is a writer and director from Fort Collins, Colorado. At UCB, Jeff wrote on the Maude team The New Deal for three years and has directed for Maude teams Hail Mary and Loveseat. He also wrote a half-hour sketch show entitled El Pollo Loco High School, a show which was memorable mainly because of an apple exploding after being thrown at the wall (but there were good characters and funny jokes too). Jeff also teaches Pilot Writing at UCB. Outside of UCB, Jeff wrote on the NBC comedy Brooklyn Nine-Nine.

people.name: Jeff Whitaker
people.bio: Jeff Whitaker is an improviser and sketch comedian living in Los Angeles by way of Austin. Jeff’s sketch comedy has been featured in San Francisco Sketch Fest, Chicago Sketch Fest, Moontower Comedy Festival and received a writeup in Vulture. In 2018, his sketch “Nice Ass” premiered at SXSW and was a Vimeo Staff pick. Currently, Jeff produces and performs in the monthly show Fuck This Month featuring some of LA’s best improvisers.

people.name: Jeff Wucher
people.bio: Jeff is a writer and performer originally from Pittsburgh, now living in Brooklyn. He currently performs on Harold Night.

people.name: Jeffrey Elizabeth Copeland
people.bio: Jeffrey Elizabeth Copeland is a writer, comedian, and tiny dog owner. Her writing has premiered at the American Black Film Festival, The Brooklyn Comedy Collective, UCB, The Big Apple Film Festival, and The Baltimore Improv Group. She was selected for the inaugural Saturday Night Live/People’s Improv Theater scholarship, and she regularly performs standup at the Harlem Comedy Club when it isn’t raining. Check her out @Jazzi_Jeff for see cute pics of her dog, and medicore photos of her.

people.name: Jeffrey Kitt
people.bio: Jeffrey is a writer and actor from The Bronx New York. You can catch him doing improv and sketch with his indie teams Branded Silk and Young Douglas. He enjoys doing impressions, creating digital shorts, and podcasting.

people.name: Jeffrey Mondoro
people.bio: Jeff Mondoro is a writer, improvisor, director and actor living in Brooklyn, NY. He has written and produced several award-winning short films, sketches, pilots and comedic plays. He lives with his wife, their dog and brand new baby.

people.name: Jeffrey Murdoch
people.bio: Born in Detroit and trained in Chicago, Jeff Murdoch is now an actor/comedian/writer currently living in LA. He has worked on such shows as The Bear, Shameless, and Utopia and is an alumni of the world-famous Second City Mainstage in Chicago as well as the Just For Laughs festival in Montreal. A comedian at heart, Jeff can be seen around LA working on shows and mics in front of audiences regularly and on his weekly podcast Mystery County Monster Hunters Club.

people.name: Jemima Lauriston
people.bio: Hey, I’m Jemima! A Haitian American comedian from Boston, Massachusetts. I’ve been studying and performing at UCB for 7 years. In that time, I’ve written sketches for Disney, been a guest performer on A Little Late with Lilly Singh, and played a lead role in an award nominated mockumentary .

people.name: Jen Albanese
people.bio: Jen Albanese is a comedy writer, performer, and voice actor based in NY. She currently writes on UCB Maude night with her team Dumptruck. If you need the perspective of a sweet dumb Italiano from Staten Island, Jen would love to meet you and write/perform for your show.

people.name: Jen Littlewood
people.bio: I have been improvising for 15 years performing in Chicago, New York, Toronto, and I now teach and perform regularly at The Free Association in London. I am one of the organisers of Big Scene Improv Festival which had its inaugural edition last month in London.

people.name: Jen McCartney
people.bio: Jen McCartney is an LA-based writer, originally from Dallas, Texas. She was most recently a co-producer on Solar Opposites, an animated show for Hulu, where she has written for the past four seasons. Previously, she wrote for The Big Show Show, a Netflix family multi-cam about a retired wrestler and his family. She has also written and performed on house sketch teams at the Upright Citizens Brigade and iO West. Her sketch comedy videos have gotten millions of views and been featured on Buzzfeed, Upworthy, WhoHaHa and more.

people.name: Jen Stoughton
people.bio: Jen Stoughton is a writer and comedian based in Los Angeles. They can currently be seen on the UCB stage at Lloyd Night. They just started taking a woodworking class, ladies. Ladies, did you hear that? Jen does woodworking now. Ladies?

people.name: Jenae Galang
people.bio: Jenae Galang is a Filipina-American actor and improvisor from the Bay Area, currently getting hyphy in LA. Born to goof, forced to grind so she can spoil her cute pitbull Onyx with endless treats. When she’s not saying “hella,” she’s speaking in onomatopoeias #skrtskrt

people.name: Jenna Barron
people.bio: Jenna has been performing on teams at UCB since 2015 and is very excited to be back on Harold Night with Tiptoe. She currently resides in NJ with her husband and 3 kids so if she seems tired, she is.

people.name: Jenna Bryson
people.bio: Jenna Bryson is a creative polymath who couldn’t pick a lane if she tried, and so now she has a background in dance, digital content, improv, music, producing, sketch comedy, and voiceover. She co-created the popular web-series “According to Kids”, wrote & published her first children’s book “Grace from Outer Space”, and wrote & recorded several songs that have been featured in some movies and TV shows. Also, she writes pilots that she really wants to see on her TV (and that sometimes place in contests), makes super-niche TikTok videos, and cooks a drool-inducing Filipino Chicken Adobo.

people.name: Jennifer Bartels
people.bio: Jennifer Bartels is an actor, writer, and comedian known for her work on the sketch series Friends of the People , where she served as writer, performer, and executive producer. She starred as Diana in Paramount Network’s American Woman and was featured in acclaimed series including Better Things and Shrill . A proud alum of the Upright Citizens Brigade in New York, Jennifer was named one of the “New Faces of Comedy” at the Just For Laughs Festival and has appeared in numerous national commercials and studio films including Men in Black 3 .

people.name: Jennifer Bologna
people.bio: Jennifer Bologna is a comedian originally from San Diego with a silly (but real) last name. She has been improvising for over ten years and is passionate about every style of improv from slow and grounded to quick and crazy. Jenn also has a heart full of love, a curious mind, and a twinkle in her eye. She hopes to melt your troubles away with laughter.

people.name: Jenny Arimoto
people.bio: Jenny is a Brooklyn-based writer and performer whose work has been featured in The New Yorker, Reductress, and Vulture. Before the pandemic, she was a writer and performer on Lloyd and Maude teams, and regularly performed with Asian AF. In 2022, she was chosen by New York Comedy Festival as a Content Creator to Watch. She is the co-host of the podcast Asian Not Asian, where she discusses everything from her Asian American identity to her emotional spirals.

people.name: Jenny Lorenzo
people.bio: Jenny Lorenzo is an award-winning content creator and actor known for co-founding BuzzFeed’s Pero Like. Her comedic and relatable content as a 1st generation Cuban-American has gained millions of online views, particularly for her Cuban Abuela character. She has received awards such as the Tecla Award for Best Fictional Character and Creator of the Year, and the NALIP Trailblazer award. Additionally, she has voiced multiple characters in popular shows like Disney’s Hamster & Gretel and HBOMax’s Jellystone, as well as hosting the Hyphenated podcast and the Disney+ show What’s Up, Disney+.

people.name: Jenny Matthews
people.bio: Jenny Matthews (she/they) is a Brooklyn based actor, writer, and comedian. She produces the monthly show “Heated Rivalry but GAYER” at UCB where two teams compete to be the gayest improv team in all the land. On social media, she has gone viral for her sketches and tricking the internet with satire.

people.name: Jenny Shaw
people.bio: Jenny Shaw is an actor, improviser, and AMC A-List member. Currently, Jenny is in her hot festival era, performing with her musical improv team, Ephemera, at comedy festivals across the country (recent credits include: iO Fest, Out of Bounds Comedy Festival, Last Word Comedy Festival, etc.). You can also catch her every Thursday at Musical Megawatt at the Magnet Theater.

people.name: Jenny Silber
people.bio: Jenny is a writer and comedian living in Brooklyn, NY. She currently writes for the UCB Maude team Fridge Magnet and has written for publications like Time Out New York and Business Insider. She also writes card games you’ve probably been forced to play at bachelorette parties or someone else’s kid’s birthday party for companies like Relatable (previously What Do You Meme?) and Exploding Kittens. When not writing behind the scenes, you can see Jenny on stage improvising with her team Lewis Improv at Brooklyn Comedy Collective or doing stand-up at venues across the city.

people.name: Jenny St. Angelo
people.bio: Jenny started performing improv in 2012 and never stopped. She also writes, has been in many national commercials, birthed and is currently raising two children, and resides in Brooklyn. www.jennysaint.com

people.name: Jenson Titus
people.bio: Jenson Titus is an all around clown. After studying at The Pig Iron School in Philly, he went on to develop new works with The Pig Iron Company and Lightning Rod Special (creators of Underground Railroad Game). Jenson has trained and worked with the likes of Geoff Sobelle, The Russo Brothers, Josephine Decker, Trixie Mattel, and many more spanning the realms of experimental theater, television, and film. The sole focus of his work is joy through the lens of queer absurdity and the occasional horror. And most importantly, he’d like to thank God for making him gay.

people.name: Jerah Milligan
people.bio: Jerah is a DC born multi-hyphenate creative artist. He co-created and starred in Netflix’s “Astronomy Club,” which was executive produced by Kenya Barris and Dan Powell, and co-wrote, executive produced, and starred in an original presentation of “Sherlock Homies” for HBO Max.

people.name: Jerami Monreal
people.bio: Co-host of UCB’s monthly stand-up showcase “Mind Melt.” I’m from Albuquerque, and love a good breakfast burrito.

people.name: Jeremy Culhane
people.bio: Jeremy Culhane is an actor/writer/improviser/performer from Pasadena, California. His credits include American Vandal, Heathers, 9-1-1, Yes Day, and Jimmy Kimmel Live!. He performs weekly with UCB’s Harold team GHOST and sketch team MOON GOON. Check out some of his videos on TikTok (@jazzyjelly: 130,000 followers), or listen to his Sugar23 podcast Artists on Artists on Artists on Artists.

people.name: Jeremy Guskin
people.bio: You may know Jeremy Guskin from television. Or a few films. Or content on the internet. Or, perhaps, from his teaching. All of these are very easy to find nowadays.

people.name: Jeremy Klein
people.bio: Jeremy Klein is beyond honored to join Sing Out Louise performing musical improv with his castmates! You might have seen Jeremy on America’s Got Talent or John Krasinski’s Some Good News with his brother Kyle performing as ThoseKleinBoyz! Their work has also been mentioned in The LA Times ! When Jeremy’s not on stage, you can find him reading America’s sweetheart, Stephen King! Send him book recommendations, he’d love them (this is not a bit)!

people.name: Jeremy Moulton
people.bio: Jeremy Moulton is an actor and writer based out of NYC. He was formally a member of the pandemic disrupted UCB Maude team Yes, Chef!. He has written, directed, and performed in way too many sketch and improvised shows across the US and Canada. His latest short film screenplay The Father, The Son, And The Horny Spirit has been selected as a finalist, semifinalist, and quarterfinalist by several different film festivals. You can find his viral web videos and beefs with Pixar and LeVar Burton on his TikTok.

people.name: Jeremy Olson
people.bio: Jeremy is above all just a lovely guy. He is also an improvisor, actor, musician and podcaster. He’s really doing it all. He even hosts a dang comedy show out of his garage in his backyard. You gotta respect that.

people.name: Jeremy Schmidt
people.bio: Jeremy Schmidt is a Los Angeles based writer/director by way of Dallas, TX. As an improviser, he has performed with the Mess Hall teams Powerline (2018) and Payday (2019). As a Maude writer, he has written for The New Deal (2018), Hail Mary (2019), and Loveseat (2023-2024).

people.name: Jeremy Sender
people.bio: Jeremy Sender is a Los Angeles-based comedy writer and filmmaker who grew up in New Jersey like everybody else. He’s improvised on house teams at theaters like iO Chicago, The Annoyance, and UCB… and it really is just so much fun, you guys. You can check out small structural script changes to movies he’s worked on including The Mitchells vs. the Machines and Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse , or tune into the Sony Animation/Netflix series he’s currently working on that will be out one day in the laughably distant future.

people.name: Jess Elgene
people.bio: Jess Elgene is a stand up, singer-songwriter, and improviser who was named a Creator to Watch by the New York Comedy Festival. She can currently be seen in Second City’s Improv Brunch and was previously a member of the mainstage company at Asylum NYC where she performed alongside the likes of Collin Mochrie, Roy Wood Jr., and more. Jess has performed in the New York Comedy Festival and the Fun and Dumb Improv Festival. Originally from the lone star state, she hosts an annual fundraiser show, Texas Pride: Everything’s Gayer in Texas, benefitting the Transgender Education Network of Texas. Her solo show, Less of a Man, has played to sold-out audiences across the country.

people.name: Jess Fee
people.bio: Jess Fee is a writer, director, and performer in Los Angeles. She is originally from the Bay Area, and her earliest memory is being run over by a sheep.

people.name: Jess Morgan
people.bio: Jess is a Brooklyn-based improviser who used to do the splits on stage, but then had a kid and now that sort of “physical comedy” is not medically advisable. Jess cannot believe you are still reading and is very thankful. IDK book her? jessmorg.com

people.name: Jess Moukios
people.bio: Jess Moukios is an improviser, writer, and actor based in Brooklyn. You can catch her every Harold Night performing with Hot FM. She previously performed with the Lloyd Night team The Board. Her indie team Go Dummy won the 2025 UCB Cagematch Championship and they perform monthly at the Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Her improv teams have headlined the Fun & Dumb Improv Festival and been featured in the New York Comedy Festival, Third Coast Comedy Festival, and SteelStacks Improv Festival.

people.name: Jess Svendsgaard
people.bio: Jess is a writer, actor, and stand-up comedian from Northern California who writes for UCB Maude team TOWN CRIER. Jess is the creator and executive producer of M.I.L.F (Mach Improv L.A. Festival ) and showrunner of it’s impetus, long running indie show MACH IMPROV. They’ve headlined Improv Fest Ireland with their musical improv team ADELE DAZEEM, and performed at UCB and festivals across the country with improv team WILD.

people.name: Jesse Esparza
people.bio: Jesse is a writer and performer from Sacramento whose TV credits include Mixedish on ABC and Maggie on Hulu. Jesse has performed improv and sketch comedy at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater since 2009, and was on the Maude team Bomardier and the Mess Hall team Bad Seed. He wrote and directed “Tonya Harding The Musical” which ran for 2 years to sold out audiences. In addition to UCB, he wrote for the CBS Diversity Showcase, and was accepted to the NHMC Writers Program and the Disney Writers Program.

people.name: Jesse VandenBergh
people.bio: Jesse is a writer, actor and filmmaker based in New York City. He has written for AwesomenessTV, CBS, The Dodo, Thrillist and Wondery and is the cofounder of Frog Boyz Animation. Jesse wrote, directed, produced and starred in the award-winning animated short film “Buildings.” He is currently a writer on Maude Night.

people.name: Jessica Damouni
people.bio: Jessica Damouni is an Aussie-American actress, comedian and filmmaker based in LA. She co-hosts Strawberry Jam at UCB with her team Ladies Night Improv. TV Credits include: God Friended Me (CBS), Crashing (HBO), and Platonic (Apple TV+). Film credits: Inspector Ike , An Arrangement (SBIFF), Salam (Tribeca), Choke (Outfest), and Virgin (Cinequest). She has a background in Marathon running and Martial Arts. When she’s not performing she can be found skateboarding or teaching herself how to play bass!

people.name: Jessica Eason
people.bio: Jessica Elaina Eason is an actor and writer, born in Bogota, Colombia and raised in West Newbury, Massachusetts. It’s a journey. You may recognize her from The Baby-Sitters Club, Who Killed Santa? A Murderville Murder Mystery, The Goldbergs or a hilarious commercial that interrupts your game. Jessica writes on the reboot of NBC’s Night Court with her fabulous writing partner Rebecca Delgado-Smith. She performed at The UCB Theatre in New York for years, and currently lives in Los Angeles, where she teaches and performs at UCB LA. Jessica is married to fellow UCB alum James Eason, and they are parents of two of the most wicked funny and sweetest boys on planet Earth.

people.name: Jessica Haymond
people.bio: Jessica is a Story Editor on the upcoming Kakao animated show, THE RYAN SHOW, and previously wrote for Adult Swim’s BIRDGIRL. She created the MTVother show FAST FOOD HEIGHTS, starring Nicole Byer, which ran for two seasons. She also directed the UCB Comedy series MOTHER MARY starring Drew Tarver and Betsy Sodaro (currently in development with Pop). For over ten years Jessica has written and performed several shows at UCBLA, including her recurring show REDUCED! with her writing partner Ele Woods. She has been in various national commercials, including spots for Google and Dunkin’ Donuts. In addition to writing, Jessica is a licensed attorney in California. She once wrote a joke Beyoncé reposted.

people.name: Jessica Jean Jardine
people.bio: Jessica is from Los Gatos, California and has had the great pleasure of performing at UCB since 2011. She was on the Maude team The Get Go as well as the Harold teams Mister Town City and The Dragons.

people.name: Jessica Keung
people.bio: Hi, I’m Jessica Keung! You might remember me from such Chicago comedy show as KEEPIN’ TABS, Stir Friday Night’s hAAPI hour, and Second City’s Victor Wong Fellowship: GLOSSED IN TRANSLATION. When I am not writing and performing comedy, I am at home on my phone.

people.name: Jessica McKenna
people.bio: Jessica McKenna is an actor, writer and improvisor based in Los Angeles. Credits include the Fox sketch show Party Over Here, produced by The Lonely Island, Jalen Vs. Everybody, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Sunnyside, Modern Family, The Goldbergs, and Drunk History. Along with her writing partner, Zach Reino, Jess writes musical comedy for film and television. Their writing has been featured in Baking It, Bumper in Berlin, and Rick and Morty. They host “Off Book: The Improvised Musical Podcast” and star in the Dropout musical series, Play it By Ear.

people.name: Jessica Ross
people.bio: Jessica Ross is a LGBTQ+ writer/performer living in Los Angeles. You can see her on the streaming service, Dropout.tv, in several series including Game Changer, Dimension 20: Tiny Heist, Um Actually, Dirty Laundry, Kingpin Katie, and Ultramechatron Team Go! Prior, she was a cast member at CollegeHumor and co-hosted the fanfic vodcast, Erotic Clubhouse. She’s done freelance work for Adult Swim, Amazon’s Wondery, NBC’s Red Nose Day, Riot Games, and Hunt A Killer. Jessica is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Jessica Uhler
people.bio: Jess Uhler is a LA based comedian, writer, and stop motion animator. They were born on a Wednesday and in an earlier stage of life wanted to get their commercial sailing license, but found improv along the way. They have studied and performed the craft at UCBLA and iO West, have written and performed in various short films and plays, and currently co-hosts The Milly Milly Show & the podcast Chompin’ with Sophie and Jess. Jess is very happy to be here and would love a Diet Coke.

people.name: Jessie Weinberg
people.bio: Jessie Weinberg hails from Washington, DC. She is a founding member of Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre Maude teams The Summer Of and The Get Go. Her writing and directing work have been featured on The Huffington Post, Jezebel, Upworthy, Funny or Die, The Hairpin, Bustle, and more. Her short films have been screened at The Ann Arbor Film Festival, The Florida Film Festival, LA Shorts Fest, HollyShorts, Women in the Director’s Chair, and Ladyfest.

people.name: Jetta Juriansz
people.bio: Jetta Juriansz is a winner of the Diversity Scholarship at The Groundlings, was a cast member in the CBS Showcase 2023, and most known for sketch comedy TV show, Studio C. Television: Station 19 (ABC), S.W.A.T. (CBS) and Kickin’ It (Disney). Theatre: Pre-Broadway premiere of The Karate Kid The Musical, The Mystery of Edwin Drood at The Goodspeed, Incomplete List in Prism Fest. Instagram: @shmetta

people.name: Jeung Bok Holmquist
people.bio: Jeung Bok (they/them) is a half-Japanese, half-gay performer, silly guy & artist from the Midwest known locally as “the mixed race Paul Giamatti.” They currently perform in Maude night at UCB.

people.name: Jiavani
people.bio: Jiavani (she/him) is a queer, Filipinx+Black musical improviser, freestyle rapper, drag king, sketch actor and more. Jiavani can be seen onscreen in Between Two Ferns: The Movie, Reno 911: Defunded, Dead to Me, and others. Voice acting credits include Digman!, We Baby Bears and many upcoming projects.

people.name: Jill Donnelly
people.bio: Jill Donnelly is an actress and improviser in the Chicago area. Her improv credits include 14 years at UCB NYC and LA where she was an improv instructor and a performer on Harold night, Maude night, the Teacher’s Lounge, and completed a seven year run as a rotating member of ASSSSCAT. Her theatre credits include the national tour of Crazy For You, and regional productions of Into the Woods (Papermill Theatre), 365 Days / 365 Plays (Public Theatre, NYC), Bush Wars (Rattlestick Theatre, NYC), and News in Revue (Coconut Grove Playhouse). Jill’s film and TV credits include Curb Your Enthusiasm (HBO), The Comeback (HBO), Arrested Development (HBO), and Hello Ladies (HBO). She has a B.A. in Theatre from Oberlin College, and a Masters in Health Policy from UCLA.

people.name: Jim Woods
people.bio: Jim started his improv, sketch and stand up comedy career in his hometown Raleigh, NC. He then moved to Chicago where he performed on house teams at iO. He then was a cast member at Boom! Chicago in Amsterdam and co-founded the Free Association Theater in London. Jim eventually settled in Los Angeles performing at iO West and was an original member of Last Day of School and The Smokes at UCBLA. You can see him as Jimmy Weber on Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat , as well as roles on numerous other TV shows such as The Office , How I Met Your Mother and Reno 911! and was a writer on The Last O.G.

people.name: Jimmy Fowlie
people.bio: JIMMY FOWLIE is an actor, writer, and comedian based in NYC. Jimmy is currently a writer on Saturday Night Live . Previously, he was a writer on season 3 of The Other Two, where he also plays the role of Cameron Colby – leader of the Instagays. Jimmy can be also seen in the latest season of Search Party, The Goldbergs, The Comeback, 2 Broke Girls , Teachers , and Good Girls . Jimmy’s list of theatre credits include his solo show “So Long, Boulder City” where he imagined, wrote, and performed what Mia Dolan’s one woman show from the movie La La Land would have been. Jimmy cut his teeth performing live sketch and improv shows at The Groundlings and UCB in LA. He is an alum of the Sunday Company at The Groundlings theater.

people.name: Jimmy Kehoe
people.bio: Jimmy was born on a steamy Summer morning in the hot swamps of Maryland’s Eastern Shore. Since then he has won the lottery 6 times and given all proceeds to his memorial checking account. Jimmy lives in Los Angeles with his dog and two cats.

people.name: Jimmy Marino
people.bio: Jimmy is a Southern California-based actor, musician, improvisor, and corgi dad. His original musical Into Light has been performed on stages around the country, and his music project (helpdesk) can be seen playing around Los Angeles. When he isn’t performing with Sing Out, Louise or sitting at home overthinking, he can often be found in either an escape room or the macaroni and cheese section of his local Ralphs.

people.name: Joan Ford
people.bio: Joan Ford is a comedian who has written on shows for Disney, HBO, Netflix, Cartoon Network, Quibi and a bunch of other places. You can currently see her on the UCB stage with her all tras/gnc/non-binary team QTs. You can also catch her performing with her best friend and sketch partner, Raft, in their two person sketch team; Joan and Raft.

people.name: Joanna Borns
people.bio: Joanna Borns is a New York-based writer and comedian. She was a writer at The Onion for a decade and has been performing improv (and musical improv) in NYC since 2009.

people.name: Jocelyn DeBoer
people.bio: Jocelyn DeBoer is an award-winning writer and director known for the dark comedy feature, Greener Grass, which Variety deemed, “The most pleasant surprise of this year’s Sundance” following its world premiere. Jocelyn co-wrote and co-directed alongside Dawn Luebbe and their screenplay was nominated for an Independent Spirit Award. IndieWire named Jocelyn and Dawn among the “25 Rising Female Directors To Know.”

people.name: Jocelyn Hillen Newham
people.bio: Jocelyn is an actor and improvisor from Pittsburgh, PA. She has spent a lot of time coaching and teaching at Arcade Comedy Theater. She is very excited to return to DCM with The Latchkey Kids!

people.name: Joe Corcoran
people.bio: Joe Corcoran is a writer and improviser originally from Queens, New York. Joe was a Staff Writer on Netflix’s The Dragon Prince for four seasons and is currently using the strike as a convenient excuse for why he’s not working. He performs with the WE Improv Residency team Tucci and the musical improv team, Dr. Sweatpants.

people.name: Joe Harrington
people.bio: JOE HARRINGTON is an NYC-based comedian/writer/actor who specializes in characters, sketch, and improv. He is currently a member of Betty at UCB, performing original characters each month. Additionally, he is a cast member of the weekly sketch show “Demon Time” at The Brooklyn comedy collective. He is also a part of the improv group “SLOP” which has a monthly show at UCB, and a part of the sketch duo “Disco Horses” which performs regularly across the city.

people.name: Joe Kady
people.bio: Joe Kady is an improvisor, a cartoonist, and a little kid. He performs on Lloyd Night. If you want to watch his animations, look up Mummy Joe.

people.name: Joe Leonardo
people.bio: Joe hosted the show, Flying Blind Sketch Show at UCB Hell’s Kitchen back in the day and was on the Lloyd teams, Southpaw, Seventeen, Havoc, and Dog People. He thinks there may have been one more but he’s getting old and his memory isn’t what it used to be.

people.name: Joe Saunders
people.bio: Joe Saunders is a comedy writer living in Los Angeles and a longtime writer and performer at the UCB Theater in LA. He has written for TV shows including Solar Opposites, Comedy Bang Bang, and several projects for Cartoon Network.

people.name: Joe Wengert
people.bio: Joe is the showrunner of the adult animated comedy KEVIN on Prime Video, which he co-created with Aubrey Plaza. Other writing credits include all eight seasons of BIG MOUTH , NEW GIRL and KROLL SHOW . Joe filmed a Comedy Central Half Hour stand up special in 2014 and was named a “New Face” at the 2012 Montreal Comedy Festival. Joe’s acting credits include MODERN FAMILY , KEY & PEELE , and HOW TO LIVE WITH YOUR PARENTS (FOR THE REST OF YOUR LIFE) , as well as voice over work for BIG MOUTH and HUMAN RESOURCES .

people.name: Joel Perez
people.bio: Joel Perez is an actor, writer, and director based in NYC. As an actor he has appeared on Broadway in Fun Home and stars as Valentino in the hit A24 animated series Hazbin Hotel for Prime Video. His play Stir premiered at the Old Globe Theater and his short films Villa Encanto and The Upper Room are currently playing at film festivals.

people.name: Joel Spence
people.bio: Joel has been performing long form improv and sketch comedy at UCBTLA since 2005. He’s a member of the house teams Sentimental Lady and Toledo Rep. He’s also appeared in ASSSSCAT, Soundtrack, America’s Best Comic, Quick and Funny Musicals, Dirtiest Sketch Contest and Cleanest Sketch (winner!). He had a recurring role as James Van Der Beek’s agent Donnie Jarvis on ABC’s Don’t Trust The B—- In Apt 23 and a recurring role as Detective Hodges on The Good Guys on FOX. He has also appeared on Key & Peele, Newsreaders, Modern Family, How I Met Your Mother, Big Love, Reno 911, My Name Is Earl, and Burn Notice. Most recently Joel has been seen on USA’s Pearson, as Hal Carpet on Lifetime’s American Princess and had recurring roles as Mr Chandler on the Paramount Channel’s Heathers, Matt on HBO’s Big Little Lies and Joel in ABC’s digital series, Boondoggle.

people.name: Joey Clift
people.bio: Joey Clift is a comedian, TV writer, and enrolled Cowlitz Indian Tribal Member. He went to college to be a local TV weather person, because as a kid he didn’t see any Native American comedians on television which led him to believe he wasn’t allowed to work in comedy. Over the years he’s learned that’s absolutely not true and he’s written for many shows and projects including Spirit Rangers on Netflix, New Looney Tunes on Cartoon Network and Molly of Denali on PBS. He also created, wrote and directed “Gone Native,” a Comedy Central digital series about weird racial microaggressions Native American people often deal with, and his award-winning short films have screened everywhere from Just For Laughs to The Smithsonian Museum. Most importantly, he started the LA Underground Cat Network, which is a 16,000-member strong Facebook group for Los Angeles comedians to share pictures of their cats. He’s kind of a cat guy.

people.name: Joey Lieberman
people.bio: Joey Lieberman is a Northwestern-trained improviser, comedian, and the founder of Obsessed With Us, an improv show that has become a staple of the New York comedy scene. He performs regularly at the Brooklyn Comedy Collective and Second City, bringing the same sharp instincts to the stage that have earned him two Emmy Awards in his day job as an Olympic feature producer. Turns out making the world laugh and making the world cry are not that different.

people.name: Joey Price
people.bio: Joey has worked as an actor, director, comedian and podcaster in San Diego, New York City and San Francisco. He was a longtime performer and improv teacher at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in New York and has taught improv workshops across the country. He is currently the producing artistic director at San Diego Junior Theatre. He’s also acted in a handful of commercials, which he says are “fine.”

people.name: Joey Welsh
people.bio: Joey Welsh is a devilishly handsome, New York-based comedian, actor, improviser, and writer. Joey formally began his theatrical training in college at Cornell University, but has since trained at American Conservatory Theater, Hangar Theater, Upright Citizen’s Brigade, and more. He has had the honor of performing on off-broadway stages (The Actors Studio), on television ( Furious, HULU), and most enjoyably… at the UCB (ASSSSKETCH, Night School, Maude Night)!

people.name: John Flynn
people.bio: John co-wrote Showgirls: The Best Movie Ever Made! Ever! and is the co-host of the podcasts Two Old Queens and The Celebration Celebration. His memoir Baked! was recently released by Pretty Great Publishing. In 2021 he won the LA Ovation Award for Best Book of a Musical for IT! The Musical Parody. He has won five Moth StorySLAMs and one GrandSLAM.

people.name: John Gemberling
people.bio: John Gemberling has been performing comedy (and light drama) for over 20 years. A veteran of the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, he came up through the NY improv scene of the early 2000s. When it was cool. In that time, he has delighted audiences on stage and screen with his bubbly, cherubic moron schtick. He co-created and starred in the show FAT GUY STUCK IN INTERNET for Adult Swim and has written and performed for Comedy Central’s THE PRESIDENT SHOW . John’s long list of acting credits includes BROAD CITY, THE AFTERPARTY, MARRY ME , MAKING HISTORY , BIG MOUTH, HUMAN RESOURCES, MIXED-ISH, THE GREAT NORTH , BOB’S BURGERS, MODERN FAMILY and many more! John is, more-importantly, a loving father and devoted husband…which is, like, really fulfilling for him. So don’t talk to him too long after shows, because he really does have to get home and go to bed.

people.name: John Hartman
people.bio: John Hartman is a former member of The Second City Mainstage in Chicago, where he wrote and performed in several revues, and won the Joseph Jefferson Award for Best Actor. He has also performed solo shows at the TBS Just for Laughs Festival, UCB Theater in New York and the Comedy Central Stage in Los Angeles, earning Time Out Critic’s Picks. Most recently he has appeared in recurring roles on Young Sheldon (CBS), The Good Place (NBC), Trial & Error (NBC), Scorpion (CBS), and he can currently be seen on the CBS comedy Ghosts.

people.name: John Higgins
people.bio: JOHN HIGGINS is one in the internet and cultural phenom comedy group, PLEASE DON’T DESTROY . They joined SNL three years ago as writer/performers and are the brain trust behind the show’s most viral sketches and digital shorts. Their first feature, THE TREASURE OF FOGGY MOUNTAIN, premiered in November ’23 with Judd Apatow producing for Universal. John co-wrote alongside Ben Marshall and Martin Herlihy, and they all starred and executive produced. In 2023, Please Don’t Destroy embarked on a 25-city live tour that had them selling out theaters and adding shows all across the country. New York Magazine/Vulture named them to Comedians You Should and Will Know in 2021 List , THR featured on the cover of their New York issue , and Variety named them to the New Power of New York List .

people.name: John Kennedy
people.bio: John is an improviser and writer from NYC who is very lucky to have a girlfriend he loves very much and very unlucky to be a lifelong New York Mets fan. He can be seen performing improv around the city with his indie team, Up to No God, and at UCB as a cast member of THE TRIAL: An Improvised Court Experience.

people.name: John Kennedy
people.bio: John is an improviser and writer from NYC who is very lucky to have a girlfriend he loves very much and very unlucky to be a lifelong New York Mets fan. He can be seen performing improv around the city with his indie team, Up to No God, and at UCB as a cast member of THE TRIAL: An Improvised Court Experience.

people.name: John Lutz
people.bio: John Lutz has been acting, improvising and writing for over 30 years. You may know him best as the character of Lutz from 30 Rock. He wrote for Saturday Night Live from 2004-2010 and is currently in his tenth year writing for Late Night with Seth Meyers. He created, co-wrote and produced the series Mapleworth Murders which can currently be seen on Roku and is featured in the 3rd season of Girls5Eva on Netflix. Check out his website www.john-lutz.com and follow him on Twitter @ShutupLutz or Instagram @shutup_lutz.

people.name: John McGuire
people.bio: John is an improviser and UCB grad from Austin, TX with over 10 years of improv experience. He currently co-produces shows with the group Hot Goss, currently hosted at ColdTowne Theatre.

people.name: John McInnes
people.bio: John McInnes has loved “The Harold” since 2014, he studied improv at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre and has learned a lot from Carleen MacDermid, Will Hines, Billy Merritt, Julie Brister… and many more. He was a member of every cool improv team in Bristol (UK) from 2016-2023 and is now a member of Couch, helping to run the Glasgow Improv Theatre while performing at the Del Close Marathon, Das Improv Fest, Amsterdam Improv Marathon, Reykjavík Fringe Festival, Hoopla Improv Marathon and the Robin Hood International Improv Festival.

people.name: John Milhiser
people.bio: John is a long time UCB performer and former cast member of SNL. He has appeared on tv shows, movies and many many Maude teams such as Bombardier. John is a founding member of everyone’s favorite sketch video group, SERIOUS LUNCH. John also created Baguette-Me-Nots: A photo series of comedians using a baguette as something else.

people.name: John Murray
people.bio: John has been performing and teaching at UCB for over a decade. He has appeared on such TV shows as 30 Rock, Broad City, Difficult People and The Daily Show. His work has been written about in New York Magazine, Esquire, Rolling Stone and The New Yorker.

people.name: John Racioppo
people.bio: John Racioppo (pronounced ra – chop – oh) is an actor and improvisor currently based in Brooklyn, NY. Originally from Toronto, Canada, he has spent much of his adult life in the States in pursuit of his creative passions, though he retains a deep love for Toronto sports, flannel shirts, and cold weather. He’s also the Senior Producer of Fault Line Theatre, a downtown company that creates and produces socially relevant, character-driven plays.

people.name: John Randall
people.bio: John Randall is a comedian, actor, and filmmaker based in Brooklyn.

people.name: John Ross Bowie
people.bio: JOHN ROSS BOWIE has over 100 credits in TV and film, including: “The Big Bang Theory,” (11 seasons as Barry Kripke) “Speechless,” (3 seasons as Jimmy Dimeo) “Fresh off the Boat,” “Episodes,” “Brooklyn 99,” “House of Lies,” “Heroes” at least two “CSIs,” Jumanji: The Next Level, He’s Just Not That Into You, the Heat, and the cult oddity What the Bleep do we Know? He has performed on stages from Texas to Alaska.
