In 2022, he published No Job For A Man, which Publishers Weekly called “smart, pithy memoir with an earnest emotional arc.” He has been performing at UCB since 1998, and can often be seen in SUPERMARRIED, the two person improv show with his wife, the polymath Jamie Denbo.

people.name: John Sartori
people.bio: John is a NYC-based writer, actor/voice actor, stand up, and guy. He’s been featured, both physically and vocally, on the Daily Show, the Disney Channel, as a voice actor/writer for Dodo Kids, and in productions for Universal Kids, MTV, College Humor, Pinna and more. His UCB Theatre credits include writing/performing on Maude Night, Characters Welcome and the running shows “That’s Bullshit!”, “Branded Content Will Set You Free”, “The Scumbags of ’77” and “Language Is A Virus.”

people.name: John Shepard
people.bio: John Shepard is a writer, actor and comedian who originally hails from central Illinois. He currently lives in Brooklyn with his cat/supreme overlord Kia

people.name: John Tan
people.bio: John Tan is a Philippines-born, Los Angeles-raised comedy writer. Currently a member of the Upright Citizens Brigade Maude team LOVESEAT, he previously wrote for the IO West (RIP) sketch team JUNE’S CLEAVER, the Acme Comedy sketch team THE BABES, and THE COLOR COLLECTIVE. He was part of the writer’s room for the 2018 CBS Diversity Sketch Showcase and a semi-finalist for the 2023 Disney Writing Fellowship. His professional goal is to one day have a subreddit dedicated to hating him. His non-professional goal is to own a dishwasher.

people.name: John Thompson
people.bio: John’s been paradiddling since he could hold sticks and has been a part of more bands than he can count. He’s excited to bring Three Hearts Astride to DCM and will not be doing long form improv.

people.name: John Torres Chapman
people.bio: John is an actor and writer originally from San Diego. In addition to writing on Maude night, you can catch him improvising all over town with his teams, or hosting the We Improv 2prov show with his good friend Jess. He also writes and preforms in online sketches all over the web.

people.name: John Trowbridge
people.bio: John is a comedian who currently writes for WWE’s Monday Night RAW on Netflix. Past jobs include: the reboot of All That on Nickelodeon, Night Late for NBC, Franchesca and Show for Comedy Central and Sketched, an animated pilot that he also starred in. John was named a New Face of Comedy at the 2017 Just For Laughs Festival. You can sometimes catch John getting yelled at by wrestlers every Monday on Netflix OR you can see John perform his solo show “THE GREATEST SOLO SHOW OF ALL TIME” at the UCBT NYC.

people.name: John X. Miller
people.bio: John Xavier Miller is a comedy titan; on Monday’s he can be seen doing musical improv in the show On The Spot, Tuesdays on Lloyd Night with The Board, sketch comedy with 15 Minutes Away and even teaching sketch right here at UCB. Previously teams/shows include After School Snack (Maude), poHa, We Will Slay, Block Party, The Spotlight, Queerball and Sketch Cram. With a BFA in Acting from Ithaca College, maybe there’s nothing John can’t do…on stage.

people.name: John-Michael Sedor
people.bio: John-Michael Sedor is a voice over artist, actor, and comedian from Philadelphia, PA. Hit him up for the best cheesesteak. He now live in Los Angeles, CA – City of Stars – although far from one, he does have the literal voice of an angel and some people would describe him as a miracle, although not his parents, they would say he was a mistake. As an actor/voice artist, he has worked with numerous clients including Disney, Walmart, Trek, and Epson just to name a few.

people.name: Johnathan Fernandez
people.bio: Johnathan is an Afro-Latino actor/filmmaker from Brooklyn/The Poconos. You’ve seen him on Gossip Girl, Lethal Weapon, Younger, Girls, Bull, other stuff, on stage, in your wildest dream(s), and standing behind you right now.

people.name: Johnathan Ross
people.bio: Johnathan is an actor, producer, editor, and writer in NYC. He’s performed in festivals all over including Austin, Seattle, Chicago, and Toronto. You can catch find him online with his digital troupe, Off Q Comedy (offqcomedy.com). He performs comedy regularly all over NYC where he can get stage time and restaurants that will tolerate him hosting trivia.

people.name: Johnny Fullmer
people.bio: Johnny is a regular performer at Imposters Theater in Cleveland, Ohio. Performing with “Sorry For Your Loss” as well as, Imposters house team “Ripley’s Believe It.” Thriving on threading the needle between the absurd and reality. Always up for a laugh. Buy the ticket, take the ride.

people.name: Johnny McNulty
people.bio: Johnny McNulty is a comedian and other things who lives in Brooklyn. In addition to improv, he is a former contributor to The Onion, Weekend Update, and executive editor of Someecards.com. A former Harold night performer from the Obama administration, Johnny is excited to return to the stage.

people.name: Johnny Meeks
people.bio: Johnny is an actor/ improviser/writer originally from St. Louis, MO. He performs regularly on the UCB house team Sentimental Lady. You may have seen Johnny on TV on such shows as Crazy Ex-Girlfriend, Brooklyn 99, Modern Family, Up All Night, NTSF:SD:SUV::, Comedy Bang Bang, and numerous commercials. Johnny co-wrote and starred in the film Alleyball, winner “Best Feature” of both the SoCal Independent Film Festival and the New York Independent Features Festival. Meeks has been performing improvisational and sketch comedy at UCBTLA since 2005. He is an improv teacher and serves as the Academic Director at the UCB Training Center. He has appeared in many shows at UCBTLA including Asssscat, Soundtrack, Brand New Ball, ROUGH CUT: The Improvised Movie and Sketch Cram.

people.name: Jolene Kennedy
people.bio: Jolene is a writer and comedian from Creston, Iowa. She’s written sketches for Comedy Central, Studio C, and her former Maude team The Audacity. She was recently a semi-finalist in the Disney Writing Program, and has developed with Kapital Entertainment and TrillTV.

people.name: Jon Bander
people.bio: Jon Bander is a Character and Sketch teacher at UCB, and a current/former member of Betty night/Characters Welcome. Jon has also performed at Montreal’s Just For Laughs Festival and worked for The Second City aboard Norwegian Cruise Lines. Otherwise he performs/teaches standup, musical improv, sketch and characters nationwide. TV credits: MAX’s Search Party, Netflix’s Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt, Master Of None and The Characters. Most people call him Bander – you can too!

people.name: Jon Bershad
people.bio: Jon is a writer, director, and animator who previously performed on Maude Night and Harold Night at the UCB in New York. He’s currently making his UCB LA debut on the Maude team The Faculty. He promises to write sketches that are just as funny as before, but which feature way less references to subway trains.

people.name: Jon Dimakopoulos
people.bio: Jon Dimakopoulos, or Dimo, is an Actor/Improviser from NYC. He’s been performing around the scene since 2011 and you can catch him performing Improv with his Improv team Wooodstock, acting with his Sketch team Both Hands!, or on Thursday nights doing something weird at Cage Match!

people.name: Jon Durnell
people.bio: Jon grew up in the mean streets of a suburban community in Nashville, TN. He ignored the pressures of a society encouraging a life in dentistry, law enforcement or accounting and decided to bring laughter to the masses. As a comic, he has performed in colleges, coffeehouses, dive bars, elegant country clubs, theaters, comedy clubs, backyards, abandoned/condemned buildings and skateboard shops across the USA.

people.name: Jon Gabrus
people.bio: Gabrus is a comedian/actor/writer/podcaster/lifeguard from Long Island.

people.name: Jon Hudson
people.bio: Jon is a sketch comedian who was birthed in the droll wittiness of the UK, forged by the clownish irreverence of Montreal, and trained in the gauntlet of 2010s UCB. Trained in Meisner technique (Neighborhood Playhouse) and competitive foil fencing (D-2019 rating). Acting credits include Sketchfests across North America, being a niche YouTube microcelebrity as the Stat Geek (soccer) and Buffalo Phil (football), and a Cingular commercial with Rob Huebel when he was 11. Catch him on Maude night with DumpTruck!

people.name: Jon Lu
people.bio: Jon Lu is a comedic writer, actor, and modest dungeon master. He has performed improv and sketch comedy at UCB, PIT, and BCC since 2016. He has recently successfully grown tomatoes on his rooftop.

people.name: Jon Mackey
people.bio: Jon is a writer/director/improviser/podcaster, from long running improv/sketch group Big Grande, and veteran house team, Winslow. You can hear him on the hit podcast The Teacher’s Lounge, and DePodcast with Brett DeMott and you may have seen a commercial or two that he’s directed during a Dodger game or on your Instagram feed.

people.name: Jon Millstein
people.bio: Jon Millstein is a comedy writer who’s written and created digital stuff for companies like Funny Or Die, Crooked Media, Comedy Central, Clickhole, and more. Currently he writes for a new show from Disney Television Animation. At UCB, he used to host Sketch Showdown and was a longtime member of Maude Night. And he accomplished all this just by saying “yes” to the universe.

people.name: Jon Monje
people.bio: Jon has been studying and performing comedy in New York City since 2008. He’s appeared on Late Night with Seth Meyers, ads on television, billboards, and the internet, and once randomly on Jeopardy! He’s a regular performer on Cagematch!

people.name: Jonathan Anderson
people.bio: Jonathan Anderson is an improviser, writer, actor, and standup comedian from Boston who now performs all over New York City. He is currently a member of The People’s Sketch Revue, PIT ensembles, and several indie improv shows including The Honeymoon Stage and Psyched Out. He has performed at comedy clubs and theaters across the East Coast and has been featured at comedy festivals including the Boom Chicago Comedy Festival, the Edinburgh International Improv Festival, Boston Fringe, and more!

people.name: Jonathan Braylock
people.bio: Jonathan Braylock is a veteran and current performer at UCB. He has written for several television shows including Ted Animated (Peacock), Grownish (Freeform), Golden Axe (Comedy Central), The G Word with Adam Conover (Netflix), and Astronomy Club: The Sketch Show (Netflix) of which he also co-created and co-starred in. His acting credits include Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt , Broad City , NCIS: New Orleans , The Amazing Spider-Man 2 , and the New Line Cinema feature How to Be Single . Jon also co-hosts the popular podcast BLACK MEN CAN’T JUMP IN HOLLYWOOD, celebrated by Splitsider, Rotten Tomatoes (“one of the best comedy podcasts”) and the BBC.

people.name: Jonathan Lee-Rey
people.bio: Jonathan Lee-Rey is an actor, writer, and filmmaker from Yonkers, NY with a splash of the Bronx. Currently, he is based in Chicago where he is the Executive Artistic Director of Stir Friday Night, a teacher at iO theater, a cast member of Escándalo.

people.name: Jonathan van Halem
people.bio: Hi, my name Jonathan van Halem. I’m a stand-up comedian based in Brooklyn, NY. I used to work at Stephen Colbert Presents Tooning Out The News, and currently work at The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. I host a great stand up show at Littlefield/Union Hall called Hot Gossip. I’ve performed on some of New York’s best showcases, like 50 First Jokes and Newcomers at the Bell House, and One Liner Madness. In 2022-2023, I contributed to the parody website Clickhole. When I’m not doing comedy, I’m patiently waiting for the New York Mets to win the World Series. My favorite Family Guy character is Stewie.

people.name: Jonny Svarzbein
people.bio: Jonny Svarzbein is a performer, host, writer and UCB teacher. UCB credits include Better Movies w/ Tommy Wiseau, Teacher’s Lounge, the Harold teams Mister Town City, 8 Babies and Landlord, the graduated Maude team Bonafide, Tournament of Nerds (9-time champ), hosting The Cagematch and many, many… too many… more shows.

people.name: Jordan Hull
people.bio: Jordan Elizabeth Hull is LADCC Award winner known for The L Word: Generation Q (Showtime and Paramount Plus), and Hustle (Netflix); She produced and starred in the Tribeca Festival-winning audio drama Red for Revolution (2024). Other credits include Caity (Sundance), The Second Best School Shooting (Adam McKay and Shira Piven).

people.name: Jordan Katcher
people.bio: Jordan Katcher is a comedian, actor, writer, & director who regularly performs improv, sketch, characters, and tracheotomies in Los Angeles. Find him @jordankatcher pretty much everywhere online except for AIM where you can message him at FrisbeeKing23

people.name: Jordan Kohler
people.bio: Jordan’s grandparents met at in line at DCM 1. Improviser & musician from Harrisburg, Pennsylvania.

people.name: Jordan Myrick
people.bio: Jordan is a comedian, actor, and writer who spends most of their time with their elderly chihuahua. You might recognize Jordan from Good Mythical Morning, Dropout (particularly as the host of Gastronauts, their comedy cooking competition show), or her popular relationship advice podcast, Happy Wife Happy Life. Jordan has written for places like Wondery, Calm, and Story Pirates. Come see Jordan in a show!

people.name: Jordan Obey
people.bio: Jordan is a sketch writer with a beautiful bald head. He wrote for the Maude house team Villain and currently writes for the house team Pamela. His spec script for “What We Do in the Shadows” won best comedy spec script at the 2023 Austin Film Festival which was pretty cool. He’s also written for Assssketch and has had work featured in the New York Comedy Festival and the Art of Brooklyn film festival. He’s half Black, half Arab, and fully marketable.

people.name: Jorge Berrios
people.bio: JORGE “MIAMI” BERRIOS: actor, writer, and comedian in Los Angeles, California. He grew up in Miami and is the youngest of six siblings. He graduated from MDC, where he played Duke Orsino. Then Jorge drove to Hollywood and signed up for improv classes at Second City and UCB. After graduation he toured and performed in various showcases, including the CBS Diversity Showcase. When his Solo Show was paused in 2020 Jorge attended LACC’s Acting Academy. His strongest influences are Jim Carrey and Robin Williams. Shoutout to his parents for leaving the Caribbean. No pares, sigue, sigue!

people.name: Jorge Oyola
people.bio: Originally from Orlando, FL., Jorge is a ‘Florida Man’ at heart. Writer, comedian, and actually professional DJ and Emcee, he’s hosted gameshows, acted on TV, and was even walked over by Evan Rachel Wood in Westworld. Alex Moffat once told him being from Florida “isn’t an excuse”, which he took personally.

people.name: Jorge Thomson
people.bio: Jorge Thomson is a comedy writer and performer with Cuban, Puerto Rican, and Irish American roots. Jorge disappointed his two lawyer parents by going to NYU’s Tisch School of The Arts to study acting. Jorge has appeared on MODERN FAMILY (ABC), UNCOUPLED (Netflix), and TWISTED METAL (Peacock), where he co-wrote an episode of the upcoming second season. Jorge is an alumni of the National Hispanic Media Coalition Writing Program and of the STARZ #TakeTheLead Writers Intensive. Currently, Jorge is in the 2024 Disney Writing Program, finally proving he was right to pocket his law school tuition.

people.name: Joseph Elston
people.bio: Joseph Elston is an actor, writer, comedian, and improviser based in Los Angeles. At UCB, he co-created and co-hosts the hit improv show, BADPACKS, and performs musical improv on a Sing Out Louise team. He created, co-wrote and starred in the web series, Schlubs , as well as the short film, Magnus the Magnificent . He has performed for sold out crowds at the New York Comedy Festival and opened for Kristen Schaal at San Francisco Sketchfest.

people.name: Joseph Knopke
people.bio: Joseph Richard Knopke is a brooklyn-based comedian, writer, actor and tax-payer. He is originally from Kansas City, Missouri (although he was technically born in Kansas). Joseph has been doing improv comedy for 15 years and has held the following occupations in that time: altar boy, pool cleaner, drive-thru worker, janitor, dishwasher, barista, production assistant, intern, theater attendant, call-center representative, research assistant, used-car salesman, and IT support engineer.

people.name: Joseph Lymous
people.bio: Joseph Lymous is a New Orleans born actor/comedian/puppeteer/producer. He was recently cast in the upcoming Tim Robinson HBO series, The Chair Company. In 2024 his sketch comedy group, Uncle Function, was invited to perform in the Netflix is a Joke Festival. Joseph is a 2023 CBS Showcase alum and a 2021 Just For Laughs New Face. You can catch him in The Other Two (MAX) and Awkwafina is Nora from Queens (Comedy Central). Joseph is currently developing his original tv series with Abominable Pictures… and as exciting as this all is, he mostly wants to be remembered for the fact that back in 2019 Beyoncé shared a video of him shakin’ his a$$ in her Instagram story.

people.name: Joseph Porter
people.bio: Joseph Porter is a writer and actor from a little island with no cars. Credits include DreamworksTV, Peacock Kids, the CBS Diversity Showcase, Maude Night, and a couple things he’s not proud of but he really needed the money. Currently, he can be seen writing, directing, and acting in Quick and Funny Musicals at UCB, and directing sketch throughout LA.

people.name: Josh Brekhus
people.bio: Josh Brekhus is a writer/actor/performer who currently performs with his team LEROY. He loves to work his lil buns off except for a couple of weeks in August when he goes back home to Montana to fly fish and drink beer with his dad.

people.name: Josh Covitt
people.bio: Originally from Encinitas, CA, Josh bravely made the 2-hour trek northward to Los Angeles to study Theater at UCLA. As a performer, he’s an alumni of Maude and Harold Night, recurred on multiple TV shows, and been in over 70 commercials. As a writer, he’s worked for Sony, Disney, Tubi, and has was named a Quarterfinalist in the Nicholl Fellowship. Most recently, he is the exhausted dad of a toddler – so he’s probably leaving soon to go to sleep.

people.name: Josh Ellman
people.bio: Josh Ellman is a writer, improviser, and stand-up based in Brooklyn. He identifies as gay – and sometimes as queer, if it will increase his cachet. He is currently a writer for UCB’s Maude Night, as well as member of improv house teams at the PIT and Armory. As a stand up, he was a 2025 Finalist in the New York Queer Comedy Festival. He also independently produces comedy shows, including the bimonthly show Ghost Stories at UCB and BCC.

people.name: Josh Fleury
people.bio: Josh is a half Taiwanese/half whatever comedy writer, songwriter, performer and musician based in LA. He is best known for his work with Moon Goon, Smosh, Starkid, Dropout’s “Thousandaires,” and his unhinged TikTok Songs about road trip rest stops. With Moon Goon, which originated as a Maude team in 2019, he’s written and performed CityWalk: The Musical, PRIME! An Amazon Musical, A Hallmark Christmas Movie in 4D, Cirque du Soleil The Play, The Mr. Peanut Talk Show, and (briefly) helped co-host Not Too Shabby. Other shows he’s been apart of at UCB include Asian AF, Late Night Monthly, Fuck FL, Great Moments In Comedy Made Worse, Maude After Midnight, and Coachella For A Dying Boy. Outside of UCB he’s been writing, producing, and performing comedy content in various formats for well over a decade. His work has been featured at Dropout, Groundlings, Dynasty Typewriter, iO West, The Pack, Nerdist, SF Sketchfest (x2), Titmouse, Tribeca, Funny Or Die, CollegeHumor & Entertainment Weekly.

people.name: Josh Grimes
people.bio: Hey, I’m Josh Grimes! I’ve been introduced to improv and comedy through K-State’s team, On the Spot, and have loved my 4 years being a part of it. I can’t wait to continue doing improv post-college!

people.name: Josh Marcus
people.bio: Josh Marcus is an actor, writer, and director in Los Angeles. Josh grew up outside of Chicago and as a teen started performing improv at The Second City, ImprovOlympic, and Annoyance Theatre. Josh once hosted a popular LA indie improv show for years called “Monday Night Improv”. When Josh isn’t performing or making films, he spends his time hiking, cycling, and camping in nature.

people.name: Josh Mattingly
people.bio: Josh Mattingly is a Chicago-based improvisor and comedian. His credits include work at iO Comedy, The Second City Chicago, and UCB-NY. A multi-instrumentalist, Josh has performed professionally across the Chicagoland area both on stage and in studio.

people.name: Josh Nasser
people.bio: Josh Nasser is an NYC based actor and stand-up comedian. His comedy has been featured on Comedy Central’s YouTube Channel. Vulture and onmogul.com have both named Josh as one of the best up and coming comedians in NYC. Josh currently hosts the NYTimes recommended comedy show, Oh, Such a Huge Show, Oh at the Bell House and has also written episodes for the Story Pirates Podcast, which adapts children’s stories into musical comedy sketches. You can find his previous work at joshnasser.com. And of course his favorite food is plain cheese pizza. He knows that’s a boring choice and he will work on it.

people.name: Josh Simpson
people.bio: Josh Simpson is a comedy writer and actor from Milwaukee. He’s written and acted on things like Dave, CONAN, Reno 911! and Funny or Die. He’s currently a staff writer and series regular on an office comedy called PBC. Josh likes to make videos, play music and teach his old dog new tricks.

people.name: Josh Ssettuba
people.bio: I caught the acting bug playing the Dogcatcher in a sixth grade production of Annie . After improvising some lines, and getting a laugh I was hooked. I then went on to do a bunch of acting, short form improv (gasp!), and comedy all around Vancouver, BC after high school. I am a proud Canadian and will give you maple syrup.

people.name: Josh Tobin
people.bio: An actor based in New York City, Josh’s credits include “Kaleidoscope” on Netflix, “The Other Two” and “The Gilded Age” on HBO, “Hello Tomorrow!” on AppleTV+, “The Last O.G.” on TBS, the Pulitzer Prize-winning Off-Broadway hit Fairview at Theatre for a New Audience (u/s, performed), 4000 Miles at Baltimore Center Stage (directed by Lila Neugebauer), Cabaret at PlayMakers Rep (with Taylor Mac), and the film Ricki and the Flash, starring Meryl Streep and directed by Jonathan Demme. Josh is also the creator and star of the original web series Men in the Park, which was featured in Vulture’s Funny Videos of the Month. At the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in New York, Josh improvised on Harold Night and wrote and performed original characters on Characters Welcome​.

people.name: Joy Regullano
people.bio: Joy Regullano is a writer / actor based in LA who recently wrote on the animated show MONSTERS AT WORK on Disney Plus. Previously, she has written on KUNG FU PANDA: DRAGON KNIGHT (Netflix / Dreamworks), THE HEALING POWERS OF DUDE (Netflix), several Sesame Workshop digital shorts, and she was a Sesame Street writing fellow. TV acting credits include CRAZY EX-GIRLFRIEND, MODERN FAMILY, SPEECHLESS, HOUSE OF LIES, and SUPERNATURAL. Check out the concept album for her Asian American comedy musical SUPPORTIVE WHITE PARENTS, now available on all streaming platforms!

people.name: Jozef Fahey
people.bio: Joe’s a comedian, actor, and filmmaker based in LA. As an actor you can catch him in various TV/Film and commercials including the feature film Stripper Boyz , a film he wrote and starred in. He is a main-stage UCB performer with Harold team Lohan, hosts One Big Jam, and he is a performer/producer of Midnight Rumble, a comedy-wrestling show. That’s everything I think.

people.name: Jp Bremer
people.bio: JP is an improviser in Austin Texas. He’s been doing it for oh 8 years now. He’s in too many shows.

people.name: JR Atkinson
people.bio: JR is a Brooklyn-based writer and performer, originally from Chicago. She writes with the UCB Maude Team First Wife, and performs with the indie improv team Midwives, which is currently a Home Team at Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Her two-woman show Indoor Kids was featured at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival.

people.name: Juan David Alvarez Betancur
people.bio: Juan D. Alvarez from Medellín founded the improv troupe CincoImpro, which has been active for 9 years and counting. He has also worked in Bogotá with The Awesome “quintopulgar” Fifth Thumb (Quinto Pulgar Teatro de Bogotá), directed by Alejo Cardona. Alongside his work in theater and improvisation, he also works in finance, banking, and many things that no add nothing to this.

people.name: Juanita Andersen
people.bio: Juanita is an LA-based actor from Spokane, WA. She is a Quick & Funny Musicals Company Member and an Emeritus Company Member with Playmakers Laboratory in Chicago. You can hear hear offer unqualified advice and loud opinions on her comedy astrology podcast Super Sister Signs.

people.name: Jules Starn
people.bio: Jules Starn’s character comedy specializes in costume, puppetry and stagecraft. He’s new to the live comedy scene, but he’s a keen actor, writer, and filmmaker whose megawatt smile has charmed audiences up and down these United States.

people.name: Julia Germeroth
people.bio: Julia Germeroth is an actor, writer, and comedian. She can be found wearing wigs at UCB and The Pack Theater every month with “Back Pocket” and “Flight Risk.” You can catch her stand up on stages such as The Ice House, The Comedy Store, and The Comedy Chateau, where she won the Audience Pick for Best Comic at WXMAN’S Night 4C Comedy Contest. She also worked at a zoo once, but we don’t have the time.

people.name: Julia Meltzer
people.bio: Julia is a writer, director and improviser who acts occasionally. She sold her spec script YOURS, MINE & PAUL’S to 20th Century Fox and it later sold to ABC with Sarah Hyland attached to star. Her second pilot sold to CBS, and most recently she wrote a feature film for Amazon. She is a mom (dog and human).

people.name: Julia Schroeder
people.bio: Julia Schroeder is an Astoria-based actor/improviser/comedian, originally from Detroit, MI. You’ve seen her in the unhinged horror comedy Screamboat, doing bits on The Tonight Show, and in a handful of commercials if you don’t pay for premium. Julia’s TikTok series ‘Sound Guy’ won Best Microepisode Series at NYC Web Fest in 2025, and her indie sketch team Time to Kill has been featured at NY Comedy Festival and SF SketchFest. She’s a proud member of Story Pirates, BoogieManja’s character team Typecast, and Grove 34’s Improv Night. Catch her with her sketch team Fridge Magnet on Maude Night here at UCB!!!

people.name: Julia Whitehouse
people.bio: Julia Whitehouse is a writer, performer, marathoner, and mother and wife and daughter and sister and aunt. But what she really wants to do is direct. She hosts, Happy Hour Story Hour, NYC’s longest running weekly storytelling open mic every Monday at The Duplex. Her words are in New Yorker Daily Shouts, McSweeney’s, and Mutha Magazine.

people.name: Julia Zhen
people.bio: Julia Zhen is a comedian, writer, and actor based in Brooklyn, NY. Her credits include New York Comedy Festival and San Francisco Sketchfest.

people.name: Julian Hernandez
people.bio: Julian Hernandez is a Cuban-American based in Brooklyn, New York. He teaches improv and clown across NYC, having studied at iO (Chicago), UCB (NY), and FIZGIG (NY). A UCB house performer and a cast member of The Second City’s first ever digital company, you can see Julian’s audience-driven physical comedy across the city as Jooby or with his improv duo Havana Club, seeking to boost up-and-coming Latine performers. He’s been featured on screen in Search Party (TBS) and in the indie hit, Free Time (to name a few).

people.name: Julianne Lang
people.bio: Julianne Lang is an actor, singer, writer, and comedian originally from Atlanta by way of Chicago’s comedy scene. She is in her third season of Maude night, performing with The Family Business. Recent credits include The Comeback (Season 3) or that time she was in a Taco Bell commercial with Doja Cat. She is also a cast member of American High Digital’s Barely Adult . Her solo show, Best Friend: The Musical , was recently featured in New York Comedy Festival.

people.name: Julie Brister
people.bio: Julie Brister is an actor, writer, and director. She was a series regular on Goliath on Prime and recurred as Daisy the attorney on Review on Comedy Central and as Laura Lee Hickock on the Onion News Network. Other TV credits include Sex Lives of College Girls, Transparent, Brooklyn Nine-Nine, New Girl, and Reno 911, among many more. Julie started at UCB NY in 1997 and has been a part of millions of shows on both coasts since then.

people.name: Julie Hoang
people.bio: Previous Maude teams: Lasers, Gotcha Gotcha, Clapback, and Jimmy Buick. Previous Mess Hall Team: Payday.

people.name: Julie Marchiano
people.bio: Julie Marchiano was recently featured on Season 3 of the HBO series BARRY. She spent several years in Chicago, where she wrote and performed on The Second City’s e.t.c. Resident Stage, and traveled across the country with their National Touring Company. In addition to Maude Night, Julie has performed on Characters Welcome and Mess Hall at UCB, and continues to perform with Story Pirates all over Southern California, and on their award-winning podcast.

people.name: Julie Whitesell
people.bio: Julie Whitesell wrote for a Maude team for 5 years and then taught at the theatre. She also wrote and performed in her own sketch show and directed The Sitcom Show. She is a working TV writer who has written for over 150 episodes of TV.

people.name: Julien Darmoni
people.bio: Julien is a writer and performer living in New York. You can see him perform with his team Ducks, read his writing in places like the New Yorker, or find him grinding out on the tennis court. He’s currently writing his first novel

people.name: July Diaz
people.bio: July Diaz is a writer/performer from Los Angeles and is a founding member of the improv team Menudo. Listen to his podcast Inside Video Games.

people.name: Justin Borak
people.bio: Justin Borak is a Lebanese-American playwright and comedian based in New York. Starting his comedy journey in Chicago, he has been seen writing for The Second City, performing on harold night and with house team Liar at iO and writing and performing at the Cards Against Humanity Show at The Blue Theatre. He was a producer and founding member of the iO Podcast Network where he developed This is Gonna Work, The Armando: Podcast, and the D&D Show, The Explorer’s Pack, which played sold out live shows at the Del Close Theatre. He has written for Adult Swim, The Onion, and is currently a playwright with Playscripts and Concord Theatricals.

people.name: Justin Carty
people.bio: Justin Carty is an improviser who is originally from New Jersey. He’s lived all over the country and will talk to you about it if you ask him! You can see him on Lloyd Night or doing improv with friends on teams like SICKOS and TURBOSQUID.

people.name: Justin Catchens
people.bio: Justin Catchens is a stand-up comedian, actor, and improvisor based in New York City. His credits include Vulture, Timeout NY, New York Comedy Festival, etc. He currently hosts a monthly variety show called Justin For Laughs. His indie improv team, My Momma’s Biscuits, has headlined festivals all around the country. Justin is from Detroit and would like to meet Sam Richardson and Tim Robinson.

people.name: Justin Kim
people.bio: Justin is a comedian, writer, and Costco member from Grand Rapids, Michigan. He studied improvisation and sketch comedy at Upright Citizens Brigade, where he was a member of the Maude teams Honey and Laser. You can see him perform at Asian AF!

people.name: Justin Liu
people.bio: Born and raised in the small town of Los Angeles, CA.

people.name: Justin Michael
people.bio: Justin Michael is a writer, performer and voice actor from Los Angeles. He’s written for Infinity Train (HBO Max), Among Us (Paramount+), and served as a head writer on the animated adaptation the graphic novel Bone (Netflix). He created Audible’s improvised comedy Bad Reception , the Annie-nominated stop-motion series Friendship All-Stars of Friendship , and hosts the improv podcast Nature Talks to Itself and Batman: The Animated Podcast . You can hear his voice on cartoons like Robot Chicken , Tom & Jerry , The Fungies and more. See him live with his improv team Winslow or his improvised dubbing show Dub-a-Dub-Dub!

people.name: Justin Page
people.bio: Justin Page is an actor and improvisor originally from San Jose, CA. You can see him performing with PTO on Lloyd Night and monthly on The Morning Show at Night: Live with Rebis and Kellie Lee. He also loves steak and pizza.

people.name: Justin Patterson
people.bio: Justin is a writer, performer and actor hailing from the Midwest. Currently, you can watch his written sketches every month at UCB with his Maude team, Detention!!! Justin performs with his improv team, Rat King at UCB and the Clubhouse. Previously, Justin has improvised with the Mess Hall team, Possum, cohosted the Inner Sanctum shows Check Out My Web Series! and Deuce Ex Machina which achieved over 100 shows all around LA!

people.name: Justin Tyler
people.bio: Justin Tyler is a writer, director and actor in New York. He recently served as the writer of the ABC special ‘A Night at the Academy Museum’, a Director/Supervising Field Producer on ‘Desus & Mero’ for Showtime, and a staff writer on ‘The Opposition w/ Jordan Klepper’ on Comedy Central. He’s the supervising producer of ‘101 Places to Party Before You Die’ a comedy travel show for Max and recently directed season 1 of ‘McEnroe’s Places’ for ESPN+ /NFL Films as well as Tracy Morgan’s Shark Week special ‘Sharks! with Tracy Morgan’. He is a co-host of the weekly long running comic book podcast ‘Comic Book Club’ and the co-creator of Characters Welcome.

people.name: Justina MacNeil
people.bio: Justina is an actor, writer, and Italian based out of LA. She performs regularly with her improv team “Wet Bench” and can be found across town at character open mics. The script she’s proudest of is her screenplay “Finding Bigfoot,” in which Bigfoot goes on a journey to find himself. She’s equally proud to own the setlist from the 2023 Paramore concert in Columbus, Ohio. Best night of her life!

people.name: JZ Zepeda
people.bio: QT’s Presents … JOY!

people.name: Kaela Rosenbaum
people.bio: Kaela is a Brooklyn-based actor-comedian who credits her no-nonsense attitude to growing up in lean, mean NYC. Kaela was trained in theater and improv at Northwestern University and has since been performing at venues like iO, The Brooklyn Comedy Collective, and the Annoyance.

people.name: Kahlil Maskati
people.bio: Kahlil “K-Swish” Maskati is a half-Indian, half-Puerto Rican television writer from New Jersey. He most recently wrote for the CBS hit “So Help Me Todd”. Previously, he wrote for Netflix’s “Act Your Age,” and the CW’s “Tom Swift”. You can find him doing musical improv at Sing Out, Louise! and with his indie teams, Hot Cakes and Curl Pattern.

people.name: Kale Hills
people.bio: Kale Hills is an actor and writer originally from the deep Evangelical recesses of Missouri. His credits include Adam Ruins Everything and CollegeHumor, and his dark comedy screenplay ST JOHN was recently a LaunchPad top 100 selection.

people.name: Kalen Rae Garnett
people.bio: Kalen is an actor/comedian from the Ozarks, an NYU Tisch graduate, and in a lot of debt. Kalen is on Lloyd Night at UCB. Kalen is single.

people.name: Kaley Morrison
people.bio: Kaley is an actor, comedian, and writer originally from North Carolina. Inspired by pop culture, real life characters, and her perspective as an Indigenous person (enrolled member of the Waccamaw Siouan tribe) Kaley finds humor everywhere she goes. Kaley is a two time recipient of the PIT/SNL scholarship, a Sesame Workshop Writers’ Room Fellow ’23, and First Peoples Fund Performing Arts Fellow ’25.

people.name: Kalynn Chambers
people.bio: Kalynn Chambers is a North Carolina native actor, writer, and improvisor living in Brooklyn. Kalynn was recognized as an Exceptional Student at the Terry Knickerbocker Studio for her commitment to excellence in the craft of acting. She has studied and performed across DC and NYC including Character’s Welcome. Kalynn was a Comedy Writing Intern for Amanda Seales’ Smart, Funny, and Black and she completed the Saturday Night Live/Peoples Improv Theater Scholarship Program. IG – @KalynnChambers.

people.name: Kami Dimitrova
people.bio: Kami is an actor/writer in Los Angeles originally from Bulgaria (whoa!). She’s previously performed on UCB Betty and Maude Night in NY and LA. She loves the beach and her enormous cat, Tuna!

people.name: Kamryn Tate
people.bio: Kamryn is a Georgia native actor, writer, improviser, and hot wing connoisseur. A recent University of Southern California graduate (Fight On!) and currently based in Los Angeles, California. She believes in upholding the duality of southern hospitality and southern passive aggressiveness, so you will never know what she’s thinking. That’s why she decided playing pretend for a living was her passion. Above all else, she loves being silly, especially for pay. Kamryn is seeking representation! IG: @kamryn.tate.

people.name: Kara Kinsey
people.bio: Kara Kinsey is a multi-faceted writer, comedian, and performer known for captivating audiences with her indoor possum persona. With a unique blend of heart and horror, Kara explores in her comedy the worlds of lovable savage monsters otherwise known as women.

people.name: Karel Ebergen
people.bio: Karel can’t be contained. His “bio,” his “credits,” his “backstory” are too powerful to fit into 3 to 5 sentences. I’m a chameleon, a sponge, an enigma. Good luck describing me. I do improv and sketch around town. Watch please!

people.name: Karen Baughn
people.bio: As a kid, Karen dreamed of being classical theater actress performing meaty and thoughtful Shakespearian roles. Then she took a hard left turn and decided to do improv instead. She has studied and performed at The Groundlings, iOWest, Bang, The Shared Experience Studio, and of course, UCB where she had the honor of being on Harold, Maude, and Betty teams. But no matter where she goes, she treats her comedy with the seriousness of a Lady McBeth.

people.name: Karen Bolipata
people.bio: Karen is a writer in NYC. She started studying sketch at UCB in 2018. It made her heart skip a beat. Then a second. Then a third before an underwhelming blackout. When she’s not writing for Maude Night, she can be found copywriting for brands, making zines, and writing about all the sufficient fun she’s having in a webcomic called DIARY OF A SUFFICIENTLY FUN PERSON.

people.name: Karen Davidson
people.bio: Karen Davidson has been a fixture in the Glasgow improv scene since 2019. Having trained with the Glasgow Improv Theatre, the Upright Citizens Brigade and the World’s Greatest Improv School she has been taught and inspired by some of the best improv teachers around. She has appeared in the legendary podcast Improv4Humans, with Matt Besser of UCB, and has performed in events such as Edinburgh Fringe, Glasgow International Comedy Festival, Edinburgh International Improv Festival, Robin Hood Improv Festival and others.

people.name: Kat Ellison Williams
people.bio: Kat Ellison Williams is a writer, actor, and stand-up comedian. He teaches improv at Coldtowne and Fallout Theater in Austin, TX. His debut special, Daddy’s Boy, can soon be viewed on YouTube. He has three different tattoos of toilets.

people.name: Kat Garcia
people.bio: Kat Garcia is a comedy writer and product designer currently writing for a Maude house team. She’s originally from Maryland and has spent most of her adult life perpetually moving between Boston and Paris. Her work has been published at McSweeney’s, Slackjaw, and The Belladonna Comedy. She has a fluffy French cat named Cécile who REALLY likes carnitas.

people.name: Kat Smith
people.bio: Host of We Just Find It Funny, an all female Late-Night Show based in NYC. Sketch writer and standup, performed at Edinburgh Fringe Festival, LA, Chicago.

people.name: Kate Bera
people.bio: After a decade as a circus artist, Kate traded fire juggling for improv and still maintains that performing her first long-form set was the scarier experience. Kate toured for several years internationally before making LA her home base. She loves bringing her background in movement into her improv and character work.

people.name: Kate Elliott
people.bio: Kate Elliott is an improviser, performer, and experiential producer based in Philadelphia, PA. An alum of PHIT, she currently performs with her indie Slacker team Hey Hi Hello alongside Kimmy Nazaruk, Matt Woodward, and Sofía Cordero. With a background in dance, Kate gravitates toward physical comedy and bold character choices.

people.name: Kate Grogan
people.bio: Kate Grogan is an improviser originally from Dallas, TX. She has been performing improv for over 15 years and has trained at the Dallas Comedy House, Stomping Ground Comedy Theater, iO, and the Annoyance. She currently lives in Chicago where you can see her perform with Wet Pizza, iO house team Baby Grand, and Improvised DCOM. She is also the co-founder and producer of the improvised PowerPoint show, People Presenting Things. She is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Kate Shine
people.bio: Kate Shine is a Boston-born writer and actor living in Queens. Credits include American Horror Stories, The Job Interview, commercials for Johnson & Johnson, M&M’s, Tempur-Pedic and more. Kate has worked as a Producer’s Assistant on projects such as Uncoupled (Netflix) and Girl Haunts Boy (Max). Kate has performed across the country at sketch comedy festivals such as San Francisco Sketch Fest, Austin Sketch Fest and Portland Sketch Fest. Kate and her writing partner Molly Kiernan are currently shopping their disability-inclusive pilot script, based on Kate’s experience living with epilepsy.

people.name: Kate Sisk
people.bio: Kate Sisk (he/she/they) is a professional comedian, amateur drag king, and retired professional soccer player. Kate improvised on several UCBT Lloyd and Harold house teams from 2016-2020. Kate is also a stand up comedian who has appeared on Comedy Central.

people.name: Katherine Stiegemeyer
people.bio: Katherine is an improviser and comedian from Kansas City. Though unfortunately she’s not that into barbecue sauce (sorry).

people.name: Katherine Taylor
people.bio: Katherine Taylor is an actor, comedian, and writer. She has been on house improv and sketch teams at UCB, The Second City NY, and the PIT, as well as appearing in comedy shows around New York otherwise. She recently performed in the Off-Broadway musical, “The Trojans,” which sold out for the final month of its six-week run and received glowing reviews, including praise from The New Yorker.

people.name: Kathy Yamamoto
people.bio: Kathy Yamamoto is a writer and performer based in Los Angeles. She has appeared in multiple national commercials and has written for Character Media’s Unforgettable Gala, Wong Fu Productions, Cosmopolitan.com and more. She doesn’t ever drink the recommended amount of water, even though she tries.

people.name: Katie Hartman
people.bio: KATIE HARTMAN is an actor, writer and comedian in NYC. She can be seen in High Maintenance (HBO), The Other Two (MAX), Younger (TV Land) and the Robert Smigel/Adam Sandler joint “The Week Of” (Netflix). Other credits: Just for Laughs in Montreal as a “New Face of Comedy,” staff writer and performer on the WGA nominated Paid Off with Michael Torpey on truTV. Her web series “Made to Order” was listed as one of “6 Brilliant Web Series” by Marie Claire.

people.name: Katie Marovitch
people.bio: Katie Marovitch is a comedy writer, actor, and director who performs improv and standup across Los Angeles. Katie currently writes, directs, and acts in Reality Check, a weekly YouTube series for FOXtv that is on its fifth season. She was a writer and cast member at CollegeHumor, and now regularly writes and performs in many of the popular shows on their streaming platform Dropout. She has written content for an eclectic mix of celebrities, including Miley Cyrus, Billy Ray Cyrus, Howie Mandel, Richard Blais, Alex Wagner, Kelly Rizzo, Lil Nas X, Lil Yachty, Bella Poarch, and Bassem Youssef. Check out her website to learn more!

people.name: Katie Nathan
people.bio: Katie is an improviser, writer, and actress. Her credits include national commercials for State Farm (Super Bowl), Sprint, Jiffy Lube, and more, as well as projects for NBC and Showtime. She’s passionate about television and will talk about it as long as you let her.

people.name: Katie Roletto
people.bio: Katie is an actor, writer and producer hailing from San Francisco. She made her way to LA via a trolley cable car where she studied at Groundlings, World’s Greatest Improv School and of course, UCB. You can see her on The Morning Show at Night! Live with Rebis and Kellie Lee! You can probably also see her anywhere they serve a good mozzarella stick.

people.name: Katie Sewell
people.bio: Katie Sewell performs improv regularly in Cleveland, Ohio. She performs with Sorry for Your Loss, a troupe that focuses on grounded scenes that launch into lunacy and finding the real in otherwise absurd scenarios.

people.name: Katy Fullan
people.bio: Katy Fullan is an actor, writer and comedian originally from Chicago, IL. Her credits include Curb Your Enthusiasm (HBO), I Think You Should Leave (Netflix), Killing It (Peacock) and Let’s Be Real (Fox). She co-created/wrote/starred in original short series for Adult Swim and Cake (FX), and her work has been featured at Tribeca, Slamdance and International Film Festival Rotterdam.

people.name: Katy Matz
people.bio: Katy Matz is a clown and professional chismosa currently seeking representation in Austin, TX. A trained dancer and performer who is not lactose intolerant, Katy can most certainly do the splits! She would like to thank her parents, nieces, and her Grandpa Daddy Mike who died the first time she came to New York City 14 years ago for always believing in her.

people.name: Kausar Mohammed
people.bio: Kausar Mohammed is a queer, Muslim, Pakistani actress and writer who stars in ‘APPENDAGE’, which just released on Hulu this fall. Mohammed wrote and stars in the holiday romantic comedy THE SYED FAMILY XMAS EVE GAME NIGHT, which was bought by a major studio for development. Mohammed recently starred as Dr. Meena Dhawan AKA Fast Track on The CW series THE FLASH, and earlier as Soraya Abbas on The CW series 4400. She starred in Paul Feig’s series “EAST OF LA BREA” and voices Yaz, one of the fan favorite lead characters, on Dreamworks and Amblin’s hit show JURASSIC WORLD: CAMP CRETACEOUS.

people.name: Keith Newman
people.bio: Keith is an LA-based musician, improviser, artist, and huge nerd. You can find him on the 2nd and 4th Wednesdays at Sing Out, Louise! as well as around town with his indie musical improv teams Hot Cakes, GRINDHOUSE, and The Improvised Jewsical. He’s currently working on an upcoming pop-punk comedy album with his band Mom, Get Out Of My Room!

people.name: Kel Cripe
people.bio: Kel Cripe is an LA based comedian, writer and actor. In addition to standup, they are part of the sketch comedy trio Babe Motel, where they write and perform live/digital sketch shows. In 2023, Kel wrote, produced and starred in their debut play, Concessions: The Show (A Journey Through the Four Kingdoms of the Candy Realm). Kel has been featured in NY Comedy Fest, SF Sketchfest and you can hear their Netflix is a Joke Radio Set on SiriusXM. In their free time, Kel collects hot wheels, draws comics and plays fetch with their orange cat named Lasana.

people.name: Kelly Vrooman
people.bio: Kelly Vrooman is an actor/writer who The New York Times called “a master class in rapid-fire comedy.” She most recently appeared on Black-ish , Grey’s Anatomy , Nickelodeon’s Warped! , and as a pink genie for the Real Genius commercial campaign. Her short film, Rekindled , which she wrote and starred in, screened at 12 Oscar-Qualifying festivals, won 16 awards, and is now being developed as a feature. Kelly is often described as having sassy mom energy, to which she says, “you’re welcome, now eat your peas”.

people.name: Kelsey Murphy
people.bio: Kelsey Murphy is a children’s book editor, writer, and improviser! She was on the Lloyd team Big Time, as well as indie teams Bear Golf and Freight, among many others. She now performs with DUCKS.

people.name: Kelsi Roberts
people.bio: Kelsi Roberts was born during an earthquake (sorry Mom) and was on an MTV dating show in the early 2000s. She’s been doing improv and sketch for 17 years, and has a show with her sweet buds called The Show That Never Was here at UCB. She’s also a Sunday Company Alumni at The Groundlings Theater where she teaches. She’s stoked out of her mind to be on Maude! Okay bye! Love you!

people.name: Kendahl Landreth
people.bio: Kendahl Landreth is a comedian who is known for her Midwestern mom character and reality TV impressions on the internet. You may also have seen her on Comedy Central, Buzzfeed or in the drive-thru line of your local Del Taco.

people.name: Kendall Relf
people.bio: Originally from Chicago, Kendall Relf is a New York-based comedian, writer, and actor. He has opened for national comedians including Sam Jay, Loni Love, Josh Johnson, Zainab Johnson, and Robby Hoffman. Kendall has appeared on SiriusXM’s Love Thang with Punkie Johnson and was featured in The Bellhouse’s Newcomer’s Showcase, streaming on Veeps.

people.name: Kendra Singh
people.bio: Kendra Singh is an improviser and writer in New York City. She hosts the weekly Forever Jam at BCC, and performs around the city with her indie improv team Lil’ Faithful. She is also a member of BCC’s improv and sketch house teams, What Made You Laugh? and Demon Time. She has previously worked as an editorial assistant and wrote at Reductress. She played herself in a two-woman show, Estranging, at Caveat’s Projectorfest in 2023. Yay!!!!

people.name: Kenneth Jackson
people.bio: A native of Atlanta, GA – Kenneth has been doing comedy since his sophomore year in college and is proud to performing on UCB’s Harold Night with his team, Keanu! He has trained at UCB and The Pack theatre under countless teachers throughout the years. When not performing, you can find him analyzing data for marketing firms or more likely pretending to be Lenny Kravitz in the bathroom mirror.

people.name: Kenny Gray
people.bio: Kenny Gray is a comedian/musician originally from New Hampshire. He has been a part of Mainstage at ImprovBoston, Sketch Night at The Pack Theater, and his solo show Kenny Goes to Sleep was featured at the 2024 Edinburgh Fringe. Currently he is part of Maude Night, the character team Back Pocket, and makes lots of silly little videos for the internet. He is thrilled to be a part of UCB, and thanks his wife and son for their endless support and love.

people.name: Kenny Harvey
people.bio: Kenny Harvey is a NY-born & LA based actor, producer, and improviser. From Staten Island, Kenny fell in love with theatre and improv comedy during high school. After he escaped, he attended Elon University and graduated with a Bachelors of Fine Arts in Acting. You can find Kenny hosting a monthly variety show around town on his indie team Emotionally Available, shopping on Facebook Marketplace, watering his plants, or hanging with his favorite pet, Peppino, who is definitely real and not fake.

people.name: Kenny Stevenson
people.bio: Kenny Stevenson has been doing comedy in LA for 20 years, and has been performing at the UCB for over 10. He has had recurring parts on Parks & Recreation , Comedy Bang Bang , and Brooklyn 99 . He has also been on the shows This Is Us , Hawaii Five-0 , Preppy Little Teen$ , and The UCB Show . His impressions can be heard on the podcasts Cheap Seats , Improv 4 Humans , and Dumb People Town . He voiced and puppeteered the character “Edgar” on Duff’s Happy Fun Bake Time , and he worked as an additional puppeteer on the Netflix show Julie’s Greenroom starring Julie Andrews.

people.name: Kenny Yi
people.bio: Kenny Park Yi is a comedian who lives in New York City. He co-produces Boba Gays, a queer Asian comedy variety show with Alex Kim. He performs improv and stand-up at places like the Brooklyn Comedy Collective, Caveat, and even your dad’s house.

people.name: Kento Morita
people.bio: Kento, an award-winning Japanese American comedian who performs sketch comedy at UCB and improv at The Second City New York and The Story Pirates and writes for The Onion. He co-produces the improv gameshow “Duty Free Live!” at Caveat and lends his voice to acclaimed projects like the Ambies Award-winning “Cat in the Hat Cast” and the video game “Rise of the Ronin”.

people.name: Kerry Maguire
people.bio: Kerry McGuire is a writer and sometime performer living in the City of Lost Angels. She has written for CollegeHumor, Hot Date, Reductress, Jest.com, Heads of Space, and other platforms that have been lost to history (and the vagaries of the internet economy). She loves live performance, and is down with dumb.

people.name: Kerry O'Neill
people.bio: Kerry O’Neill is a writer and filmmaker from New York Living in Los Angeles. After a decade writing and performing live comedy at The UCB Theatre in LA, Kerry turned her focus to narrative storytelling. Her recent television writing credits include Murderville (Netflix) and Jury Duty (Amazon/Freevee), on which she was also a series regular. Kerry’s films have screened at festivals worldwide and have been awarded The Panavision New Filmmakers Grant, The Vanishing Angle Post Production Grant, and earned her a spot as a fellow in The 2021 Short to Feature Lab as well as the prestigious American Film Institute Directing Workshop for Women.

people.name: Kevin Braunschweig
people.bio: Kevin Braunschweig is a writer/performer from Alamo, California. Kevin loves hanging with his wife and dog and playing rugby and hockey in his free time!

people.name: Kevin Brunette
people.bio: Kevin is a Maude Night writer and recovering theatre kid. The Christopher Newport University alumni, and aerialist in a collegiate production Pippin, fell in love with comedic writing his senior year and made the move to Los Angeles in 2023. Kevin’s love for comedy, stupidity, and baseball sparked the concept for his animated series, Liminal Summers . The Northern Virginia native is honored to be making the leap from fan to teammate at UCB!

people.name: Kevin Froleiks
people.bio: Kevin Froleiks is a sketch comedy writer on the UCB Maude Team, Ponyboy. Before the pandemic, he was also a writer for House of Birds. In addition to sketch writing, Kevin writes and performs comedy music under the alias “Goodbye Charlemagne”.

people.name: Kevin M Palermo
people.bio: Kevin Palermo is a writer and producer from the great state of New Jersey. He lives in Brooklyn with his cat, Tuba, who has never done anything wrong. He is very physically strong and good at fighting.

people.name: Kevin Mahoney
people.bio: Kevin is an NYC-based actor and improviser. People generally find him to be reasonable and polite, except when he denies any science that criticizes Diet Coke. He originally cut his improv teeth at Washington Improv Theater in DC and now performs regularly with the improv team Siblings in NYC.

people.name: Kevin McNulty
people.bio: Kevin McNulty is a Brooklyn-based comedian who wants you to like him. He has been focusing on comedy for the past couple of years, and his mom says it’s going well. When not writing sketches for his Maude team, Dumptruck, he performs standup, and runs a business satire journal, The Morning Bagel. He knows he needs to start doing short-form video. He is seeking representation despite thinking every rep he meets has hip-pocketed him.

people.name: Keylan Haynes
people.bio: Keylan Haynes is an Austin based actor and improviser. He started his improv journey in 2017 and is a graduate of Coldtowne and a UCB teacher.

people.name: Kiana Jones
people.bio: Kiana Janée Jones (she/her) is a graduate of New York Univeristy’s Tisch School of the Arts, where she earned her BFA in drama. She trained at Playwrights Horizons Theater School, Atlantic Acting School, and Stella Adler Studio of Acting. Her credits include Daisy in The Rhinoceros, directed Jedadiah Schultz, and is a recent alumni of the improv comedy group Dangerbox.

people.name: Kiki Mikkelsen
people.bio: Kiki Mikkelsen is an improvisor, actor and musician living in Brooklyn. Kiki is a co-creator of Shitzprobe and has been performing and teaching improv in New York City since 2012. She has performed and taught improv internationally with Baby Wants Candy, Blank! The Musical, Vern, Premiere, and more. Kiki holds a Bachelor of Music degree from Oklahoma City University and when she’s not improvising, she can be seen singing with Accord Treble Choir in NYC.

people.name: Kiko Soirée
people.bio: Long before Kiko was any of the following, Kiko began their comedic roots at UCB performing in basements all across New York City. Kiko Soirée is a Brooklyn based, gender fluid drag artist and performer, producing and hosting shows at venues like The Bell House, The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, Ars Nova, Joe’s Pub, Club Cumming, Bubble_T, and MoCA. They’ve been featured in Paper Magazine, Them, Vogue, NY Times, Time Out New York, and Gregory Kramer’s DOWNTOWN, a portrait documentation of downtown life in New York City.

people.name: Kim Cooper
people.bio: Kim Cooper is an actor, writer, and Korean adoptee from Iowa (go Hawks). She performs with Voltron and Number One Son and truly has the best time. Kim can be seen on Black Monday, Adam Ruins Everything, and in national commercials. She can be heard on the podcast Asians in Baseball, the Korean Drama Podcast, and in karaoke rooms scream-singing early 2000s jams.

people.name: Kim Egner
people.bio: Kim is a designer, illustrator, performer and director in Austin, Texas. Drawn to all types of absurd and surreal creativity, she is performing with The Rose at DCM26. She hopes this trip to New York ends the way all fairy tales do: Being carried into the sky by a flock of pigeons.

people.name: Kim Seltzer
people.bio: Kim Seltzer is a writer/comedian from the suburbs of Los Angeles. She has a degree in Computer Science from UCLA and is known for incorporating technology into comedy in new, exciting, and ultimately, stupid ways. Some of her credits include ClickHole, Hallmark, McSweeney’s Internet Tendency, and Team Coco Digital. She is one half of the acclaimed comedy duo, Kim & Aliya.

people.name: Kimia Behpoornia
people.bio: If Kimia looks familiar to you maybe you’ve seen Reboot, Atypical, Modern Family… Abby’s? JK no one watched that one season NBC sitcom. Or you’ve probably just seen her in the hills of Silver Lake, alone, on an aimless 5 hour walk.

people.name: Kimmy Nazaruk
people.bio: Kimmy Nazaruk is an improviser from Philadelphia, PA. An alum of PHIT and SideQuest Theater, Kimmy has performed longform improv across the city of Philadelphia with her indie Slacker team Hey Hi Hello (team members: Kate Elliott, Matt Woodward, and Sofía Cordero). She also appeared in the ComedySportz Double Feature Season 5 show The Hotel.

people.name: Kirby Howell-Baptiste
people.bio: Kirby is an actress from London who is best known for her memorable roles in Killing Eve , The Good Place , Barry , and Cruella . In 2021 she co-led the comedy Queenpins , a two-hander with Kristen Bell also starring Vince Vaughn and Paul Walter Hauser. Additionally, Kirby is a well established voice actor known for HBO Max’s Infinity Train , Jurassic World: Camp Cretaceous on Netflix, Tig and Seek and the DC animated feature Catwoman .

people.name: Koschka Bahr
people.bio: Koschka is an actor, writer and classically trained fencer (épée) raised communist-pagan by squatter-punk cats in East Berlin, Germany. Currently based in L.A., they divide their time between Berlin, London, and Reykjavik (as well as any other city with a cat café!). They are currently in development for a podcast about never having dated, writing their first novel (a supernatural thriller based on their stay in a haunted Swedish prison) and reworking their absurdist-expressionist solo show (Pure Evil: A One Man Show About My Cat) for North American audiences. Stay tuned!

people.name: Kris Hillesheim
people.bio: Kris Hillesheim is a performer from the Bay Area who is heading into his sophomore year in the BA Dramatic Arts degree at the University of Southern California. For the last year he has had the privilege of performing with USC’s premier improv comedy group Commedus Interruptus. He hopes to continue sharing his theatrics and humor with likeminded creatives everywhere!

people.name: Kris Wiener
people.bio: Kris Wiener is a New York-based improviser and actor. She has performed and coached improv comedy for 10 years, and was on the UCB Harold Teams Some Kid and Too Damn Much. She has studied improv with Anthony Atamanunik, Will Hines, and Shannon O’Neill, and acting with Terry Knickerbocker. She lives in Brooklyn with her family which includes two cats, a dog, and a very fun 5-year-old.

people.name: Kristen Rozanski
people.bio: Kristen was born and raised at UCB NY & LA (if one disregards the first 23 years of her life). You can see her on shows like Blackish, Silicon Valley, Adam Ruins Everything, Tiny Toons Looniversity, The Birthday Boys, Comedy Bang Bang, Jimmy Kimmel, Law and Order, Law and Order SVU, and other stuff.

people.name: Kristen Tucker
people.bio: Kristen Tucker is an improviser and actor based in New York City. She performs at UCB Theatre with The Trial, an improvised courtroom experience, and the musical improv team Book Club. She also performs regularly with The Armory Comedy, former Pit Ensembles team Nero’s Fiddle, and the improv duo Tucker & Miles. Outside of comedy, Kristen holds a BS in Electrical & Computer Engineering from The University of Texas at Austin and is the co-inventor on a U.S. patent related to timing closure in clocked logic circuits. In her spare time, she enjoys competitive beach volleyball, cooking, and reading.

people.name: Kristen Vaganos
people.bio: Kristen Vaganos is an LA based actor, writer, and producer. She graduated from NYU Tisch in just three years *hair toss*. Wildly enough, in addition to her comedy and online sketches, she is known for starring on a very serious show, General Hospital . But really, this is all a ploy to become a celebrity guest judge on the Food Network Channel.

people.name: Kristina Grosspietsch
people.bio: Kristina Grosspietsch is a writer, performer, and former cheesemonger from the Midwest now based in LA. She’s worked in writers’ rooms at both Netflix and BET+ and is a freelance entertainment contributor for Mashable and Indiewire. On stage, Kristina’s NYC improv team was NYTimes-reviewed, and her two-woman play “Opening Night” received critical acclaim at its NYC Fringe Festival debut. Kristina currently writes and performs with her UCBTLA Maude sketch team.

people.name: Kristina Stapelfeld
people.bio: Kristina Stapelfeld is a Boston-based comedian and musician who performs regularly with Neighbors at Union Comedy. She also writes and performs musical comedy songs about the impending apocalypse. You can find her on Instagram @moonbrains and at kristinastapelfeld.substack.com.

people.name: Krys Tabujara
people.bio: Krys Tabujara (they/them) is a multidisciplinary artist, performer and magician member of the Magic Castle. Their performances as AZN FETÏSCHE and “S*fespace” explore anti-Asian racism through satire and comedy. They can be seen on stage at UCB with QT’s (UCB’s first all trans, gender-nonconforming and non-binary house team).

people.name: Krystal Ali
people.bio: Krystal Ali is a DC-based improviser, instructor, and director. She performs across the country with teams including Lena Dunham, Kinfolk and Hellcat. She has brought her unique style of play to comedy festivals nationwide, including this one.

people.name: Kunal Dudheker
people.bio: Kunal Dudheker grew up in Orange County, CA where he eventually left behind a glamorous life of accounting to pursue acting and comedy in Los Angeles. Since then he has performed regularly at the Upright Citizens Brigade (UCB) and iO West theaters, and has had numerous TV/Film appearances including Shang-Chi (2021), Silicon Valley (HBO), Fresh Off the Boat (ABC), Hacks (HBO), Crazy Ex-Girlfriend (CW) and recurred on Better Things (FX) and Life by Ella (AppleTV). He also performed in the 2017 ABC Showcase.

people.name: Kurt Cruz
people.bio: Kurt Cruz is a musician, comedian, and Technical Director. He is the Technical Director at Second City NYC. When he’s not behind the tech booth, you can find him performing improv and music at various venues throughout NYC. Check out his solo indie music project, Kuko, on all streaming platforms.

people.name: Kurt Maloney
people.bio: Kurt Maloney is a comedian from LA. You might recognize him from Dropout.tv or as that one guy in that one commercial, but most likely you know him from being related to him! Thanks for coming to the show mom. Kurt has written for Disney, SMOSH, Dropout and is a part of the WGA!

people.name: Kyle Colorado
people.bio: Comedian and Balloon Twister (for real) based in NYC, lives in NJ (sorry). Currently Co-Produces and Performs on “The Building: Contingencies” after winning the 2025 UCB 3×3 Tournament with The Building. If you ask nicely, Kyle will make a Spider-Man balloon for you.

people.name: Kyle Erf
people.bio: Kyle Erf is a writer and game developer based in Brooklyn, NY. He is a founding writer of Hard Drive, the internet’s premiere nerd satire outlet, and one time VICE called him “NYC’s Best Goth Comedian.” Kyle is a co-founder of the video game studio Moving Pieces Interactive, where he has directed games like Dodo Peak (Switch, Apple Arcade), Shoulders of Giants (PC, Xbox, PS5), and Entropy Survivors (PC, Xbox, PS5).

people.name: Kyle Irion
people.bio: Kyle Irion is an improviser, and actor living in Austin, Texas. He currently performs improv with his duo, The Blade, and as the Bizarro version of Larry McMurtry in the two-person show, “Larry McMurtry’s Cowboy Cavalcade.” His favorite food is popcorn.

people.name: Kyle Kasabian
people.bio: Kyle Kasabian is an actor, writer, and director from Tyngsboro, Massachusetts. His work has been featured by Vulture.com, Vimeo Staff Picks, The Webby Awards, and The Critic’s Choice Awards. Credits include GIRLS5EVA (Netflix) SCAM GODDESS (Hulu) THE SUBSTITUTE (Nickelodeon) and CBS SHOWCASE.

people.name: Kylie Brakeman
people.bio: Kylie Brakeman is a comedian who has written for The Tonight Show , Dropout, and The Kids’ Tonight Show . She is known for her viral comedy videos, which earned her a spot on the “Great Performers” issue for The New York Times , as well as features in Vulture , The Atlantic , Wall Street Journal , Guardian , The Washington Post , and many more. She cohosts the improvised Hollywood roundtable podcast “Artists on Artists on Artists on Artists.” Her character comedy special “Linda Hollywood’s Big Night” can be streamed on most platforms.

people.name: Laci Mosley
people.bio: Laci Mosley is an actress and comedian who currently stars in Paramount+’s hit reboot of “iCarly” and in NBC’s new comedy series, “Lopez vs. Lopez”. Additional television credits include: “A Black Lady Sketch Show” on HBO, ”Florida Girls” on Pop, “Kenan” on NBC, “Single Parents” on ABC, “Better Call Saul” on AMC, and “Insecure” on HBO. Laci also helms her award winning podcast Scam Goddess which is dedicated to fraud and all those who practice it. Scam Goddess was awarded Best Crime Podcast at the 2022 iHeart Radio Podcast Awards in addition to winning the Webby Award (2x) for Best Crime and Justice Podcast. Scam Goddess was recently recognized by Time Magazine as a Top 10 Podcast of 2022.

people.name: Lane Kwederis
people.bio: Lane Kwederis is a Brooklyn based comedian. You might recognize her from ‘Broad City’, ‘The Late Show with Stephen Colbert’, ‘Master of None’, The Characters’ with Tim Robinson, or ‘The Rundown with Robin Thede’. She was also the standby for Baby in the first national tour and original Toronto production of ‘Dirty Dancing’ and has a BFA in musical theater from The Boston Conservatory. She is a AmSAT certified Alexander Technique Teacher and Somatic Experiencing practitioner. Book a session with her if you want your body to feel good! www.LaneKwederis.com www.SomaticEmbodying.com

people.name: Laney Serface
people.bio: Laney is an actor and writer from Vacaville, CA (707 what’s up). She does improv maybe every night of the week (needs a nap) at UCB, WGIS, and WE improv as well as on the indie circuit. She can be frequently found using the BeanBot at 9pm at The Clubhouse.

people.name: Langston Darby
people.bio: Langston Darby performs on stage, camera, and voiceover. Originally from Mississippi, he has somehow managed to survive in Philadelphia and New York. He is excited to work on character comedy with his Betty Team. Langston has also performed Off-Broadway; narrated audiobooks; voiced characters in scenes with Bugs Bunny and Daffy Duck; and a bunch of other stuff on his fiscally irresponsible path of acting.

people.name: Lani Harms
people.bio: Lani Harms is an actress, writer and comedian based in Los Angeles by way of New York, originally from Minneapolis. She has been seen on Discovery ID killing her husband (what a twist!), in national commercials, and in viral videos for Scary Mommy and The Dad. Her voice has been heard on the sketch comedy podcast These Were Humans , a Tribeca Film Festival Audio Selection, and as host of the feminist Olsen Twins podcast, They Thought I Was You . Her solo show, Reservoir Dolls: The Women of Quentin Tarantino premiered with a sold out run at the Hollywood Fringe Festival, was nominated for the Fringe Italia Off Exchange Award, a finalist for the Namba Performing Arts Splash Award, and featured in the New York Comedy Festival. She trained at The Groundlings, UCB, Moscow Art Theater School, and the Fordham Theatre Program.

people.name: Lara Dunkley
people.bio: Lara Dunkley is a rising senior at Kansas State University. She is the president-elect of On the Spot Improv, K-State’s Premier Improv team. You are most likely to find her frolicking about in the nearest bog.

people.name: Larry Dorsey Jr
people.bio: Larry Dorsey Jr. has graced the stage with the Tony award winning Broadway musical Freestyle Love Supreme (Lin-Manuel Miranda). He is a San Francisco Cali Native who has traveled the world performing/teaching improv + stand-up comedy, formerly an iHeartRadio personality, and has in some capacity worked with numerous celebrities including: Craig Robinson, Tommy Davidson, etc. to name a few. He graduated from SF State University in Creative Writing, Academy of Art University in Motion Pictures & Television, as well as the Meisner Technique Studio 2 year Acting intensive program…In his free time he creates content for social media!

people.name: Larry Saperstein
people.bio: Larry Saperstein is an actor, director, tap dancer, and writer based in LA and NY. Most notably, he can be seen as Big Red in High School Musical: The Musical: The Series on Disney+, the hit series based on the Emmy Award winning High School Musical franchise. Born and raised in New York, he is an alumni member of the prestigious Tap City Youth Ensemble, and developed his own tap dance masterclass series, available at TapWithLarry.com. He is so excited to be performing regularly on the UCB stage with Quick and Funny Musicals!

people.name: Lars Midthun
people.bio: Lars is just another dyslexic Minnesota boy with LA dreams. He is a filmmaker, turned comedian, turned actor. Catch him at UCB on LLoyd night with Perks, or online with his award winning, comedy film production company Grandpa’s Secret!

people.name: Lars Montanaro
people.bio: lars montanaro (he/him) is a multi-disciplinary performer from the California mountains. he is a member of Chicken Big, an experimental comedy troupe (UCB, iO, Annoyance, Asylum NYC, Brooklyn Comedy Collective), and worked as the director of The Poetry Brothel NYC, an immersive poet-theatrical experience. his work has been featured at La MaMa ETC, PhysFest NYC, SF Sketchfest, & Chicago’s iOFest and he has recently performed and developed projects with HERE Arts Center, En Garde Arts, ArtsEmerson & The Center at West Park.

people.name: Laura Canty-Samuel
people.bio: Laura Canty-Samuel is an award winning comedian and four-time EMMY Award-nominated writer who uses her keen observations to provide insight into culture and the realities of the human condition. She is an actress and writer for the seminal UCB NY weekend sketch team The Foundation . She also is proud of the impact of her work at Sesame Workshop, where she wrote the groundbreaking EMMY nominated short form piece Explaining Race . As an Artistic Director of NYC’s Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre, Laura uplifts voices and points of view from across the comedic spectrum.

people.name: Laura Chinn
people.bio: Laura Chinn recently wrote and directed the feature film SUNCOAST for Searchlight. She created/starred in the TV series FLORIDA GIRLS. Previous TV writing credits include: THE MICK, GRANDFATHERED, and CHILDRENS HOSPITAL. Previous acting credits include: GREY’S ANATOMY, WARRIOR, MY NAME IS EARL, and HAPPY ENDINGS. Laura trained as a writer/performer at Boom Chicago in Amsterdam and Upright Citizens Brigade in Los Angeles.

people.name: Laura de la Fuente
people.bio: Laura de la Fuente is a queer, Latin writer and actor based in Los Angeles. She was cast in the 2021 ViacomCBS Showcase and the UCB Maude sketch team Loveseat. Laura began her comedy career in Austin, TX, at ColdTowne Theater, where she performed on house improv and sketch teams including Loverboy, Martini Ranch, and Prima Doñas. She has performed at The Del Close Marathon in NYC, SF Sketchfest, and SketchFest Seattle. Laura wrote and starred in her sold-out musical solo sketch show Liz Behan: One Woman at Dusk , which she later brought to The Second City Diversity Festival in Los Angeles. You can catch her at UCB in sketch shows like Dykegeddon, a lesbian Armageddon movie parody that she created, co-wrote, and starred in, as well as in other sketch and improv shows at UCB and throughout LA.

people.name: Laura Di Lorenzo
people.bio: Originally from Venezuela and raised in Miami. She has been featured by multiple digital companies such as Mitú, Buzzfeed, WhoHaha, and Mas Mejor as a producer/ actress/ writer focusing on creating original comedic content. She also provided the Spanish voice for Selena in Netflix’s SELENA: THE SERIES. She is best known for co-creating the comedic web series “The Chonga Diaries” which led to her own comedy segment, “Laura’s Corner” for Latino-focused company Mitù.

people.name: Laura Fabius
people.bio: Laura Fabius is a Brooklyn based writer & comedian typically found satirizing politics, pop culture, and modern life. She performs with Young Douglas, a sketch group formed by Upright Citizens Brigade’s Maude Night, and direct sketch teams & solo-shows throughout the city. Credits include The Onion, Reductress.

people.name: Laura Willcox
people.bio: Laura is a writer, actor, and improviser living in beautiful, affordable Los Angeles, America. She recently wrote A Little Late with Lilly Singh (NBC), The Jim Jefferies Show (Comedy Central), and Kevin Hart & Keenan Thompson’s Back That Year Up comedy special (Peacock.) She co-wrote a live action comedy feature for Nickelodeon with her writing (and life) partner Don Fanelli. They’ve also collaborated on a couple kids if you wanna babysit.

people.name: Lauren Adams
people.bio: Lauren Adams is an actor, director and writer. You may recognize her from TV shows like Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt, Modern Family, Special, and Deadbeat or feature films like Greener Grass and The Disruptors. Lauren has a BFA from Elon University, and has performed regularly at The UCB Theatres in New York and Los Angeles since 2007. Lauren has directed countless plays, sketch shows, and the short film Animal, starring Cathy Curtin. She lives in LA with her husband Seth and their perfect dog Bear.

people.name: Lauren Arnett
people.bio: Lauren is a comedian, writer, and director based in New York City. She performs improv regularly at UCB and the Brooklyn Comedy Collective in shows such as The Trial, Boldly The Show, and Morgan & Morgan & Morgan etc. Lauren also co-created the boundary-pushing and impactful webseries Oddballs.

people.name: Lauren Brickman
people.bio: Lauren Brickman is a Brooklyn based performer, writer, and stage director whose credits include FBI: Most Wanted, Bull, Alterlatino, Gloria Bell, Lisa Lampanelli’s Stuffed, and more! When not performing or creating Lauren can be found, most likely, teaching in the CUNY system or at NYFA where she’s the Associate Chair of Liberal Arts & Sciences.

people.name: Lauren Coggiola
people.bio: Hailing from Plano, Texas, Lauren is a filmmaker, focusing on comedic narratives that inspire conversation around larger concepts, especially helping shift the narrative around psychedelics. She is absolutely THRILLED to be making her UCB debut with Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Lauren Holt
people.bio: Lauren Holt is an actor, writer, and comedian from Charlotte, NC. She got her BFA in Fine Art at UNC Greensboro, and decided to do absolutely nothing with her degree and move to LA to become a comedian. After her time on UCB’s improv, sketch & character house teams, she got the call from Lorne to join the cast of Saturday Night Live in 2020… which was such a great year! Nothing crazy going on in the world or ANYTHING! After SNL she’s gone on to have roles in Film/TV projects such as Barbie , You’re Cordially Invited , Curb Your Enthusiasm & Pixar’s Dream Productions . Currently you can watch her alongside Rachel Sennott & Leighton Meester on HBO’s I Love LA .

people.name: Lauren Killer
people.bio: Lauren Killer is a writer-actor-comedian-multihyphenate who is so excited to be performing on the UCB Franklin stage.

people.name: Lauren Knutti
people.bio: Lauren Knutti is so flattered you asked! Knutti (Canoe like the boat, Tea like the gossip) is a beautiful, famous Actor-Writer-Comedian in Los Angeles by way of Columbus and Austin. She enjoys making short films, petting her mini schnauzers, and receiving external validation.

people.name: Lauren Lapkus
people.bio: Lauren is an Emmy-nominated and SAG award-winning comedian best known for her roles in Orange is the New Black, Jurassic World, The Big Bang Theory, and The Wrong Missy.

people.name: Lauren Raff
people.bio: Lauren Raff is a poised actor. She has a BFA in Theatre and Performance from Emerson College, note that the F stands for “fine”. There she met her best friends and creative partners with whom she went on to form, “Same Great Taste”, an LA based comedy sketch troupe. Lauren is currently seeking representation and more friends who didn’t go to her college.
