people.name: Lauren Scharf
people.bio: Lauren Scharf is a writer, performer, and stand-up from Boulder, Colorado. She got her start at Second City Chicago and has since been on teams at The Ruby, The Pack, and iO West (RIP). You can find her on everything at @LLcoolScharf.

people.name: Laurie Magers
people.bio: Laurie Magers is a Filipino-American writer, actor, and director proudly hailing from the San Francisco Bay Area. She’s a former competitive poet and has been working in TV/Film for over 10 years. She wrote and appeared in Netflix’s Obliterated as well as the upcoming Netflix animated series Mating Season . She has also worked on projects at CBS, AppleTV+, Amazon Prime, VH1, Disney+, Comedy Central Digital, Roku, and more.

people.name: Lawrence Marshalian
people.bio: Lover of theater, improv comedy, sketch, voice-over, and caffeine-fueled writing sessions.

people.name: Leah Knauer
people.bio: Leah is a multi-talented musical comedic powerhouse, dazzling all audiences with her quick wit & sparkling sass. Improv, stand-up, impressions, & musical theater – there’s nothing this Philly bish can’t do. Leah often goes viral on TikTok for her hilarious celebrity impressions.

people.name: Leanne Velednitsky
people.bio: Leanne Velednitsky is a bi-coastal actor, comedian, writer, creator of things, dancer, clown, producer, and owner of a waterproof watch. Recently she premiered her short film Bike Thief on NoBudge, and is performing her solo show Exhaustion Power Hour at The Elysian. She co-hosts Big Sour Hour, LA’s best hang, and Backyard Show at Brooklyn’s premiere comedy venue Union Hall. Leanne is happy you’re here! Thank you for supporting live theater in the land of film. It’s all we have left.

people.name: Leila Nematzadeh
people.bio: Leila Nematzadeh is a comedy writer from Los Angeles, who writes for UCB’s Maude team, Detention!!! Her work has been performed at UCB, Magnet Theater and on projects for NBC.

people.name: Lena Skeele
people.bio: Lena Skeele (she/they) is an actor, comedian, and writer living in Manhattan with her husband and two cat daughters. When she isn’t doing improv comedy, she can be found performing in her friends’ weird plays, singing at jams throughout the city, and writing musicals. We are!

people.name: Lennon Parham
people.bio: Lennon Parham is an actor, writer, director and body roll queen. You might have seen her work on Minx, Somebody Somewhere, Playing House, Veep, The House or heard her dulcet tones on Womp It Up!

people.name: Leonard Smith Jr.
people.bio: Leonard Smith Jr. is a stand-up comedian, actor, and improviser. Catch him on Harold Night and every month with Lemon Pepper Wet.

people.name: Lesley Osuala
people.bio: Lesley Osuala is a Nigerian American actor, writer, and stand-up comedian currently performing hilarious characters on Betty Night! His comedy credits include Comedy Central Digital and Bleacher Report. In 2017 he was a finalist in the NBC Universal/UCB Diversity Showcase. He is also a proud alum of Maude Night at UCBTNY!

people.name: Lesley Tsina
people.bio: Lesley Tsina is a writer, actor and baking afficionado from Palo Alto, CA. She’s written for Warner Bros Animation and also appeared on Black-ish , Community , Conan , and many commercials. She’s an alum of Harold teams Hugboat and The Galleria, Maude teams Paddington and Extreme Tambourine. She currently teaches sketch writing at UCB-LA and directs Sketch Cram.

people.name: Leslie Meisel Ellis
people.bio: Leslie is a teacher, actor, writer, director, producer, and a long time Kenny Loggins fan. She has performed with UCB on Harold and Maude teams while also performing on stage in a few sketch and one person shows. You might have seen her other work on the UCB stage or on HBO, The Other Two, Bachelorette, Orange Is The New Black, 30 Rock and more.

people.name: Levi Collins
people.bio: Levi Collins is a improvisor and actor based in Brooklyn NY originally from Seattle WA. He recently claimed 9 consecutive victory’s in UCB’s Cagematch with his improv trio SLOP. Catch him performing at BCC, UCB, the PIT, and all over the city! and he’s gonna try stand up…soon.

people.name: Levi Friedman
people.bio: Levi grew up in Seattle and lives in New York. Besides doing comedy he makes comics and works in a museum.

people.name: Levi Penley
people.bio: Levi is an actor/writer and proud member of Cereal Mascots! Other projects include: @thedrunktexts @tornalfredo @texasseesaw @brodywentmissing

people.name: Lexi Lewis
people.bio: Lexi Lewis is a producer, writer, songwriter and the founder of Magic String Productions. Catch her at UCB in Sing Out, Louise! or hosting The Understudies: One Night Only on the indie circuit—when she’s not busy making your guilty-pleasure romcoms.

people.name: Lexie Elton Skye
people.bio: Lexie Elton Skye is an LA based comedian who performs on stages all around LA theaters including WGIS, The Pack, The Clubhouse, The Broadwater, The Stray, The Shared Experience, and, of course, UCB. She is also a show producer who hosts and runs shows such as “Revolution 2 Presents: Improv To Save Music” and “Nepo Mommy for Dummies” at multiple theaters. Lexie took on the position of Stage Manager at The Elysian Theater and Dynasty Typewriter for “Puck Bunnies: A Heated Rivalry Drag Musical” which had back to back sold out runs. You can find her performing on her improv teams Nepo Mommy and Revolution 2 as well as on various mashup teams and festival lineups.

people.name: Leysan Khabibullina
people.bio: Leysan is an LA-based Tatar-American comedian. Leysan immigrated to the United States all on her own as a teenager because she was tired of walking through snow to get to school. After eventually getting her Master’s in International Commerce and Policy in Virginia, Leysan worked for the Department of State and then took the next logical step to start doing comedy. In her free time, Leysan tries to make her cat Sour Cream love her, drinks way too much iced coffee, and kills plants.

people.name: Liam Clancy
people.bio: Liam is an improviser and data scientist originally from the San Francisco bay area. Liam regularly performs improv across LA with numerous teams including his long form narrative team “Syndicated Broadcast,” as a member of the Westside Resident team duo “The Park,” and as a member of the Improv for The People Main Company. He graduated from the University of Southern California with degrees in Neuroscience and Economics.

people.name: Libby Dorot
people.bio: Performer and musician, Libby is a spicy Gemini from our nation’s capital. She loves musical improv, big characters, horror flicks, and combining all 3!

people.name: Lida Darmian
people.bio: Lida Darmian (she/her) is an actor/comedian/clown/writer/ podcaster/master-of-slashes from LA, but based in NYC. She performs with the Maude Team Pamela and helps run the Diversity Jam. She was previously on the Harold team babygirl, and the Maude teams DumpTruck, BedRock, Night Eaters, Archnemesis. She was an artist in residence at Ars Nova CAMP comedy residency and created One Clown show. She holds a BFA in Drama from NYU’s Tisch School of the Arts, and has trained in improv, sketch, and clowning at UCB, Annoyance, Magnet Theater, BCC, and with Chris Bayes & Ècole Philippe Gaulier. You may have seen her on NBC, IFC Shorts, the Tribeca Film Festival, or a random commercial.

people.name: Lilah Taber
people.bio: Lilah Taber is a Chicago-born, Los Angeles-based TV, video game, and sketch comedy writer & director. She is currently a dialogue writer for an upcoming title at ROCKSTAR GAMES, and has worked in the writers rooms of FRASIER, YOUNG ROCK, and DINNER WITH THE PARENTS, among others. She wrote for Maude Night from 2019 to 2025. You can catch her as a writer for ASSSSKETCH, in comedy festivals with her indie team Tracksuit, or in your arms if you ever see her trip and fall.

people.name: Lilliana Winkworth
people.bio: Lilliana Winkworth is a former member of The Second City’s National Touring Company (Chicago) where she performed sketch and improv comedy on The Second City’s Mainstage, ETC stage, and stages all across the country. Her comedy has appeared on Adult Swim, Funny Or Die, and she’s worked as an Animation Story Writer at Nick Jr. Born and raised in LA, she’s thrilled to be back in her hometown to finally become the child star her mom always wanted her to be.

people.name: Lily Du
people.bio: Lily Du is an actor and writer who got her start at the UCB Theater in New York, where she performed on Harold Night and with the UCB Touring company. Lily currently hosts the show “Dirty Laundry” on Dropout. She was previously a cast-member at CollegeHumor. As an actor, she has appeared on shows such as as “New Amsterdam,” “Broad City,” “FBI,” and “Happy!” Lily grew up in Rockville, MD, and is a Smith College alum.

people.name: Lindsay Ames
people.bio: Award winning Canadian comedian, writer, host and producer, Lindsay Ames has been seen on the stage and screen on Netflix, Hulu, Comedy Central and maany more. When she’s not touring or putting up sold out shows like Make That Sandwich, My Diary or Moonlit Malibu she’s making the best sandwiches on planet earth.

people.name: Lindsay Calleran
people.bio: Lindsay Calleran is a filmmaker and improviser based in Brooklyn. Her award-winning short films have played at festival across the U.S. and her debut feature, CAITY, is supported by Sundance, OUTFEST, The Gotham & more, and is currently in post-production. Lindsay has been improvising since [redacted] and swears she is bad at it.

people.name: Lindsay-Elizabeth Hand
people.bio: Lindsay-Elizabeth Hand is a proud SAG-AFTRA member, a NY Emmy-Nominated producer three times over, formerly on the nominating committee for the SAG Awards, an award-winning screenwriter, and a mother to two human children. “Played with precision, expertise, humor and love by Lindsay-Elizabeth Hand.” – Front Row Center Review of Cherry Lane Theatre’s How We Love/Fuck

people.name: Lindsey Ford
people.bio: Lindsey is just coming off of a stint as a series regular on The Late Late Show and is looking forward to the fame and fortune that is surely to follow.

people.name: Lindsey Leonard
people.bio: Originally from Anchorage Alaska, Lindsey has a BFA in Theatre from Cornish College of the Arts. She is a former member of repertory Chekhov company The Seagull Project and current member of improv groups Anne Rice, Day Trip, and God’s favorite improv team Mercy Santos.

people.name: Lindsey Mallard
people.bio: Lindsey is a Groundlings Sunday Company Alumnus, and studied and performed at Second City Chicago, iO Chicago, The Annoyance, Groundlings and UCB! You can catch her on season two of Grand Crew and she has amazing things coming your way!

people.name: Linzy Beltran
people.bio: Linzy Beltran (she/her) is a Salvadoran-American actor, clown and comedy writer from Houston Texas. After surviving an ultra-conservative Pentecostal upbringing (think Footloose, but more latine people), she packed her bags and moved to Los Angeles in 2022, Linzy originated the lead role of Abril in the play, El Paso at the Hollywood Fringe Festival (2023); premiered her solo show, Linzy with a Z at the Second City Diversity in Comedy Festival (2019); and was one of ten hand-picked writers for the Disney-NHMC Series Screenwriters Fellowship (2021). In her spare time, Linzy works a full-time job, is a loving dog mother and cries daily (good thing!).

people.name: Lionel Ward
people.bio: Lionel Ward is a native of Ashland, Oregon, home to the renowned Oregon Shakespeare Festival, where he began performing onstage at just eight years old. He is currently entering his final year as a Dramatic Arts major at University of Southern California, continuing to refine his craft across stage and screen. In addition to his work as a writer and performer with USC’s oldest improvisational comedy troupe, Commedus Interruptus, Lionel is actively involved in a range of student-produced short films, building a versatile body of work that reflects both his comedic instincts and dramatic training.

people.name: Lisa Albano
people.bio: Lisa Albano is a comedian, writer, and actor based in New York. With a degree in International Relations and zero desire to work for the government, she moved to Chicago where she performed improv and sketch at various theaters including iO Chicago and the Second City Conservatory. She has enjoyed successful runs of her sketch shows at iO Chicago, with multiple nominations for “Best Scripted Show” at the Del Awards. Nowadays, you can find Lisa performing in New York with her improv team The Good Time, at The Second City NY, and with her UCB Betty team.

people.name: Lisa Gilroy
people.bio: Recently named one of Vulture’s “Comedians You Should and Will Know,” Lisa Gilroy is a two-time Canadian Comedy Award winner from Alberta, Canada. Known for Comedy Bang Bang! (Earwolf), @fter Midnight (CBS), Jury Duty (Amazon) and DropoutTV.

people.name: Lissette Jean-Marie
people.bio: Lissette is a writer and improv comedian, born in Los Angeles. In years past you might have caught her in various narrative improvised plays paying homage to everything from Jane Austen to Star Trek: The Next Generation . She also directed a run of improvised teen romcoms called Almost Adults. Catch her performing Shakespeare in bars with the wonderful Shakes on the Rocks. She’s thrilled to join the UCB ranks as part of Sing Out, Lousie!

people.name: Liv Drury
people.bio: Liv Drury is a Brooklyn-based writer, actor, and comedian. Their sketch duo, Boy Crazy, was featured as a “thing to do in NYC” (woo) at New York Comedy Fest 2025. Liv has also written for ASSSSketch and the Maude teams, Baby Todd and Pamela. Check out more of their work at Livdrury.com

people.name: Liz Arcury
people.bio: Liz Arcury is a writer and comedian living in New York City. She has written for Reductress, McSweeney’s, Marie Claire, Refinery29, The Daily Beast, The AV Club, and more. Her horror comedy screenplay “Slay Me” won Script Pipeline’s 2022 contest and her memoiric essay collection “Text Me Back” has been signed for representation. Liz has three rescue cats.

people.name: Liz Butler
people.bio: Liz Butler (she/they) has been performing improv since 2013 and performing improv in New York since 2024. Before that she was in New Zealand also performing improv. She was nominated for The Most Promising Emerging Artist Award at the 2021 NZ Fringe Festival. Sounds fake right? It’s true. Nobody lies in these things. Liz plays with Wet on UCB’s Lloyd Night, as well as Swingers, Happy to Be Here, and GUPS.

people.name: Liz Fitzgerald
people.bio: Liz Fitzgerald is a writer/performer who’s played with house ensembles at iO Chicago and across numerous improv theaters in Los Angeles. She was awarded a Harold Ramis Film Program fellowship at The Second City in recognition of her writing. Her work has appeared on stages like the Elysian and in publications such as Reductress, and the projects she’s directed have earned honors at the Women’s Comedy Film Festival Atlanta, the Chicago Film Festival, and others. She’s stoked to be performing at UCB!

people.name: Liz Labacz
people.bio: Liz Labacz is an improvisor, writer, and frazzled newish mom in Pittsburgh. Her comedy home is Arcade Comedy Theater where she has at various times been a teacher, performer, wall painter, producers, board member, and toilet cleaner.

people.name: Liz Leimkuhler
people.bio: Liz Leimkuhler is an actor, comedian, musician, and sculptor living in Brooklyn. TV: American Horror Story (FX), The Gilded Age (HBO). MFA: NYU.

people.name: Liz Noth
people.bio: Liz Noth is an actor, stand up comedian, producer and writer based in Los Angeles. Most importantly she is a member of the two most beloved indie improv teams of all time- Wicked City and Warren.

people.name: Logan lasiter
people.bio: Logan is an Austin-based actor and improvisor. She’s been in several plays and musicals in Texas, and has a special knack for singing. She went to school for business and tried a few grown up jobs, but it sucked so she’s sticking to performing and hoping for the best.

people.name: Logan Reeder
people.bio: Logan is an actor, singer, dancer, improviser, writer, comedienne, and yes… a natural blonde. Originally from Pensacola, Florida (hence her southern charm), she has a BFA in Musical Theater and a minor in Dance from Otterbein University. She can be seeing performing improv, musical theater and sketch at venues across NYC and the country- including frequent appearances on FINAL CALLBACK and LLOYD NIGHT at UCB!

people.name: Lola Davis
people.bio: Lola Davis is an improviser and standup comedian based in Brooklyn, NY. She likes her coffee brewed fresh, then cooled to “kids temp,” then re-heated with scalding goat’s milk. Her assistant is desperately seeking work as an assistant to literally anyone else – please contact me at gakukelliherffkdgh-g-g—hl—grgj33%%%^^t/NOOOOOO

people.name: Londale Theus Jr
people.bio: Londale Theus Jr. is an actor, comedian & comedy teacher from Los Angeles. After a knee injury ended his Division 1 College Basketball career, his interest in comedy & performing led him to CSU Long Beach’s Theatre Program. After finishing his two years in the program, he began working as a full-time actor in TV (HBO’s Insecure , CBS’s 2 Broke Girls , HULU’s Future Man , TBS’s Conan ), Film (HULU’s Greener Grass ), and National Commercials (State Farm, Wayfair). He’s been doing stand up and improv since 2012. His first one-hour stand-up special, “BIGGER, BLACKER, VEGAN-ER,” was recorded at UCB and released on YouTube in 2024. He’s been on Harold Night (Rococo), Maude Night (Bombardier), Weekend Teams (Benetton) and currently improvises with ASSSSCAT and Friday Block Party (3rd Fridays). He teaches Intro to Stand Up and Advanced Stand Up at UCB and hosts Mic It Up! Every 1st, 2nd, & 3rd Tuesday at 7 p.m., where comedians can workshop their 5-minute stand up sets. Follow him on IG @LondaleJr for comedy tips, joke breakdowns and other lessons.

people.name: Lorena Russi
people.bio: Lorena Russi is a failed pro soccer player turned comedian, filmmaker and educator. Most recently, Lorena was honored as an IMDB emerging filmmaker of the year, a featured stand up for Comedy Central and “Netflix is a Joke Fest,” and a writer/performer for CBS Showcase. Lorena is a Queens native and is, in fact, “walkin’ here.” In a past life, she almost pursued a PhD in Media & Gender Studies (true), but instead opted for a stint as a pro soccer player (also true). And somehow, she’s straight (this is a lie). Lorena’s work is heavily influenced by her Colombian immigrant guilt and she apologizes in advance.

people.name: Loring Griggs
people.bio: Loring Griggs is an actor, comedian, and musician from Westerly, Rhode Island. He studied acting at the Stella Adler Studio of Acting in New York City (aka The Big Apple.) He can currently be seen on the improv teams Sorry, I’m in London (UCB Lloyd Night), Pleather (Pack Theater), and he was proudly NOT at January 6th.

people.name: Lou Gonzalez Jr
people.bio: Lou Gonzalez Jr (all pronouns) is a comedian born and raised in the Bronx. They have been in some tv shows and movies (Black Bear, The Other Two, Search Party) but they mostly perform around NYC doing improv and standup.

people.name: Lou Wilson
people.bio: Lou Wilson is a gentle man who lives in LA and has performed comedy from coast to coast. He currently performs with former UCB Harold Team, now weekend team- YETI. Outside of UCB, you can see Lou in GO90’s TWEENFEST as Family Guy Impressionist/Vape Trick Artist Dusty Delgrosso, and on The Guest Book, which is coming to TBS February 2017. Also Jimmy Kimmel Live Sometimes.

people.name: Louis Birdsong
people.bio: Louis Birdsong is a ISA certified arborist who does improv comedy. Louis is an honest 5’ 7” and performs with Fuck This Week every Monday at Fallout Theater in downtown Austin, Texas.

people.name: Lucy Boisvert
people.bio: Lucy Boisvert (she/her) is a Brooklyn-based writer, improviser, musician, and filmmaker. She writes for her Maude team, Reunion Tour, and writes and performs in Chimp Cocktail, the PIT’s Best New Sketch Show of 2024, now running off-Broadway. She has trained with UCB, BCC, and The Second City Conservatory. The lead singer of the jazz-pop band Aunt Judy, she is, contrary to popular belief, capable of being earnest. She hopes to keep making people laugh at a near-compulsive pace until she inevitably dies—hopefully of natural causes.

people.name: Lucy Hamilburg
people.bio: Lucy is a born-and-raised Angeleno, former middle school teacher, actor, improviser, sketch performer, and lifelong silly gal. She performs monthly at the UCB Annex with her indie team The Craft at their show The Craftmando, and with her Harold team Paltonica at WGIS. Most importantly, she loves a wig.

people.name: Lucy Porter
people.bio: Lucy Porter is a writer, comedian, and gal with extraordinarily wide thumbs. She performs live regularly on Harold Night at UCB, around LA with her signature cabaret-style stand-up, and socially performs every day on Instagram Stories.

people.name: Luis Jimenez
people.bio: Actor, Lover, Man-Bear

people.name: Luke Brown
people.bio: Luke started improv in 2019 at Monkey Toast in London and has since trained at the FA and performed all across Europe. He plays with high levels of commitment, emotion, joy, and chaos. He is also currently riding the high of Arsenal winning the league for the first time in 22 years.

people.name: Luke Clements
people.bio: Catch Luke doing improv, clown, and dumb bits all over the city!

people.name: Luke Halferty
people.bio: Luke is an actor originally from Pittsburgh who has worked with theaters around the country, and has been running his various schemes and lying to people about liking avocado in New York since 2023. He holds both a BFA and an MFA in acting, which is, frankly, excessive, right? He is repped by BlocNYC and will do nudity if you allow him—but don’t.

people.name: Luke Maynus
people.bio: Luke Maynus is a brooklyn based comedian and multi-hyphenate. Originally from West Virginia, Luke yearns for the mines. Catch him performing with his improv team Blumpkin and sketch team Burnt Toast all around this big beautiful world.

people.name: Luke Moran
people.bio: Luke Keali’ipono Moran is an AAPI writer, comedian and musician hailing from the mean streets of Alameda, CA (just like Jim Morrison). Luke got his start at UCB as a writer for Maude teams Somebody’s Baby, Duchess and Don’t Be A Stranger, and has since written for the likes of The Onion, Comedy Central, Studio C as well as some musicians you may have heard of. He’s a UCLA grad (also like Jim Morrison). These days Luke spends his time looking for the Daniel Scheinert to his Daniel Kwan and hosting a semi-regular variety show where he dresses up like Johnny Carson (Jim Morrison could never).

people.name: Luke Wink-Moran
people.bio: Luke Wink-Moran is a writer, actor, and comedian based in Los Angeles He’s also a photographer and videographer who covers the LA comedy scene.

people.name: Lydia Hensler
people.bio: Lydia Hensler is a 20+ year veteran improv performer, writer and teacher. She’s best known for her work at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade Theatres in NYC and LA. Her credits include ASSSSCAT 3000, Grandma’s Ashes, Take It Personal: The Hip Hop Improv Show, The Female Gaze, Characters Welcome, Teacher’s Lounge, and Bastian. Lydia has also taught and performed improv and characters with theatre communities across the U.K. and Europe.

people.name: Lyndsey Frank
people.bio: Lyndsey Frank is the greatest comedian and actor of all time.

people.name: Mackenzie Hunt
people.bio: Mackenzie Hunt is thrilled to be making her Broadway debut at Maude Night! Mackenzie is a writer, performer, and speech language pathologist specializing in voice and pediatric communication disorders. Huge thanks to her teachers, friends, and family for always believing in her and responding positively when she consistently asks, “Was that ok? Did that suck? Are you mad at me?” And THANK YOU UCB!!! *Some of my very favorite nights have been spent in this theater.

people.name: Macy McGrail
people.bio: Macy McGrail is a comedian, actor, and native New Yorker. Most recently, she performed stand-up, sketch, improv, and more in AsylumNYC’s Outlaugh Season 3, where she finished as the competition’s runner-up. She can also be seen performing improv at venues across New York City with the indie teams COURSE NOT! and Blumpkin. In addition to her comedy work, Macy is currently appearing off-Broadway as Katie McDaniels in the drama Pied à Terre.

people.name: Maddi Roy
people.bio: Maddi is a comedy writer originally from Maine, which some people apparently don’t even know is a state. She is one of the four talented people from there, alongside Patrick Dempsey, Anna Kendrick and Stephen King. She has significantly less money and power than the aforementioned people, but her primary goal is to one day topple them and become the best person from Maine. Being part of the UCB is an invaluable 8th step in this 746-step plan!

people.name: Maddie Cordovano
people.bio: Maddie is a Chicago trained improvisor who has been terrorizing stages across the country for decades. After some time in LA she is happily doing her wild and fun comedy in Austin where she teaches, performs, and loves her teammates.

people.name: Maddy Casale
people.bio: Maddy Casale is a writer-performer from Pittsburgh, PA, who is now based in Chicago. In addition to taking the stage at The iO Theater, Annoyance Theatre, and Second City in Chicago, she has performed in Australia’s 2019 Melbourne International Comedy Festival and interned for The Chris Gethard Show in 2018. For more, follow her on Instagram and TikTok @maddycasale, read her opinions of Hallmark movies at decider.com, or visit her website maddycasale.com.

people.name: Maddy Lenny
people.bio: Maddy Lenny is an improviser, writer, and actor with formal training from UCB and Improv Asylum. She is currently a member of PIT Ensembles and several indie teams, including Granny Fully Loaded, Honeymoon Stage, and Psyched Out. Originally from Boston, she has performed at theaters across the East Coast and has been featured at festivals including the Boom Chicago Comedy Festival, the Edinburgh International Improv Festival, the Boston Fringe, and more.

people.name: Madeleine Lloyd-Davies
people.bio: Software engineer, two-time winner of her WNBA fantasy league, improvisor.

people.name: Madeleine Paul
people.bio: Madeleine Paul is a writer/producer who was raised in the Chicago suburbs before going to school in NYC and later moving to Los Angeles, where she currently resides with her wonderful partner and their unbelievably adorable dog (*dog photos available for viewing upon request). Her feature screenplay CHICAGO FOR ONE appeared on the 2020 Annual Black List, and she currently writes on the Maude team, Town Crier.

people.name: Madeline Cook
people.bio: Madeline Cook is an actor, comedian, and podcast host from Mount Horeb, Wisconsin. She can be seen in the LA-based improv group Cuter in Person, or at UCB’s Lloyd Night. She spends her free time watching The Andy Griffith Show and cross stitching.

people.name: Madelyn MacBryde
people.bio: Madelyn MacBryde is a comedian, visual artist and singer/songwriter from Connecticut. She performs regularly at the Sea Tea Comedy Theater in Hartford as a house team member on Campfire Dragons and Robot Love. Madelyn also loves NYC and performing with her friends here!

people.name: Madelynne Whitt
people.bio: Madelynne Whitt is a writer, comedian, and singer born and raised in the city that never sleeps… Oxnard, California. She studied musical theater and moved to Los Angeles with the hopes to work a minimum wage job and never be able to afford rent for the rest of her life.

people.name: Madison Dahm
people.bio: Madison Dahm is a comedian, actor, and writer as well as a proud UCLA alum. She does standup all around LA, and performs with “unHINGEd” (the improvised musical rom com) and with her indie team Friday Friday. You can catch her doing musical improv at UCB in “Sing Out, Louise!”

people.name: Mae Molina
people.bio: Mae Molina is a Puerto Rican actress with a passion for the stage and screen. She enjoys playing both comedic and dramatic roles, blending playful spontaneity with emotional depth. Have fun and enjoy the festival, WEPA!

people.name: Maggie Carey
people.bio: Maggie Carey has directed acclaimed comedies including The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins, Adults, The Sex Lives of College Girls, Barry, Silicon Valley, Never Have I Ever, Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt, Twisted Metal, and Brooklyn Nine-Nine. She got her start in comedy with the UCB New York Harold team Bastian, which formed on January 23, 2007, and went on to become one of the theater’s most legendary and longest-running teams — according to The Internet.

people.name: Maggie Carr
people.bio: Originally an actress working primarily in theatre, Maggie pivoted to comedy for the stability. “Escape at Dannemora”, “The Deuce”, and “The Equalizer” are all shows Maggie had callbacks for and then didn’t book. Alum of the BoogieManja sketch team Big Apartment (Toronto Sketch Fest 2024) and former host/producer of I’ve Got Notes at QED in Astoria, where she lives.

people.name: Mahayla Laurence
people.bio: Mahayla Laurence (they/she) is a Colorado-raised, Brooklyn-based multidisciplinary artist, theatermaker, and educator with a penchant for genre-expansive work that seeks to generate action toward reciprocal stewardship of our communities and selves. In addition to performing and obsessing over plants, Mahayla regularly facilitates creative development programs and directs independent theater projects all over NYC, teaches playwriting to K-5th graders through Chautauqua Institution’s Young Playwrights Project, and seasonally roams the country with The Story Pirates Tour. Recent work has been seen: Ars Nova, HERE Arts, The Bushwick Starr, Space at Irondale, Radio City Music Hall, Clubbed Thumb, The Brick, & Smith Street Stage. Mahayla is also a certified breathwork practitioner and an uncertified nerd. www.mahaylalaurence.com

people.name: Mahyn
people.bio: Mahyn is an actor, comedian and writer from Mississauga, Canada. You may have seen her performing improv & sketch around Los Angeles. Or in a commercial on TV, or as a robot in a viral short…or on Netflix as the voice of Veronica in The Archies …or maybe even crying in a national park whilst admiring the beauty of nature.

people.name: Makena McElroy
people.bio: Makena is an NYC-based actor and comedian. A 2021 graduate of NYU Tisch, she can be seen performing original musical sketch with her writing partner Owen O. Makena also recently was seen in the new musical “Operation: Destroy Beff Jezos” at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival.

people.name: Malika Ray
people.bio: Malika Ray (she/her) is an Indian-American writer/performer based in New York City. She is the winner of the 2024 UCB X SNL Diversity Scholarship Program as well as a writer on Maude Night at UCBNY. An alumni of UCBNY’s improv, character, and sketch writing programs, she is currently creating comedic art that amplifies the South Asian experience. She is seeking representation.

people.name: Malin von Euler-Hogan
people.bio: Malin von Euler-Hogan is a writer and performer living in Los Angeles. She is the co-host of Riding Around, an improvised weekly recap podcast for the beloved (fictional) 2000s sitcom of the same name, and you can also see her perform at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre with her team PONY. She has appeared in sketches for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, College Humor, Above Average, and Funny or Die, written for Reductress, guested on some fun podcasts, and acted in some commercials. Most recently she co-wrote and starred in the Vimeo Staff Pick short film I Am In Control. Malin is originally from North Carolina.

people.name: Mandy Ranero
people.bio: Mandy Ranero is a Brooklyn-based improviser, writer and actor. She can be seen performing at UCB in The Trial, The Building and with her indie teams Corner Bitchstro and Hoopty.

people.name: Mano Agapion
people.bio: Mano Agapion is a queer brown comedian from North Carolina who found out he was a secret love child at the age of 20…. I know, yikes! She’s a TV writer who sold tv shows, written for sitcoms, and even directed, hinny. She gets messy on podcasts like Drag Her and We Love Trash with Betsy Sodaro. She even does some disgusting live drag at Bad Drag Race at Dynasty Typewriter. You can see Mano performing with Benetton, QueerWorld, and Winslow at UCB.

people.name: Marc Mendoza
people.bio: Marc Mendoza is a Bolivian-American actor, writer & comedian based in Los Angeles. Marc has appeared at the San Francisco Sketchfest, ImprovCon, Del Close Marathon, San Diego Fringe Festival, & Orange County Improv Festival. You can catch him performing regularly with the improv group Rat City & producing live comedy shows such as The Prank Call Show, Creature Feature, & Psychic Medium Show.

people.name: Marc-Sully Saint-Fleur
people.bio: Marc-Sully Saint-Fleur is a Haitian-American actor, comedian, stand-up, singer and writer that hails from Miami, FL. They are an alumni of Maude Night and has performed at UCB LA since 2019. They can be seen on Home Economics (ABC), Curb Your Enthusiasm (HBO), The Good Place (NBC) and Space Force (Netflix) along with national commercials for Geico and Aldi. They were a Writer/Performer in the 2019 CBS Diversity Showcase.

people.name: Marcelle Pallais
people.bio: Marcelle was born in Peru and grew up in Fargo, North Dakota. She may be a former model but she’s also quite stupid. She could mention her many credits but her mom only remembers when she was Two-Bit in her 8th grade production of The Outsiders.

people.name: Marco Torres
people.bio: Marco Torres is a Mexican-American producer and comedian living in Los Angeles. Marco had a humble start in San Diego first learning improv at National Comedy Theater and has gone on to perform at the San Francisco Sketchfest, ImprovCon, Del Close Marathon, San Diego Fringe Festival, & Orange County Improv Festival. You can catch him performing regularly with the improv group Rat City & Revolution 2, producing live comedy shows such as The Prank Call Show & Revolution 2 Presents: Improv to Save Music

people.name: Marcos Gonzalez
people.bio: Marcos Gonzalez is a Television Writer for NBC’s reboot of Saved by the Bell and Amazon Freevee’s Jury Duty. He can be found performing for Comedy Central, Funny or Die, and live at UCB. As well as character showcases across town.

people.name: Marcus Folmar
people.bio: Currently in his 3rd Harold Season, Marcus has appeared in over 80 TV shows, films and commercials yet somehow is still best recognized for his 2012 portrayal of one-hit-wonder Dr. Rhapsody, the fictitious artist behind “5 Fingaz To The Face” on Nickelodeon’s VICTORIOUS. Marcus wrote and co-directed THE FALLEN a music driven feature currently streaming on AMC owned ALLBLK and has another feature he co-wrote set to be released summer 2023. Looking for a laugh stop by alleywayseries.com and check out Marcus’ web series ALLEY WAY, 2020 winner at SeriesFest for Best Comedy.

people.name: Marcus Hunter
people.bio: Marcus is an improviser who can be found performing around New York with Gups, Worst Child, Ghost Stories and more. This bio is not legal advice. By reading this we have definitely not formed an attorney-client relationship, so stop asking.

people.name: Marcy Jarreau
people.bio: Marcy Jarreau is a writer/actor/comedian based in Los Angeles. Marcy was a writer on BROOKLYN NINE-NINE. She is currently the Co-Host of three comedy podcasts: “L is for Losers”, “A Funny Feeling”, and “90 Day Bae” hosted with Nicole Byer. Marcy has been performing improv for literal decades, most recently in her monthly show at UCB Franklin with House Team “Search History”.

people.name: Margot Krauss
people.bio: Margot Krauss is a comedian, writer, and actor born and raised in New York City. She holds a double BFA from NYU Tisch in Drama and Dramatic Writing and performs in monthly sketch comedy, musical comedy, and improv shows at Brooklyn Comedy Collective, The PIT, Caveat and more. She won “Best Play” for her play Houselives produced by the Gene Frankel Theater this December. Her writing has been showcased at Radio City Music Hall, The Mezzanine Theater, and The Alab Festival, various short films and formal readings of her television pilot scripts.

people.name: Maria Fernanda Diaz
people.bio: Colombian improviser with years of experience, member of the cast at El Quinto Pulgar Theatre, and performer in improv events across Bogotá. Passionate about improv, joy, and human connection.

people.name: Maria Nally
people.bio: Maria Nally (she/her) is an improviser, coach, and actor who has been performing in New York City for several years. In 2025, she made her Off-Broadway debut in The Others of Oz: The Improvised Musical, and performs musical improv regularly at The Magnet Theater with her house team Houndstooth. Maria has a BFA in Musical Theatre from Missouri State University where she studied with Lisa Brescia, Ann Marie Daehn, and Robert Westenberg, amongst others. Past roles (in case you’re curious) have included Sister Act (Sister Mary Robert), Legally Blonde (Vivienne Kensington), and lots of other classic high belters. Offstage, she is a huge fan of Robert Caro. If you’ve read his books, she would love to talk with you about them. If you haven’t read his books, she highly recommends them.

people.name: Maria Randazzo
people.bio: Maria Randazzo is an NY-based comedian, writer, and actress. She’s currently a writer-performer on the UCB character show BETTY, she was a staff writer on THE PROBLEM WITH JON STEWART on AppleTV+, and she performed with THE SECOND CITY in Chicago with The Second City Touring Company and in original sketch revues. She’s a member of SAG and WGA.

people.name: Marie Buck
people.bio: Marie Buck is an actor, writer, and photographer. Recent television credits include Matlock (CBS), Minx (MAX), The Goldbergs (ABC), Tacoma FD (truTV), Daisy Jones & The Six (Amazon), Bosch: Legacy (Amazon), and Colin in Black & White (Netflix). Marie is a graduate of Syracuse University.

people.name: Marie Denny
people.bio: Marie Denny is an actor, voice over artist and improviser from London, England now based in Houston, Texas. She studied and performed improv and musical improv at UCB, Magnet and The Pit.

people.name: Marie Lively
people.bio: With almost 20 years of improv and sketch experience, Marie has always been active on the stage and on the page. Notably Marie’s past UCB lives include being on Harold Night with The Dragons, multiple Mess Hall teams, her one woman show-True Hustle, and many Quick and Funny musicals which she still writes and performs with. When not on stage Marie is a writer, VO actor and devoted mother of two.

people.name: Marilyn Papet
people.bio: Marilyn is a San Diego improvisor and feels most at home on stage. She loves to travel with her family and make her friends laugh. She believes those who are clowns at heart go to heaven. She loves her teammates and is thankful to be performing with her team Push Play.

people.name: Marina Gasparyan
people.bio: Marina is a tall Russian-Armenian human, writer and actor. She is the other half of Tall Women Productions and is currently a resident at Ars Nova’s comedy makers program.

people.name: Marina Mastros
people.bio: Marina has been a professional improvisor for over twenty years. Born and raised in LA, she is Chicana, biracial, and queer. She can be seen on the fifth season of Mission Unstoppable, currently airing on CBS. Catch her live onstage with Sassy Bluff and MAX.

people.name: Marina Tempelsman
people.bio: Marina Tempelsman is a New York-based writer. Her work has been featured by Comedy Central, The New Yorker, College Humor, Fusion, Funny or Die, Calm, Wondery, Radiotopia, and many other places. She was a resident playwright at The Flea Theater and Ars Nova (CAMP), and a sketch teacher at The UCB. She is also half of the comedy duo Marina & Nicco. Their pilot, Smüchr, won Best Comedy at The New York Television Festival in 2018, and their four-episode audio drama series The Off Season about the #MeToo movement was included in The Guardian’s list of Best Home Entertainment and Refinery29’s list of Best Podcasts of 2018. They are currently in pre-production for their first feature film.

people.name: Marissa Moorhead
people.bio: Marissa Moorhead is a Brooklyn based actor, improviser and sketch comedian. She studied clown, Shakespeare and improvisational comedy at UC Santa Cruz, UCB and BCC. When she’s not on stage you can find her behind the camera taking headshots and portraits all over Brooklyn.

people.name: Marissa Strickland
people.bio: Marissa Strickland is a writer, actor and director from Los Angeles. She’s been performing on the UCB stage for years and loves grounded bittersweet comedy.

people.name: Maritza Montanez
people.bio: Maritza is a Brooklyn-based improviser, improv teacher, and bookseller originally from California’s San Joaquin Valley. She taught and performed at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre and Training Center up until the pandemic settled in. She improvised with weekend house team The Law Firm and with Living Room: The First Generation , an improv show based on stories about growing up in immigrant households as first generation Americans. Before that, she performed on Harold Night for two years with At Last! and Party City, and on Lloyd Night for two years with seventeen and the Nightmare. Maritza previously hosted long-running indie show Kaleidoscope , and has been featured in videos for Disney+, MTVNews, Fusion, and lots of miscellaneous fun friends’ videos. She studied improv, sketch, and characters at UCB Theatre, and history and Spanish at New York University, and sometimes she plays flute!

people.name: Mark Burns
people.bio: Mark Burns is an actor, writer, and comedian who performs with the Harold Team babygirl and with his indie improv team Full of Bones which headlined the Fun and Dumb Improv Festival. His pilot Dumb Dumbs was recently selected to be featured in the Brooklyn Comedy Collective’s Pitchfest. He is also a standup comedian and he can be found performing all over Brooklyn.

people.name: Mark Dowling
people.bio: Mark Dowling is a writer and performer from New York. His writing has been featured on Funny or Die, IFC, The Earwolf podcast network, and MNN. He is the host of the podcast, First Album Ever, and regularly submits cartoons to The New Yorker. Mark’s fake daytime TV show “Dowling” can be seen live at The Upright Citizens Brigade Theater.

people.name: Mark Little
people.bio: Mark Little is an award-winning actor, writer and comedian. He created the acclaimed animated series Gary And His Demons (Amazon) and has acted in various television shows, including Ghosts (CBS), Revival (SyFy) and Mr. D (Netflix). Mark made his American TV debut on Conan and was named one of Variety’s 10 Comics To Watch for 2013.

people.name: Mark Melton
people.bio: Mark Melton is an NYC based writer and performer, and has performed on Mainstages with UCB, The Second City, Brooklyn Comedy Collective, the Magnet Theater, the Peoples Improv Theater, and the Armory. He also works in production for Last Week Tonight with John Oliver on HBO.

people.name: Mark Rennie
people.bio: Mark Rennie is an AMC A-Lister and yet has no representation. Go figure! He would like you to check out his podcast Two Old Queens.

people.name: Marnina Schon
people.bio: Marnina Schon is a Los Angeles-based actor, violinist, and noodle. Marnina stars in the dramedy series Chanshi (featuring Henry Winkler and Caroline Aaron), an Official Sundance Selection and winner of best independent comedy pilot at SeriesFest. In LA, Marnina has performed in the award-winning productions of “Once” at South Coast Repertory, “More Guns!” at Second City, “Anne, A New Play” at the Museum of Tolerance, and in workshops at Center Theatre Group, A Noise Within, and the Geffen Playhouse. You may have also seen Marnina ruining Kat Denning’s date in Dollface (Hulu), fiddling in Play It By Ear (Dropout), goofing with the Story Pirates, or arguably doing all of the above in musical sketches co-written & created with her partner, Michael O’Konis.

people.name: Marques Ray
people.bio: Marques Ray was found and raised in Bakersfield, California where he learned to speak almost every single language. Shortly after graduating from a pretty good college with good grades, he moved to Los Angeles and found his love for performing at UCB where he also writes and directs. You may recognize him from shows like Curb, The Good Place, and Mind of Mencia. He is smart and tall and knows a lot about knowledge. I’m also available for birthday parties.

people.name: Marshall Givens
people.bio: Marshall Givens is an improviser/writer/stand-up/producer from the south side of Chicago. He performed on the Harold team THE RUCKUS and the Maude team OH BROTHER! for several years. He is currently teaching at UCB. Any more information just feels like an invasion of privacy.

people.name: Marshall Stratton
people.bio: Marshall is a actor/writer/director who you’ve seen on TV and the big screen. For more goofs and spoofs, go to http://www.bing.com and type in, “Marshall Stratton”

people.name: Martha Fearnley
people.bio: Martha is writer, performer, and astrologer from Los Angeles. She currently co-hosts the podcast It Girl Theory with Kaitlin Gleason.

people.name: Martin Herlihy
people.bio: MARTIN HERLIHY is one in the internet and cultural phenom comedy group, PLEASE DON’T DESTROY . They joined SNL three years ago as writer/performers and are the brain trust behind the show’s most viral sketches and digital shorts. Their first feature, THE TREASURE OF FOGGY MOUNTAIN, premiered in November ’23 with Judd Apatow producing for Universal. Martin co-wrote alongside John Higgins and Ben Marshall, and they all starred and executive produced. In 2023, Please Don’t Destroy embarked on a 25-city live tour that had them selling out theaters and adding shows all across the country. New York Magazine/Vulture named them to Comedians You Should and Will Know in 2021 List , THR featured on the cover of their New York issue , and Variety named them to the New Power of New York List .

people.name: Martin James
people.bio: Martin James founded the Glasgow Improv Theatre and studied improv at the Upright Citizen Brigade Theatre in Los Angeles. He has been performing and teaching improv across the UK and internationally for over a decade. Festival appearances include the Del Close Marathon in New York, the Edinburgh Fringe, Reykjavík and Berlin.

people.name: Marty Miller
people.bio: Marty Miller is Brooklyn based comedian, actor, and content creator. After performing live in NYC for the past decade Marty has recently amassed a large following across social platforms where he spreads belly laughs and painful positivity. With his comedic partner Missy McIntosh, Marty strives to remind his audience (and himself) that they are truly “completely awesome, girl.” Marty recently began touring his solo show “Beth”, is a teaching artist with The Chautauqua Institution, and can be found performing monthly on the UCB mainstage

people.name: Marty Scanlon
people.bio: Marty is a comedian, writer, performer, and musician/composer. He was previously on Lloyd Night (UCB NY) and also performed with long-running NYC musical improv team Good Catch. In LA, he hosts the indie musical show ‘Aunt Hill’s Deck of Forms’ and is the creator of the solo comedy-business summit ‘Freemium Bizspirations: 10 Essential Steps to Business Success, now. And then, then.’

people.name: Mary Anthony
people.bio: Mary is originally from Oklahoma but would prefer to die in an earthquake over a tornado so she moved to LA. She’s also nearsighted, has asthma, and has worked on some TV shows.

people.name: Mary Cella
people.bio: Mary Cella is a stand-up comedian and writer based in New York City. Her stand-up has been featured on the BBC and her writing has appeared in The New York Times and on The New Yorker, McSweeney’s, Jezebel, The Hairpin, Comedy Central, and more. She’s the co-founder of Little Old Lady Comedy and the host of the long-running stand-up show Tip Top Tits.

people.name: Mary Lordes
people.bio: Originally from Pennsylvania, Mary attended Temple University majoring in Film & Media Arts with a minor in English. After graduating, she moved to New York and began studying at SVA and the Upright Citizens Brigade in television writing and sketch comedy respectively. She was a member of various house teams at UCB before moving to Los Angeles to teach sketch at their West Coast location. She’s written for Adam Ruins Everything and Just for Laughs as well as writing and producing the short film PACT and the web series Last Two People on Earth and Pricks.

people.name: Mary Lou
people.bio: Mary Lou is a former child actor (Disney, Nickelodeon, the whole shebang), Princeton grad, and LA local (go Burbank High Bulldogs). She performs with her sketch team MOON GOON on weekends UCB Franklin and musical improv with her team CANDY APPLE RED.

people.name: Mary McDonnell
people.bio: MARY MCDONNELL is a New York City based actor/writer/comedian. She is a contributing writer for The Truth Podcast (Wedding Objector, After The Hum, The Joke) with episodes featured in the NYT. As an improviser, she can be seen performing at UCB with The Trial and on Harold Night (Your Girlfriend); at BCC with her award-winning team, Bad Magicians, or around town with her other beloved indie darlings including 2025 CageMatch Losers: The Lake at Dawn, Morgan&Morgan&Morgan&Morgan&Morgan&Morgan&Morgan&Morgan, and Very Good Boys.

people.name: Mary Sasson
people.bio: Mary Sasson is a writer/performer/director from Pittsburgh. She has written for Comedy Central, Netflix, College Humor’s App Drop Out, and McSweeney’s. She has performed improv with the house team Winslow and stand up all around the country.

people.name: Maryann Alspaugh
people.bio: Houston native Maryann Alspaugh discovered her improv passion in NYC at the Magnet Theatre. Now based in Austin, Texas, she performs with Harold team All Night, All Night, All Night at Coldtowne Theater and musical improv Austin legend Girls Girls Girls. She is so excited to be back in NYC and performing at DCM again!

people.name: Matt Alspaugh
people.bio: Matt Alspaugh brings 15 years of stage experience from the NYC and Austin scenes to this year’s DCM. A seasoned performer, director, and instructor, Matt currently teaches for UCB ATX and occasionally coaches house teams at Fallout Theater. He is a proud product of the UCB NY, Magnet, PIT and Story Pirates systems, and spends his Sunday nights performing with his house Harold team, “All Night, All Night, All Night.” He also performs as half of the duo “Gavin and Matt” with Gavin Speiller. You can find him on LinkedIn.

people.name: Matt Apodaca
people.bio: Matt Apodaca is a comedian, writer and podcast producer. He hosts the video game podcast Get Played , has appeared frequently on Comedy Bang! Bang! , Dropout and can be heard in the Netflix animated show Strip Law .

people.name: Matt Barrett Miller
people.bio: Matt Barrett Miller is an LA-based actor and comedian and Emerson College alumni. Catch him on the UCB stage every month performing with his team, Big Time, as a part of Maude Night. Also check him out in the upcoming second season of Stick on AppleTV+ or with sketch team, Same Great Taste, at the Elysian!

people.name: Matt Besser
people.bio: Matt Besser is a founding member of The Upright Citizens Brigade. He is known for improv shows like “A.S.S.S.S.C.A.T.” and the Comedy Central sketch show “Upright Citizens Brigade”. His character acting has been seen in such movies as “Walk Hard: the Dewey Cox Story” and “Freak Dance” to such sitcoms as “Modern Family” and “Parks & Recreation”. He hosts the improv comedy podcast “improv4humans with Matt Besser” on SiriusXM where he also has produced other audio series such as “My Dead Wife, the Robot Car”, “Stolen Idea: the Punk Rock Musical”, and “Estates of Perfection”. He can also be seen in his stand-up specials “Pot Humor” and “Besser Breaks the Record”.

people.name: Matt Czap
people.bio: Matt is an Improvisor and digital artist specializing in Online comics and animation.

people.name: Matt Devine
people.bio: Matt Devine is an LA based actor and writer. You may have seen him in TV commercials or in line at Van Leeuwen buying ice cream. He grew up in New York and went to the University of Michigan. He thanks his wife Lauren, daughter Della, and son Roger for supporting his relentless need to do bits.

people.name: Matt Fisher
people.bio: Matt is a writer and performer whom you may have seen on TV shows like “30 Rock” or “Late Night With Seth Meyers,” or in any number of enchanting commercials. Matt has performed at the UCB since 2010, in shows like Harold Night, The Law Firm, Tremendous Machine, Waiting For Obama, and many others. He lives in Brooklyn with his wife, two sons, and 2.6 million others.

people.name: Matt Gehring
people.bio: Matt Gehring is a director, educator, and comedy creator. He has directed over 100 shows, spanning sketch comedy, solo performances, and musical theater. Noteworthy productions include Harry Clarke featuring Billy Crudup, written by David Cale (Berkeley Rep, assistant director to Leigh Silverman), and Pop Roulette’s Amazing Earth featuring SNL’s Bowen Yang at Amy Pohler’s Upright Citizen’s Brigade theater. Matt has also directed at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival with the acclaimed works of Ellie Macpherson’s Happy Birthday, Mr. President (2022), and Lane Kwederis: Sex Job (2023). He has garnered recognition for his character comedy at the New Faces showcase, part of the Montreal Just For Laughs festival, and is a featured performer in Amazon’s The Tick, Netflix’s Set It Up, and more. While currently achieving his MFA at Brooklyn College for Theater Directing, Gehring is an educator, instructing at Tisch School of the Arts, NYFA, and other institutions.

people.name: Matt Giroveanu
people.bio: Matt Giroveanu is an actor, director, musical improviser & award-winning voiceover artist originally from Astoria, NY. For over 10 years, Matt has toured the world improvising musicals with Baby Wants Candy, Your Love, Our Musical, Broadway’s Next Hit Musical, Blank! The Musical and VERN. Television credits include Amazon’s The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, CBS’ God Friended Me, NBC’s Law & Order: SVU, HBO’s Boardwalk Empire, The Deuce, Netflix’s Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt & SyFy’s Happy! IG: @matt_giro www.MattGiro.com

people.name: Matt Grote
people.bio: MATT GROTE is a writer and comedian based in Brooklyn. He helped found and currently performs with the sketch group QUEER WINDOW and previously helped found the satire magazine AWFmag, where he worked as a staff writer. His latest project, a Kaufmanesque bit, is getting a PhD in mathematics. He will not do your homework.

people.name: Matt Jeakle
people.bio: Matt Jeakle is a writer, performer, and comedian from Seattle, Washington. He’s currently a sketch writer on UCB’s Maude team, Rodeo Baby. To hear more about how cool and nice he is, please call his grandma.

people.name: Matt Kempner
people.bio: Matt is a standup, improviser, and sketch team member based in Los Angeles. He hosts Mic Club 2nd and 4th Mondays at The Clubhouse and is a member of Speakeasy at the Pack Theater.

people.name: Matt Linton
people.bio: Matt’s an actor and writer with credits across multiple streamers, networks, and films. He has several screenplays at various stages of development, spanning adult comedy, YA murder mystery, and animated horror, and live-action horror.

people.name: Matt Manser
people.bio: Matt Manser is one of the directors/producers of UCB’s Sketch Cram, where an entire sketch show is written/rehearsed/performed in one day. Matt has also been on four Maude teams, and has written for Funny or Die and Jimmy Fallon. Originally from Michigan, Matt has seen Hall & Oates in concert seven times, and feels like this is important info that should be in his bio.

people.name: Matt Newell
people.bio: Matt is an improvisor, actor, and writer from North Carolina and has been performing at UCB since 2010. In addition to being an alumni of both Harold and Maude night, he has been a UCB teacher since 2014. When he’s not in movies (Golden Arm, Happiest Season), TV shows (Adam Ruins Everything, Comedy Bang Bang!) and commercials (Spectrum, Snickers) he’s usually hiking with his dog or annoying his wife.

people.name: Matt Pozzuolo
people.bio: Matt is an Improviser/Actor/Writer known for his work with Peter & Matt (PIT March Madness 2025 Winners) and Zero (Edinburgh Fringe Fest 2026). He can be seen every month at UCB in shows such as Improvised The Office (Performer), The Great American Shit Carnival (Performer/Writer), and La Lumiere (Performer). His mom had been to every show he’s ever done.

people.name: Matt Radlow
people.bio: Matt Radlow is a comedy guy that lives in Brooklyn. He started improvising at UCB on January 3, 2011 and shows no signs of stopping. You can watch him perform on Harold Night with the team babygirl. He also has a very funny podcast called Wow! What A Story! that he will update if you ask him to.

people.name: Matt Visconage
people.bio: Matt is an improviser and writer from Maryland. He can currently be seen performing with his Harold team Local Tycoon and proudly as one of the co-hosts of CAGEMATCH. He previously has performed at UCB with the Harold team GAG and the Mess Hall team Roxbury. He was also a member of the I.O. Harold team Dual Citizen when he lived in Chicago. In his non improv time he was co-written 2 different books, was a headline contributor for Clickhole, and is about to release his first solo satirical novel The Day Job Survival Handbook! published by Humorist Books.

people.name: Matt Walsh
people.bio: Matt Walsh is a founding member of the Upright Citizens Brigade. He is perhaps best known for starring in HBO’s Emmy-lauded comedy series Veep as Mike McLintock, the sharp-witted, deadpan Director of Communications to the first female U.S. Vice President, Selina Meyer (Julia Louis-Dreyfus). Other credits include The Hangover, Old School, Due Date, Step Brothers, Elf, Cyrus and Role Models. Other TV shows created and/or written by Walsh include Comedy Central’s Dog Bites Man, Daisy Does America, and Human Giant.

people.name: Matt Woodward
people.bio: Matt Woodward is a Philadelphia-based improviser, writer, and performer. He’s a graduate of the core curriculum at Philadelphia Improv Theater and has taken additional courses and workshops there, at ComedySportz, and with Eric Feurer (character monologue). Matt’s improv team Hey Hi Hello (w. Kimmy Nazaruk, Sofia Cordero, and Kate Elliott) performs all over the indie scene in Philadelphia and on the festival circuit. Outside of comedy, Matt is a writer, researcher, and amateur adult French horn player.

people.name: Matthew Brian Cohen
people.bio: Matthew Brian Cohen has written, produced, directed, and performed in shows at UCB NY and LA for over ten years. He wrote for Maude Night, performed at Harold Night, and produced and co-hosted the late night variety show Cool Shit/Weird Shit. You can see his writing on Epic How To, Honest Trailers, McSweeney’s, Weekend Update, and The Onion, as well as various other places.

people.name: Matthew McMahan
people.bio: I am an Assistant Professor in Comedic Studies at Emerson College, where I teach the history of comedy. I’ve been performing with Neighbors for the last ten years, and lead applied improvisation workshops throughout New England.

people.name: Matthew Newman
people.bio: Matthew Newman is a writer, actor, and comedian from a small town an hour outside New York City. He has a BA in Screenwriting from Emerson College and has appeared in numerous commercials. His sketch comedy group Flambe Comedy! can be found on all Social platforms and he would love for you to come say hey at Birds after the show!

people.name: Matthew Patrick Davis
people.bio: Songwriter and co-writer of many an original UCB musical, from “PUN-OFF” to “7th Grade: The Awkward Musical,” and musical versions of “Jurassic Park,” “The Shining,” “King of Kong” and more. Longtime member of “Quick & Funny Musicals.” Most recently portrayed “The Mother” in the 20th Century Studios’ hit horror film, “Barbarian.”

people.name: Mattia Day
people.bio: Mattia is a LA based actor, writer and producer that works in Social Services by day and entertains the masses at night – well hopefully! She has landed two residencies with her improv team Tight Like Glue at Westside Comedy Theatre & with her duo improv team Homegirls in Outer Space at Hollywood’s Broadwater Theatre. Mattia has been welcomed at UCB and is currently reigning Cage Match champions with the illustrious Fab Four.

people.name: Maudlin Vo
people.bio: Maudlin Vo is an Austin-based performer and cast member of the improv comedy troupe Y’all We Asian. Outside of goofing off on stage, she delights in reading, gardening, making desserts, running with friends, failing gloriously at bar trivia, and bothering her cats.

people.name: Max Ash
people.bio: Max do comedy. Max play drums. Max love escape rooms. End of bio.

people.name: Max Bank
people.bio: Max Bank is a New York City born, raised, and based actor. He has studied comedy at UCB, Magnet, the PIT, and WGIS, and drama at the Barrow Group and the Atlantic Acting School. He performed with several improv, musical improv, and sketch teams the Magnet Theater and has done a lot of plays in Queens.

people.name: Max Christofferson
people.bio: Max Christofferson is a comedian and actor originally from Tacoma, Washington. He has performed at venues including the Hollywood Improv, the Ice House, and one time for 7 people at a Mexican restaurant on Hollywood Boulevard. He is the co-creator and host of Too Many Cooks, a standup cooking show at UCB LA.

people.name: Max Feldman
people.bio: Max Feldman is a pianist, musical director, improviser, and composer who lives in Brooklyn. Performance venues include Magnet Theater, UCB, Caveat, Brooklyn Comedy Collective, Peoples Improv Theater, The RAT, and more. He is the MD for Hell Yeah! improvised musicals and the fantasy adventure show The Legend of You.

people.name: Max Hawksford
people.bio: Max Hawksford is a tall (6’2″) actor/comedian/writer from Wisconsin. His versatility and range has gotten him coined as *the young gay Bill Hader*. He’s amassed a TikTok following of over 300k. He’s a UCB Betty Performer. His credits include Monster (Netflix); The Rookie (ABC); CollegeHumor; The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills. And he DOES play basketball, unironically. And pickleball now, too.

people.name: Max Hegley
people.bio: Max is a NYC born and raised actor, arts educator, and comedian living in NYC. Outside of UCB, you can find him performing monthly on a BarProv house team at Young Ethel’s in Brooklyn and as a member of his indie team Tiny Bikini (@tinybikinicomedy), running AriZona Iced Tea’s TikTok, petting his two cat children Fig and Persimmon, as well as touring the country with the Story Pirates. poHa forever.

people.name: Max Housner
people.bio: Max Housner is an LA based improviser, musician, and aerospace engineer, originally hailing from Ann Arbor, MI. You can catch Max performing musical improv at UCB with “Sing Out, Louise!” or at various other theaters in LA with teams Lunch Money, unHINGEd, and Friday Friday. He is stoked to be performing at UCB and absolutely loves the improv community!

people.name: Max LoParco
people.bio: Max has previously written for the Maude teams The Audacity and Don’t Be a Stranger. He’d like to eat chicken wings on national TV if the opportunity becomes available.

people.name: Max Sosna-Spear
people.bio: Max Sosna-Spear is an actor and comedian who can be seen at the theater with Maude team LOVESEAT and on Hulu’s Tiktok page as MR. HULU. A former member of the Groundlings Sunday Company, he has been performing improv and sketch comedy for more than 15 years.

people.name: May Darmon
people.bio: May Darmon is an improviser and writer from North Hollywood. She’s written on the shows SOLAR OPPOSITES and STAR TREK: LOWER DECKS. Above all, she loves writing her personal bio. Everyone agrees she’s really good at it. You can catch her at UCB on Harold Night or at WEimprov with her teams PB&J and Shag.

people.name: Maya Sharma
people.bio: Maya Sharma is a comedian/actor/writer/interesting woman who’s been doing improv and weird bits in NYC for over 10 years. She’s also the Associate Director for the Brooklyn Comedy Collective, an alternative comedy theater and training center in Williamsburg. She’s a member of the comedy supergroup Ladies Who Ranch (Paramount+, NYT, Edinburgh Fringe), as well the live-podcasting project Weird Brown Idiots. And guess what she mains Yoshi in Smash as well.

people.name: McKenzie Fleischer
people.bio: McKenzie is writer and performer based in NYC. She is a member of the award winning sketch comedy group Craig, performing monthly at UCB and the improv team Help Me I’m Drowning, performing monthly at Brooklyn Comedy Collective. She was a cast member of BCC’s 2025 MainStage residency show, We Need a Win. Other credits include NYC Queer Comedy Festival, Flyover Comedy Festival, Tides Improv Festival, and Columbus Improv Festival.

people.name: Meg Buzza
people.bio: Originally a dancer from Northern California, Meg Buzza is now a main stage cast member at the BOOM! Chicago Comedy Theater in Amsterdam. A graduate of the UCB Musical Improv program, Meg joined Baby Wants Candy and Shamilton: The Improvised Hip Hop Musical at the 2025 Edinburgh Fringe Festival. Outside of performing improv 5-6 nights a week, Meg can be found rock climbing and spoiling her dog Kuzco.

people.name: Meg Joh
people.bio: Meg is originally from Napa, California— yes, she loves wine. She was a writer on Maude and hosts Wild Indie Nights with her all-woman improv team, Wild. As a longtime Copywriter/Creative Director she’s created the brand voices behind a long list of TV shows and films like Pen 15, Top Gun Maverick, Step Up, and so many more.

people.name: Meg Ruocco
people.bio: Meg Ruocco is an improviser and comedy writer currently living in New York City. Meg earned her MFA in Screenwriting from Brooklyn College, is a proud graduate of the 2024 WGA East Support Staff training program on the Writers’ Assistant track, and is currently performing in Lloyd Nights at UCB with her team, Clippy. When Meg isn’t writing or performing, she’s probably gossiping with her friends over drinks and a delicious meal, Sex and the City-style.

people.name: Megan Bitchell
people.bio: Megan Bitchell is a Brooklyn based comedian and actress. She is the host of Borrow A Boyfriend and the podcast Yay! with Andrea Rose.

people.name: Megan Rose McCarthy
people.bio: Megan is an actor and improviser from San Francisco and is based in London. She has performed regularly at The Free Association with former house harold team NANA, Fabled and headline show This Doesn’t Leave the Room. She has performed internationally at The Edinburgh Fringe, EIIF, Das, and Big Scene. Megan is also a comedian and storyteller. She has performed at The Moth Story Slam and The Last Friday Club.

people.name: Mehdi Barakchian
people.bio: Mehdi has been around the improv scene since 2012. You’ve seen him on TV in network shows and commercials. He has a fridge that makes its own ice.

people.name: Mel Cowan
people.bio: Mel Cowan is a writer/performer from South Texas. Most recently, he wrote for LEVERAGE: REDEMPTION and has written for multiple seasons of THE ARRANGEMENT and BROCKMIRE. He has sold pilots to 20th Century Fox, FX and Eko and co-created the Emmy-nominated digital series BOONDOGGLE. A weekly performer at Upright Citizens Brigade since 2006, he has taught and performed improv internationally. He has been in a bunch of movies, TV shows and commercials, including one where he had robot arms.

people.name: Melia Hagino
people.bio: Melia Hagino is a queer, Asian-American comedian, writer and actress based in Brooklyn, New York. After producing audiobooks at her day job, Melia can be found writing and performing sketch with Burnt Toast Sketch Show, doing stand-up and improv at UCB, BCC, and the Pit, and putting up weird freaky plays at literally any venue that will let them in. Melia’s writing can be found in Mcsweeney’s and PointsInCase.com. Full disclosure: Melia is a nepo baby…. (their dad is also Asian)

people.name: Melissa Aquiles
people.bio: Melissa Aquiles is a Portuguese-American Comedian, Writer, Actor. She co-runs a production company with her partner Jack Frederick called Jack and Melissa Comedy where the produce everything from internet shorts to films to live events. You’ve seen her on TBS, Disney +, on the film festival circuit, and more. Most recently she understudied the Second City NY Mainstage.

people.name: Melissa Peng
people.bio: Melissa is a comedy writer and performer (in that order). In addition to writing for Maude night, she’s performed improv at UCB and the Pack Theater. In her free time she likes to drive out to a marshy area and practice getting out of quicksand. In her words, “there’s a lot of sand out there, and I don’t know that I’ll always be able to tell whether it’s quick or regular just by looking at it.”

people.name: Meltem Saricicek
people.bio: Meltem Saricicek is a multidisciplinary Turkish artist, performer, and producer based in Los Angeles. She works across comedy, photography, graphic design, and video. She is a producer of the late-night comedy wrestling show Midnight Rumble.

people.name: Mercedes Hesselroth
people.bio: Mercedes is a writer, performer, and producer repping Peru and the Midwest in the Big Apple. She was named a 2025 Diversity Scholar at both the Upright Citizens’ Brigade and Magnet Theater. Mercedes has developed television through the PBS Ready to Learn Writer’s Program, trained with the Eugene O’Neill Theater Center National Critics Institute, and published original humor through The Onion Diverse AF Workshop. As an actor, she been cast in multiple developmental workshops of new plays such as Indian Princesses by Eliana Theologides Rodriguez (Round House Theatre), premieres such as Terce: A Practical Breviary (HERE Arts Center), and revivals such as the 2026 production of Animal Wisdom (Signature Theatre Center). Past UCB character shows include Characters Welcome, Ninjas Assemble, and Crisis on Infinite.

people.name: Meredith Palomo
people.bio: Meredith is an improviser, writer, and actor based in Los Angeles, CA. You can catch her on Harold Mondays with Dance Club Remix and on Betty every first Wednesday of the month. You can also see her performing with all-women team indie team Shag.

people.name: Meredith Paul
people.bio: Meredith is an actor, writer and director based in Los Angeles.

people.name: Mia Schauffler
people.bio: Mia Schauffler is a LA based comedian, writer, and actress. She performs weekly at UCB with her improv team LEROY. You can see her in NETFLIX’s upcoming David Harbour film “We Have a Ghost” or starring in APPLE’s viral short films “The whole work-from-home thing,” “The Underdogs,” and “Escape from the Office.”

people.name: Micah O'Konis
people.bio: Micah O’Konis (they/them) is a writer and comedian based in Los Angeles. Micah writes for Quick & Funny Musicals at UCB, performs musical comedy as Couplet (@coupletcomedy) with romantic & creative partner Marnina Schon, and created and music directed the award-winning musical comedy More Guns! which ran for two years at Second City. As a musician, they compose music for films and commercials, and secretly release soft indie folk music under the artist name Lookalike.

people.name: Michael Brantl
people.bio: Michael is a Brooklyn-based comedian from Worcester, Massachusetts. He hopes to one day make you laugh. Follow his poetry account (@michaelbrantlpoetry)

people.name: Michael Busch
people.bio: At UCBTLA Michael was on the Harold Team Mr. & Mrs. All-Star and the Maude Team Wall of Sound. He also started The Midnight Show and produced it for 4.5 years and was a producer with Nick Thune on Nick’s Big Talk Show. His acting credits include Nope, Miss March, Conan, Pretty Little Liars, Key & Peele, and Community. He is currently the Artistic Director of Imposters Theater in Cleveland, Ohio.

people.name: Michael Christian
people.bio: Michael Christian is a writer, director, and actor originally from New Jersey. He’s currently Head Writer for Studio C–a sketch comedy show with over 2 billion views on YouTube–where he’s written sketches for Will Forte, Terry Crews, among others. You can watch his webseries “Dinosaur Portals” on Youtube.

people.name: Michael Cruz Kayne
people.bio: Michael is a Peabody and WGA award-winning comedian who’s been seen on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, Late Night with Seth Meyers, Severance, High Maintenance, and other stuff. He has also lost a few Emmys, a Drama Desk, a Lucille Lortel, and some Webbys. You can listen to his one-man show, Sorry For Your Loss, on Audible (Best of Audible 2023).

people.name: Michael Ganley
people.bio: Michael is a writer born and raised in NYC. His work has been performed at stages all around the city as well as comedy festivals across America and Canada. Michael currently writes for UCB, but he mostly learned about comedy from sitting in front of the TV for way too many hours since the age of 3.

people.name: Michael Greene
people.bio: Michael Greene is a UCB-trained improviser and actor. He is the owner of Faraway Theater in Birmingham, AL, where he teaches and performs improv, sketch, and stand-up.

people.name: Michael Hartney
people.bio: Michael is a UCB NY veteran, doing improv, sketch and characters there for over a decade. He has been a JFL New Face, a Comedy Central Comic to Watch, and was in the original Broadway cast of School of Rock The Musical. TV credits include 30 Rock, WeCrashed, The Politician, The Break with Michelle Wolf, The Who Was? Show, and Throwing Shade, where he was also a staff writer. Michael can next be seen as Josh on Hulu’s How to Die Alone.

people.name: Michael McCollor
people.bio: Michael McCollor is a writer/host in Los Angeles who has worked for Outfit7, TwentyTwo Words, Bunny Ears, and Smosh. He is, physically, very strong.

people.name: Michael Nottebart
people.bio: Mike has been doing improv for about 4 years in various theaters around Austin Texas and joined Father Figures about 2 years ago

people.name: Michael Sanky
people.bio: Michael Sanky is a comedian, writer, musician, and actor based in New York City. He currently performs at UCB on his Betty Team Good Bones and on his Maude Team Gorgeous Stank, and has been on Characters Welcome. Michael regularly performs around New York, including with his solo show “Make The Cut,” and with his live game show “Spell Checc,” a spelling bee meets try-not-to-laugh challenge. Michael is also an award-winning musical director, arranger and multi-instrumentalist.

people.name: Michael Sturgis
people.bio: Michael performs on Harold Night. He serves as Literary Manager at Echo Theater Company, where he received the Los Angeles Drama Critics Circle Award for his portrayal of Francis in Poor Clare . His short film Every Other Week is a Vimeo Staff Pick. TV: Room 104 (HBO), Call Me Kat (FOX).

people.name: Michael Tocci
people.bio: Like most LA residents, “Tocci” majored in Film at Emerson College. He has dabbled in stand-up, writing, and performing, notably as a member of the short-form improv team, “The Sauce,” and the interactive-stage-fantasy-adventure show, “Dungeon Master.” He is also a freelance photographer for the Daily Bugle.

people.name: Michelladonna
people.bio: Michelladonna is a Webby Award-winning dynamic director, comedian, actor, and community organizer from Queens, NY. Michelladonna is the bilingual host of the internet sensation and Webby Award-winning show SHOP CATS (@shopcats). As well as the creator and host of BODEGA CHAT (@bodegachat), a platform highlighting New York culture + up-and-coming creatives. Follow Michelladonna on Instagram + TikTok @michelladonna.

people.name: Michelle Chan Bennett
people.bio: Michelle Chan Bennett (she/her) is an NYC-based writer and actor whose work has been featured in Vulture, New York Sketchfest, Montreal Sketchfest, and Chicago Sketchfest. She is a proud alum of the CAMP Residency at Ars-Nova, where her musical comedy with Josh Nasser, Bada Swing! premiered Off-Broadway. You also may have seen her dancing in Ziwe’s Pop Show or starring as “Cheryl” in the BIPOC murder mystery play, The Art of Killin’ It.

people.name: Michelle Fahrenheim
people.bio: Michelle is an actor, writer and director, born and bred in London. She performs improv regularly at The Free Association and works across theatre, film, and television.

people.name: Mickey Woo
people.bio: Mickey is an L.A. based actor and comedian. You can find him in support staff roles for you favorite television shows such as Twisted Metal , Umbrella Academy and Shrinking . Also he went to clown school. Please ask him about it.

people.name: Mike Kelton
people.bio: Mike has been performing at the UCB theater for close to a decade on teams like his longtime Harold team, Some Kid, the touring company and with his one-man storytelling show, Yaz Hunni! Outside of the theatre, Mike works in TV & digital creating, producing & directing docu-style formats for networks like TLC, MTV, LogoTV & more. Currently, Mike lives in Brooklyn with his husband and his haunted dresser (yeah, babe, she haunted) and performs monthly with What I Did For Love.

people.name: Mike Leffingwell
people.bio: Mike Leffingwell has written cartoons for Dreamworks and Netflix, jokes for the Onion and The Late Late Show, and a series of Shorty Award-winning commercials for Tubi. On TV he has appeared on The Goldbergs, Conan, and commercials for many exciting products. He has been with the UCB since the theater opened in LA and was on several harold teams, each with a sillier name than the last.

people.name: Mike McLendon
people.bio: Actor, improviser, and writer of short bios, Mike McLendon has performed in resident companies across Los Angeles. Mike’s credits include Jimmy Kimmel Live!, Comedy Bang! Bang!,and My Crazy Ex-Girlfriend. You can follow Mike on social media @themikemclendon or in real life if you’re shy and sneaky.

people.name: Mike Millan
people.bio: Mike Millan is a Musical theater monster from Long Island. He’s performed everywhere from basements to Broadway and is so excited to be a part of DCM this year.

people.name: Mike Poole
people.bio: Mike Poole is a writer, performer, and improvisor based in Brooklyn. He’s written for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon and The Amber Ruffin Show. He has performed and taught improv across the country for over a decade, and is excited to once again be teaching and performing at UCB. He is STILL patiently waiting for them to make Sisterhood of the Traveling Pants 3.

people.name: Mike Rhodes
people.bio: Mike Rhodes is a Pittsburgh-based improviser, producer, coach, and award-winning filmmaker. His hobbies include collecting VHS tapes and giving very little thought to what he gets tattooed. His social security number is 771-45-2890.

people.name: Mike Scollins
