people.bio: Mike Scollins is an Emmy-nominated writer and actor from Marshfield, MA. He is currently a supervising writer and performer on Late Night with Seth Meyers. He wrote the coming-of-age feature BRIAN which recently premiered at SXSW. Other writing credits include Saturday Night Live, The Golden Globes and The White House Correspondents’ Dinner.

people.name: Mike Smith Rivera
people.bio: Mike is a Brooklyn-born bilingual Mexican-American actor, comedian, & voice-over artist located in NYC. He currently performs with UCB’s weekend teams The Foundation and ASSSSKETCH. Previous Maude teams include Night Eaters, BedRock, DumpTruck & 11:11. TV: Only Murders in the Building, The Equalizer, Billions, The Blacklist, The Green Veil, Brooklyn Animal Control, Hack My Life VO/Animation: DORA, Burning Rubber, Yu-Gi-Oh!, Dora the Explorer, Dora & Friends, Tickle Me Silly, Grand Theft Auto V www.mikesmithrivera.com

people.name: Mikey Stephens
people.bio: Actor, writer and comedian Mikey Stephens is originally from Seattle Washington and moved to LA in 2014 to live on his friend’s apartment floor. Mikey has his own floor now and is overjoyed to perform on Bitchin’.

people.name: Miki Ann Maddox
people.bio: Half Japanese. You might have seen her on TV (The Office, Two Broke Girls, indebted, Awkward.) Or flirting with Toomgis in a commercial. She’s also going to be on Season 11 of VPR (iykyk).

people.name: Miles Calderon
people.bio: Miles Calderon is a Clown, Theater-Maker, Actor, Improviser, Dignitary, and Website, best known for his universally adored character, Mr. Cardboard, host of Mr. Cardboard Presents, his work touring with the infamous variety show Stamptown, and his involvement with many other shows at the Edinburgh Fringe. He graduated from the renowned French physical theatre school École Philippe Gaulier and has a movement background in parkour. Miles is also really nice to work with and seeking representation!

people.name: Milly Tamarez
people.bio: Milly Tamarez is a Brooklyn-based stand-up comedian, writer, and actress whose work spans television, podcasts, and satire. She has written for shows like ABC’s The Celebrity Dating Game and E!’s Jason Biggs’ Cash at Your Door, and co-hosts the Headgum podcast Go Touch Grass. A 2022 winner of the Yes and Laughter Lab, Milly is also a co-creator of Flexx, a satire magazine for people of color, and the Diverse as F*** Festival, which celebrates diversity in comedy.

people.name: Mimi Universe
people.bio: Mimi Universe is an actor, comedian, producer, voice over artist, model, host, humanitarian, a woman who comes from a line of mothers, and a dreamer. A Meisner trained actor…It’s no surprise she loves improv so much. Mimi is constantly living in her imagination by generating so much creativity, she’s the most talkative person in the room. This woman cannot be stop, literally… She. IS. Everywhere! If she’s not producing live shows or posting on social media, she is working somewhere in a theater near you. Follow her on Tiktok, Instagram, and Youtube at yourdoseofmimi, because everyone needs their dose of mimi.

people.name: Mimi von Schack
people.bio: Mimi is a comedian, host, actor, and writer, and she is honored to be a part of the UCB community. She’s performed in Las Vegas, New York, London, and on the main stages of the Groundlings, The Cavern Club, and UCB, among many others. Her award-winning work has been featured in the LA Scripted Comedy Festival, RAW Hollywood Showcase, and the Hollywood and Edinburgh Fringes. Her one-woman character show, “Women & Things,” has performed to sell-out audiences across the country. von Schack hosted “the Variety Hour” at the Open Space in West Hollywood for 2 years, and she continues to produce and host variety comedy shows in LA.

people.name: Miranda Coker
people.bio: Miranda Coker is a Los Angeles based actor and improviser. You can see them at UCB cohosting Strawberry Jam with their team Ladies Night. You can also see them on stage around town with their house team at WGIS, and their various indie teams including Sea Hag. You can also find them in various show audiences taking photos and/or cackling like a witch.

people.name: Missy McIntosh
people.bio: Missy McIntosh is a comic, musician, voiceover artist, and actor with a mix of Midwest charm and Brooklyn grit. You can find her performing stand-up, musical comedy, creating bizarre viral characters or oversharing on the internet. She’s explored every corner of her creativity—and she’s just getting started.

people.name: Mitch McMahon
people.bio: Mitch McMahon is an actor/director in Los Angeles. He performs all around LA and his shorts/sketches can be found on his YouTube page. You can see him on Betty the first Wednesday of every month.

people.name: Mitchell Styer
people.bio: Mitchell is an Austin based actor and improviser that has appeared in marketing and commercials for brands such as Liquid Death and Indian Motorcycles. He recently co-starred in the TV Pilot Hysterical that premiered at the ATX TV Festival in 2025. He studied Improv Comedy at the Hideout Theater where he now performs regularly. Mitchell is also a Cast Member, Co-Director, and Co-Owner of the B. Iden Payne award winning Improv Troupe, Jane “Austin” Improv.

people.name: Mj Duran
people.bio: An actor, writer, and producer from the southern streets of Houston Texas. After completing training at the Stella Adler Studio of Acting, he wrote the book for the new musical “Costco Burns Down Inside the Head of a Dedicated Employee”. He also starred in the short film “Bartleby” and appeared in an Off-Broadway production of “The MotherF**ker with the Hat”.

people.name: Molly Doan
people.bio: Molly is a writer, host, and comedian from Wheatland, CA. They produce and host comedy shows all across LA including Strawberry Jam 2nd & 4th Wednesdays at UCB. Molly is a Leo and let’s not forget that.

people.name: Molly Gaebe
people.bio: Molly Gaebe is an NYC based writer, comedian, and musical theater nerd. She can be seen performing various types of hijinx and street theater with Abortion Access Front. Check out her band, BRVNCHES, for having the best time while losing your grip on reality.

people.name: Molly Kiernan
people.bio: Molly Kiernan is a Los Angeles-based comedian. She has written for The Conners (ABC), Lopez vs Lopez (NBC), Dad Stop Embarrassing Me (Netflix), and some other fun stuff. You can also see her acting in Russian Doll (Netflix), The Conners (ABC), and Real Husbands of Hollywood (BET Plus). She is a writer on the very gorgeous UCB sketch team Loveseat, and an improviser on the very hot UCB Harold Team LOHAN.

people.name: Molly Lloyd
people.bio: Molly is thrilled to be back performing with her Bastian family. After over 10 years at UCB as a performer and teacher, Molly now divides her time between her acting career and her career in Healthcare Communication. Her 9-year old son is the funniest person she’s ever met and the rest of her life will be spent trying to convince him to think the same about her.

people.name: Molly McLean
people.bio: Molly McLean does standup, sketch, and improv comedy. She adapts and performs plays with her theater group Being Time.

people.name: Molly Moseley
people.bio: Molly is a TV writer, actor, and director. She has written for CALL ME KAT (FOX) and TOONED OUT (HBOMAX). We was selected for the WB Writer’s Workshop and as a Just For Laughs New Face Creator. As an actor, she has appeared in Westworld, Grey’s Anatomy, and Daddy’s Home 2 among others. She also was a SoulCycle instructor for several years, which was… weird. Ask her about it.

people.name: Molly Thomas
people.bio: Molly performs and teaches improv at UCB and around NYC. Catch her in ASSSSCAT and Teacher Feature. Past shows at UCB include: Higgins, We Know How You Die, Damned if you Do, Harold and Lloyd Night and Standards and Practices. Molly is a member of SAG-AFTRA and AEA. Check out her Substack “Improv City” for many more words about improv.

people.name: Moni Oyedepo
people.bio: MONI OYEDEPO is a writer, actress Nigerian-American with charming southern sensibilities. After working as a government consultant she forsook her degree and ambitions of being a senator for comedy writing. Moni is part of the inaugural class of the Women Write Now fellowship through HARTBEAT where her short film ‘Take the Job’ premiered at SUNDANCE and is streaming on PEACOCK. She’s part of the SONY supported 2022 Creative Corridor, an Independent Film Trust and Roadmap Writers initiative. Moni’s sweet spot is writing heartwarming comedies. When she’s not busy being funny you can find Moni playing the violin, dabbling as a matchmaker or teaching celebs how to mitigate embarrassment and win on game shows as a talent producer on CELEBRITY GAME FACE and 25 WORDS OR LESS. Moni is an improv and sketch performer at the Upright Citizens Brigade theatre. Her comedy has been featured on Freeform, WhoHaHa, CollegeHumor, Funny or Die and VoyageLA.

people.name: Monica Loomba
people.bio: Monica Loomba is an actor, writer/director, and homeopathic liar! She’s part of the award winning clown trio Best Actress, a Host of the UCB show Sabo & Loomba, and performing monthly on UCB Betty team. Her credits include The Brother’s Sun (Netflix), Platonic (Apple TV), UCB Maude, Groundlings Sunday Company, Host of Funny or Die Lab , and being ABSOLUTELY RANDOM!

people.name: Monika Smith
people.bio: Monika Smith is an actor, writer and comedian from Canada who modeled internationally for years until realizing she preferred food and smiling. Monika toured with The Second City and has been performing improv and sketch at UCB since 2007 in shows like ASSSSCAT, Harold Night, Form Night and more. She’s written for numerous comedy websites, and has directed and written comedy shows that toured America and Asia. You might not recognize her from pranking celebs on Punk’d for 3 seasons, and shows like Blunt Talk, Two and A Half Men, Comedy Bang Bang and many more. When she’s not hanging with her dog in Los Angeles, Monika is touring the world performing her solo improv show.

people.name: Monique Moses
people.bio: Monique Moses is a Canadian born writer, director and actor based in LA. She was co-head writer and a co-executive producer for Season 4 of HBO’s A Black Lady Sketch Show and has consulted on projects for Jimmy Fallon, Mike Myers and John Mulaney. She’s written for and appeared on shows for Adult Swim, HBO Max, Netflix, Peacock and as a member of all black comedy team Astronomy Club, co-created and starred in ‘Astronomy Club The Sketch Show’ (which premiered on Netflix in 2019 to stellar reviews). She loves loves loves writing bios (so so much), performing, making spreadsheets (truly), eating Korean BBQ and falling asleep in the sun with her dog Matsu.

people.name: Monte Montepare
people.bio: Monte is a comedian and adventure guide originally from Colorado. He’s been the Keynote Speaker at The Ouray Ice Fest, is on the cover of The Alaska Packraft Guidebook, and is part owner of Kennicott Wilderness Guides in Wrangell-St. Elias National Park, Alaska (all real things and places). Monte’s a touring storyteller for The Moth Mainstage, a skilled stand up comic and an excellent event MC. He lives in a little house on a hill with his partner Jill, their baby Rocky and chiweenie Sage.

people.name: Mookie Blaiklock
people.bio: Michael “Mookie” Blaiklock is an actor, writer, and comedian from BOSTON MASSACHUSETTS and he would be THRILLED if you checked his IMDb page for credits. He’s been performing at UCBLA since 2006.

people.name: Morgan Phillips
people.bio: Morgan has been doing improv since 1994 (one million years ago). He began studying at UCB in 2003, and teaching there in 2017. UCB house improv teams have included Big Revival, Lisa From School, Dr. Snake, Ice Cold Bev, At Last and Days Of Thunder.

people.name: Moujan Zolfaghari
people.bio: Moujan Zolfaghari is a writer, performer and VO artist who has been on TV (At Home with Amy Sedaris, Last Week Tonight, Tooning Out The News), written for TV (The Helpsters, Tiny Chef, TBS’s The Detour), and is the co-creator of Mission to Zyxx, a sci-fi comedy series podcast on MaxFun. Her mom is her accountant.

people.name: Muireann Kelly
people.bio: Muireann is an Irish performer and writer based in the UK. She performs with long-form ensemble NANA, trio Can We Be Friends, and the Pretend Company Ensemble. She previously wrote, performed, and produced Next Level Sketch at Hoopla in London. Her writing has appeared in McSweeney’s Internet Tendency and as a former contributor to The Onion.

people.name: Murphy Baker
people.bio: Murphy Baker (she/her) is an actor, singer, improviser, and frankly? A delight. Originally from Houston TX, she moved to New York City in 2021 after graduating with a BFA in Musical Theater from Colorado Mesa University. Currently, you can see her performing improv with her indie team PIE TOWN (2 time Cagematch champs mind you), performing in THEATRE: THE IMPROVISED PLAY at UCB, and monthly on Maude Night with REUNION TOUR!

people.name: Myles Hewette
people.bio: Myles Hewette is a writer and comedian from Kapoho, Hawaii. He’s a Maude Night Alum and has produced multiple independent sketch shows at various NYC theaters and festivals throughout North America. His pilot script “Rottenburgerfield” is one of the most downloaded scripts on Coverfly, and he’s currently seeking literary representation when he’s not complaining about unrealistic depictions of volcanos in media.

people.name: Nabeel Muscatwalla
people.bio: NABEEL MUSCATWALLA is an Indian-born, LA-based actor and writer from Dallas, Texas. He can be seen on THE SEX LIVES OF COLLEGE GIRLS (HBO Max), GOOD TROUBLE (Freeform), and as a field correspondent on MISSION UNSTOPPABLE (CBS). A standout in the 2020 CBS Showcase, he now performs monthly comedy with Upright Citizen’s Brigade Theater house team THE FAMILY BUSINESS. During his time at Northwestern, Muscatwalla created and starred in the web series BETTER DAYS , which premiered at the 2019 Tribeca Film Festival.

people.name: Nakul Makkad
people.bio: Nakul is a comedian, writer, and actor in Los Angeles whose preferred form of procrastination is taking his dog for long walks that end in food. He spends most of his time improvising at UCB and performing standup at clubs and colleges around the world. Nakul also works as an ML/AI consultant and developer to afford his crippling oat milk cappuccino dependency.

people.name: Namrata Prakash
people.bio: Namrata is from Texas and she’s proud of it!!! You can see her Mondays at 10pm performing at Lloyd Night.

people.name: Nanie Mendez
people.bio: Nanie is an actor/improvisor/writer based in Brooklyn. Born and raised in Puerto Rico, she is a recovering former lawyer. Previously a writer/performer on Characters Welcome and currently an actor on Maude night at UCBT NY. A NBC/UCBT Diversity Showcase finalist and a Story Pirates company member. You can catch her on tv on shows like Inventing Anna, Search Party, and Girls5Eva. Dog mom to a 60lbs pit bull named Menta.

people.name: Naomi Rubin
people.bio: Naomi Rubin is an improviser, musical comedian, and writer living in Brooklyn, NY. She is currently an improviser on the UCB Lloyd team “The Board,” and also forces her roommates to do improv with her around the apartment. She lugs her keyboard all around the city performing her musical stand-up, and has been featured at SF Sketchfest. You can find her mockumentary web series “Subletters” on Youtube. Her satire writing has been featured in The Broadway Beat and Sweaty Penguin.

people.name: Naomi Stuart
people.bio: Naomi is an improviser who has performed on Lloyd at UCB and indie teams in NYC. She’s from New Jersey.

people.name: Nat D'Amico
people.bio: BIO: NAT D’AMICO is a house performer at the UCB . Also can be seen around NYC with the improv team Day Trip and Boobie Trap Sketch . They recently toured to SF Sketchfest , ATX Sketchfest , and MTL Sketchfest before returning for a sold-out hour headlining the New York Comedy Festival at their home theater, the Brooklyn Comedy Collective . Film/TV: Just Another Immigrant (SHOWTIME), Needle in a Timestack (LIONSGATE), & RP (ATLFF, TIFF New Wave).

people.name: Nat Silverman
people.bio: Nat Silverman is a writer, director, and performer from New York City. She is the current head of the Magnet Theater’s Livewire sketch program. You can see her perform sketch comedy, improv, and stand-up onstage at the Magnet Theater, UCB, Brooklyn Comedy Collective, Caveat, and festivals around the country. She has also written for McSweeney’s, Awf Magazine, Off Q Comedy, and many many wonderful sketch teams. Nat is a 4th generation New Yorker, and as such has access to the hidden secret subway system that they don’t let transplants ride.

people.name: Nat Swyer
people.bio: Nat is a writer and filmmaker, which really means he’s a bartender. He’s on the Maude team Baguette and is pursuing an MFA in film production at USC. He can also juggle.

people.name: Natalia
people.bio: Natalia is an actor and improviser based in Austin, TX. She is a cast member of various award-winning troupes including The Rose, and a founding member of the Latine troupe Misterios. This is her first time performing in the DCM and she’s excited for the opportunity to hehehaha in the great city of New York!

people.name: Natalie Berg
people.bio: Natalie Berg is an actor from Gig Harbor, WA. Before landing in LA she performed with theater companies in Seattle including Macha Theater Works, Copious Love, and Ghost Light Theatricals. Catch her doing improv with UCB, WGIS, and WE Improv.

people.name: Natalie Ortega
people.bio: Natalie is an LA based actor, writer, comedian, and musician. Recent credits include the final season of “Search Party” on HBO Max and the feature film “Maybe I Do” opposite Susan Sarandon. Follow her comedy on instagram and TikTok @nataliegraceortega!

people.name: Natasha Vaynblat
people.bio: Natasha Vaynblat is a writer/performer/ improvisor. She’s written and appeared on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon and A Little Late with Lilly Singh. She’s performed on Comedy Central’s Up Next and her standup album “We’re All Dads Here” was featured in Paste’s Best Albums of 2023. She’s also been a performer on Harold Night, Maude Night, and What I Did For Love.

people.name: Nate Bennett
people.bio: Nate Bennett is a writer for UCB Maude team Pamela. Previously he wrote for Maude with House of Birds at UCB and for BoogieManja, an experimental sketch comedy collective at the People’s Improv Theater. His sketches have been featured on Vulture. He has also written videos for Cracked and Comedy Central.

people.name: Nate Lang
people.bio: Nate is an actor, writer and musician based in NYC.

people.name: Nate Lawler
people.bio: Nate Lawler is a writer, actor, and comedian from Long Beach, California. You can catch him throughout Los Angeles alongside Flight Risk Comedy, Midnight Diner, and his mother Debbie, who is usually the woman yelling at his shows.

people.name: NaTonia Monét
people.bio: NaTonia Monét (She/Her), a native of Richmond, VA and a graduate of Thee Florida State University (BFA & MPH), has been performing practically since birth- whether in the womb, stage, or in the deli meat section of the grocery store (She was 5 & saw dancing meat on Sesame Street). Her theatre career has taken her around the world, from the Kennedy Center in Washington DC, to the Theatre du Chatelet in Paris, France, and the NYC Broadway stage in Tina: The Tina Turner Musical. Now her comedy has landed her here at UCB in Maude Night! You can also catch her on T.V.- some faves include: THE OTHER TWO (HBO), FBI (CBS), THE LAST OG (TNT). Follow her to the end of the earth or just Instagram if that’s too much of a commitment. @NaToniaMonet

people.name: Neel Ghosh
people.bio: Neel Ghosh is a comedian and musician and feels totally normal writing in the third person. He has a digital set with Dont Tell and tours the country regularly. He hopes you and your loved ones are doing well

people.name: Neil Campbell
people.bio: Neil Campbell is a writer, performer, and director. His writing credits include Brooklyn Nine-Nine, Comedy Bang! Bang!, and Between Two Ferns. He is the executive producer and co-creator of the animated series DIGMAN! on Comedy Central. Neil has been involved with the UCBTLA since it was founded in 2005 and served as the Artistic Director from 2008 to 2012.

people.name: Neil Casey
people.bio: Neil Casey is an award-winning (Emmy, WGA, Peabody) writer and actor originally from Wilmington, Delaware. He improvised on the main stage at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater in New York City from 2002 until 2014 in shows including The Two-Man Movie, ASSSSCAT, and Your F*d Up Family. He wrote, directed, and acted in dozens of other shows at UCB and on the road doing sketch and improv at the San Francisco Sketch Fest, The North Carolina Comedy Arts Festival, the Piccolo-Spiletto Festival, South By Southwest, and colleges all over the country. He has written for Saturday Night Live, Inside Amy Schumer, Kroll Show, the MTV Movie Awards, the Film Independent Spirit Awards, Mystery Science Theater 3000, The President Show, Making It and Baking It. He has appeared on film in Ghostbusters, A Futile and Stupid Gesture, Adult Beginners, Fort Tilden, and Greener Grass and on television in Broad City, Other Space, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Veep, Silicon Valley, Superstore, Making History, White House Plumbers, Avenue 5, A League of their Own, Kenan, Black Monday, Mrs. Fletcher, Champagne ILL, and The League.

people.name: Neil D'Astolfo
people.bio: Neil D’Astolfo (he/him) is a New York-based actor, writer and comedian who can next be seen on Season 3 of Max’s “And Just Like That…” He spent three seasons as a series regular on Comedy Central’s Emmy-nominated “Stephen Colbert Presents Tooning Out the News” and recurred as Anthony Fauci on Showtime’s “Our Cartoon President.” Other TV and film credits include Hulu’s “Fire Island,” “High Fidelity” and “The Path.” His critically-acclaimed solo show, “Mister Miss America,” premiered off-Broadway at Rattlestick Playwrights Theater in Summer 2022, and earned Neil an Off-Broadway Alliance Award for his performance. Neil earned his MFA from Columbia University and is an adjunct professor at Pace University’s BFA Acting Program for Film, TV and Voiceover (FTVC).

people.name: Niccolo Aeed
people.bio: Niccolo Aeed is a Black Palestinian writer, director and comedian. He has directed the Maude Teams House of Birds, The Foundation, Gorgeous Stank and Pretty Boys. He is half the comedy duo Marina and Nicco, who recently finished production their first feature film Intervention. Their plays have been featured at Ars Nova, Here Arts, the PIT, UCB among others. Nicco hosts the podcast Copaganda! and hosts the storytelling show Telling Your Problems to Strangers.

people.name: Nicholas Caycedo
people.bio: Nicholas Caycedo is shizzing himself to be back with the MTiest comedy troupe in NYC. Theatre: The Great Gatsby Immersive Show (Off-Broadway), John Leguizamo’s Kiss My Aztec (Hartford Stage), La Mama Cantata (La MaMa), Gypsy (Lyric Theatre, Vermont) with Shaina Taub & Liana Hunt. Training: Walnut Hill School, Ithaca College. Native Vermonter/thoroughbred Colombian. For my boys.

people.name: Nicholoi Xavier
people.bio: NYC

people.name: Nick Ciarelli
people.bio: Nick Ciarelli is a comedian who has written for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, ClickHole, Billy On The Street, the podcast Comedy Bang Bang, and has developed projects for Comedy Central and TBS. He’s been named a Comedian To Watch by Vulture and a New Face at the Just For Laughs Festival. He’s made a bunch of videos and stuff with Brad Evans that you can watch online.

people.name: Nick Desai
people.bio: Nick is an Austin-based performer and director who just tried improv as a way of meeting people after moving to a new city, and really didn’t expect it to turn into most of his social life. They have played in many productions at various comedy venues, most often the Hideout, Fallout, and ColdTowne theaters in Austin. They now most actively play in Jane ‘Austin’ Improv, delighting in creating regency rom-coms with the rest of the incredible cast.

people.name: Nick D’Agostino
people.bio: Nick is NYC based filmmaker and improviser. As a member of the improv troupe Father Figures, he performed in Austin Sketchfest and iO Fest in Chicago. He’s written and directed two feature films and is looking for representation.

people.name: Nick Kocher
people.bio: One half of BriTANicK. Former writer for SNL. Current writer of this exact sentence…

people.name: Nick Mandernach
people.bio: Nick Mandernach performs with house team JV, the improvised emo band Every Place I Cry, and silent soundtrack team Sassy Bluff. He’s written for TV shows like: The Great North , Duncanville , and Young Sheldon . His fiction has appeared in New England Review , EPOCH , and The Florida Review .

people.name: Nick Mestad
people.bio: Nick Mestad is a New York City based writer and comedian. He was a 2022 Just For Laughs New Face and can be seen in the sci-fi rom-com Molli & Max In The Future. He has appeared on The Tonight Show, Nathan Fielder’s The Rehearsal, Girls5Eva, Chicago Fire and Seeso’s Shrink. His work has been featured in Vulture and Clickhole, and he has amassed millions of views online with his highly physical brand of comedy.

people.name: Nick Murhling
people.bio: Nick is an LA performer with a background in improv, sketch, stand-up and characters. Nick can be seen doing shows all across LA, most notably on Betty Night at UCB and with his indie team Gunk! Clap and tell him he’s funny the next time you see him!

people.name: Nick Piacente
people.bio: Nick is an actor, director, VO artist, improv comedian, and teacher originally from Yonkers, NY. He has been seen on HBO Max, CBS, Amazon Prime, and AppleTV+. He teaches Scene Study and Movement for Stella Adler/NYU. When not working, Nick is hanging out with his partner and Aussie pup, watching NY sports, playing video games, or debating the best Marvel movie.

people.name: Nick Tiffany
people.bio: Nick Tiffany is a writer, performer, and pastel goth from the Big Little Lies part of California. Come see Nick perform Mondays with Harold Team This Is She!

people.name: Nicole Asava
people.bio: Nicole Asava (she/her/they/them) is an actor, director, improviser, and teacher who’s been featured on Barry, The Undoing, Younger, and The Break with Michelle Wolf, usually playing a masterful waiter or eccentric front desk staff. Nicole was in the NBC/UCB 2016 Showcase, the 2021 CBS Showcase, and studied comedy at the American Musical and Dramatic Academy, UCB, and Second City Chicago. Today you can see Nicole performing around NYC, with Asian AF, UCB Tourco, and really anywhere they can play and dance.

people.name: Nicole Weiss
people.bio: Nicole Weiss (she/they) is an actor, singer, songwriter, and redhead. She’s currently obsessed with the evolution of white women’s singing voices. @nicole___weiss although she’s “not” “on” “there” “right” “now” & www.nicoleweiss.com

people.name: Nicolette DeMarco
people.bio: Nicolette DeMarco is an LA-based actor, improviser, and award-winning writer from New Jersey. She has trained and performed at UCB, WGIS, and The Shared Experience, both in LA and NYC. She also has extensive acting training and a degree in film production. She can usually be found driving around LA listening to show tunes or a podcast. While she wishes she could say she splits her time equally between LA and New York, it is more like a 70/30 situation. You can catch Nicolette performing at UCB Lloyd night with her team, Freak Trap, or listen to her weekly improv podcast, Blank Stage Playhouse .

people.name: Nidhi Reddy
people.bio: Nidhi Reddy is a filmmaker, writer, and improviser based in Austin, TX. She performs in both The Rose and Hot Goss due to her love of the dramatic arts (gossip and scandal), but in her personal life remains conflict avoidant.

people.name: Nigil Whyte
people.bio: New Jersey in the house! New to Maude Night & thankful to be here. Let’s play a game; if you actually read this bio and can remember it, when we run into each other say, “Wu-Tang”.

people.name: Nikhil Deshpande
people.bio: Nikhil Deshpande is a writer, actor and stand up from Milwaukee, Wisconsin. He performs at UCB with his Lloyd team Odyssey, Asian AF and any other show that will have him. If you’re looking for more Nikhil-centric content you can listen to his podcast “Reality Bits”, follow him on social media, read one of his many pilots and sketches, or tell everyone you know to put him in things. Nikhil knows there should be a bit somewhere in this, but he’s currently late for a dinner. He he promises to update this with one soon.

people.name: Nikita Burdein
people.bio: Nikita Burdein was born in Ukraine, raised in Chicago, and now performs on Harold Night with his team The Prophecy. He can also be seen performing musical improv around NYC with Shitzprobe!, Premiere: The Improvised Musical, and Baby Wants Candy.

people.name: Nikki Denton
people.bio: Nikki Denton is a perfect specimen from Mount Juliet, TN. She has trained in improv, sketch and clown in Chicago, LA, and NYC (what’s next hunny?). You can catch her most recently in BCC’s MainStage Residency, Can You Take Our Picture?, or around town as Big Nikki.

people.name: Nikki Mckenzie
people.bio: Nikki McKenzie is a Filipina American actress and professional goofball, known for rom coms, prank shows and procedurals. Notable credits include The Wedding Contest, A Perfect Christmas Pairing, Prank Encounters, Seal Team, Hawaii Five O, Lone Star 911 and other shows with numbers.

people.name: Nikki Muller
people.bio: Nikki Muller is a writer, actor, comedian opera singer known for her sharp lyrics and coloratura flair. When not improvising music with friends, you’ll find her performing Robocopera, her new parody opera where she’s translated the 1987 action film into arias in German, French, and Italian.

people.name: Nikki Rodriguez
people.bio: Nikki Rodriguez is an actor, writer and comedian based in Los Angeles. On UCB stages, she’s performed with Mess Hall team Eclair Danes, indie team Harold Ramis and in various character/sketch shows. You can now catch her performing in the monthly Menudo’s Feliz Hour and check out her short film, Happy 4 U , which features an all-Latine cast of UCB talent! Nikki is also a freelance illustrator so hit her up for those show posters, baby!

people.name: Nikki Star
people.bio: Nicole “Nikki Star” Roberts is a talented actress and writer that hails from the city of Brotherly Love. She enjoys making people laugh and cultivating important conversations through comedy. When she’s not performing at UCB or on her Youtube page: Nikki Star TV. You can find her on a screen near you.

people.name: Niko Thiel
people.bio: Niko Thiel is an improviser, comedian, and actor from cornfield adjacent Fairfield, Iowa. You can see him perform at UCB on Harold Night and across town with his comedy trio, Gunk.

people.name: Nina Concepción
people.bio: Nina Concepción is an award-winning Latina actor, writer and comedian in Los Angeles. She was a performer on Harold Night for several years and can currently be seen as part of the ASSSSCAT cast. TV credits include 9-1-1 Lone Star , Shining Vale , Tacoma FD , Shifting Gears and NCIS . Nina co-hosts the comedy show “Obsessed: A comedy show about your hyper-fixations.” Her award-winning solo show, “Shame-Full,” has played to sold-out audiences in Los Angeles. Nina is a graduate of New York University’s Tisch School of the Arts, and has the student loans to prove it, honey!

people.name: Nina Rose Carlin
people.bio: Nina Rose Carlin is an actress, comedian, singer, and writer, originally from Long Island, New York. Her comedy cabaret, Seeking Representation, premiered at Edinburgh Festival Fringe in 2024 to critical success and sold-out shows. With a background in clown and experimental theatre, she has had her original plays produced around the country. A former ABC Casting showcase writer, Nina’s new one-woman show premieres at Hollywood Fringe Festival 2026!

people.name: Nishant Harshe
people.bio: I wrote for Maude: Fresh Faces two different times. I also write for the Pack Theater house sketch team Ugly Cry. And sometimes I do standup.

people.name: Nnamdi Ngwe
people.bio: Nnamdi Ngwe is an Emmy Award-winning Actor, Writer, and Comedian. He has written and performed on shows like The Amber Ruffin Show , The Bold and The Beautiful , A Black Lady Sketch Show , Blippi , and Tab Time . He can be seen performing live globally with his award winning sketch/improv group 3Peat.

people.name: Noa Worsek Rosenberg
people.bio: Noa Worsek Rosenberg is a Chicago born, Brooklyn-based writer, actor, and comedian with unused vocal potential and training from Vassar College, The Second City, Brooklyn Comedy Collective, UCB, and HB Studio. Most recently, she performed in BCC’s Mainstage Residency and, most often, she performs with her all-female improv and online short form group, Midwives. She is a contributor for Polyester Zine, and her first short film, Tofu Piccata, screened at the Big Apple Film Festival, New Faces New Voices, and more. Ask her about being ex-orthodox.

people.name: Noah Ramirez
people.bio: Noah is an LA-based artist who is just happy to be here and can barely ride a skateboard.

people.name: Nohely Quiroz
people.bio: Better Call Saul (NETFLIX) Monsters at Work (Disney TV Animation) Prep & Landing: The Snowball Protocol (Disney TV Animation) Stunts: On Call (AMAZON Wolf Entertainment) Sketch: CBS Showcase, Quick and Funny Musicals

people.name: Nolan Constantino
people.bio: Nolan is a native New Yorker, avid basketball fan, and graphic designer, in his little free time, he’s trying to get into tufting.

people.name: Nolan Purvis
people.bio: Known to casting directors as the poor man’s Charles Grodin, Nolan is a writer and comedian from North Carolina. He performs improv for UCB, The World’s Greatest Improv School and The Shared Experience, and has contributed to The Onion. His Social Security number is 807-14-7662.

people.name: Nour Hajar
people.bio: Nour (they/them) was born Mary Louise Streep on June 22, 1949, in Summit, New Jersey. Known for her versatility and adept accent work, she has been described as “the best actress of her generation.” When not chasing an EGOT (fingers crossed on that Grammy!), she can be found in LA going fully method in what some are calling her most chilling role to date: the life of a queer writer/director/performer with no reps.

people.name: Noël Wells
people.bio: Noël Wells is a filmmaker, writer, musician and comedic actor from San Antonio, Texas. After graduating from the University of Texas at Austin in 2010, she moved to Los Angeles and began training at UCB where she performed on the Maude team “New Money.” She was hired on Saturday Night Live as a featured player for the 2013-2014 season, and the following year was cast in the debut season of Netflix’s “Master of None” as the character Rachel. She wrote, starred, and directed the indie feature “Mr. Roosevelt” which played dozens of film festivals and was bought by Netflix. In 2019 she released her debut album “It’s So Nice!” Throughout the years she’s voiced over a dozen animated characters and continues to develop her own projects in TV and film.

people.name: Olivia Barahal
people.bio: Olivia is Swedish-American comedian and writer from Hawaii. She is known for her appearances on Yelp, where she was described as “the face of gentrification in Echo Park”.

people.name: Olivia Connolly
people.bio: Olivia Connolly is a performer and writer from Kentucky. She studied Creative Writing and Screenwriting at the University of Southern California. Olivia is a proud cat mom and currently works as a Content Creator & Strategist collaborating with brands like Samsung, Amazon, HBO Max, Bath & Body Works, Dancing with the Stars , and Love Island USA .

people.name: Olivia Heifetz
people.bio: Olivia Heifetz is an improvisor, actor, and director based in San Diego, California. She has been making shit up semi-professionally for over a decade, performing at festivals including San Francisco Sketchfest, Del Close Marathon, and Fracas! Improv Festival. You can see her perform across Southern California as part of Rat City and The Joey Summers Show. She will also happily identify birds for you.

people.name: Olivia Oreskovich
people.bio: Olivia is an actor, writer and comedian. She’s written for Maude Fresh Faces, Sketch Cram, Sketch Cagematch, and bit shows at UCB. Olivia also writes and performs around LA with her independent sketch team, Goose. She studied Theatre at Loyola Marymount University, with an emphasis in physical comedy performance and comedy writing. She is currently seeking representation.

people.name: Olivia Scenna
people.bio: Olivia Scenna is a comedian, actor, and producer based in NYC. You can catch her performing standup around the city, doing improv at Upright Citizen’s Brigade and Brooklyn Comedy Collective, or writing on the Boogiemanja sketch team Picture Day. She has also been in a food fight before.

people.name: Onyi Okoli
people.bio: Onyi Okoli is a writer, comedian and actress based in New York. She improvised weekly in UCB’s Dime Piece and Dog House. Catch Onyi performing with her indie improv troupe Branded Silk as they perform both within the United States and Internationally. Follow @BrandedSilk on IG

people.name: Orly Lewittes
people.bio: Orly Lewittes is an NYC based comedian and writer! She makes up one half of the sketch duo Boy Crazy, which performs regularly at UCB, she writes for Maude night, and performs stand up all around New York. In 2024, Orly’s screenplay, Bat Zilla, won the Grand Prize in the ScreenCraft Short Screenplay Competition, and she went viral for her commencement speech at Northwestern University alongside Billy Eichner. When she’s not writing and performing, you can catch Orly eating sushi 🙂 She loves sushi.

people.name: Oscar Landry
people.bio: Oscar Landry is a writer and performer with the sketch comedy group Craig. He spent a miserable childhood in Los Angeles, a horrific four years of college in Michigan, and is currently in agony in Brooklyn. In addition to comedy, Oscar is a filmmaker and graphic designer.

people.name: Oscar Lemus
people.bio: Oscar comes from a frenetic (yet loving) Latinx family in South Florida, a proud first-gen byproduct of divorced immigrant parents transposing his “life experiences” into a silly sense of humor — as if joking about things was the only way to process life. Currently, Oscar is a Staff Writer for Phineas and Ferb at Disney Television Animation. He is also legally obliged to express an undying love for his cats.

people.name: Oscar Montoya
people.bio: An actor, writer and choreographer hailing from Palmira, Colombia, Oscar has been performing at UCB LA since 2015. An alumni of Harold Night and Maude Night, he is also a teacher at UCB. He’s appeared in shows like Reno 911!, FOX’s Bless the Harts and Starz’ Minx along with many national commercials for Snickers, Pizza Hut and Jack in the Box.

people.name: Otto Graham
people.bio: Otto Graham is an actor and writer from Atlanta, GA. He has been in the movie business since high school when he started working at a movie theatre. A job he stills holds today. He has always been the “funny” guy. He has sharpened his comedy skills at the Upright Citizens Brigade, going through all improv and sketch comedy levels. He created Music Video Improv.

people.name: Owen Engesser
people.bio: Owen Engesser is an actor and improvisor originally from the Chicagoland area. Owen has a B.F.A in Performing Arts from the Savannah College of Art and Design. He performs regularly with the sweetest improv group NYC has ever seen: Pie Town.

people.name: Owen Kreger-Stickles
people.bio: Owen Kreger-Stickles is an actor and writer at the University of Southern California where he studies in the School of Dramatic Arts. At USC, Owen is a member of Commedus Interruptus, the university’s premier improv and sketch comedy group. Owen has performed in LA at UCB’s College Comedy Night, and in NYC at The PIT’s Annual Sketchfest.

people.name: Owen Matthew Welsh
people.bio: Owen Matthew is a writer, actor, and comedian from Colchester, UK. He’s worked for HBO and writes/edits/produces for influencers. He loves near-future sci-fi and pretentious books. Also singing.

people.name: Owen O.
people.bio: Owen O. is a singer, performer, comedian, composer, friend, lover, and viral tiktok sensation. In 2025 his interactive comedy musical “Operation: Destroy Beff Jezos” made waves at the Edinburgh Fringe Fest after two previous sold out runs in Brooklyn. His high-camp drag comedy musical “The Worlds Most Interesting Sisters” is currently running monthly at UCB NY. This guy loves comedy and musicals.

people.name: Paige Elson
people.bio: Paige Elson is an actor, writer, comedian, and a Harold Night/Maude Night Alum. Her sketch team, Anointed Greens, has performed several sketch revues and been commissioned by comedy legend Chris Rock. Paige likes to explore themes of intersectional identity, female friendships, and unconventional families and often produces, writes, and stars in her own work, including her short film, Drunk Girl Therapist , which screened at major comedy festivals and won honorable mention at the HBO Women in Comedy Festival. Her writing training came from esteemed fellowships such as the Hillman Grad Mentorship Program, the Nickelodeon Writing Program, and the Black Boy/Black Girl Writes Mentorship. When she’s not creating, you can catch her belting Mariah Carey songs, snapping film photos, and telling all her curly-haired friends which products to try. You can also catch her on Substack.

people.name: Paige Williams
people.bio: Paige is a talented comedic sketch writer whose unique life experiences pour out into her work in unordinary ways. Never afraid to go against the grain, her use of dark humor and uncomfortable scenarios force her audience to laugh despite the misfortunate her may characters encounter. She strives to create work that can appeal to a wide and diverse audience. If she can make both her parents and her little brother laugh, then she considers it a success.

people.name: Pam Murphy
people.bio: Pam Murphy is a writer, actor and comedian who has been performing improv and sketch comedy at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatres in NY and LA since 2005. Pam has appeared on Night Court, Corporate, Superstore, Single Parents, Mapleworth Murders, Get Shorty, Crazy Ex-Girlfriend, Another Period, Angel from Hell, Key & Peele, Girlboss, Hotwives of Las Vegas, Hotwives of Orlando, Chasing Life, Welcome to Sweden, 30 Rock, Happy Endings, Comedy Bang! Bang!, and much, much more. She currently voices Stupendous on the FOX animated show Krapopolis.

people.name: Paris Adkins
people.bio: Paris Adkins (they/them) is a non-binary improviser, stand-up, and writer based in Brooklyn, NYC. They can be seen at their monthly improv show at UCB, The Assignment, their monthly stand-up show at Sleepwalk, Old Souls, or around town with their improv team, Dangerous Marine Life.

people.name: Patrick Keene
people.bio: Patrick is an improviser in New York City. You can currently see him performing around town with Duchovny and his good friend, Steven Polletta. If you like what he does or just want to watch people yell at each other for two hours, he’d love for you to watch the fully-improvised feature he made, Carroll Gardens Cobble Hill: https://vimeo.com/user50328293/cgch

people.name: Patrick McDonald
people.bio: Patrick McDonald is a queer writer, director, and comedian from Mansfield, Texas. Acting: “Army Of The Dead”, “Zack Snyder’s Justice League”, “AM I OK?”, “The Late Late Show”. Writing: SMOSH, Dropout, Melon’s House Party (Wondery), FunnyOrDie, and commercials you’ve seen. Patrick is a DJ on Radio Margaritaville and host of their interview podcast “License To Chill”. At UCB, Patrick has been on a performer and director on Harold Teams and Maude Teams since 2012, when he used to spend much more money on props than he does now. He went to Emerson College. Surprise!

people.name: Patrick Noth
people.bio: Patrick Noth is an Emmy nominated comedian. You may have seen him on Comedy Central, MTV, IFC, TV Land, NBC, Nickelodeon, and a many digital platforms. He has worked as a writer and director for Comedy Central, UCB and more. Produced music for a bunch of stuff too. Patrick is writing this bio and earlier today I threw up.

people.name: Patrick Shen
people.bio: Patrick Shen is a comedy writer/performer based in LA. He currently writes for the Maude team TOWN CRIER. He also performs with the sketch team DAYTRIP and improv team CHONK, both at the Pack Theater. Catch him around town trying to find the best bowl of ramen! Yum Yum!

people.name: Paul Heredia
people.bio: Paul Heredia is a queer Mexican comedian from Issaquah, Washington. He studied improv and sketch at Second City Hollywood, iO West, The Groundlings, and UCB, and has performed on house teams at UCB (Lloyd Night, Maude Night) and The Groundlings, where he is an incoming member of the Sunday Company. As a writer, he has worked with Warner Bros., Legendary Pictures, and the YouTube sketch channel SMOSH (25M+ subscribers), as well as freelancing for Cartoon Network. As an actor, he has appeared on Fox’s I Can See Your Voice and truTV’s Laff Mobb’s Laff Tracks and You Can Do Better , along with national commercials for DoorDash, Manscaped, Zelle, and Apple. Independently, he writes, acts, and runs the social media channel The Daily Misinformer , which has amassed over 300K followers and over 80 million views. You can catch him performing live around Los Angeles.

people.name: Paul Robalino
people.bio: Paul Robalino is improviser, writer and producer from New Jersey, best known for his work with Dropout (previously CollegeHumor). His work can be seen on series like Game Changer, Make Some Noise, Smartypants, Very Important People, Dirty Laundry, Monét’s Slumber Party and more. Former improv teams include UCB Mess Hall team Rumple, indie team Gringos, and Second City team Snacks.

people.name: Paul Scheer
people.bio: Paul Scheer has performed at UCB since 1997 and was a part of the very first UCB Harold team, Gigantic Man. He went on to co-found the improv and sketch team Respecto Montalban in 1999, which performed weekly together and was known for their award-winning show George Bush is a Motherfucker (co-directed by Adam McKay). Paul then moved to Los Angeles where he started the long-form show MySpace that eventually became Facebook and ran every Wednesday night at the theater until 2020. Paul is a SAG Award-winning actor, writer, podcaster, and director. He co-created and starred in Human Giant with Aziz Ansari and Rob Huebel. He also was the creator and star of the Adult Swim show NTSF:SD:SUV::. He starred in FX’s The League, ABC’s Fresh Off the Boat, and Showtime’s Black Monday. In addition to these shows, he produced The Real Housewives series on Hulu and took his decade-long running UCB Show Crash Test into a special for Comedy Central. You can hear his award-winning podcast, How Did This Get Made, and Unspooled wherever you get your podcasts. His upcoming book will be released by Harper Collins. Paul also writes for Marvel and DC comics.

people.name: Pavar Snipe
people.bio: Pavar Snipe is a comedian, actor, writer, disability advocate, and Emmy-award nominated producer. Her credits include: DreamWorks’ Dragons the Nine Realms (VO actor), ABC’s When Nature Calls with Helen Mirren (writer, VO actor) and ABC 7 NY, NBC NY, NBC LA (producer). She was a writer for the 2018 CBS showcase and has performed all over the country including at the 2024 Austin Sketch Fest, where her one-woman show “Codeswitch” sold out and at Lincoln Center with “ReelAbilities LIVE!” which was also a sold-out show. When she’s not hosting her disability comedy podcast “It’s Not Even Like That” or teaching the next generation of comedy geniuses as a professor at The New School, you can find Pavar at the UCB Theater in New York where she co-produces and performs in the monthly Ninja’s Assemble comedy show. Pavar’s production company Limitless Laughs Productions, is dedicated to amplifying the voices and talents of the disabled community through various forms of storytelling.

people.name: Peter Grosz
people.bio: Peter Grosz is an American actor and writer. TV Writing Credits include: The Colbert Report, Late Night With Seth Meyers, The President Show. Grosz can also be seen on-screen in HBO’s VEEP. In April 2023, Grosz made his Broadway debut playing Robert Sarnoff, President of NBC, in the Tony Award-winning play Good Night, Oscar.

people.name: Peter Johnston
people.bio: Peter is a writer and game designer based in Brooklyn. He’s written for and been featured in work for The Discovery Channel, Gimlet Media, and the Late Show with David Letterman. He is thrilled to be a part of Harold Night, and is more than happy to discuss your most recent slice of pizza.

people.name: Peter McNerney
people.bio: Peter has been teaching improv for over 20 years. Originally from Chicago he has served as Head of the Advanced Improv Program at the Magnet Theater in New York City since 2010 and is one half of the renowned improv duo, Trike, which The New York Times calls “reliably one of the best improv performances in the city.” As an actor Peter has appeared in High Maintenance (HBO), Search Party (TBS), The Tonight Show (NBC) as well as a whole bunch of commercials. Peter also serves as the NY Artistic Director and Casting Director for the nationally respected education and media company The Story Pirates with whom he has written, produced and performed for such chart topping podcasts as The Story Pirates Podcast, The Cat in the Hat Podcast and Looney Tunes Presents – Bugs & Daffy’s Thanksgiving Road Trip.

people.name: Peter Walsh
people.bio: Peter Walsh (He/him) Is an actor, writer and comedian in NYC. At the UCB, Peter currently performs on the Betty Night team Heyday. Last season he performed on the Maude Night team Baby Todd, and has appeared on shows, like Characters Welcome and the monthly ‘Best Of’ showcase Starpunch. He also performs across the city, at venues like the BCC and the PIT, with his original character comedy, and improv with his team Really, Chef? Outside of comedy, Peter acts in regional and off-broadway theatre productions and independent films.

people.name: Petey Salas
people.bio: Petey Salas started has over 14 years experience in Improv comedy. Really hoping to make money off it one day.

people.name: Philip Labes
people.bio: Philip Labes is a singer-songwriter, actor, and writer with 300,000 followers for his original music. Philip opened for Jason Mraz on tour in 2021, after the Grammy winner came across Philip playing a livestream for 15 people. In 2022/3 he created an album for each season, inspired by Vivaldi’s Four Seasons, and just completed his first international tour. His viral songs have generated tens of millions of views on TikTok, Instagram, Reddit and Tumblr. He has written for multiple TV shows on the CW and digital, and as an actor, he’s somehow been in multiple HBO period pieces, like Watchmen, Minx, and the upcoming Duster from JJ Abrams.

people.name: PJ McCormick
people.bio: PJ McCormick is a mixed Filipino actor, writer, and comedian based in Los Angeles. At UCB, he’s a performer on the house team LOHAN, creator and head writer of the late-night wrestling show Midnight Rumble, host of ONE BIG JAM, and original cast member of Filipino AF. He was a contributing writer and producer at The Howard Stern Show . PJ won multiple pro wrestling championships as one half of the tag team The Nerdy Boys. He has starred in films like Ruthie Joins A Death Cult and appeared in multiple national commercials.

people.name: Prerika Chawla
people.bio: Prerika is a South Asian American improviser and comedian based in Los Angeles. She performs weekly on UCB’s Lloyd team Freak Trap and WE Improv’s Say When. You can also find her hosting improv jams at WE and WGIS.

people.name: Preston Martin
people.bio: Preston Martin (he/they) has performed in over fifty venues across New York City and in nine countries across the world. The New Yorker called their work “captivating,” The New York Times found them “fabulously committed” and “hilarious,” and Theatermania asserts they are “almost certainly homosexual.” On screen they have worked with Greta Gerwig, Quinn Shephard, and Mary Stuart Masterson. Preston also builds video art, including a one person multimedia project through a residency at Ars Nova and multiple music videos for chanteuse Grace McLean. www.prestonmartin.com

people.name: Priscilla Davies
people.bio: After an unexpected stint as a an elementary school teacher, Priscilla Davies decided to stop living a lie and move to LA to pursue writing, acting, and comedy. She has since been in numerous productions, countless commercials, and won The Fresh Voices Screenplay Competition.

people.name: Priya Pappu
people.bio: Priya Pappu is an improviser and actor in LA by way of Nashville, TN, and originally Northern California. The proud daughter of immigrants from India and Finland, Priya got her start in comedy competing for attention with her six siblings at the dinner table. Priya’s acting career spans musical theater, commercial, and indie film like you’ve never seen…4real. Catch her at Lloyd night on the UCB stage or on karaoke night at select locations.

people.name: PT Jones
people.bio: PT is a Black, gay, non-binary comedian from South Carolina — which means they can cook you collard greens, read you for filth, and process your childhood in one sitting. They’ve trained at Second City, iO Chicago, and both UCBs (because healing isn’t linear). You’ve seen them on Bad Drag Race at Dynasty Typewriter, and now they’re making their UCB debut in Sing Out, Louise! ChatGPT definitely wrote this.

people.name: Quinn Boyes
people.bio: Quinn Boyes is an improviser, writer & comedian originally from New Jersey.

people.name: Rachael Empson
people.bio: Rachael began performing improv at Istanbul Comedy Club in 2019. Since then, improv has become one of her favorite ways to experience a new city and the people who call it home. Now based in Austin, she’s thrilled to be joining the DCM stage and adding New York City to the list.

people.name: Rachael Mason
people.bio: Rachael Mason is a native New Yorker who now lives in the very hot city of Los Angeles. She has taught at UCB since 2009 and has written like probably, no exaggeration, one million sketches. She has also written for UCB Maude teams, talk shows, sketch shows, and a plethora of embarrassing journal entries that will hopefully never see the light of day.

people.name: Rachael Williamson
people.bio: Rachael is an Austin-based improviser and Coldtowne alum. She has been performing since 2021 with the troupes Sorry for Partying and Hot Goss.

people.name: Rachel Baldwin
people.bio: Rachel Baldwin is a Native American improviser and podcaster, known for her podcast Girls off the Rails. When she’s not podcasting or improvising she can be found climbing at the gym or preparing for ski season.

people.name: Rachel Bloom
people.bio: Rachel co-created and starred as Rebecca Bunch in The CW musical comedy-drama series Crazy Ex-Girlfriend (2015–2019), for which she received numerous accolades, including a Golden Globe Award, a TCA Award, a Critics’ Choice Television Award, and a Primetime Emmy Award.

people.name: Rachel Chada
people.bio: Rachel Chada is an NYC-based performer and a big proponent of doing what you love (eating Taco Bell and crying often). She has performed at UCBTNY since 2017 and was previously seen on the UCB house team Bounce House and in the shows We Will Slay and Tag Team. Currently, Rachel is featured in Woodstock at UCBTNY and performs on the indie Boogiemanja sketch team, Both Hands. In addition to being a comedian, Rachel is also a therapist which is a fun combination to try to explain to people at parties.

people.name: Rachel Chapman
people.bio: Rachel Chapman is a writer, comedian, and voice actor based in Los Angeles. She’s currently a staff writer at Elite Daily, and red carpet correspondent for Bustle Digital Group. Rachel has also written headlines for Reductress, promos for Spike TV, and been the host of podcasts You’ve Got Hanks, Inside The Disney Vault, and I Need To Talk TikTok. Her previous UCB credits include house teams The Up and Up and Shimmy, as well as host of The Telephone Show. She’s also worked at Late Night Starring Jimmy Fallon , Saturday Night Live , Freeform, and Sesame Workshop. When she’s not working, Rachel spends most of her time with her cat Lady Tom Hanks, eating food, or watching fan edits of BTS.

people.name: Rachel Horwitz
people.bio: Rachel Horwitz is an actor, writer, and director. she has performed in shows at UCB, The PIT, The Secret Theatre, and The Tank, and was recently a guest artist at The Barn at Lee residency in Lee, MA. She has directed and produced shows that have gone up at the Wild Project, The Brick, PAGEANT, The Loading Dock, The Green Room 42, Dixon Place, and The Tank. Rachel always carries her BFA in Dramatic Writing from Tisch School of the Arts with her so that when you inevitably ask about her time at NYU, she can tell you her favorite memories of four years writing and performing with Hammerkatz. xoxo @notrachelhorwitz

people.name: Rachel Marsh
people.bio: Rachel Marsh stars alongside Rob Lowe and Fred Armisen in the upcoming Netflix show UNSTABLE premiering March 30th.

people.name: Rachel McKay Steele
people.bio: Rachel McKay Steele is a writer and comedian. She was a Just For Laugh’s Stand up and Pitch competition finalist Her feature script, Adult Bat Mitzvah was shortlisted for the 2026 WScripted+ Cannes Screenplay List presented by Mubi and is currently in development with Burn Later Productions. She has studied improv at The UCB Theater and WGIS and clown with The Pandemonium Studio and The Elysian. She has not yet quit her day job as an Associate Creative Director in advertising.

people.name: Rachel Niswander
people.bio: Rachel Niswander is a writer and performer based in New York City. When Rachel isn’t writing for the Maude team Mom’s World, she can be seen performing stand-up, hosting her late-night comedy show The See You Later Show, or being a creep online. Her credits include San Francisco Sketchfest, Montreal Sketchfest, and Philadelphia Sketchfest.

people.name: Rachel Pegram
people.bio: Rachel Pegram is an LA based comedian, writer, and actor. Most recently she was a writer for Peacock’s Killing it. Prior to that she was a writer on Netflix’s Blockbuster, Harley Quinn for HBO Max, NBC’s Connecting and NBC’s Kenan. And in 2019 she was selected to participate in the New Face Characters showcase at the prestigious Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal.

people.name: Rachel Pegram
people.bio: Rachel Pegram is an LA based comedian, writer, and actor. Most recently she was a writer for Peacock’s Killing it. Prior to that she was a writer on Netflix’s Blockbuster, Harley Quinn for HBO Max, NBC’s Connecting and NBC’s Kenan. And in 2019 she was selected to participate in the New Face Characters showcase at the prestigious Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal.

people.name: Rachel Taenzler
people.bio: Rachel Taenzler is a writer and performer from Brooklyn, NY. You can see her on Harold Night with her team Christopher Basketball, all around NYC (and festivals) with her indie team Mercy Santos, or on TikTok cooking vegetarian food. Before the pandemic, she was on Lloyd Night and UCB’s Touring Company. Her scripts have placed in the PAGE Awards, the WGA-E Made in New York Fellowship, Screencraft, and more.

people.name: Rachel Van Nes
people.bio: Rachel Van Nes was born and raised on an almond farm in Modesto, CA, consistently ranked in the Top 10 Most Miserable Cities in the US on account of all the car theft, drugs, and murder. She’s come a long way (eh, about 300 miles) and now writes for television. Rachel was most recently staffed on NETFLIX’S INSIDE JOB S2 and has been published in Reductress, McSweeney’s Internet Tendency, and The New Yorker. In her spare time, Rachel aspires to be a soup witch.

people.name: Raina Deerwater
people.bio: Raina Deerwater is a California-raised, Pittsburgh-based improviser. She is one of the first teachers at UCB Pittsburgh and regularly performs on teams Barbara, A Mother and Fondue For Two. She got her improv reading from UCBLA, and got her vibes from her hippie NorCal school where they held hands in a circle each morning instead of having homeroom.

people.name: Raiza Licea
people.bio: Raiza is an actor, writer, comedian, and host from Miami, FL. Her recent credits include HBO Max’s Emmy nominated show HACKS. She can be found performing monthly or more with her comedy team Spanish Aqui Presents, who had the FIRST all Latino show at UCB, and the FIRST and ONLY Latino podcast on Earwolf. When she’s not acting, you can find her at UCB as one of the Artistic Directors or maybe ballin’ – because duh, basketball is LIFE !!

people.name: Rami Essa
people.bio: Rami is an actor/writer hailing from the DMV area. DMV stands for Department of Motor Vehicles. He is an alumni of Maude Night.

people.name: Randi DeMarco
people.bio: Randi DeMarco is a singer, actor, writer and proud Los Angeles native! She has been seen on stage throughout Los Angeles and across the country, including The Secret Garden at the Ahmanson. She also voices Twist Queen, the LOL Doll. LOVE YOU.

people.name: Randy Reiman
people.bio: Randy Reiman is an improviser with the teams Scrambled Legs, Suburban Adventure, Helix, and Peabody. He wrote and starred in the FringeNYC show ‘Attention Span’ and also performed in the FringeNYC show ‘Enlargement’. Randy designed the board game Skate Summer, an homage to Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater, which received Dice Tower’s ‘Seal of Excellence’.

people.name: Raquel Palmas
people.bio: Raquel Palmas is a New York-based improviser, actress, and musical comedian. She proudly channels her comedic energy into bringing her diverse voice to the stage and the screen. She studied theatre at Brooklyn College before shifting her focus to comedy and touring internationally. Boom Chicago (2023-2025), Shizprobe(2022- present, Baby Wants Candy (2019-present), North Coast (2018 – present)

people.name: Rasheda Crockett
people.bio: Rasheda Crockett is an award-winning writer, comedian, and actor whose sharp wit and fearless performances light up every stage. She’s a WGA writer, CBS Showcase alum, and creator of The Rasheda Crockett Sketch Comedy Show , winner of a W3 Award for comedy innovation.

people.name: Ray Cordova
people.bio: Ray Cordova is an actor, comedian, writer and producer. He has performed on Lloyd Night as well as Harold Night. In 2019, Ray co-created and co-starred in “ASTRONOMY CLUB: THE SKETCH SHOW” on Netfilix

people.name: Raymond Santos
people.bio: Raymond is a writer and comedian in New York City that has traveled across the country to perform in improv festivals with his improv teams Mercy Santos (God’s Favorite Improv Team) and Shrecc. Raymond currently writes for the UCB Maude Night team Ponyboy, and formerly wrote for the Maude team BedRock, as well as improvised in the Lloyd team Shrecc. Outside of UCB, Raymond has found success in his screenwriting with multiple projects reaching semi-finalist, and in one case finalist, rounds in several television pilot competitions (ScreenCraft, WeScreenplay, Scriptapalooza, amongst others).

people.name: Rebecca Andrews
people.bio: Rebecca has been performing improv since 2019. She’s a graduate of the ColdTowne Conservatory in Austin, TX and currently a student of UCB. She also started drinking coffee at age 2.

people.name: Rebecca East
people.bio: Based out of Austin, TX, Rebecca East is a passionate actor, improvisor, instructor and events producer involved in the Austin comedy scene. She is a founding member and player of Jane Austin Improv and has been seen in countless shows at ColdTowne Theater, Fallout Theater, and The Hideout Theatre where she also teaches beginner to advanced improv classes. She is also an Executive Producer of Austin Sketch Fest, come check it out– atxsketchfest.com

people.name: Rebecca McGivney
people.bio: Born and raised in Brooklyn, Rebecca McGivney began studying improv at UCB NY in 2013. After moving to Baltimore, MD in 2020 to earn her Masters in Library and Information Science at the University of Maryland, she began teaching and coaching at the Baltimore Improv Group (BIG) and currently plays on the BIG House Team Reggie and indie teams Melk and GiGi.

people.name: Rebecca Salzhauer
people.bio: Rebecca Salzhauer is an actor, writer, and native New Yorker. She does improv as a member of Stress Dream and is a co-creator and star of the web series “i’m so happy for you!!!!!!”

people.name: Rebecca Skowron
people.bio: Rebecca Skowron is an actor/comedian who has performed in different theatres around the country as well as onboard a Disney cruise ship* (*disclaimer: she is not a Disney Adult.) Rebecca has a BFA from Ithaca College and has performed at comedy clubs around the city as well as SketchFest at the PIT. Find her on TikTok at @rebecca_skowron.

people.name: Reece Moulthrop
people.bio: Voted most likely to have hot dog face by On The Spot improv, which i’ve been apart of for almost a year! Majoring in fashion business at Kansas State University! I’m very excited to be here!

people.name: Reed Rosenberg
people.bio: Reed Rosenberg is an actor/comedian based in LA. He’ll soon appear in two upcoming feature films, including California Skate (Henry Winkler, Bridget Moynihan, Jay Mohr) and Shadow of the Sun (Danny Trejo). Reed’s two favorite things are improv and chicken wings, so he and Alejandro Rios combined them with The Hottest Improv Show in Town. He’s having a great time!

people.name: Regina Soto
people.bio: Regina has been performing improv for almost 20 years in the Austin, Texas area, and is in the Harold troupe Candy Necklace and Glitter Witch, a three-person troupe performing weekly in Austin. Regina likes peanut M&Ms, reality TV and sitting on the couch.

people.name: Reid Robison
people.bio: Reid Robison is an Actor/Comedian hailing from Lexington, Kentucky! Too tall and with red curly hair, Reid quickly gave up on being a cool bank robber… they’d find him too fast. Reid can instead be seen performing stand-up comedy all over town, performing with his sketch group Definitely Not Robots and acting in commercials, film, and TV!

people.name: Rekha Shankar
people.bio: Rekha Shankar is an executive story editor for Grand Crew (NBC) and for Andy Samberg and Neil Campbell’s Digman! (Comedy Central). She was also a staff writer for Animaniacs (Hulu) and Head Writer for CollegeHumor’s Hardly Working series. In 2020 she was selected for Just For Laughs: New Faces – Creators. She can be seen in Between Two Ferns: The Movie, Hacks, Aunty Donna’s House of Fun, as Cupid in a Hershey’s commercial, and in a Zelle ad with Christina Ricci. She is also on stage every week at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre LA.

people.name: Rel Francis
people.bio: Rel Francis is a Jamaican-American writer, director, comedian, and editor based in New York and is currently the Tech Director of UCB NY. He broke into the industry by editing TV commercials and eventually cutting a Super Bowl ad (it was for yogurt). From there, he became a longtime Creative Director, producing promotional content for films such as the illustrious (Oscar-snubbed) Fast and Furious 9: The Fast Saga. Rel would love to write/perform for your show with the perspective of a Black Father raising a tiny tuxedo cat. He hopes to one day win an N.E.G.O.T. (Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, Tony, and NAACP).

people.name: Remy Germinario
people.bio: Remy Germinario is a NYC based actor,comedian, singer and writer. New York Theatre: Rough Trade (The Tank) More Than All The World (Theatre For The New City), Boys Who Tricked Me (MT Factory), Bradley Cole (FringeNYC Award Winner), When I Started Dating Men (Dixon Place), The Paige Turner Holiday Shows (Laurie Beechman Theatre). Regional: Buyer and Cellar (Florida Studio Theatre, Riverside Theatre, Cardinal Stage Company), America’s Sexiest Couple (Cape May Stage), Caveman Play (ArtHouse). Film/TV: The Feast of Stephen (dir James Franco), Synonymous With, An Actor Unprepared and Livin’ the Dream. Voiceover: Peter in the hit musical video game Peter Panic (Adult Swim Games), Radio Play Revival and Jasper in The World To Come musical podcast (dir.Rachel Klein). Remy is also a stand up/sketch comic and can be seen at comedy clubs all over the city and on Maude Night at UCB NY. He is also known on TikTok with 38K followers and Instagram with 16K followers for his impressions of Food Network Chefs and other viral comedic content! As a teacher, he has taught at NYU, The Lee Strasberg Institute, Actor’s Training Center, Playing for Others, Florida State Thespians and more! BFA NYU (CAP21/Strasberg Studios)

people.name: Reva Grimball
people.bio: Reva Grimball is a writer and director based in New York City. She is a Saturday Night Live Scholarship recipient and was also accepted into the Diverse As Fuck Onion writing program. She is the creator the Drunk Bathroom Girl TikTok series which won Best Original Concept at NYC Web Fest, writes for Gold Digital Comedy and Cracked.com, directs and runs the Director Program at the indie sketch comedy collective BoogieManja, sings with her musical improv team The Understudies, and teaches sketch writing at The Upright Citizen’s Brigade and The Second City.

people.name: Rich Armstead
people.bio: Rich Armstead is an Actor, Improviser, Director, and Minister who proudly represents his home state of New Jersey. Favorite memories on the UCB stage include winning the 2010 3 on 3 Cagematch Tournament with “Hot Cheese Fiesta”, Serving as the performing director for “The Spotlight”, Acting in the “Livia Scott Sketch Program”, “The 2017 Tony Awardz”, and collaborating with his Maude Team “CHUNK”. His proudest accomplishment during his time at the theater is having created the “Solid Footing” sketch initiative with Dejen Tesfagiorgis to serve as a utility to feed participation in the “Liquid Courage” sketch open mic for which he later was invited to Co-Host. He has been seen in “The Blacklist” on NBC and “Deadbeat” on Hulu, as well as having the opportunity to be featured in the cold open for an episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert”. He continues to share his love of performing with the New York community at large and endeavors to share with the world the theme of his ministry, “May We All Be Blessed To Grow” Rich Armstead can be contacted via email at [email protected]

people.name: Rich Rosario
people.bio: Rich is an actor, comedian, & writer who trained at both the Magnet Theater and Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre. He has performed at festivals across the country including: Boston Comedy Arts Festival, Chicago Sketch Fest, the Del Close Marathon, and North Carolina Comedy Arts Festival. Rich is a voiceover & commercial actor and has helped sell such items as leather jackets, e-cigarettes, toaster ovens, and paint! If you’re looking for an “ethnically ambiguous young dad” – he’s your guy.

people.name: Richy Storrs
people.bio: Richy is an actor and musician in Los Angeles. He has been told he gives people “pleasant arrhythmia” and conducts “unsanctioned sorcery” while performing. You can hear him screaming at you in video games and cartoons as a voice actor. He has acted in and composed music for live theater in L.A. for 10 years and will continue to as long as people can stand it.

people.name: Riki Lindhome
people.bio: Riki Lindhome is an actress, writer and songwriter. Along with Natasha Leggero, she is the co-creator and star of Another Period. She is also one half of the musical comedy duo Garfunkel and Oates and co-created their self-tiled show on IFC. Riki has written songs for The Lego Movie 2, The Addams Family, The SpongeBob Movie, Waffles and Mochi, Duncanville and Big Bang Theory. Her acting roles include Wednesday, Knives Out, The Wolf of Snow Hollow and Kidding.

people.name: Riley Doyle
people.bio: Riley Doyle was born and raised in the Bay Area, but now calls LA home. You can catch him on the UCB stage with Sing Out Louise or around town with his indie team Shelby.

people.name: Riley Westling
people.bio: Riley Westling (he/they/she) is a diva first, and a writer/performer second. They’ve spent the last four years at a production company spearheading the development of four network-sold shows (but they never got made!! lol!!). You can watch them as a series regular on HBO’s Book of Queer . They’re currently going through a big ants on a log and hot toddy phase. They hope to one day be a hummingbird.

people.name: Risa Harms
people.bio: Risa Harms is a comedian who performs on Harold Night with The Prophecy and all over the place with Asian AF and Old Yorkers. Her work has been published in McSweeney’s and she also moonlights in college admissions. Risa paid for her wedding with prize money she won on “Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?” (she did not have a million dollar wedding.)

people.name: RJ Williams
people.bio: RJ teaches at The Second City NY and performs with musical teams Baby Wants Candy and North Coast. He is also a musician, playwright, and rapper.

people.name: Rob Corddry
people.bio: Rob Corddry was on “The Daily Show with Jon Stewart” from 2002 to 2006. In 2008 Corddry created CHILDRENS HOSPITAL for Adult Swim which is currently in its sixth season. CH has won two Emmy’s for “Outstanding Special Class: Short-format Live-Action Entertainment Programs”. Also for Adult Swim, Rob created the show NEWSREADERS. Film/TV: OLD SCHOOL, BLADES OF GLORY, SEMI-PRO, HAROLD & KUMAR: ESCAPE FROM GUANTANAMO BAY, HOT TUB TIME MACHINE I and II, BUTTER, SEEKING A FRIEND FOR THE END OF THE WORLD, PAIN & GAIN, WARM BODIES, IN A WORLD…, HELL BABY, RAPTURE-PALOOZA, THE WAY WAY BACK, SEX TAPE and the upcoming HBO show BALLERS.

people.name: Rob Scerbo
people.bio: Rob Scerbo is a comedian from the hot deserts of Phoenix, Arizona. He is a writer, actor, and director. He’s had films premiere at Tribeca Film Festival (2023) and Beyond Fest (2023). He is also a stage 3 testicular cancer survivor! Dramatic!

people.name: Rob Warner
people.bio: Rob is an actor, singer and comedian based in LA just trying his dang hardest. He can be seen in Quick in Funny Musicals, the Real Housewives Unauthorized Musical Parody, and other shows with silly songs. He has been singing and acting since he was 6 and hopes to one day live up to his most flattering stage name: Gay Seth Meyers.

people.name: Robert Davenport
people.bio: Robert Davenport performs with Cereal Mascots, an Armory Comedy House Team, and with The Bellhops, a People’s Improv Theater (PIT) Ensembles Team. Other beloved teams include Rumspringa, Benevolent Mob, and in another era, The Purple Crayon of Yale, The Pollyannas, and Circus Maximus. He played Julius Caesar in Julius Caesar in Bogotá and banged his head against a dollhouse for the pilot episode of Twin Peaks.

people.name: Robert Johnston
people.bio: Robert Johnston is a Comedian, Actor, Singer and Writer. Robert is an upcoming performer on UCB’s ‘Lloyd Night’ team CONGRESS. He is also part of the Indie Improv team ‘Full of Bones’, The Musical improv team ‘The Understudies’. And the sketch team ‘Little Boy evil’. As well as being the Host of ‘A Bit Much (A Standup Comedy Show)’. As a Comedian he has headlined or featured in The Fun and Dumb Festival, The New York Comedy Festival, New York Sketchfest, And SF Sketchfest. As an AEA member for over a decade he’s starred in countless Musicals and Plays across the country. And as a human being he currently has a 42 inch box jump.

people.name: Robin Driscoll
people.bio: Robin majored in Theatre Arts at Brown University and performed off, off Broadway and with the Cornerstone Theatre. She took a long hiatus to raise 7 children in NYC, on a Vermont dairy farm and in Los Angeles. She plunged into improv 6 years ago, starting at the Groundlings, and has never looked back. Robin studies Musical Improv with Tara Copeland and loves it with all her might.

people.name: Rocio Prado
people.bio: Rocio Prado (she/they) is a Mexican American improviser, comic, amateur scarf juggler. In 2017, she quit a PhD program in English literature to move to Los Angeles for love and improv. Originally from Anaheim, they’ve performed for and hosted shows all over LA. She looks like Cheech Marin in drag and makes a mean bowl of spaghetti. Their apartment was recently named a national landmark because it’s the home of the world’s bossiest little black cat.

people.name: Rocky Powell
people.bio: Rocky Powell is a comedian, actress, podcaster, and content creator in NYC. She’s a member of improv team Cereal Mascots at The Armory, hosted the podcast, Wild Nights with Rocky Powell for 5+ years, and is a reoccurring guest on Cipha Sounds ‘Pay Your Friends’ She has been improvising in NYC and various parts of the US for 15 years.

people.name: Rod O'Toole
people.bio: Rod is a performer and writer living in Brooklyn. He improvises with the team Woodstock and has performed at UCB with Lloyd teams Buckets! and Blanche. You can read his newsletter “Dear Ratboy” at rodotoole.beehiiv.com.

people.name: Roe Hartrampf
people.bio: Roe is best known for his performance as Prince Charles in Diana: The Musical both on Broadway and Netflix. For his work in the Netflix film version of the musical Roe was nominated for a prestigious Razzie™️ award for the Worst Actor in a Film (2021). Roe’s fellow nominees included usch industry greats as Scott Eastwood, Mark Wahlberg, and LeBron James. In a shocking (rigged?) twist, Roe lost his Razzie™️ to none other than 4 time NBA champion LeBron James. Roe is hopeful that his work in Shiz will prove once and for all that he, and not LeBron, was in fact the Worst Actor in a Film in the year 2021. Thank you for your consideration.

people.name: Ronnie Adrian
people.bio: Bio? YOU CAN’T HANDLE MY BIO!!

people.name: Rooster Monaghan
people.bio: Rooster Monaghan is an NYC based comedian who’s been featured on The Rich Eisen Show and at clubs across the country. He performs on Maude night with Reunion Tour and has been in multiple ads you’ve definitely skipped. Along with his weekly podcast Family Friends, he regularly hosts, produces, and performs on stand-up shows across the city, which you can learn more about on his social medias: @rooster_monaghan Other credits include: Asylum Mainstage Presents, North Carolina Comedy Festival, RISE Comedy Festival, Jersey Shore Top Comic Finalist 2023.

people.name: Rory Scholl
people.bio: Rory Scholl is an actor comic and storyteller. He is an award winning author with his Amazon book Black Sheep, has appeared on The Marvelous Ms Maisel, FBIs Most Wanted, Law and Order and more. He is the producer of ARTPROV, a show mixing artists with improvisers to create new shows and paintings for charity, and travels the country as host of the game show The Big Quiz Thing. He lives in Queens with his cat Lionel.

people.name: Rory Scovel
