people.bio: Rory Scovel is an actor, comedian, and writer. His latest comedy special RORY SCOVEL: RELIGION, SEX, AND A FEW THINGS IN BETWEEN released in February, 2024 on Max. He will next be seen starring alongside Reese Witherspoon and Will Ferrell in the Amazon romantic comedy YOU’RE CORDIALLY INVITED. Most recently, he starred in the Apple TV+ series PHYSICAL opposite Rose Byrne which ran for three seasons, and appeared in Paramount’s BABYLON directed by Damien Chazelle, which garnered the cast a Screen Actors Guild nomination for Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture. Additional acting credits include Voltage Pictures’ I FEEL PRETTY opposite Amy Schumer; Netflix’s OLD DADS alongside Bill Burr, Bobby Cannavale, and Bokeem Woodbine; New Line’s THE HOUSE alongside Will Ferrell and Amy Poehler; THE LEGACY OF A WHITETAIL DEER HUNTER alongside Danny McBride and Josh Brolin; Demetri Martin’s directorial debut DEAN which premiered at the Tribeca Film Festival; NBC’s SUPERSTORE and UNDATEABLE; ABC’s MODERN FAMILY; FX’s THE COMEDIANS; TBS’s GROUND FLOOR and WRECKED; and the TruTv series THOSE WHO CAN’T. Rory’s stand up has been featured on Comedy Central, CONAN, and Late Night with Jimmy Fallon. His second album, RORY SCOVEL LIVE AT THIRD MAN RECORDS, was released in 2013 and was recorded live at their studios in Nashville. In 2015 he released RORY SCOVEL: THE CHARLESTON SPECIAL, followed by his Netflix special RORY SCOVEL TRIES STAND-UP FOR THE FIRST TIME in 2017.

people.name: Rose O'Shea
people.bio: So you’re the one who’s been sending me those articles from your school newspaper.

people.name: Roslyn Seale
people.bio: Roslyn Seale is an Actor, Improviser, Writer AND Singer born and raised in Brooklyn, NY. She has studied Improv and Sketch at UCB, The Magnet and Second City. As an Actor/Singer, she has performed at Regional theaters all over the country and was even in a National Tour. If theater isn’t your jam, you can also spot her in commercials and the occasional TV show. You can catch her writing and performing with Ghost Girl Sketch Comedy and doing Improv with many indie teams. Instagram @theroslynseale

people.name: Ross Bryant
people.bio: Ross is a writer/performer based in Los Angeles. Ross is a former ensemble member of Chicago’s Second City Mainstage. Now in LA, Ross can be seen performing regularly at the UCB with Magic to Do. He appears monthly at The Largo with The Improvised Shakespeare Company. Ross is a writer for the most recent season of Mystery Science Theater 3000, and has appeared on NBC, MTV, HBO, Comedy Central, and Dropout TV. Ross is also a TTRPG streamer with The Glass Cannon Network.

people.name: Ross Oteri
people.bio: Ross Oteri (he/him) is a stand-up comedian, sketch writer, TV writer, and co-founder of Valinof Media, a digital media production company. Ross is graduating from the University of British Columbia with a BA in Political Science. His catchphrase is: [in a Scooby Doo Voice] It’s a Rossy Rossy Roooooo Time!™

people.name: Roxie Pell
people.bio: Roxie Pell is a comedy writer and performer based in Brooklyn. She previously wrote for Maude team Dumptruck and currently writes for 11:11. She performs characters around NYC.

people.name: Ruby Karp
people.bio: Ruby Karp recently starred opposite Charlie Puth in his improvised comedy show for Roku. Her one woman show, I Don’t Trust Adults , wrapped a six-week run at SoHo Playhouse in the spring. She was a video producer at Betches , After Midnight and now at Unwell . She has been featured on JFL, Comedy Central, MTV and more.

people.name: Rudy Behrens
people.bio: Did a lot of Improv from 2009-2019. Let’s see if he’s still got it.

people.name: Ruha Taslimi
people.bio: Ruha Taslimi is an improviser, actor, writer, director, choreographer and dancer. They grew up in LA also known as “Iran-geles” since over half a million Iranians live there. Most of them are doctors or lawyers, Ruha is one of the precious few who do improv comedy. Their family thinks Ruha should “not be so proud of that, it’s embarrassing.” Ruha is known for their work on the cult-hit streaming service Dropout. Their family has never heard of Dropout so it only reminds them that Ruha dropped out of college after almost four years because they got mono. They’ve appeared in Cursed Friends (Comedy Central), which according to their family is “not HBO,” Blocks (Sundance 2020) — “who dance?” asks Ruha’s dad while still holding out hope that they come back to work at Taslimi Construction. They perform at Upright Citizens Brigade weekly on UCB House teams like Dance Club Remix; monthly with a show they created and co-produce: QT’s presents Joy; and occasionally with Asssscat and Assssketch, UCB’s signature improv and sketch shows. They perform at Dynasty Typewriter with Bad Drag Race, The Muppet Script Live and are directing and performing in the upcoming show To the Hollow. If all goes well they will never work at Taslimi Construction again.

people.name: Russell Daniels
people.bio: Russell is an actor, comedian and (oh no podcaster?) based in NYC. He’s a founding member and performer with award-winning sketch comedy group Uncle Function. Credits: Gutenberg the Musical on Broadway, the Off-Broadway hit musical TItanique. You can hear him weekly on the podcast The Downside with Gianmarco Soresi wherever you get your podcasts.

people.name: Ryan Asher
people.bio: Ryan came to LA from Chicago where she performed on the Mainstage at The Second City. You can see her performing across LA, as well as on The Joe Schmo Show on TBS, Comedy Central, NFL Network, and The Roku Channel. She’s repped by Cliff and Stefan at Entertainment 360. Follow her on TikTok while it still lasts for crazy characters.

people.name: Ryan Barker
people.bio: Ryan has been studying & performing improv in NYC since 2015. You can catch him monthly in The Trial: An Improvised Court Experience.

people.name: Ryan Barton
people.bio: Want to be one of the women on Selling Sunset , but I’m settling for UCB improviser. I think the color ‘Seafoam Green’ should be a flavor. Lastly, I think you should come watch one of my shows because I’m probably the person you’ve been looking for, whether that be platonic, romantic, or work-related.

people.name: Ryan Beggs
people.bio: You can see Ryan do sketch comedy with his friends on LISA and now with UCB’s Betty Night. You probably don’t recognize him from HBO’s “Sex Lives of College Girls,” but he’s there, alright! Follow him on Instagram and TikTok @ryanmbeggs.

people.name: Ryan Brookshire
people.bio: Ryan Brookshire is a writer, performer, and teacher from Tennessee with degrees in music education and language. He’s the newest member of faculty team Sweet Sweet Fantasy Musical and a proud graduate of Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Ryan Chittaphong
people.bio: Ryan is thrilled to be back at UCB, where he began his training in 2013, performed in Lloyd Night’s inaugural post-COVID season, and now serves as co-producer of Asian AF’s New York branch. He performs in the hit monthly comedy show “Entirely From Memory” in residency at littlefield in Brooklyn, and is a proud company member of the nationally acclaimed Story Pirates. In addition, Ryan also performs with The Armory Comedy and Shoe’s Off Improv. Ryan serves as Director of Artistic & Student Engagement at the Stella Adler Studio of Acting where he also teaches acting technique, character and improvisation. For three years, he had the great pleasure of working at the world famous Grand Ole Opry as an official Hank Williams impersonator. @ryanchittaphong | ryanchittaphong.com

people.name: Ryan Ciecwisz
people.bio: Ryan Ciecwisz is a stand-up comedian and writer. His work has been published in outlets such as McSweeney’s and Points in Case. Ryan hosts Wizman Comedy, an open mic at Alligator Lounge and 2 Blonde Boys, a stand-up show at Cobra Club.

people.name: Ryan De Marco
people.bio: Ryan De Marco is a current junior pursuing a BA in Acting at the University of Southern California. He is here as part of USC’s premier improv and sketch group, Commedus Interruptus.

people.name: Ryan Hitchcock
people.bio: Ryan is an actor, writer, and improviser from Los Angeles, California. Previous UCB credits include Betty Night, Harold Night, Maude Night, The Cagematch, and Spoiler Country. You can currently also see him with his sketch group Daytrip at The Pack Theater, or as the lead in the upcoming short film “Fred The Super Computer,” written and directed by Sam Kimbrell. Ryan has performed at several comedy and fringe festivals in the US and Canada, and in The Laugh Factory Hollywood’s first-ever (and last-ever) sketch comedy showcase.

people.name: Ryan Jantz
people.bio: Ryan is a musician and improvisor from Staten Island, NY. Ryan performs comedy regularly with The Great American Shit Carnival and Gups, and music with indie bands I’m Going To Marry Your Dad, No Big, Our Lady of Sorrows, and Best Actress. He’s been described as “cool”.

people.name: Ryan Karels
people.bio: Ryan has been a performer at UCB NY since 2002 on several Harold Teams including the beloved indie team Krompf.

people.name: Ryan Kelly Coil
people.bio: Ryan is an actor/comedian originally from Nashville, TN. He has been performing improv and sketch everywhere from Boston to LA to Edinburgh since 2012 and acted in commercials, Film and Television since 2014.

people.name: Ryan Kicklighter
people.bio: Ryan Kicklighter is an actor, improviser, and writer originally from Baltimore, MD. He has been “yes, anding” at UCB since 2014 and is looking forward to finally understanding “if I park here and don’t pay attention to street signs which results in a $73 ticket, what else is true?” Ryan can be seen performing all over town with his monoscene team Customer Service.

people.name: Ryan Kristopik
people.bio: Ryan Kristopik is an actor, comedian, and VO artist living in New York City. Ryan has studied comedy with The Upright Citizens Brigade, Second City, and Sea Tea Improv. When he isn’t performing, Ryan loves English manor homes, complaining online, and passed hors d’oeuvres.

people.name: Ryan Leach
people.bio: Ryan is an actor, improviser and satirist based in Brooklyn. Seen on The Onion, The Other Two, At Home With Amy Sedaris, Upright Citizens Brigade, The Bell House, Union Hall, Brooklyn Comedy Collective and more. He is a founder of Awf Magazine.

people.name: Ryan Myers
people.bio: Ryan Myers is a teacher, coach, and the Director of Marketing and Business Services at the Improv Shop in St. Louis, where he also serves on faculty. He teaches and tours nationally with Touch Baseball (recently featured on the Yes, Also Podcast), a two-person improv team that specializes in the monoscene. Ryan performs weekly with both independent and Improv Shop House Teams in addition to co-producing No Edit, Touch Baseball’s improvised one act play in three acts.

people.name: Ryan Parseghian
people.bio: Ryan has been improvising since the second Bush administration and been gay since Obama. Ryan hopes to work across the aisle, bringing music nerds and improv dorks together at last.

people.name: Ryan Rosenberg
people.bio: Performing with Big Grande at UCB, Member of Big Grande, Co-Host of @ManDogPod, and @chosenberg on the internet

people.name: Ryan Warsing
people.bio: Wow! The Big City! Ryan is an actor and improviser from Pittsburgh, PA who has studied at iO Chicago and the Second City Conservatory. This is his second appearance at DCM.

people.name: Ryder Tam
people.bio: Ryder is a Chinese American comedian and former aerospace engineer from Honolulu, Hawaii. He is an alum of The Groundlings Sunday Company, and is a Saturday Night Live Diversity Scholarship recipient. Ryder has performed at numerous comedy festivals around the world, including SF Sketchfest, Detroit Improv Festival (headlining act), and Austin Sketch Fest (headlining act). Fun fact: there is a polaroid picture of him in space!

people.name: Saagar Shaikh
people.bio: Saagar Shaikh can be seen as Aamir Khan in the Marvel Disney+ limited series “Ms. Marvel.” He will reprise his role in the Marvel feature “The Marvels,” starring Brie Larson. He is also a lead in the Hulu Original series, “Deli Boys” which is set to shoot early 2024. Shaikh got his start as a breakout at the Disney Television Discovers: Talent Showcase.

people.name: Sabeeh Jameel
people.bio: Sabeeh is a writer, performer, and director. His credits span kinda-silly, kinda-informative productions that include A Little Late with Lilly Singh, United States of Scandal with Jake Tapper, The Opposition with Jordan Klepper, and the 2024 White House Correspondents’ Dinner. He has also written and performed as a member of the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre, in both New York and Los Angeles.

people.name: Sabrina Herrera
people.bio: Sabrina Herrera is a Latina journalist, actor, and improviser who loves to look for the fun in things. She hosts improv workshops for newsrooms to help them become more collaborative. Yes, and TK TK TK.

people.name: Sahana Srinivasan
people.bio: Sahana Srinivasan is an actor/comedian/writer with a love for the absurd and unpredictable. She has acted in a variety of genres, ranging from comedy ( The Pradeeps of Pittsburgh , Insecure ) to action ( Fear the Walking Dead ) to drama ( For All Mankind ) children’s entertainment ( Brainchild on Netflix), and more. Sahana graduated from UT Austin with a degree in film. You can see her go silly mode on Maude Night Wednesday nights!

people.name: Sahil Desai
people.bio: Sahil is an Indian improviser originally from Botswana who now lives in LA. So technically, he’s Indian-African-American, but he tries not to advertise that… You can watch him at Lloyd night, Drunk Theatre, WGIS Broadwater Tuesdays, Toretto Sauce, and too many other improv shows around LA. He loves his Mini Dachshund Montague very much.

people.name: Sai Lang
people.bio: Sai Lang is an actress and writer who was the lead of two Tollywood feature films and has been seen on TV shows such as Superstore, Crazy Ex-Girlfriend, and Law & Order SVU. She was a writer for Maude teams for 3 years and a writer/performer for Characters Welcome and now teaches sketch and characters at UCB. She co founded an all-South Asian sketch group called The Get Brown that sold out all their shows with two successful runs at UCB. Sai is also an alumni of the CBS Paramount Showcase and was a consulting producer for their recent 2023 show.

people.name: Salim Hafid
people.bio: Salim is a writer, performer, and improviser. He trained in Chicago at the Annoyance and has performed improv and sketch at the Annoyance, iO, and The Second City Chicago.

people.name: Sally Omar
people.bio: A lover of musicals and comedy! Mama to one. Therapist to many. Doing the LA thing while also adulting just a bit.

people.name: Sam Bodenstein
people.bio: Sam Bodenstein is a performer originally from New York, who works as a project manager in Los Angeles. Catch him on Lloyd Night with Jinx, or around town with one of his many improv teams that most say are too “many to count or keep track of.” He pays annually for a premium calendar app.

people.name: Sam Brodnitz
people.bio: Sam is a comedian from Alameda, California, who can currently be seen on UCB’s Lloyd night.

people.name: Sam Di
people.bio: Sam is an actor and writer by way of Oxford, MS. Sam’s favorite thing about improv is not having to remember lines. Follow him on venmo @samdi

people.name: Sam Ditto
people.bio: Sam Ditto is a writer, comedian, and actor who hails from Mississippi, where she achieved early fame after winning a local modeling contest at the age of eight. She has written and produced sketch comedy for various YouTube channels and online content creators, and has since transitioned from the catwalk to the stage, bringing her sharp wit and love of the absurd to her work. Sam is excited to share her voice and Southern charm with audiences.

people.name: Sam Rogal
people.bio: Sam Rogal is a comedian and improviser currently on weekend teams Metal Boy and Friday Night show at the Magnet Theater. He also hosts The Power Joker as Robert Moses at Caveat on the lower east side

people.name: Sam Schaefer
people.bio: Sam Schaefer is Brooklyn-based writer and comedian. His sketch shows have performed at multiple off-broadway theaters to sold out crowds as well as New York Comedy Festival, San Francisco Sketchfest, and the Bell House. You can find his digital sketches on his instagram (@sam__schaefer) and tiktok (@samwantspraise).

people.name: Samantha Adler
people.bio: Samantha Adler, better known as Sam Adler, is a performer, podcaster, and lifelong storyteller. She produces and performs with Ladies Night at UCB and The Clubhouse, blending improv, musical improv, stand-up, and character work. Offstage, she hosts the podcast 15 Years in LA, a love letter to the city she calls home. She is commercially represented by Robertson & Taylor Agency, currently seeking theatrical representation, and is the proud dog mom of a tripod husky, Roo Bader Ginsburg, and an Australian Shepherd, Serbi Bernard.

people.name: Samantha Nugent
people.bio: Samantha is a performer and writer based in NYC. Writing and producing her own digital sketches has garnered over 20k followers on TikTok. She’s currently an actor on the UCB Maude team DumpTruck, she’s a writer and performer on the PIT’s character team Typecast, and she’s SPANKing her solo show, More Than A Fart, this May. I can also do drama.

people.name: Samantha Williams
people.bio: Samantha Jane Williams is a local NY improvisor with 15+ of experience/learning in musical and non musical improv. With a degree in theatre performance/music from Suny New Paltz, works in tv/film and theatre. Credits include Law and Order: CI, The Resident and Dr. Death. Looks for any and all opportunities to be whimsy!

people.name: Samara Breger
people.bio: Samara Breger is a writer and performer from Sleepy Hollow, New York. Her vampire Gothic, A LONG TIME DEAD, was named “the best Sapphic vampire book since…Carmilla” by the New York Times, as well as one of the best romances of 2023. She regularly performs musical improv in New York as well as around the country at comedy festivals. She lives in a little house with her wife and dog, both of whom are huge fans of her work.

people.name: Samat Turgunbaev
people.bio: Samat is a comedian and actor based in Los Angeles. You can catch him performing with his Lloyd team at UCB, or around town with his other teams.

people.name: Samiyyah Ali
people.bio: Samiyyah is an improviser based out of Washington Improv Theater. You can find her performing with Kinfolk, LIZARDGIRL, Baggage Claim, and Lena Dunham—or stopping random people on the street to say “Do improv! You could meet your future wife!”

people.name: Santiago Espinosa
people.bio: Day job: Explaining complex stuff in videos. Night job: Panicking in front of an audience to get a laugh.

people.name: Santina Muha
people.bio: Santina Muha is an actor, writer, public speaker, host, advocate, dog mom and Marshall’s shopper. A Jersey girl/LA woman, Santina brings a touch of east meets west to everything she does, and always finds a way to infuse comedy into her advocacy. Please use this bio to hire her for something cool, or to set her up with your cool single friend.

people.name: Sara Caplan
people.bio: Sara Caplan (she/her) is a writer, musician, improviser, and improv teacher based in Los Angeles, CA. She is a member of the musical improv teams Dramatique and Baby Wants Candy, as well as a member of QT’s, UCB’s first all-trans-GNC-nonbinary improv team. You can also find her teaching musical improv workshops at the Clubhouse or hear her on Nerdsquad, a queer podcast series about movies, philosophy, and The Wheel of Time.

people.name: Sara Parelhoff
people.bio: Sara is an NYC-based improvisor who regularly performs at the Magnet Theater with her house team Macbeth. She is one half of the twoprov team Mothra (Queen of the Monsters.) If you are the victim of a crime call the New York Office of Victims Services at 1-800-247-8035.

people.name: Sarafina Ludlow
people.bio: Sarafina Ludlow is an LA based actor/writer, as well as the self proclaimed and uncontested “Goblin King.” After a mentalist told her to move to Chicago for improv, she ditched her Mormon mission and her degree in science to a study the art of “yes, and.” She now performs at the UCB theater in LA with the Maude team, Rodeo Baby, and will soon be joining the cast of The Groundlings Sunday Company. You can follow her on Instagram @sarafina_ludlow for updates and chaos.

people.name: Sarah Claspell
people.bio: Sarah is an actress/comedian and has been featured on tv shows such as Brooklyn Nine-Nine, Jimmy Kimmel Live, and Comedy Bang Bang, and she teaches improv at UCB and WGIS. Sarah performs regularly with variety show Asian AF, improv teams Voltron and Cardinal Redbird, and other various shows doing improv, sketch, and characters. She has performed at SF Sketchfest, Edinburgh Fringe Festival, and taught and performed improv all over the world. Sarah co-hosts the podcast Everything is Rent, a podcast talking about the Broadway musical Rent, of course.

people.name: Sarah Dooley
people.bio: Improviser based in Los Angeles. Is a real life accountant and makes her own clothes sometimes.

people.name: Sarah Grace Welbourn
people.bio: Sarah Grace is an actor with credits in Quiz Lady, the Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, Bull, Matt Roger’s Christmas Special, and also exactly 32 commercials. She was on a UCB Harold, Maude, and weekend team and currently teaches in New York.

people.name: Sarah Griffith
people.bio: Sarah Griffith is an actor, writer, voice-over artist, and all around funny girl from Houston, TX. She’s a proud graduate of the prestigious HSPVA, and continued her theatrical training at the National High School Institute (Cherubs) and Saint Louis University. Since moving to Los Angeles, she’s written and performed with Fred Willard’s MoHo Sketch Comedy Group, as well as write and star in “New Low”, a web series from Bad Collab Productions. She is also the co-host of “Rough Stuff”, and several other podcasts on the Small Beans Network. She continues to write and perform on stage and on screen in Los Angeles.

people.name: Sarah Khasrovi
people.bio: Sarah is a MENA writer and performer from the Bay Area. She was a cast member in the 2020 CBS Showcase and has been a member of UCB House Teams on Maude Night and Characters Welcome. Onscreen credits include What We Do in the Shadows (FX), S.W.A.T. (CBS), NCIS , and Modern Family (ABC). She made a feature film called Sisters that won the Audience Award for Best Narrative Feature at Inside Out and the Grand Jury Award for Best Narrative Feature at the Florida Film Festival in 2024. She’s addicted to pickles, please don’t ask her about it.

people.name: Sarah Kullbom
people.bio: Sarah is an improviser from scenic Central California. She really loves dogs and is in her luteal phase. Catch her on Sing Out, Louise!

people.name: Sarah Reynolds
people.bio: Sarah Reynolds is a singer-songwriter, voice teacher, and proud dog mom. A classically trained vocalist, she has sung with LA Opera, LA Master Chorale and is a performer and co-producer with LA Camerata. During most of the aughts she performed improv, musical improv, and sketch as a cast member of ImprovBoston. She is thrilled to be part of LA’s vibrant musical improv community.

people.name: Sarah Smallwood Parsons
people.bio: Sarah Smallwood Parsons is a co-host of Characters Welcome, a teacher at UCB Training Center, a JFL New Face, and the owner of @_CryBabyCraft (IG). Sarah specializes in comedic character monologues with elaborate costumes and musical elements, like “The Song in Every Musical That No One likes.

people.name: Sarah Yoakley Thornell
people.bio: Sarah Yoakley Thornell is a native Texan and improviser with training from Austin’s ColdTowne Theater and Los Angeles’ The Groundlings. A Meisner-trained actress, she’s best known for the twenty-some-odd commercials in which she’s played “mom” and/or “wife” – a casting type she’s been nailing since middle school. For the past two years, she’s performed every Sunday with Austin’s favorite Harold troupe “All Night, All Night, All Night.” She’s thrilled to take on the big city with her best friends.

people.name: Sashel Del
people.bio: Straight outta Penn State’s Full Ammo Improv and the hallowed halls of the Del Close Marathon, Sa’Shel brings the perfect blend of nerdy finesse, movie references, and a heart of gold (mostly for children) to the UCB stage. When not slinging jokes, he’s probably drawing up a storm or watching anime characters save the planet.

people.name: Scarlett Bell
people.bio: Scarlett Bell is an Austin-based improviser, director, and theater technician who began performing at the Hideout Theatre in 2019. A graduate of ColdTowne Theater’s Improv Conservatory, they have performed with house teams, guest ensembles, and international online casts. Scarlett’s background in systems engineering heavily influences their creative work, blending technical innovation with fast-paced, character-driven comedy. They are especially passionate about improv, clowning, and collaborative storytelling.

people.name: Scott Adsit
people.bio: Scott Adsit is an American actor, comedian, and writer, best known for his role as Pete Hornberger in the sitcom “30 Rock” and for voicing Baymax in Disney’s “Big Hero 6”. He is also recognized for his work on the Adult Swim stop-motion animation programs “Moral Orel” and “Mary Shelley’s Frankenhole”.

people.name: Scott Campbell
people.bio: Scott Campbell (he/him/his) is a Chicago improviser and performer with comedy roots in Philadelphia and UCB NYC. Before the dark times, he spent 7 years improvising, performing, directing, teaching, and producing in Philly. Since moving to Chicago in 2021, he has performed on multiple long-term projects at The Annoyance and iO Theater. He loves compliments and gives the best consensual hugs. If you are into pics of cats, food, beer, art, and scenery, you can follow him on IG @boogievonstomp.

people.name: Scott Istvan
people.bio: Scott Istvan is a Boston-based improviser who has been studying and performing improv since 2011. He currently produces and performs in Improvised History, directs Junebug: an Improvised Space Western, and is a cast member in MishMash: an all-ages improvised musical.

people.name: Scott Lamica
people.bio: Scott is one half of improv duo Breakfast Boys and has worked in the props department on such films as Bodies Bodies Bodies, A Different Man, Between the Temples, and more!

people.name: Scott Thiede
people.bio: Scott is a very tall bisexual. He is also a writer and performer originally from Bethesda, Maryland — home to the National Institute of Health (impressive) and a very long escalator (disorienting). You can catch Scott writing sketches for his Maude team Detention!!! and performing musical improv with Candy Apple Red.

people.name: Scott Thomas
people.bio: Scott is honored to join Entirely From Memory at DCM. He is honored to frequently join Entirely From Memory at Littlefield in Brooklyn. He joins the cast of Strange Men at The Brick Aux this summer and will join your trivia team for beer.

people.name: Sean Chang
people.bio: Sean is a Brooklyn-based performer and writer originally from California. He performs around the city with The Lake at Dawn – the chillest improv team in NYC, and Magnet Theater’s house team Mommy. He is most known for being a former archer for Team USA and a competitive cup stacker (yep), and he really likes to laugh 🙂

people.name: Sean Conroy
people.bio: Sean is a comedian, writer, director, actor, and podcaster. He has performed at the UCB theater for decades, including as a founding member of The Swarm (the theater’s first-ever house team). He’s written lots of TV, done stand-up on TV, and did a one-man show that went to the US Comedy Arts Festival in Aspen, CO (not on TV).

people.name: Sean Coyle
people.bio: Sean Coyle is a really good comedian and writer from the suburbs of Chicago. You may know him from his comedic podcast on the Headgum Network, SitcomD&D. Or maybe you know him from his YouTube channel, Awesomely Awful, or from seeing PTO, his Lloyd team at UCB, perform weekly. Or maybe you don’t even know him at all… GOD!

people.name: Sean Meagher
people.bio: Sean Meagher is a performer and producer on UCB teams “Starlight” and “Quick and Funny Musicals,” and on the podcast “Tabletop Tunes: The Improvised Musical RPG.” He really likes comedy, you guys.

people.name: Sean Smith
people.bio: Sean Smith is an improviser, writer, and director from John’s Creek, GA. He’s a member of Lohan and STEM Kidz, and at times he can be caught walking around Studio City and dissociating. In a previous life he was a pastry and pizza chef, and he wants to know your favorite pizza.

people.name: Sean Williams
people.bio: Sean is a comedian, actor, and improvisor, currently performing with the teams High Score, Lemon Pepper Wet, and Friday Block Party! Between all that, you can find him at karaoke, watching Pro Wrestling, and/or talking fantasy football…any order is the correct order.

people.name: Sebastian Conelli
people.bio: SEBASTIAN is a NY-based comedian from Staten Island. Sebastian has appeared as a blue-collar worker on several TV shows. Check out his podcast Loud About Nothing.

people.name: Sebastian Martinez
people.bio: Sebastian Martinez is an NYC-based comedian/writer/actor/producer. He got his start in the entertainment industry as a child-reporter for the YouTube channel, iKidsnowdotcom, interviewing young celebrities such as Selena Gomez and Ariana Grande. He’s made guest-star appearances in projects such as The Last O.G, Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt, and Blue Bloods.

people.name: Sebastien Siclait
people.bio: Sebastien Siclait is an LA-based writer and performer from Miami. Much to the chagrin of his parents, he is also a clown. But he studied computer science, so it’s mostly okay.

people.name: Seif Rahoumi
people.bio: Seif is an LA-based actor/comedian/board game enthusiast from the great town of Robbinsville, New Jersey. He dreams of being cast as the one human in a Muppets movie and/or the masked villain in a Scooby Doo movie. You can catch him doing musical improv with his indie team, Guest Lecture, or on Sing Out, Louise at UCB!

people.name: Sergio Calvo
people.bio: I’m an improviser based in Bogotá, Colombia. I’m in two long-term relationships. The newer one is with my extraordinary wife, and the longest has been with improv. We started dating in 2006, and I’ve been amazed watching improv grow across Latin America, especially in Colombia. I’m part of El Quinto Pulgar, an improv theater and school where I teach, and a proud member of Zapote Milenario, a team of people I deeply admire and love. I also work in the corporate world, using improv and other cool tools to help people develop soft skills such as communication, teamwork, and collaboration.

people.name: Sergio Cilli
people.bio: Sergio is an award winning director, an Emmy winning writer and a non award winning actor. He’s had the pleasure of working with some of the biggest names in film and TV including Will Ferrell, Molly Shannon, Jennifer Aniston, Tom Cruise and Aubrey Plaza and a lot of other people you’d know. Sergio enjoys long walks in the forest and having a cup of tea on his deck in Portland, OR.

people.name: Seth Finkelstein
people.bio: Seth Finkelstein is a writer/performer based in NYC. He is the writer and creator of the audio comedy Lil’ Miss Kate – Ward of the State. Don’t get him started on the Haunted Mansion.

people.name: Seyoung Kim
people.bio: Seyoung Kim is a writer and comedian based in Brooklyn, NY. She hosts the monthly show Sketch Cage Match at UCB. She also performs with sketch teams Milwaukee and Queer Window and improv teams OOF! and The Slaysians at venues all over New York and beyond.

people.name: Shaan Baig
people.bio: Shaan Baig is a comedian, actor, and writer based in Los Angeles. He was recently named a New Face of Comedy (Characters) at the Just For Laughs Festival in 2022. He currently performs with The Get Brown – UCB’s first all-South Asian sketch team. Past credits at UCB include Maude Night, Characters Welcome, Asian AF, and The Golden Hour. Shaan’s videos have been featured on Vulture, Funny or Die, and BuzzFeed. He also co-hosted the Bollywood Boys Podcast with Saagar Shaikh, which was profiled on NPR. He recently appeared on A Little Late with Lilly Singh on NBC.

people.name: Shakira Paye
people.bio: Shakira Ja’nai Paye can be seen in theatres in Calmatic & Lebrons reimagining of HOUSE PARTY and upcoming tv series DEMASCUS. She can also be seen starring opposite Ashton Sanders and Jeffrey Wright in the Netflix feature ALL DAY & A NIGHT. Additionally, Shakira created and starred on an Comedy Central original sketch series that she created with her comedy troupe along with Paul W. Downs and Lucia Aniello. Shakira continues to build her multi-hyphenate career on and off the camera and recently sold her feature film script REGINA WANTS HER EGGS BACK to Fox for their streaming network. Along with a Pilot script to Netflix.

people.name: Shalewa Sharpe
people.bio: Brooklyn-based comedian Shalewa Sharpe has appeared on HBO, Comedy Central, The CW, Peacock, and Showtime. She has two albums: “Stay Eating Cookies” (2016) and “So You Just Out Here?” (2019), both available everywhere. She is currently a co-host of the weekly podcast, “The War Report with Gastor and Shalewa.” She can be found on Twitter and Instagram at @silkyjumbo.

people.name: Shand Thomas
people.bio: Shand Thomas is an actor and improviser originally from North Carolina, now performing in Los Angeles. She performed in New York with Buckets! and Dimepiece. Nowadays you can see her doing characters and improv all around LA. Outside of live performance you can see her in various shorts as well as on screen in The Other Two on HBO Max.

people.name: Shane Nielsen
people.bio: Shane can be seen on stage at UCB in ASSSSCAT, Rumpleteaser Musical Improv, and Harold Night with The Prophecy. He also performs at Caveat Theatre as a core cast member of Raaaatscraps. Besides training at UCB, he’s also a graduate of the Summer Evening Conservatory at Stella Adler Studios. Catch him acting in plays, readings, and short films all around NY.

people.name: Shannon Dee
people.bio: Shannon Dee is an absolute hoot, just ask her mom’s friends or her friends’ moms. You may have spotted her in shows like Hacks , Ted , Good Girls , It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia , I Love That For You with Molly Shannon, and The Hawk with Will Ferrell. You can also catch her on the Maude team, Rodeo Baby!

people.name: Shannon Keye
people.bio: Shannon Keye is a New York City based stand up comedian and writer. She performs at clubs and independent venues all over the city. When she’s not doing stand up she’s writing and performing sketch comedy (live and online) or performing improv. Shannon is also a writer for Lady Balls a satirical online news outlet centered around Women’s Sports

people.name: Shannon Mullery
people.bio: Improvisor, standup and sketch performer/writer hailing from ATX.

people.name: Shannon ONeill
people.bio: Shannon O’Neill has been with UCB Theatre since 2000 holding almost every position from Student to Artistic Director. You’ve seen her on Broad City, The Other Two, High Maintenance, The Chris Gethard Show and more. She currently producers and performs in RaaaatScraps: The World’s Best Improv Show in NYC, which has quickly gained a following of beautiful smart people – RaaaatScraps.com

people.name: Shareef Taher
people.bio: Shareef’s jokes have been featured in The Onion and Hardtimes News. He performs standup and improv all around NYC. He’s been featured in American Express and Liberty Mutual commercials and currently hosts pop-up game shows for Mr. Beast. As a professional drummer, he’s performed with Patti Labelle, Lionel Richie, Harry Connick Jr, Derrick Trucks, John Scofield and Ok Go. He’s performed at many notable venues like The Lincoln Center, Kennedy Center, Blue Note NYC, The Smithsonian, the Library of Congress as well as two television performances for PBS.

people.name: Sharif Corinaldi
people.bio: Sharif Corinaldi is a long time improviser who lives in Brooklyn with his partner and two young children. He performs on Lloyd Night with his team Uncle Tough and practices with the comedy coop he co-founded , the East Village Practice Group. A computer programmer by trade, he has a P.h.D in Theoretical Physics from UC Berkeley, and is mostly just grateful to be able to use the right side of his brain.

people.name: Shaun Diston
people.bio: Actor/TV writer. Fun Guy.

people.name: Shay Bares
people.bio: Shay Bares is a writer, comedian, actress, dancer, costume designer and was featured as a bridesmaid falling in the mud in an unaired Amazon commercial. She grew up homeschooled in Texas so don’t trust her on things like: the shape of the earth, when dinosaurs lived, global warming and other “provable” facts.

people.name: Sheena Santamaria
people.bio: Originally from Orange County, CA, Sheena Santamaria is a Los Angeles-based Filipina American comedy writer and SAG-AFTRA actor. Sheena wrote on the UCB Maude Night teams Emerald, Don’t Be A Stranger, and Toast. When she’s not putting up sketches, Sheena listens to k-pop, roots for the Dodgers, and plays video games such as Stardew Valley .

people.name: Shelby Barnett
people.bio: Shelby Barnett is an LA-based creative strategist by day; comedian, dancer, and writer by night. She’s also a former Reality TV Producer and Script Coordinator, having worked for years on your favorite guilty pleasure shows. Come out to Sing Out, Louise! 2nd and 4th Wednesdays at UCB to see if she can still do the splits!

people.name: Shem Pennant
people.bio: Shem Pennant is an award-winning director slash writer hypen actor colon musician who hails from the UK yet moved to America as it was somehow “less racist”. His work has been featured in the BBC, Vice, UCB, Taiwanese Government Sponsored Propaganda and his Mum’s Fridge.

people.name: Shenba Vairavan
people.bio: Shenba Vairavan grew up in LA and now lives in NYC. She’s very Brooklyn Baby by Lana Del Ray and whatever reaction you had to that was correct. Her most listened to artist is SZA (she’s okay), her favorite author is Sally Rooney (guys she’s okay), and she loves Bojack Horseman (stop asking if she’s okay). A writer, stand up, and improviser, she spends most of her time trying to define what is and is not a situationship. She hasn’t figured it out.

people.name: Shilpa Das
people.bio: Shilpa Das is a comedian/writer/actor based in Los Angeles. She is the producer and co-host of the South Asian AF show, LA’s largest South Asian variety comedy show and a writer/actor on UCB’s long running Quick & Funny Musicals. Shilpa can be found at UCB and around town performing improv, musical improv or stand up (or trying not to be weird at her day job). She dedicates all her performances to her longtime crush Nick Jonas who married an older Indian woman.

people.name: Shivani Bathija
people.bio: Shivani is an improviser from Austin, Texas. She began performing with her improv troupe at Ringling College of Art and Design. In 2022, she moved to Austin and has been part of many productions through Coldtowne Theater. You can catch her in Hot Goss Presents: Am I The Asshole, and The Rose!

people.name: Sibel Damar
people.bio: Sibel Damar is a New York City-based actor, writer, and digital creator, best known for her roles on CBS’s FBI and as Leyla Zadeh in the hit Amazon podcast series Academy. She was featured in the 2022 ViacomCBS Showcase and has amassed millions of views online, gaining over 200K followers for her sharp, character-driven comedy. Her work has been highlighted in Vulture and Forbes. You can catch more of her online @sibel_damar.

people.name: Sidney Butler
people.bio: Sidney Butler is an LA-based writer and filmmaker from Houston, TX who started studying sketch at UCB in 2019. When she’s not writing for Maude Night you can find her opinion based ramblings, essays and articles on her Substack, Space Out. She was a Disney Writing Fellow in 2021 and since has written on OUR KIND OF PEOPLE (FOX), ACADEMY (WONDERY) and TIME BANDITS (APPLE TV). She’s a big, some may say huge, fan of trashy reality TV and is constantly trying to find the best Thai food in LA.

people.name: Skander Halim
people.bio: Skander was on the Maude teams Goodman and Bonafide and the Beta team Muddleberry, and currently teaches sketch and pilot writing. He’s written for CBS’ Murphy Brown revival, The Unicorn, and Ghosts. He is one-sixth of the all-South Asian sketch team The Get Brown.

people.name: Skyler Madsen
people.bio: Skyler Madsen is a writer, director, and performer from the Bay Area. He is a core company member of UCB’s ASSSSKETCH and a member of the sketch comedy group Moon Goon. His writing credits include TV and film projects for Warner Bros., Sony, Legendary, and Roku.

people.name: Slli’m Williams
people.bio: Slli’m is a very tall and proud member of Washington Improv Theatre in DC. He has a passion for performance and savors the silliness of Lena Dunham Improv show. On a given night, you may find him performing poetry in DC, but improv is his happy place!!!

people.name: Sofia Cordero
people.bio: Sofia is an improv and stand-up comedian who finds joy in balancing bold choices with grounded realism, to create comedy that is unique to her perspective and experience. She currently performs in both NYC and Philadelphia, and is always looking for a new project and challenge! So, don’t be afraid to say hi!

people.name: Solving Your Problems With Chloe Troast
Sonia Nam
people.bio: Sonia is a performer based in New York City – – not particularly versed in speaking in the third person – – but she’s an improviser, baby – – she can do it! Her puppa son, Telly is her life… uh oh, she’s shared too much, dammit now you know how to extort her – – but jokes on you, she’s poor because all her money goes into her dog and she can’t claim him on her taxes like a human child… how unjust is this world?! (She’s also looking for representation!)

people.name: Sonya Katarina
people.bio: Sonya studied the (performance-adjacent) field of Architecture in college, where her advisor declared “you’re clearly never going to be an architect.” So she made the responsible, adult decision and moved to LA to pursue acting. In her free time, she enjoys hobbies.

people.name: Sophia Kebbede
people.bio: Sophia (or Soph) is a comedian, actress, content creator, and writer based in Brooklyn, New York. You can find her performing improv with Dr Beer on Lloyd Night, her indie team Feds with Benefits, and stand-up throughout the city. When she’s not seeking attention in person, she’s getting it online, where her content has earned over 4 million likes on TikTok.

people.name: Sophia Kinne
people.bio: Soph is an improviser in New York City. They can be found performing with various shows and classes and consistently with their friend, Lauren Arnett. Soph is famous for walking into office scenes to let everyone know there is “cake in the break room.”

people.name: Sophie Hard
people.bio: Sophie Hard moved to Los Angeles after college and began studying improv as a way to push past lifelong shyness. She trained with Impro Theatre, The Second City, and Upright Citizens Brigade, specializing in theatrical and genre-based improvisation. She currently performs in the short form Friday night show at Hideout Theatre (Big Bash( and directs and performs with Jane “Austin” Improv, winner of a B. Iden Payne Award for Best New Improv Troupe and Austin Chronicle nominee, best Comedy troupe. Jane Austen Improv has toured throughout the United States and Europe, performing fully improvised Austen-style productions for audiences of more than 200 people. Sophie’s work blends theatrical storytelling, literary improvisation, and character-driven comedy.

people.name: Sophie Zucker
people.bio: Sophie Zucker is an Emmy nominated comedian, currently writing for The Daily Show. She performs all over New York with her comedy collective, Ladies Who Ranch. She is down to hang out.

people.name: Stacey Kulow
people.bio: Stacey Kulow is a comedian, writer, director, and podcaster, who got her start in Madison, Wisconsin, where she performed with Atlas Improv Company and won the title of Madison’s Funniest Comic. Stacey moved to New York in 2014, where she wrote and directed for UCB’s Maude Night and produced several other live show shows, including You Can’t Spell Love Without Cleveland, OH – a rom com parody selected for Austin Sketch Fest. Stacey currently runs Sketch Cram – a sketch comedy show made in one day, with a brand new show every month. She also hosts and produces a podcast called Buffy the Gilmore Slayer. @gilmoreslayer

people.name: Steph Leschek
people.bio: Steph Leschek is a Brooklyn-based writer and performer. You can catch her at Harold Night performing with her team, babygirl! You can see her previous work on StephanieLeschek.com. Hopefully she keeps the website updated!

people.name: Stephanie Allynne
people.bio: Stephanie Allynne is a writer, director, actor, producer, and improviser. Unlike the palm trees, she is a Southern California native. Stephanie also lived in Buffalo, New York for some of her youth, thus gaining an understanding of sports culture, hard work, and misery based pride.

people.name: Stephanie Burchinow
people.bio: Stephanie is an improviser and writer from the gorgeous Midwest. When not at Harold Night, you can see her improvising with Shag, co-hosting the variety sketch show ‘Tini Time in Tiny Town’, or eating those little Starbucks egg bites in her car.

people.name: Stephanie Thoreson
people.bio: Stephanie Thoreson is a writer, comedian, and actor. She is one half of Baby Lion, the production team behind the indie sitcom Wind-Ups , for which she also served as head writer. Stephanie plays the lead in the feature Good Feels on Wheels , which received multiple awards during its festival run, including three “Best Actress” wins for her portrayal of Opal. Her most recent project, Getting Along , is a live sitcom/comedic play inspired by iconic 90’s television that explores themes of codependency, fluid sexuality, and the inner workings of Sbarro Pizza. You can catch her Monday nights at UCB performing with her Harold team Branzino.

people.name: Stephen Bowers
people.bio: Stephen is an instructor and performer at Finest City Improv in San Diego. A defining childhood moment was an adult yelling at him for looking too sad. He definitely wants to know if you got one of his references.

people.name: Stephen Mann
people.bio: Stephen Mann is a performer and teacher at Faraway Theater in Birmingham, AL.

people.name: Stephen Sanow
people.bio: Stephen currently performs with the Harold team The Fantasy. He was on the Mess Hall teams Biscuits and Perm and he still haunts the Inner Sanctum to this day. When he’s not doing improv, he’s making pictures. His first feature, Stripper Boyz , is on Tubi, and his second film, Band Doc , is in post-production. He also likes getting hired to act, write, or direct, because making super-micro-budget independent films is rewarding… but also a lot.

people.name: Stephen William Webber
people.bio: Stephen is an improviser, musician and songwriter who’s also an Emmy-winning composer, GRAMMY-nominated producer, and professor emeritus at Berklee College of Music. Stephen has been featured on NBC’s Today Show, NPR’s All Things Considered, and in the feature film RE:Generations alongside DJ Premier and Nas. His comedy songs and stand-up videos have garnered millions of views on YouTube, and you can catch him performing around New York with his indie improv team Sweet Treat!

people.name: Steve Han
people.bio: Steve Han (he/him) is a New Jersey-born, New York, Korea, and Florida-raised, LA-based actor and comedian. He’s had roles on TV shows such as “And Just Like That…”, “The Big Leap”, “Leverage: Redemption” and more, and has performed sketch and improv with the Second City and iO Chicago. He’s an alum of the ViacomCBS Showcase 2022 and you can watch his stand-up now on WOW Presents Plus (RuPaul’s Drag Race). He has a dog named Totoro who is a diva (well-deserved) and once watched Hairspray 18 days in a row in full each day.

people.name: Steve Jeanty
people.bio: Steve Jeanty is a writer, rapper, and actor from New York. You can find him performing all throughout the tri-state area with his beatboxer Exacto and hosting shows across the city. Steve is a big fan of sour beers, vegan sandwiches and Stereolab.

people.name: Steve Szlaga
people.bio: Steve was born and raised in Michigan, but has lived in LA long enough to be considered a local. He is an alumni of The CBS Diversity Showcase, The Groundlings Sunday Company, UCB’s Harold Night, and UCB’s Maude Night. He’s a writer, performer, and improvisor, but his real talent lies in writing quick and concise website bios.

people.name: Steven Kreager
people.bio: Steven Kreager is a writer and filmmaker based in LA. He graduated from NYU’s Dramatic Writing MFA Program, where he won the Alfred P. Sloan award for his screenplay A Long Time Ago… Before grad school he directed his debut feature film Twerp and was a finalist for multiple Sundance Screenwriting and TV fellowships. He has trained at UCB, Second City, WGIS, WE Improv, and watched a lot of funny clips online. Most recently, he was a finalist for the Blue Cat Screenwriting contest and currently writes for the UCB Maude team Town Crier.

people.name: Steven Polletta
people.bio: Steven Polletta is a designer, writer, and director based in Brooklyn, NY. He trained at the UCBT and performed in many of their shows, including the LGBTQIA+ improv show, Here & Queer. He has advertising experience in creative positions for The New Yorker, The Onion, TIME, A.V. Club, The Root, and other publications, developing scripts and directing videos. He wrote and directed the short film Cheryl with his collaborator Shelly Mar, which is currently in festival submissions.

people.name: Stevie Shale
people.bio: Stevie Shale is an actor, solo character performer, writer, and improviser. She has performed for The iO Theater Chicago, The Green Mill’s Paper Machete, The Second City Chicago, The Annoyance, Felt, Ms. Mistoffelees, Big City Boyfriend, Character Buffet, Betty Night at UCB, and various other shows around LA.

people.name: Sue Galloway
people.bio: Sue Galloway is an actress and writer who has been improvising for over 20 years. You may have seen her on 30 Rock, Inside Amy Schumer or in a literal weighted ton of commercials. She has spent the past eleven years focused on raising her children, in case you’re wondering what the heck happened to her.

people.name: Sulaiman Beg
people.bio: Sulaiman Beg took his first improv class at the Upright Citizens Brigade in either 2008 or 2009 studying under Lennon Parham and Ben Rodgers – though details like this are unnecessary unless you are doing research for his obituary.

people.name: Suni Reyes
people.bio: Suni Reyes is a seasoned UCB talent who has performed improv, sketch comedy, and stand up at all the UCB stages, in English and Spanish. Her most recent TV/Film credits include: Gordita Chronicles, Billions, FBI Most Wanted, Nora from Queens, and Don’t Wait Up For Me. Before the universal timeout, she got to star in several viral videos for Funny or Die, Above Average, and UCB Comedy. She is also a well-regarded sketch comedy director. Her biggest accomplishment continues to be her PERFECT son, whom she raised alongside her comedy career!

people.name: Sunny Huang
people.bio: Sunny Huang is an actor, improviser, and teacher based in Austin, Texas. She’s trained at UCB since 2015 and had the honor of teaching the first Improv 101 class at UCB’s Austin theater. She booked a role eating ice cream for a music video that’s since gotten over 100 million views. She has never felt more seen.

people.name: Syd Goin
people.bio: Syd is an comedian and actor, born and rasied on O’ahu. She is currently in Austin where she creates digital content with Space Cadets Comedy and performs live improv, sketch, stand up, and clown. She made her screen debut in a local indie feature, Sell Out, which premiered at the Austin Film Festival last year.

people.name: Sydney Battle
people.bio: Sydney Battle is an actor, comedian, & writer. She’s written TV (Emmy’s), Sketch Comedy (Stapleview), commercials (COACH), and many (yet to be sold) pilots. You may recognize her from TV/film (HIM, FX’s Swan Boy, Cali Sober), off-broadway (Indian Head, The Maid’s Tragedy, Irish Rep), viral digital shorts (Brooklyn Mom, Middle School Theater Teacher, Ditch, Please!), co-hosting the podcast Diss & Tell (Wondery), or even the UCB (NY/LA) stage.

people.name: Sydney Bossuk
people.bio: Sydney Bossuk is an actor, singer, and improviser based in Astoria, Queens. She can currently be found serving justice in The Trial at UCB, playing a mob bride in the touring production of Married By The Mob, and being silly all over the city with her indie improv team Disco Prescription.

people.name: Sydney Bossuk
people.bio: Sydney Bossuk is a musical theater and comedy performer based in Astoria, Queens. She can be found serving justice in The Trial, playing a mob bride in the touring production of Married By The Mob, and being silly all over the city with her indie improv team Disco Prescription

people.name: Sydney Duncan
people.bio: Sydney Duncan is a New York-based comedian, improviser, writer, producer, and actor, and is one of the newest cast members of Nick Cannon Presents Wild’NOut. She has trained extensively at UCB, The PIT, Magnet Theater, and Dallas Comedy House studying advanced improv and sketch. She has been featured on Vulture & Comedy Central’s socials, and the recipient of The SNL Scholarship Award. You can catch her regularly performing in Comedy Theaters all over NYC including her monthly character show at BCC, teaching improv at The PIT, her one-woman show, “ACAB” at Union Hall, and UCB’s Betty & Maude monthly house team shows.

people.name: Sydney Hollis
people.bio: Sydney Hollis is an actor and comedian based in NYC. His hit podcast, “The Sydney Hollis Show,” is released every Friday. He has appeared on the Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, and many other TV programs. He is a veteran UCB Theatre performer, including four years on Harold Night.

people.name: Sydney Nash
people.bio: Sydney Nash is an LA-based improviser and comedian with nearly a decade of experience, trained at The Groundlings, The Elysian, WE Improv, UCB and WGIS. She’s a proud member of the all-female powerhouse team Pyramid Scheme, and while neurodivergency is having a moment, Sydney has been squealing like a dolphin since 1995.

people.name: Sydney O'Toole
people.bio: Sydney O’Toole is an actor, writer and improviser living in LA. She’s sold pilots to MTV and Comedy Central and created web series for Comedy Central and Funny or Die. She does not currently like oysters and needs everyone to just accept that.

people.name: Sydney Steinberg
people.bio: Sydney Steinberg is a comedian and former geologist known for her musical impressions, including Kate Bush and Stevie Nicks, which have garnered over 5 million views on Instagram. Her credits include Platonic (Apple TV), Rutherford Falls (Peacock), Sunnyside (Peacock) and The Cool Kids (Fox).

people.name: Sylvie Borschel
people.bio: NYC Improviser and character comedian, her solo show craps! was selected by Caveat’s Projectorfest in 2023 and Brooklyn Comedy Collective’s Pitchfest in 2024. She’s performed her characters at Union Hall, Second City, Caveat, Club Cumming and at Characters Welcome.

people.name: Sylvie Wang
people.bio: Sylvie Wang is a writer/comedian/performer from Queens who is now based in Los Angeles. She is a contributing writer for such publications as Reductress and The New Yorker . She studied and improvised at UCB NY, and has performed on Asian AF and ASSSCAT. She is a recent recipient of the Groundlings SNL Diversity Scholarship, and was a finalist for the Yes And Laughter Lab Fellowship. She currently teaches improv at UCB in LA. Ultimately she is just a silly gal who likes to get big money laughs.

people.name: Talia Lefkowitz
people.bio: Talia Lefkowitz is a writer, improviser, and comedian from Los Angeles. She writes for the UCB Maude Wild Card team One Man’s Trash and performs with the UCB Lloyd team Freak Trap, along with several other indie improv teams at UCB and WE Improv. A graduate of Cornell University, she currently works on the CBS shows Ghosts and Eternally Yours .

people.name: Talon Bigelow
people.bio: Talon Bigelow is a writer/actor/improviser with roots in the Pacific Northwest. At a young age he caught an improv bug, filmmaking bug, and theater bug simultaneously. He found viral success making comedy on YouTube, and a home in Portland’s alternative improv scene. His comedy has been featured on Vulture and The A.V. Club. He’s a recipient of the 2024 & 2025 UCB Diversity Scholarship.

people.name: Tameka Cruel
people.bio: Tameka Cruel was born and raised in LOS ANGELES CA. Her love for performing started at a very young age. While TV/Film were her #1 choice, it was her father Sherman Wiggs who introduced her to the world of theatre . Tameka graduated from Chapman University where she received her degree in performing arts and also the Alma mater of both her parents.Due to the success she had with shaping and developing her son Seth B. Carr’s acting career Tameka decided to open CCC ACTING STUDIO in 2017 in GARDEN GROVE CA. and has been helping young actors establish their careers. Tameka is represented by Coast to Coast for both commercial & vo as well as METALENT for theatrical.

people.name: Tandiwe Kone
people.bio: Tandiwe Kone is a native Houstonian and Registered Nurse. When she’s not providing patient care, she performs improv comedy locally and nationally. If you ask what inspired her to get into improv comedy, she will answer, “Being a mother and a nurse, two roles which require quick thinking and the ability to improvise.”

people.name: Tanner Dahlin
people.bio: A pioneer of improv in North Dakota, Tanner founded of Fargo’s famed Donkey Hotey’s Improv team in 1998, soon to celebrate its 28 year anniversary. In NYC, he spent years with UCB’s touring company and on various house teams including; The Union, Moose, Reba, Too Damn Much, Sucker, and Ritual. He was also a guest performer with Higgins and The Foundation. He now lives and performs in North Carolina. You can currently see his work with the dramatic improv group, Pillow Talk, at the CaTCH theater in Charlotte.

people.name: Tao Yang
people.bio: Tao (tay-oh) Yang is a Chinese-American comedian based on LA. He is regular cast member on Dropout and has appeared on Game Changer , Smartypants , Gastronauts and has additionally written for Make Some Noise and Game Changer . He can be seen weekly on UCB’s Harold Night or daily at the Gelson’s Wine Bar.

people.name: Tara Copeland
people.bio: Tara teaches Musical Improv at UCB, loves glitter, Drag Queens, sexy Duke books, cats, and hugs. She’s an elementary school mom, Girl Scout troop leader and will sit through and generally love any and all live musical theater.

people.name: Tara Cox
people.bio: Tara Alise Cox is a bi-racial UCLA grad with a BA in Theatre & a passion for acting, musical theatre, writing, & comedy. She has used her passions to create musical parodies on TikTok that have been shared by Rosie O’Donnell, Leslie Jones, & Shania Twain, garnering over 1 million views & 30k+ followers. She just completed a 6month tour performing with “Disney on Ice” despite not knowing how to ice skate, & was just nominated for an NAACP award for Best Lead Actress-99 seat theatre for her role in the world premiere of the play “Rapunzel Alone” at The Wallis. She also wants you to know she is REALLY good at kazoo.

people.name: Tarek Ziad
people.bio: Tarek Ziad is an Afro-Arab actor, writer, and comedian from Central Florida. He trained at Yale University and can now be seen on UCB’s House Sketch & Improv teams, at The Groundlings as an SNL scholar, and showcasing with Just For Laughs New Faces. Tarek is also a stand-up comedian, viral video creator, and NYT published guest essayist.

people.name: Taryn Leggett
people.bio: Former member of Mess Hall team “Fancy” and current recurring performer on the UCB stage, Taryn is an Australian actress, improviser and musical improviser. You can catch her (and her voice) in a number of films, commercials and TV shows, notably The Good Place .

people.name: Taylor Maynard
people.bio: Taylor Maynard is a Bronx-based comedian, writer, and actor. She is currently a writer on Maude and one half of the sketch duo TwoWeeksNotice, which has performed sketch comedy at venues like UCB and BCC. She performs stand-up regularly at her therapy appointments, and less frequently than she should, on stage. Online, she makes comedy skits and parodies of reality dating shows.

people.name: Taylor Rey
people.bio: Taylor is an actor/singer/clown and puppeteer originally from New Mexico and based out of NYC. She performs original clown work and musical theater around NYC and was last seen in the Off-Broadway production of “The Little Match Girl” at Signature Theatre. She hopes someone will laugh at her.

people.name: TD Sidell
people.bio: TD Sidell has written for the Maude teams Presley, After School Snack, and Baby Todd. He’s also written and performed with other indie sketch teams across New York City. His writing has appeared online for Mel Magazine, The Boston Phoenix, The Tardy Eagle, and The Classical. He once got paid to be a centaur in a liquor commercial that unfortunately never aired.

people.name: Tej Khanna
people.bio: Tej Khanna is a comedian and clown based in NYC. He is known for his standup, improv and clown group work with his trio Chicken Big. He becomes stronger every day.

people.name: Teruko Nakajima
people.bio: Teruko is an actor, dancer, singer, writer, sculptor, and your biggest cheerleader on earth—she’ll cheer the hell you up until you say ENOUGH:) She’s also a proud single mother to her adopted baby doggie, Titi (“Tiny Teruko”), who is a psychiatric service animal (supporting everyone, whether you asked or not). Teruko, Titi, and Mr. John Flynn created Teruko’s raw autobiographical solo show, MADE IN AMERICA . It’s won 7 awards, became an Off-Broadway show, and completely went to Titi’s head. Teruko and Titi are super honored Upright Citizens Brigade performers—UCB is clearly the most progressive theatre ever for hiring a dog to host CAGEMATCH!

people.name: Tess Bennett
people.bio: Tess Bennett just graduated from KSU with a Bachelor of Arts in Theatre and a minor in Musical Theatre. She has been a part of On the Spot for four years and has loved every second. She is currently looking for work, and saving up some money while she’s at it. If describing her in two words most would say whimsical and silly.

people.name: Tess Higgins
people.bio: Tess is a former theatre kid and worked in musical theatre for 5 years in Washington, D.C. before moving to LA. She was on a Harold team at Washington Improv Theatre, and currently plays on the LA team Superbloom. Tess is also a writer and filmmaker, having produced her first feature film in 2023 titled “Him.” She is also an advanced pianist and considers herself a Bachelor Nation Scholar.

people.name: Tess Restaino
people.bio: Tess loves to giggle! They are on an improv house team at The Armory and they are a tech in comedy theaters across the city. I love you!

people.name: Thomas Fowler
people.bio: Thomas has performed at IO West, UCB LA, Second City Hollywood. If he isn’t funny, none of that matters.

people.name: Tim Daly
people.bio: Tim Daly is a writer and performer based in Brooklyn, NY. He writes for the UCB Maude team, Reunion Tour, and performs regularly around town with his sketch team, YAP. He’s been a finalist in screenwriting competitions, including the NY Intl. Screenplay Awards, Big Apple Film Festival, and Filmmatic Comedy Awards. And one time, he had a line in a very dramatic CBS show.

people.name: Tim Girrbach
people.bio: Tim (he/him) is an actor/writer/musician based in NYC. Credits include Black and White (A&E), Joseph (Broadway), Birthday Sax (Joe’s Pub at The Public, Ars Nova), and several commercial projects, including playing Danny McBride’s cockney sidekick in a series of spots for Coral UK directed by David Gordon Green. He is a Leo and his favorite word is marzipan.

people.name: Tim Lyons
people.bio: Tim Lyons is a writer/performer from Chicago and LA! You can hear him improvise weekly on Improv is Dead or see him live with GOAT Sauce!

people.name: Tim Mahoney
people.bio: Tim Mahoney is a comedian/writer/producer. They’ve worked with brands like Paramount, Animal Planet, Marvel, MTV, Wendy’s, Discovery, MLB, NBC Sports, etc.

people.name: Tim Mountain
people.bio: Tim Mountain is a writer, improviser, musician, and total sweetheart. You can see him perform around the city with Scubsy, Scubsy 2, final_final_FINAL_final(3).pdf.doc, and Akmal Tajihan’s Pure Improv. He is a researcher at The Daily Show. Go Knicks.

people.name: Tim Murray
people.bio: Tim Murray has performed stand-up all over the world including SF Sketchfest, The Brooklyn Comedy Festival and The New York Queer Comedy Festival for which he won Best Stand-Up Comedian of 2024. After a viral campagin he was hired as a consultant on the 2025 Tony Awards starring Cynthia Erivo. Tim’s original musical comedy show Tim Murray is Witches received rave reviews from critics, including 5-stars from Entertainment Now, North West End, Broadway Baby, and Broadway World at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. The show was nominated for an Off-West End Award for Best Fringe show, Murray was shortlisted for the Comedian’s Choice Award and WON Best Musical Show.

people.name: Timm Sharp
people.bio: Out of curiosity, I asked ChatGPT to write my bio and this is what it spit out… “It appears there might be some confusion surrounding the actor Timm Sharp. There are limited details available, so it’s possible that Timm Sharp may not be a widely recognized figure, or that there may be a typo or mix-up with the name. There is an actor known as Timm Sharp who has appeared in television and film. He might be best recognized for his role in the television series UNDECLARED, where he played the character Marshall Nesbitt, or for his role in ENLIGHTENED, where he played the character Dougie Daniels. If you have any specific details or if there’s another person you were referring to, please let me know, and I’ll do my best to assist you!” Thanks ChatGPT! For less limited details, go to IMDB.

people.name: Timothy Agius
people.bio: Timothy Agius is a writer and improviser trained at New York’s Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre and Magnet Theater. He co-starred in the UCBT Advanced Performance shows “The Excellent Cadaver,” “Director’s Cut,” “The Dearly Departed,” “Final Frontiers,” “The Apocalypse,” “The Improvised Play” and “Hell’s Kitchen: A Nightmare Show.” Tim performs weekly with the Magnet Megawatt improv team Macbeth. He is also 1/2 of the improv duo Mothra.

people.name: Timothy Stanton
people.bio: Timothy is a human being from earth. He loves pizza and Dr. Pepper. He is not secretly an alien.

people.name: Tirosh Schneider
people.bio: Tirosh Schneider (He/They) is a Berlin-born, California-raised, LA-based, used-to-live-in-New-York actor, writer, and kvetcher. They have been on shows like “American Auto,” “Little America,” “Bull,” and “High Fidelity” (role of “Lame Hipster Dude”), and have performed sketch and improv all over the darn place, including as part of Hammerkatz NYU and “Gary Austin’s Dog Bread” at The Groundlings. Their original plays and screenplays have been produced by various theater companies around the country, and they currently teach middle school theater and high school improv at the Windward School in LA. They also write Crossword puzzles and can be seen zipping around town on a blue off-brand Vespa.

people.name: TJ Norton
people.bio: TJ is a comedian, actor and writer from Colorado Springs, Colorado. He earned his BA in Musical Theatre from the University of Northern Colorado, and he’s currently a member of the Groundlings Sunday Company. You may have seen him improvise on The Kevin Langue or performing Musical Improv at UCB with Orion’s Belt.

people.name: Todd Fasen
people.bio: Todd Fasen has been performing since his days in preschool when he was cast as the role of Max in his preschool graduation production of Where The Wild Things Are. In 2000 he began improvising as a member of the Vassar College improv troupe boringly named Improv. Todd studied improv at both UCBTLA and IO West. He currently performs at UCBTLA and around Los Angeles with his teams Convoy and Sentimental Lady. Todd had a recurring role on the critically acclaimed FX Series Terriers, and co-created and starred in a pilot for Comedy Central. He has also appeared on The Goldbergs, Reno 911!, Conan, Key & Peele, and in various national commercials. Other UCBTLA credits include Thirty:30, Comedy Death-Ray, CDR Sketch, Quick and Funny Musicals, and Sketch Cram.

people.name: Tom Buffalo Powers
people.bio: Tom Buffalo Powers is a Brooklyn based writer and performer originally from Wyoming. Yes that Wyoming. He’s been on improv and sketch house teams at UCB and currently writes for Villain! on Maude night.

people.name: Tommy Kang
people.bio: Tommy Kang is an Asian-American actor, comedian, writer, and martial artist. He is best known for his work in the video game, Ghost of Yotei ; Gazer (Cannes – Directors’ Fortnight), and his digital sketch comedy work: “Edible Sex Doll” (featured in Funny Or Die), “True Detective Pikachu” (which AV Club described as “shockingly good execution of a delightfully dumb idea”), and his Bob’s Burgers sketch, which Tommy released in panicked-isolation during the height of the Pandemic.

people.name: Toni Adeyemi
people.bio: Toni Adeyemi is a comedy writer, performer, and producer despite her Nigerian parents’ wishes. Since graduating from Stanford, she’s worked at Disney, Pluto TV, SuperSpecial, and Honey Chile, and is currently the showrunner’s assistant on Netflix’s Survival of the Thickest . Toni writes fast-paced, ensemble comedies with humor and heart, mixing hard funny with warm fuzzies. She is a 2026 semifinalist for Disney Entertainment Television’s Writing Program and a 2025 YALL Comedy Fellow with her pilot Oakdale Country Club, which also won the 2024 ATX TV Pitch Competition. Her films have premiered at HollyShorts and the American Black Film Festival, and she’s appeared in national campaigns for Apple Vision Pro and her dentist. You can catch Toni improvising on Harold Night at UCB LA or on game shows, trying to win another $10,000.

people.name: Toni Charline
people.bio: Toni Charline Ramos is a comedic improviser, actor, director, teacher, and writer based out of Los Angeles. She teaches and performs regularly at the UCB Theatre with her house team Bangarang, Soundtrack, and is a cast member of ASSSSCAT, the theatre’s longest-running show. She was also on the UCB sketch team, Judith, and she is the co-creator of Room 101, one of LA’s most popular indie improv scenes. Toni has taught and performed all over the world. She has an upcoming tour across the UK in 2026.

people.name: Tony Ferraro
people.bio: Tony is an improviser, writer, and actor based in Brooklyn, NY. He can be seen on Lloyd Night with Dr Beer, Brooklyn Comedy Collective’s Home Teams, as well as with his Indie Teams Full of Bones and Granny Fully Loaded. His credits include SF Sketchfest, New York Comedy Festival, iO Fest, and the Fun & Dumb Festival.

people.name: Tony Garbanzos
people.bio: Tony Garbanzos is a Filipino American performer from San Diego, California. He plays on the team Miss Golightly, the hosts of Happy Hour—one of the longest-running UCB Inner Sanctum shows. (RIP) Tony has also produced and performed on the hit variety show, Asian AF, as well as the spinoff shows, Filipino AF and Voltron And Friends. His biggest comedy achievement is being named by Storm Lorraine as the Hottest LA Male Indie Improvisor of 2018, 2019, and 2021.

people.name: Tony Harkin
people.bio: Tony Harkin is an actor, writer, and former Kids Jeopardy champion based in NYC. After studying musical theatre, Tony began their work with respected regional theaters, including the Public Theatre. They are a member of musical improv team Hell Yeah!, featured five months in a row in TimeOut’s “Best Things to do in NYC. Additionally, they frequently perform in whatever show is willing to let them be on stage.

people.name: Tony Rodríguez
people.bio: A longtime performer of the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatres in New York and Los Angeles, Tony Rodríguez’s extensive comedy background made him a perfect fit to assume the role of Julio in Fox’s longest running hit show, The Simpsons . Tony’s other voiceover credits include Renaldo Raccoon in Tiny Toons University , and Blaine in Disney’s Hamster and Gretel . His screen credits include Late Late Night with James Corden , Crazy Ex Girlfriend , Adam Ruins Everything , Conan , Playing House , Jury Duty , the Emmy-winning webseries Eastsiders , and Netflix’s Gente-fied . He also starred in the film Maximum Truth alongside Ike Barinholtz and Dylan O’Brien. Tony shines behind the camera as well, having acted as consulting producer for Transformistas , a highly-rated documentary that follows the original group of Cuban drag queens as they sacrifice everything for their art. In 2020, Tony won ‘Outstanding Male Actor in a Webisode’ at HBO Latino’s Official Latino Film Festival. He also gained notoriety on social media with his stellar comedic timing in the popular Latine comedy podcast Spanish Aqui Presents , and used his Instagram influence to help save the beloved Silverlake queer bar Akbar.

people.name: Tristan Lee Griffin
people.bio: TRISTAN LEE GRIFFIN is a NYC-based comedic actor and writer, originally from Rome, Georgia. A house sketch comedy performer at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade Theatre NYC and a Just For Laughs New Face, Tristan can be seen performing at the Second City NYC and with his sketch team, The Foundation.Tristan’s acting credits include: Dickinson, American Horror Story, White House Plumbers, That Damn Michael Che, WeCrashed and The Politician.

people.name: Tsilala Brock
people.bio: Tsilala Brock is an actor, playwright, and singer-songwriter. She’s sang, danced, even marched in Suffs, Book of Mormon Tour, Parable of the Sower and other small and big screen roles. She is very freakin stoked about making her UCB/Shiz de-butt and even more excited to make some funny with funny people.

people.name: Tucker Iverson
people.bio: Tucker Iverson is a half-Asian actor and improviser, trained in improv at UCB and in acting at William Esper Studio. He performs with the 21-time Cagematch-winning improv team Go Dummy. He has written/performed a solo show called Work/Life Balance at Caveat and written/directed/performed in a play called 107 Minutes In Heaven which had a sold-out run in 2025. He also was featured talent for BuzzFeed’s YouTube Channel.

people.name: Tucker Reynolds
people.bio: Tucker is HONORED to be performing with his first Harold Team GAG. You may have seen him haunting your TikTok For Your Page, or maybe you haven’t and that’s equally okay!

people.name: Tyler Ray Kendrick
people.bio: What can be said about Tyler Ray Kendrick that hasn’t already been said about the Arby’s on McKnight Road? For more than a decade, Kendrick has taught improv, stand-up, and comedy-based courses to over 300 students from all walks of life. His approach blends humor with purpose—helping students build confidence, sharpen communication skills, and learn to think on their feet. But beyond the punchlines, his classes foster genuine human connection, professional growth, and stronger communities. He’s brought his sharp wit and commanding stage presence to comedy hubs like New York City, Los Angeles, and, naturally, the cultural capital of Bridgeville, Pennsylvania. A four time member of Pittsburgh Magazine’s Best Improv Team The Latchkey Kids and two time Best Comedian winner. In 2025, he was named one of the New Pittsburgh Courier’s 40 Under 40, recognizing his impact both on and off the stage. Kendrick has built a reputation for clever writing, magnetic performances, and deeply human humor. Known for being “tall, dark, and a grandson,” Kendrick describes himself as a Gen X soul trapped in a millennial body. He continues to use comedy as a tool for connection, healing, and good old-fashioned fun.

people.name: Tyler Stuart
people.bio: Tyler Stuart is an award-losing writer, actor, director, and Canadian. He hates writing bios, but loves writing in third person.

people.name: Vanessa Lynne Smith
people.bio: Vanessa Lynne Smith is an actress, writer and filmmaker based in LA. She studied at Boston Conservatory as a Musical Theatre major and also spent some time in New York, where she graduated from the Neighborhood Playhouse. Vanessa has been on numerous improv and sketch teams at iO West and The Second City Hollywood. Listen to her podcast, Blank Stage Playhouse Podcast . Catch her on Harold Night at UCBTLA! Note to casting directors, she looks great on camera according to her grandmother.

people.name: Vanessa St. Clair
people.bio: Vanessa St. Clair is an LA based sketch and character comedian. Currently performing with Back Pocket at UCB Franklin and on numerous internet shows including Public Relations, Famous Woman.TV, FRIENDZONED, Birth Order, SHANKED, and Pain in the Neck; with the last two being featured at Tribeca Film Festival and Dances with Films LA in 2026. Check out her Youtube channel @vanessa.stclair!

people.name: Vasthy Mompoint
people.bio: Vasthy Mompoint is a multi-hyphenate dividing her time between in Los Angeles and NYC. She has been in 8 original Broadway shows including The Prom , Spongebob The Musical , Mary Poppins . Select film/tv credits include: High School Musical The Musical The Series (Krystal), The Bold and the Beautiful (Dr. Ricks), and Awkwafina is Nora from Queens (Janique). She has also danced with Micheal Jackson, performed in the CBS Showcase, and Choreographed Weird: The Al Yankovic Story .

people.name: Venice Ohleyer
people.bio: Venice Ohleyer is a native New Yorker who grew up in Brooklyn and never really left. She is a graduate of LaGuardia High School and Columbia University, but I guess she’d move to LA if you had an amazing job for her? Her DMs are open. She does comedy.

people.name: Veronica Gruba
people.bio: Veronica started improv at UCB in 2014. After working as an intern, then a manager at UCB-LA, she moved onto brighter (read: non-entertainment adjacent) pastures. She is now a therapist, cat mom, and certified silly goose who loves to play pretend by doing monoscenes with her team Customer Service.

people.name: Veronica Osorio
people.bio: Veronica Osorio is an actress, director, comedian, rapper and clown from Venezuela. She’s also an astro babe. You’ve seen her on Hail, Caesar! (Coen Bros.), The Laundromat (Steven Soderbergh), Strange Angel (Paramount +), Mosaic (HBO), Girlsboss (Netflix), The Carmichael Show (FOX) and more.
