You may have spotted her pink hair in several national commercials including being the Spokeswoman for Flex Alert Hispanic Market. She’s on the internet and Spotify as Cherry V.

people.name: Vic Michaelis
people.bio: Vic is an American & Canadian improviser and actor. They can be seen on shows across various networks’ most recently on season two of Amazon’s UPLOAD and projects for the streamer Dropout. Vic’s dad is very proud.

people.name: Victor Cristobal
people.bio: Victor Cristobal is a musician and comedian from New Jersey who loves to make noise and make people laugh. If there’s an audience, too, then that’s icing on the cake! Victor can be seen performing with their improv team, A Thousand Lifetimes, and crying over really happy music.

people.name: Victor Samuel Lopez
people.bio: Victor is a writer/actor living in LA. His TV acting credits include: TULSA KING (Paramount+), TACOMA FD (truTV), MODERN FAMILY (ABC), NEW GIRL (FOX), and KEY & PEELE (Comedy Central). He was a writer on Maude Night and a writer/performer in the CBS Diversity Showcase. Recently, he was hired to co-write a comedy feature film for Coyote Studios, coming out in 2025. As a child, he was a series regular on the Nickelodeon sketch show, ALL THAT.

people.name: Victoria Koenitzer
people.bio: Victoria Koenitzer is an improviser and instructor from San Diego, California who is the founder of Divergent Improv. She has performed at dozens of festivals, and she has taught at the Baltimore Improv Festival, Countdown Improv Festival, Northwest Improv Festival, and the San Luis Obispo Improv Festival. She has taught/teaches core curriculum classes at Finest City Improv and Mockingbird Improv, and has taught electives and workshops at these theaters, as well as Cornerstone Improv. She is the co-creative director of Finest City Improv house team Push Play and co-producer of indie team Bumbled.

people.name: Victoria Longwell
people.bio: Victoria is an actor and writer from Pennsylvania- not Amish. You can catch her performing with her beloved improv team: YETI.

people.name: Viktor Rodriguez
people.bio: Viktor grew up in Texas and attended college in Georgia at The Savannah College of Art and Design. It’s a really good school where he learned to write, direct and be funnier than everyone else. Let’s see, what else? He also has perfect teeth, a butt you could bounce a dime off of, and he hates facists.

people.name: Vince Caldera
people.bio: Vince Caldera is a Mexican-American writer and comedian based in Los Angeles. He’s opened for Maria Bamford, Melissa Villasenor, and Gabriel Iglesias. He’s been invited to perform at such festivals as Netflix Is A Joke Fest, San Francisco Sketch Fest, and many more. Vince also worked as a writer for S2 of the Hulu comedy series, THIS FOOL . His half hour special Heaven + Earth , was recorded at The Upright Citizen’s Brigade Theater in Los Angeles and is available to watch on YouTube.

people.name: Vince Moran
people.bio: Vince Moran lives his sad little life in New York City 🙂

people.name: Vince Peagler III
people.bio: Vince Peagler III is an actor, writer, and one of Los Angeles’ top real estate agents, seamlessly blending entertainment and entrepreneurship. Whether he’s creating stories or helping clients find their next chapter, Vince brings charisma, passion, and purpose to every room he enters. Looking to buy or sell? Feel free to send him a DM.

people.name: Vince White
people.bio: Vince White is a Bronx based improvisor, voice actor, podcaster, and washed up athlete. He is a graduate of the UCB improv program and Boogiemanja actor. When not on stage you can hear him talk Dragon Ball on the Kame House Party podcast every week. You can see him perform monthly with UCBLK , Improv Woodstock , Shonen Chumps and Fine!!! He tall and does it all, baby!

people.name: Vincent Biello
people.bio: Vincent Biello is a Brooklyn based comedian, filmmaker and actor. He started his comedy journey about a decade ago when he lied about his age to take UCB classes at 15, something which seemed cool at the time but is now very strange in retrospect. You can see him performing regularly as a member of Blumpkin Improv and The Fourth Space and as the creator and host of The Limbo Talk Show at the Brooklyn Comedy Collective.

people.name: Virgil Shelby
people.bio: Virgil Shelby is a non-binary actor, comedian, and performer based in Austin, TX. They are one of the owners of Fallout Theater in downtown Austin, and have been performing on stage and on screen for 20 years. You can catch them performing with the award-winning improv troupe Y’all We Asian (@yallweasian) or outside the pub befriending stray cats.

people.name: Viva Rose
people.bio: Viva Rose is an actor, comedian and extremely young woman, practically a baby. Her freshman year of college, Viva was selected to join a writing team for an independent comedy film produced by John Malkovich and Billy Joel. The film was never made. She performs stand-up comedy and storytelling all over the country, recent credits include Don’t Tell Comedy and losing The Moth!

people.name: Vivian Gil
people.bio: Vivian Gil is a Dominican-American writer/actor from Plantation, Florida. Yes, it’s still called that! She’s trained at UCB, Groundlings, IO West, and Second City and often refers to herself as trash, which is a very high compliment. You can find her performing around town, but you’ll most likely hear her first. She’s very loud.

people.name: Wallis Brown
people.bio: Wallis Brown is a comedian, musician, actor and artist based in Texas. You can find Wallis improvising, standing up (comedy), singing her original music, and teaching children all around Austin. But on this very special occasion, she invites you to attend a dramatic reading of her Three Stooges Fanfiction! Nyuk Nyuk Nyuk…

people.name: Walter Somerville
people.bio: Walter is a designer from Asheville, NC who got into the theatre the way everybody does – dating an actress! He loves making and playing board games, playing synthesizers, and reading Stephen King. He does tech for “It Was a Dark and Stormy Night” and many other tech-heavy shows at UCB that involve live video projection.

people.name: Wander Boesjes
people.bio: Wander Boesjes is a comedian, writer, and actor based in Brooklyn. Wander can be seen every Lloyd Night performing on the Alchemy. Wander can also be seen performing with indie teams Hooper and Go Dummy, who have been featured in the New York Comedy Festival, the Third Coast Comedy Festival, and the SteelStacks Improv Festival.

people.name: Wayland McQueen
people.bio: Wayland has been performing at UCB since 2008. He’s been alive longer than that.

people.name: Wilfredo Diaz
people.bio: Wilfredo Diaz is a Brooklyn-based improv and sketch comedian. He got his comedy roots from training in the Philadelphia comedy scene and paved his way to New York in 2020.

people.name: Will Bangs
people.bio: Will Bangs (he/him) is a performer and writer based in Brooklyn and the former world record holder for youngest person ever. He can currently be seen on UCB’s Lloyd Night and as a writer/performer for Brooklyn sketch team ‘Procrastination’. You can follow him on instagram @willbangs or on his substack ‘Literature Imperfect’.

people.name: Will Choi
people.bio: Will Choi is a SAG actor based in Los Angeles, best known for his voice-over roles in BoJack Horseman , Bob’s Burgers , Central Park , The Loud House , The Casagrandes , Tokyo Revengers , Clash Royale , and many more. Will is also the creator of the Asian American comedy variety show Asian AF.

people.name: Will Cleveland
people.bio: Will Cleveland was trained in improvisational theater as a boy by one Sir. Mortimer Clarke. He took his first UCB improv class in 2008, and now teaches UCB improv classes in Austin, TX where he has served as Artistic Director of ColdTowne Theater since 2016. You can see him play in Stool Pigeon every Friday at ColdTowne Theater.

people.name: Will Gonzalez
people.bio: Will Gonzalez is a comedian and writer based in Brooklyn, NY. He currently writes on UCB’s Maude Night with his team Mom’s World, and he’s a cast member of the Brooklyn Comedy Collective’s 2026 MainStage Residency. He previously performed on UCB’s Lloyd Night with team Day Trip, and he also performs improv with indie teams Boy Basement and The People’s Princess.

people.name: Will Heitert
people.bio: Will Heitert is a model, actor, stylist, and comedian (MASC for short). They’ve gone from being the funniest girl in fashion, to the most fashionable girl in comedy and it’s done wonders for their mental health. Venmo: @William-Heitert

people.name: Will Hines
people.bio: Will grew up in New England and was a computer programmer throughout his 20s until he discovered the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre in NYC when he was 30. He’s been on a million improv and sketch teams at UCB, including running the school for a few years in NYC. By his mid-40s he was doing commercials and small TV parts including: Brooklyn 99, Broad City, Arrested Development. Today he dominates both film and television as an unshakably powerful comedic presence.

people.name: Will Jacobs
people.bio: Will Jacobs is an Actor, Writer, and all-around New York City Big Shot. He’s performed on several house teams at the Upright Citizens Brigade and appeared on HBO’s The Other Two. His webseries, Olympus: A Retrospective, won various awards, including the Best of Late Night at Denver CO’s Seriesfest. He’s a fan of NBA basketball, old videogames, and terrible gen-z meme rap.

people.name: Will Martinez
people.bio: Will Martinez is a Puerto Rican writer, performer and musician, based out of the Bronx. He has performed in comedy clubs and theaters all across the country.

people.name: William Gianetta
people.bio: Will Gianetta is an improviser from Somerville, MA.

people.name: Woody Fu
people.bio: As an actor, Woody Fu (he/him) has appeared on ABC, CBS, FX, HBO Max, HULU, Comedy Central, Peacock, Netflix, AppleTV+, and Amazon. As a creator, you may have seen him as a New Face of Comedy at the Just For Laughs festival, or in Edinburgh Fringe touring his show, Woody Fu: One Man John Wick . He has written for the Onion, and his digital shorts have been featured on Vulture and at the Slamdance Film Festival.

people.name: Wyatt Cenac
people.bio: Wyatt Cenac is an Emmy Award-winning comedian, whose credits include Wyatt Cenac’s Problem Areas, People of the Earth, The Daily Show, King of the Hill and the feature film Medicine for Melancholy. He’s released four stand up albums, two stand up specials and hosted a long running stand up showcase in Brooklyn called Night Train. He’s not really on social media, but he’s got a website: wyattcenac.com

people.name: Wyatt Gildea
people.bio: Wyatt is a comedian and writer in Brooklyn (like everyone) from North Carolina (like some). He has never played croquet but would like to give it a try.

people.name: X Mayo
people.bio: X Mayo is a producer, writer, comedian, and taco expert. Her brand Who Made The Potato Salad has done entertainment workshops that have fostered community and have gotten minorities hired within the industry. Their alumni have gone on to win NAACP Awards and gain employment at; The Daily Show, Showtime, Netflix, and Comedy Central. When X is not building her own table you can catch her on your screen starring in The Blackening, Freakier Friday, Season 3 of LOOT, Scrubs Reboot, and the new Marvel Series Wonderman on Disney Plus.

people.name: Xavier Curry
people.bio: Xavier is an improviser, singer and songwriter currently performing with Dr Beer on Lloyd night! When not performing improv around the city, he is crafting funny and heartfelt music. His most notable TV appearance is on NBC’s a cappella competition The Sing-Off with the Dartmouth Aires. Under the artist name Xav A, his song Let It Be has surpassed 400k streams on Spotify.

people.name: Yaki Margulies
people.bio: Yaki Margulies is an actor, writer, musician, stand-up comedian, and improviser from Seattle, WA. He is a current UCB performer on the Lloyd team, Freak Trap, co-hosts the long-running indie improv show, “Carol’s Poolhouse: Lost & Found,” has been a part of the improvised movie show at UCB for 8 years, and was on the Mess Hall team, Shimmy! He’s also been in every commercial ever.

people.name: Yamara Taylor
people.bio: Yamara Taylor graduated from UCLA’s School of Theater and Film and studied improv and sketch comedy at the Upright Citizen’s Brigade Theater in Los Angeles. She got her start on animated series, THE BOONDOCKS. Some of her credits include the ABC comedy, BLACK-ISH, DAVE (FXX), SAVED BY THE BELL (Peacock) and consulting for THE MAYOR (NBC) and RAP SH*T (HBO Max). She also writes features, including projects for Kevin Hart, Gabrielle Union and a musical comedy for contemporary artist Hebru Brantley’s Angry Hero production company. She’s served twice as a mentor for LOL’S Women Right Now screenwriting fellowship. Yamara is a Scorpio.

people.name: Yasi Mousavi
people.bio: Yasi Mousavi is an LA-based standup and writer. When she dies, she wants to turned into ash and rolled into a pack of Parliament 100s for selected friends and family to enjoy.

people.name: Yazmin Monet Watkins
people.bio: Yazmin Monet Watkins is a poet, comedian, screenwriter, actress, educator, organizer, avid roller skater and adventurer. She can be seen on season two of Grand Crew and has co-developed and written pilots with Netflix and Comedy Central alongside Paul Downs, Lucia Aniello and the all Black female comedy group, Obama’s Other Daughters. She has empowered audiences at countless universities, festivals, and institutions and has had commissioned work with prestigious institutions including the Los Angeles County Museum of Art, the California African American Museum, Center Theatre Group and The Lincoln Center. Her full length book of poems, A Vessel Born to Float with Andrews McMeel Publishing is available now. Watkins’ serves as the co-chair of the Arts & Culture committee for Black Lives Matter Los Angeles and her body of work weaves art and activism, exploring the intersection of race, gender, sexuality, self-love and all things Black Girl Magic. Once Beyoncé said she liked her hair.

people.name: Yoni Lotan
people.bio: Yoni Lotan is a comedic actor/writer based in NY. An alumni to Harold, Maude, and as well as the Touring Company, Yoni has been heavily involved with the UCB theatre for years. With an extensive resume including roles in Russian Doll, High Maintenance, and more recently the PLEASE DON’T DESTROY movie, He currently voices a lead role in the new animated show “Unlimited Squirrels” for HBO MAX. In 2017, Yoni was selected for the “New Face: Characters” showcase in the Just For Laughs Comedy Festival in Montreal. Follow Yoni on his instagram (@yonilotan) to see his videos that have been shared and seen on Buzzfeed, Gawker, CNN, The Today Show, and much more!

people.name: Zach Jobe
people.bio: Zach Jobe is a Los Angeles based comedian, writer and actor. He’s the creator and co-host of the acclaimed podcast True Deception. You may have seen him performing with his many improv comedy teams or online in a comedy sketch (though based on the views I wouldn’t bet money on it).

people.name: Zach Olsen
people.bio: Zach is a comedian, writer, actor and musician. He performs as Tommy Wiseau in Better Movies w/ “Tommy Wiseau” at UCB and can also be seen in many other shows at the theater. He often performs with his Japanese-language improv/sketch team Nihongo Dake. He is also the co-host of the podcast Baywatch Watch.

people.name: Zach Young
people.bio: Zach Young is a performer and writer who grew up in Brooklyn. He teaches improv classes at the UCB Training Center and performs on Harold Night with his team Sharlene. He loves cheap coffee and gaming.

people.name: Zachary Clark
people.bio: Zachary Clark is a Latinx African American actor, comedian, writer, and artist based in NYC. He is known for playing Cory Flock in Kyle Mooney’s Y2K (A24), as well as appearances in Only Murders in the Building (Hulu) and the upcoming film Jackrabbit. After training in the Strasberg Method and at the Upright Citizens Brigade, Zach can be seen performing with The Trial, directed by Achilles Stamatelaky and his epic indie team Blumpkin. You can also catch him attempting the perfect kickflip on his skateboard.

people.name: Zachary Clark
people.bio: Zachary Clark is a Latinx African American actor, comedian, writer, and artist based in NYC. He is known for playing Cory Flock in Kyle Mooney‚Äôs Y2K (A24), as well as appearances in Only Murders in the Building (Hulu) and the upcoming film Jackrabbit. After training in the Strasberg Method and at the Upright Citizens Brigade, Zach can be seen performing with The Trial, directed by Achilles Stamatelaky and his epic indie team Blumpkin. You can also catch him attempting the perfect kickflip on his skateboard.

people.name: Zack Colonna
people.bio: Zack Colonna is an actor, comedian, and creator. Most recently Zack can be seen on CBS’s The Neighborhood and NBC’s Kenan . He was also a cast member for the 2020 CBS Showcase in Los Angeles. Other comedy credits include numerous shows with Second City Chicago, iO Chicago, and The Rockwell (RIP). His theatre credits include National Tours with the Kennedy Center as well as regional productions at The Lyric Opera, Drury Lane, Ford’s Theatre, Signature Theatre, Olney Theatre Center, Minetta Lane, and many more.

people.name: Zack Phillips
people.bio: Zack is a New York-based writer and performer. He co-wrote the Audible comedy series “64th Man” starring John Cena. Previously, he was head writer at Above Average and its sports site, The Kicker, and wrote at The Onion, Clickhole, and other places. At UCB, Zack has been teaching for many years, and wrote and performed sketch and improv on numerous Harold and Maude teams. He’s a big Cincinnati Reds fan, unfortunately.

people.name: Zack Willis
people.bio: Zack Willis is a New York City-based writer, musician, actor, and comedian. Zack has been performing and writing comedy in NYC since there was a V subway line. He is a founding member of musical improv group Rumpleteaser. His band, BRVNCHES, released their first single in 2023. They are an NYC-based-LA-indie-synth-bedroom-pop rupture.

people.name: Zackery Alexzander Stephens
people.bio: Zackery Alexzander Stephens most recently staffed on the popular pirate comedy Our Flag Means Death (HBO Max) and the late night series The Amber Ruffin Show (Peacock). They currently write and direct for ASSSSKETCH, UCB LA’s premier sketch show. Zackery learned to drive on a tractor and were raised on a farm outside the rural and inaccurately self-titled, “Palm Springs” of Washington State. Feel free to ask them about the time they were dumped on the Coney Island Ferris Wheel.

people.name: Zackry Colston
people.bio: Zackry Colston is an actor and writer from Tucson, Arizona. He has appeared in Apple TV’s Roar , Netflix’s Boys in the Band , and dozens of national commercials. He also wrote a book titled Squinting at the Moon .

people.name: Zahaira Curiel
people.bio: Zahaira Curiel, a New Yorker with Dominican and Puerto Rican roots, is a LA & NYC-based artist. She is an actress, singer, voice actress, writer, and producer with a BA in Music from Hunter College. Her journey began in theater in 2011 and since then has had the pleasure of performing in NYC, LA and touring around the country. She is currently on two sketch teams on two different coasts! Dumptruck at the Upright Citizens Brigade in New York City and Kissyface at The Pack Theatre in Los Angeles. She co-produced, co-wrote and starred in the sold out all-women production of CrazySexyCruel and Mama Always Said at The PIT. She recently made her t.v. debut on FBI: Most Wanted. When she’s not performing, writing or producing, you can find her spending time with family and friends or somewhere eating dessert (she even has a dessert company! Sassy’s Sweets) or on a rollercoaster. You can follow her @zahairacuriel.

people.name: Zeke Nicholson
people.bio: Zeke Nicholson is a writer/actor/producer from a small town in upstate New York where people go leaf peeping for fun. He’s written on shows like Marvel’s “Wonder Man” (Disney +), “Loot” (Apple TV+), “A.P. Bio” (NBC), and the DC Studios Jimmy Olsen spinoff (HBO). As an actor he’s been on “The Good Place” (NBC), “Miracle Workers” (TBS), “The Sex Lives of College Girls” (HBO), and more. Zeke won the Special Jury Award in the Independent TV Pilot Competition at SXSW in 2024 for his short “Halfrican American,” which he wrote, directed and starred in. He’s also a regular performer on Dropout TV. Dope.

people.name: Zheryk Badugu
people.bio: This millennial multi-hyphenate has been performing improv for almost 20 years. He has trained and performed in Dallas, Chicago, and Los Angeles. Catch him if you want some hip hop tinged, musical forward, high energy, character work.

people.name: Zhubin Parang
people.bio: Zhubin Parang is a writer and comedian in New York City. He was on the Harold teams RAYNARD and THE BISHOP, and the Maude teams THUNDER GULCH and CHARLEMAGNE. He currently performs with WHAT I DID FOR LOVE.

people.name: Zoe Abdurrahman
people.bio: A Performer, Model, and all-around creative based in NYC.

people.name: Zoe Harley
people.bio: Hello! My name is Zoe (she/her) and I’m a rising senior at NYU Tisch with interests in improv and writing/performing sketch. I’ve also worked at UCB as event staff/tech for about a year and a half, and am currently taking Improv 201. I have written, performed in, and self produced several shows with workshop groups at NYU but am looking to expand on that. I’m seeking representation as I near graduation.

people.name: Zoe Johnson
people.bio: Zoe Johnson is a comedy writer and stand-up comedian based in Brooklyn, NY. She has honed her comedic skills through training at UCB, The PIT, The Pack Theater, BCC, Asylum, and more. Zoe currently writes for the Maude team, Gorgeous Stank, and performs stand-up around NYC. Also, “Zoe” rhymes with “Chloe” or “Joey” (not “Toe”), but she probably won’t correct you either way.

people.name: Zora Bikangaga
people.bio: Zora Bikangaga grew up the only Ugandan kid in small-town Northern California (which is a whole other story). After being a full-time 7th grade US History & Government teacher, he took a hard pivot to pursue a different kind of comedy. Zora’s writing credits include THE ‘BURBS (PEACOCK), NO GOOD DEED (NETFLIX), GHOSTS (CBS), I LOVE THAT FOR YOU (SHOWTIME), THE FIRST LADY (SHOWTIME), and ZOEY’S EXTRAORDINARY PLAYLIST (NBC). His acting credits include I LOVE THAT FOR YOU , DRUNK HISTORY , KROLL SHOW , and he starred alongside Sarah Hyland, Tyler James Williams, and Wanda Sykes in the romantic comedy, THE WEDDING YEAR . As a storyteller, Zora’s infamous college prank tale was featured in an episode of THIS AMERICAN LIFE .

people.name: Zoë Tanton
people.bio: Zoë is a Brooklyn-based actor, writer, and comedian. She is from Arizona, which is unique. She performs with Dr Beer on Lloyd Night and with her indie improv team, Midwives, who have been featured in iO Fest, Boom Chicago Comedy Festival, and Fun & Dumb Improv Festival. She says hi!
